ALCOHOLIC LIQUORS
Page
1. An Act providing the means whereby the
sense of the people of any county , city, town,
district or precinct may be taken as to
whether spirituous , vinous or malt liquors
shall be sold , bartered or loaned therein ;
providing for committees of interested parties
and their representation at the election ; pro-
viding a means for contesting said election ;
prohibiting the sale , barter , loan procurement
and giving of such liquors ; declaring certain
acts and conditions as nuisances and provid-
ing for the abatement of same and forfeiture
of property ; providing for searches and sei-
zures ; conferring jurisdiction upon inferior
federal courts ; and providing penalties for its
violation . ( Senate Bill No. 182 , Mr. Wesley.) ..
Chapter 2.
APPROPRIATIONS
An Act authorizing , empowering and directing
the Governor to cause to be made an audit ,
study and survey of Departments , Institu-
tions , Boards , Commissions and Agencies of
the Commonwealth , and providing an appro-
priation in the sum of $120,000.00 , to be used
by the Governor in carrying out the pur-
poses of the Act . (Senate Bill No. 14 , Mr.
McDonald .)
Chapter 3. An Act appropriating the sum of $ 75.00 per
month for the use of the widow of Powell
Hayes , deceased . (Senate Bill No. 38 , Mr.
Moore.)
Chapter 4. An Act appropriating the sum of $ 75.00 per
month for the use of the widow and children
of Robert Rowland, deceased . (Senate Bill
No. 37 , Mr. Moore.)

providing State aid for the improvement , re-
construction and maintenance of county
roads ; prescribing certain duties of the
State Highway Commission and the Fiscal
Courts in relation thereto ; making an annual
appropriation from the State Road Fund .
(Senate Bill No. 329 , Mr. Nickell .)

98a -2 , and subsection 98a -5 of Section 98 of

method of appointment , removal and salary.
(House Bill No. 153 , Mr. Wells .)

Fixing the number of persons who may asso-
ciate themselves together to establish a loan

An Act to repeal , amend and re -enact sub-
section 2 , Chapter 28 , of the Acts of 1918 ,
being Section 2223b -2 , Kentucky Statutes ,
Carroll's Edition , 1930 , relating to the organi .
zation of loan and investment companies in
cities of the first and second classes and ex-
tending the terms thereof to cities of the
fifth class ; designating the number of per-
sons necessary to associate themselves to-
gether in order to establish such company ,
and prescribing the amount of capital stock
necessary for the organization of such com-
pany in cities of the different classes as
herein set out , and providing that the cor-
poration formed under this Act may contain
the words "industrial bank " . (Senate Bill
No. 1, Mr. Gibson .)
Chapter 11. An Act to promote the objects of the Na-
tional Housing Act by authorizing the banks,
savings banks , trust companies , insurance
companies , building and loan associations ,
credit unions , trustees and other fiduciaries
to make loans eligible for insurance under the
National Housing Act , and by authorizing
banks , savings banks , trust companies , insur-
ance companies , building and loan associa
tions , credit unions , trustees and other fidu-
ciaries , the Commonwealth of Kentucky and
its political subdivision and any agency or in-
strumentality thereof, to invest in mortgages

Housing Administrator , and debentures issued
by the National Mortgage Associations as col-
lateral or deposit security where required by
any statute of this State . ( Senate Bill No. 4,
Mr. Turner .)

and 613 of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , 1930

ate Bill No. 94, Mr. Harrison .) 38

Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's Edition , 1930 , to
increase the surplus of banks , trust com

panies , organized under the laws of this
State . ( Senate Bill No. 140 , Mr. Harrison .) .... 43

and combined banks and trust companies and
to amend Section 579 , Kentucky Statutes ,
Carroll's Edition , 1930 , to provide exemption

eral Deposit Insurance Corporation . ( Senate
Bill No. 95 , Mr. Harrison .)

INSTITUTIONS
Chapter 15. An Act relating to the release of prisoners
from penitentiaries , state reformatories and
houses of reform in the Commonwealth of
Kentucky , and providing for notice to county
officials and district attorneys . (House Bill
No. 18 , Mr. Scheben .)
Chapter 16. An Act to amend Chapter 16 of the Acts of
1928 by adding after Section 77 thereof, and
before Section 78 thereof, a new section to be
entitled Section 77a , and providing for the
temporary commitment for care and obser-
vation of persons whose mental condition is
so abnormal as to require their temporary
44

commitment for care and observation pending
a determination whether or not they are
insane . ( Senate Bill No. 201 , Mr. Trager .)
Chapter 17. An Act relating to prisoners now or here-
after confined in State prisons ; providing for
the working of such prisoners on prison
farms, highways , and other public works ; pro-
viding for the care , supervision , disciplining ,
maintenance , transportation, and housing of
such prisoners while so engaged and for the
payment of costs and expenses thereof ; au-
thorizing payment of compensation of such
prisoners and their dependent families .
(House Bill No. 121 , Mr. Brown .)
CLAIMS AGAINST COMMONWEALTH
Chapter 18. An Act to permit J. W. Beams to sue the
State of Kentucky . (House Bill No. 229 , Mr.
Hammons .)
CONSTITUTIONAL AMENDMENTS
Chapter 19. An Act proposing an amendment to the Con-
stitution to be submitted to the electorate ;
which amendment authorizes the General As-
sembly to reorganize government within the
territorial confines of counties . To be known
as the Garnett-Mayer Act . (House Bill No.
573 , Mr. Garnett .)
Chapter 20. An Act to amend Section 256 of the Constitu-
tion of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , pre-
scribing the mode of amendment of said Con-
stitution ; providing that amendment to the
Constitution may be proposed in either house
of the General Assembly at a regular or spe-
cial session thereof , and if such amendment or
amendments shall be agreed to by three-fifths
of all the members elected to each house ,
such proposed amendment or amendments ,
with the yeas and nays of each house taken
thereon , shall be entered in full in their re-
spective journals . (House Bill No. 561 , Mr.
McDermott .) -----------------------

of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , Baldwin's Re-
vision , Supplement to 1933 , relating to the
issuance of bonds or other obligations of the
county , city, municipality or other taxing
districts , and providing for the approval of
said bonds by the court , (Senate Bill No.
203 , Mr. Gibson .)
Chapter 22. An Act empowering and authorizing the
Court of Appeals of Kentucky to render ad-
visory opinion as to the constitutionality of
any bill or measure pending in either the
House or Senate . ( House Bill No. 8 , Mr.
Ollie J. Bowen .)
Chapter 23. An Act to subject to execution and garnish-
ment salaries of state , county , school board
and municipal employees ; and providing in
which courts any such suit shall be filed .
(House Bill No. 189 , Mr. Baker.)
Chapter 24. An Act relating to fiscal courts composed of
County Judge and County Commissioners , re-
pealing and re- enacting Section 1850 , Ken-
tucky Statutes , Carroll's Edition , 1930 ; and
providing for the County Judge to cast the
deciding vote in a case of tie vote or deadlock
which has continued for a period of not less
than fifteen days . (House Bill No. 156 , Mr.
Shields .)
Chapter 25. An Act to amend and re-enact Chapter 66,
Article 11 , Section 2117 , Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , 1930 , relating to divorce by adding
thereto certain causes for divorce to the hus-
band . Such cruel beating or injury , or at-
tempt at injury , of the husband by the wife
as indicates an outrageous temper in her or
probable danger to his life or great bodily
injury in his remaining with her . (Senate
Bill No. 136 , Mr. Richardson .) ............................

Bill No. 25 , Mr. Tackett .)
Chapter 28. An Act amending and re-enacting Section

tion (Baldwin's Revision , 1933 Supplement ) ;
providing for the appointing of jury commis-
sioners by the circuit judge of each county in
this State . (Senate Bill No. 153 , Mr. Gibson .)
Chapter 29. An Act exempting persons engaged in , con-
nected with or employed on any newspaper
from disclosing in any legal proceeding or
trial before any court, grand jury or petit
jury, or the presiding officer of any tribunal ,
or his agent or agents , or before the General
Assembly, or any Committee thereof, the

tained by him and published in the newspaper
on which he is engaged , connected with or
employed . ( House Bill No. 203 , Mr. Joe E.
Robinson.)
Chapter 30. An Act to provide for the postponement of
the rendition of judgment in criminal cases
and empowering the Department of Public
Welfare to appoint a director of probation
and parole; defining the powers and duties of
such parole officer ; repealing page 148 , Sec-
tions 1, 2, 3 and 4 of the Acts of the General
Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky
for the year 1934 , being Sections 979b -1 ,
979b-2, 979b -3 and 979b -4 of Baldwin's 1934
Edition of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes .
(House Bill No. 553 , Mr. Robinson .)

tucky. ( Senate Bill No. 110 , Senator Wil-

An Act to prohibit marathons , danceathons ,

person , firm or corporation participating in ,
aiding in or promoting such contests . (House
Bill No. 337 , Mr. Ward .)

252 , Mr. Scheben .)

arate drainage districts ; providing that in

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
OF THE
COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
PASSED AT THE REGULAR SESSION OF THE GENERAL
ASSEMBLY , WHICH WAS BEGUN IN THE CITY OF
FRANKFORT , KENTUCKY , ON TUESDAY , JANUARY THE
SEVENTH , AND ENDED ON SATURDAY , FEBRUARY THE
FIFTEENTH , NINETEEN HUNDRED THIRTY -SIX .
CHAPTER 1 .
AN ACT providing the means whereby the sense of the people
of any county , city , town, district or precinct may be taken
as to whether spirituous , vinous or malt liquors shall be sold ,
bartered or loaned therein ; providing for committees of in-
terested parties and their representation at the election ; pro-
viding a means for contesting said election ; prohibiting the
sale , loaning, furnishing or giving of intoxicating liquors on
the day of election ; defining spirituous , vinous or malt liquors
and other terms ; prohibiting the sale , barter , loan procure-
ment and giving of such liquors , and the possession and
transportation thereof for said purposes , in territory voting
therefor, excepting sacramental wine and certain other alco-
holic liquors and uses , and sales by distillers and manufac-
turers ; declaring certain acts and conditions as nuisances
and providing for the abatement of same and forfeitures of
property in certain instances ; providng for searches and
seizures ; declaring the duties of courts , judges and other
officers ; conferring jurisdiction upon inferior courts concur-
rent with circuit courts ; and providing for enforcement of the
Act and penalties for its violation .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
DIVISION ONE (I)
Section One (1 ) . Definitions . As used in this
Act the following terms , unless the text otherwise

2 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
indicates , shall have the following meanings respec-
tively .
(a) " Local option territory " or " territory "
means such county , city , town , district or precinct
for which a petition shall be filed asking for an
election to take the sense of the voters regarding the
sale , barter and loan of intoxicating liquors as pro-
vided , and such governmental unit where a majority
shall have voted in favor of prohibiting the sale ,
barter or loan of said liquors or the possession or
transportation thereof .
(b) " Spirituous , vinous or malt liquors " means ,
respectively , intoxicating liquor . " Intoxicating
liquor " means alcoholic brandy , whiskey , rum , gin ,
beer , ale , porter and wine and in addition thereto any
spirituous , vinous , malt or fermented liquors , liquids
and compounds , whether medicated , proprietary ,
patented or not , and by whatever name called con-
taining one per centum or more of alcohol by volume
which are fit for use for beverage purposes .
(c) The word " person " includes the singular
and plural number , and shall mean and include a
natural person , association , co -partnership or cor-
poration .
(d ) " Election " means an election to be held for
the purpose of taking the sense of the people as to
the prohibition or permission of the sale , barter or
loan of spirituous , vinous or malt liquors in any
territory .
(e) " Prohibition " means that the sale , barter
or loan of intoxicating liquors have been rendered.
unlawful under the terms of this Act .
(f) " Local option law " refers to the provisions
of this Act .
DIVISION TWO (II )
Section Two ( 2 ) . Petition for election . Upon ap-
plication by written petition filed with the clerk of

CHAPTER 1 3
the county court of any county signed by a number
of legal voters in said county , or any city , town ,
district or precinct thereof to be affected , equal to
twenty-five ( 25 ) per cent of the votes cast in said
territory at the last preceding general election , it
shall be the duty of the judge of the county court of
such county at the current or the next regular term
of said court thereafter to make an order on the
order book of said court directing an election to be
held in such county , city , town , district or precinct
to be affected thereby , for the purpose of taking the
sense of the legal voters of such county , city , town ,
district or precinct upon the proposition whether
or not spirituous , vinous or malt liquors shall be
sold , bartered or loaned therein .
Section Three ( 3 ) . Subscription to petition .
Said petition , in addition to the subscription of the
name of the voter , shall state his postoffice address
and the correct date upon which same was signed .
No such subscriber shall be permitted to withdraw
his name or have it taken from such petition after
same shall have been filed as aforesaid . Provided ,
that if the name of any person shall have been sub-
scribed to such petition without his authority , he
may appear before the county judge before said
election shall be ordered held and upon proof to
that effect his name may be eliminated by an order
of the court and when so eliminated , the same shall
not be counted as a petitioner . Said petition may
consist of one or more separate units .
Section Four (4 ) . Day of election . The day upon
which the election shall be held may be stated in the
petition . If it is not so stated , it shall be designated
by the county judge ; provided
(a) The election shall not be earlier than sixty
or later than ninety days after the date said appli-

DIVISION TWO (II )
Section Two ( 2 ) . Petition for election . Upon ap-
plication by written petition filed with the clerk of

CHAPTER 1 3
the county court of any county signed by a number
of legal voters in said county , or any city, town ,
district or precinct thereof to be affected , equal to
twenty-five ( 25 ) per cent of the votes cast in said
territory at the last preceding general election , it
shall be the duty of the judge of the county court of
such county at the current or the next regular term
of said court thereafter to make an order on the
order book of said court directing an election to be
held in such county , city , town , district or precinct
to be affected thereby , for the purpose of taking the
sense of the legal voters of such county , city , town ,
district or precinct upon the proposition whether
or not spirituous , vinous or malt liquors shall be
sold , bartered or loaned therein .
Section Three (3) . Subscription to petition .
Said petition , in addition to the subscription of the
name of the voter , shall state his postoffice address
and the correct date upon which same was signed .
No such subscriber shall be permitted to withdraw
his name or have it taken from such petition after
same shall have been filed as aforesaid . Provided ,
that if the name of any person shall have been sub-
scribed to such petition without his authority , he
may appear before the county judge before said
election shall be ordered held and upon proof to
that effect his name may be eliminated by an order
of the court and when so eliminated , the same shall
not be counted as a petitioner . Said petition may
consist of one or more separate units .
Section Four ( 4) . Day of election . The day upon
which the election shall be held may be stated in the
petition . If it is not so stated , it shall be designated
by the county judge ; provided
(a) The election shall not be earlier than sixty
or later than ninety days after the date said appli-

4 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
cation is lodged in the office of the clerk of the county
court .
(b) The election shall not be held on the same
day that a primary or general election is held in the
territory or any part thereof , nor within thirty days
next preceding or following a regular political elec-
tion .
( c ) No election in any territory less than the
county shall be held on the same day on which an
election for the entire county is held .
(d ) No election shall be held in the same terri-
tory oftener than once in every three years .
Section Five ( 5 ) . Notice of election . Within
five days after said order of the county court calling
an election is made , it shall be the duty of the county
clerk to give to the sheriff of the county a certified
copy of the same. It shall then be the duty of the
said sheriff to have same published in some weekly
or daily newspaper published in the county for at
least two weeks before the election , and also to ad-
vertise same by written or printed handbills posted
at not less than five conspicuous places in each pre-
cinct of the county for the same length of time when
the election is held for the entire county ; and when
the election is ordered held in a city , town , district
or precinct , then at five conspicuous places therein
for the same length of time . In case there is no daily
or weekly newspaper published in the county , or the
proprietor of such newspaper refuses to publish
said advertisement , the printed or written handbills
posted as herein provided for shall be sufficient . It
shall be the duty of the sheriff to report in writing
to the county court that the notices have been duly
published and posted .
Section Six ( 6 ) . Form of ballot . The proposi-
tion to be voted upon shall be stated on the ballot

CHAPTER 1 5 .
without emblems and two spaces left upon the right
side of same , one for votes favoring the proposition
to be designated by the word " Yes ," and one for
votes opposing it to be designated by the word
"No." The elector shall designate his vote by a
cross mark , thus ( X ) , placed opposite the word
"Yes" or the word " No. " Whenever at the time
of the election intoxicating liquor is being legally
sold in the territory the form of the proposition
may be thus stated :
Are you in favor of adopting
the local option law in name
of county , city , town , district
or precinct
Yes
No
Whenever at the time of the election intoxicating
liquor is not being legally sold in the territory the
form of the proposition may be thus stated :
Are you in favor of continuing
the local option law in effect in
(name of county , city , town , dis-
trict or precinct
Yes
No
Section Seven (7 ) . Elections according to gen-
eral laws ; duties of officers ; cost . The election , in-
cluding the canvass and counting of the ballots , shall
be held in accordance with the provisions of the
general election laws of the State , and the duties
of all officers pertaining thereto shall devolve upon
and shall be performed by each of them in relation
to said election the same as in general elections ,
except as same may be modified by or are incon-
sistent with the terms of this Act .
The cost of the election shall be borne by the
county and allowed by the fiscal court and paid in
the same manner as costs and expenses of general
elections .

6 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
4444444
Section Eight ( 8 ) . Election offenses . Any act or
deed denounced as an offense by the general laws
of the State concerning elections shall also be an
offense in elections held under the provisions of this
Act , and shall be punished in the same form and
manner as is provided for the punishment of similar
offenses by the general laws .
Section Nine ( 9 ) . Citizens ' committees . Not
more than twenty days prior to an election any
group of citizens or committee which in good faith
advocates or opposes the proposition to be sub-
mitted may file with the chairman of the county elec-
tion commission a petition asking that such peti-
tioners be recognized as the committee entitled to
nominate election officers and challengers and wit-
nesses to the count of the ballots and certification
of the results , and to name guards to assist in
guarding the ballot boxes during such period of time
as said committee may desire . If more than one
group claim the right to such recognition as repre-
senting the respective opponents by filing such peti-
tion, the county board of election commissioners
shall promptly decide and publicly announce which
committee is entitled to nominate such officers ,
challengers , witnesses and guards . Such decision ,
however , shall not be final , but any aggrieved party
may institute proceedings in the county court, and
upon hearing the county judge shall determine
which of such committees shall be recognized .
Section Ten ( 10 ) . Election officers , challengers
and witnesses . The respective committees advo-
cating and opposing the proposition shall each file
with the chairman of the election commission before
the time designated for the selection of officers a
list of not more than four persons possessing the
qualifications of election officers for each voting
precinct , and it shall be the duty of the election com-

CHAPTER 1
mission to appoint said officers from such lists , as
provided in the next following section . Said com-
mittees may sign and issue certificates designating
challengers at the respective polls and witnesses to
the count of the ballot in the same manner as politi-
cal committees under the provisions of the general
election laws .
Section Eleven ( 11 ) . Appointing election offi-
cers , challengers , witnesses and guards . The county
election commission not more than fifteen and not
less than ten days before the day appointed for the
holding of the election shall appoint special officers
to conduct the same in the manner provided by law ,
except they shall make equal division between those
favoring and opposing the proposition instead of
between opposing political parties . The same equal
division of challengers at the polls , of witnesses
at the count , and of guards to guard the boxes , shall
be made .
Section Twelve ( 12 ) . Certification of result . The
result of said election shall be certified by the elec-
tion commission and the certificate shall be imme-
diately lodged with the county court clerk and by
him safely kept until the next regular term of the
county court, at which time the judge of said court
shall have same spread on the order book . The
entry of said certificate , or an attested copy thereof,
shall be prima facie evidence in proceedings under
this Act .
Section Thirteen (13 ) . Contests . Any qualified
elector may demand a recount of the ballots or con-
test the election in the same manner as is now pro-
vided by law for the recount of ballots or contest
of general elections of county officers , being Section
one thousand five hundred ninety-six a - twelve
( 1596a - 12 ) of Baldwin's Supplement to Kentucky
Statutes of one thousand nine hundred thirty -three
T

8 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
( 1933 ) . The members of the county election com-
mission shall be named as contestees and process
shall be served upon them . But any qualified elector
may intervene by filing a petition to be made a party
in the action , and he shall thereupon become a con-
testee .
Section Fourteen ( 14 ) . Sale on election day pro-
hibited . Upon each day that an election is held
under the provisions of this Act , all saloons and
other places where intoxicating liquors are sold by
authority of law in the county in which said terri-
tory affected is situated shall be closed and shall
be kept closed during the whole of said day . It shall
be unlawful during said day for any person in said
county to sell , loan , furnish or give to any person
any intoxicating liquor except in such cases as are
excluded from the provisions of this Act . Any per-
son found guilty under the provisions of this section
shall be subject to the penalties prescribed in Sec-
tion twenty -eight ( 28 ) of this Act .
Section Fifteen ( 15 ) . Effective date of local op-
tion law ; refund of portion of licenses paid . When-
ever a majority of the votes cast at an election shall
be in favor of prohibiting the sale of intoxicating
liquor in the territory in which the election shall
have been held , the law prohibiting the same shall
be in force and effect at the expiration of sixty days
from the date of the entry of the certificate of the
election commission in the order book of the county
court . After the expiration of said period , no license
theretofore issued in said territory under the laws
of this State shall be of any force or effect , but the
owner of said license shall be entitled to recover
from the county , city , town or district , to which the
license money was paid, such proportional part
thereof as the unexpired period of the license bears .
to the whole period covered thereby .

CHAPTER 1 9
Section Sixteen ( 16 ) . Rejection of local option
law ; effective date . Whenever a majority of the
votes cast at an election favor the discontinuance
of the local option law in the territory in which the
election shall have been held ( the said law having
been previously in effect in such territory ) the same
shall be in force and effect at the expiration of sixty
days from the date of the entry of the certificate
of the election commission in the order book of the
county court.
Section Seventeen ( 17 ) . Effect of county adopt-
ing. When an election is held in an entire county
and the majority of votes cast at said election are
against the sale , barter or loan of intoxicating
liquor , then the terms of Division three ( 3 ) of this
Act shall be applicable to every portion of said
county . If at such election for the entire county the
majority of votes cast are in favor of the sale , barter
or loan of such liquors , said vote shall not operate to
make the terms of Division three ( 3 ) of this Act in-
applicable in any territorial division of said county
where the local option law has been previously
adopted , but the status of such territorial division
shall remain as if no such election had been held .
DIVISION THREE ( 3 )
Section Eighteen ( 18 ) . Prohibition in local op-
tion territory. Whenever prohibition shall have
become effective under the provisions of this Act
through the adoption by a majority vote , it shall
be unlawful for any person in such local option ter-
ritory to sell , barter , loan , give , procure for or
furnish another , or keep or transport for sale ,
barter or loan directly or indirectly , any spirituous ,
vinous or malt liquors , or intoxicating liquor , as de-
fined in Section one ( 1 ) of this Act ; subject , how-

Section Twenty -Two (22 ) . Shipments in transit
not affected . The provisions of the foregoing two
sections shall not apply to the transportation of
such liquor through local option territory to a point
or destination in some other State , or to a point in
this State where intoxicating liquor may be lawfully
sold ; nor to the receipt or acceptance by a common
carrier from a distiller or manufacturer for trans-
portation to a point in another State or to a point
in this State where the sale of intoxicating liquor
is not prohibited by law ; nor shall they apply to one
taking wine into such territory and delivering the
same to an officer of a church to be used for sacra-
mental purposes .
Section Twenty -Three ( 23 ) . Where sale takes
place in C. O. D. shipments . All shipments of in-
toxicating liquor to be paid for on delivery , com-
monly called C. O. D. shipments , into local option
territory shall be unlawful and shall be deemed
sales at the place where the money is paid or the
goods delivered . The carrier and his agents selling
or delivering such goods shall be severally liable
with the vendor thereof .
Section Twenty -Four ( 24 ) . Renting of property ,
or hiring vehicle for transportation ; forfeiture ;
sale of property ; prior liens ; extent of forfeiture .
Any person knowingly or intentionally renting , hir-
ing, letting , lending , leasing or permitting the use
of, or using, any building , house , structure or prem-
ises , or part thereof, or any automobile or other
vehicle or the animal used in hauling the same , for
the purpose of unlawfully selling or transporting
or possessing intoxicating liquors , shall be con-

12 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
sidered guilty of a nuisance and of a violation of
this Act. Any property so used shall become for-
feited to the Commonwealth of Kentucky . Any
Commonwealth's attorney , county attorney , mayor
of a city or town , or any private citizen may main-
tain an action in equity in the name of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky upon relation of said officers or
citizens against the owner to forfeit such property .
In such action evidence of the general reputation
of the place or property and of the defendant shall
be admissible . Upon a judgment of forfeiture , the
court shall direct the sheriff to sell the property .
Said sale shall be had in the same manner as sales
under execution , and the same fees shall be allowed
the officers conducting such sale . The sheriff shall
pay first out of the purchase money any valid bona
fide recorded liens on the said property so sold .
The court shall have power in its discretion to order
a sale subject to said liens .
If the complaint herein is filed by a citizen , it shall
not be dismissed except upon a sworn statement
made by a complainant and his attorney , setting
forth the reasons why same should be dismissed , and
the dismissal approved in writing by the Common-
wealth's attorney or the county attorney in open
court . If the complaint be filed by a private citizen
and a sale ordered , said citizen shall receive ten
( 10 ) per cent of the net proceeds of sale of any
property under any judgment of forfeiture ,. to be
ordered paid in the judgment of the court and the
sheriff shall pay the same to said citizen out of the
proceeds of sale after deducting costs and all valid
liens.
No lien on any property so sold shall be paid un-
less same is recorded prior to the committing of
said nuisance ; unless the lienor can establish he had

CHAPTER 1 13
no knowledge of said nuisance . The burden shall
be upon the lienor to establish same .
The sheriff , after payment of liens , costs and fees ,
shall pay the balance of the proceeds of sale to the
trustee of the jury fund , who shall report same as
in all other fines and forfeitures . The forfeiture
herein provided for shall extend to the whole of the
premises , building or structure owned by the de-
fendant , or to his interest in said property , includ-
ing all land and buildings in one boundary , and
shall not be construed to mean a part thereof .
sance .
Section Twenty -Five ( 25 ) . Abatement of nui-
An action to enjoin any nuisance defined in
the next preceding section may be brought in the
name of the Commonwealth by any Commonwealth's
attorney, county attorney or any private citizen .
Such action shall be brought and tried as an action
in equity and may be brought in any court having
jurisdiction to hear and determine equity cases . If
it is made to appear by affidavits or otherwise , to
the satisfaction of the court , or judge in vacation ,
that such nuisance exists , a temporary writ of in-
junction shall forthwith issue restraining the de-
fendant from conducting or permitting the continu-
ance of such nuisance until the conclusion of the trial .
If a temporary injunction is prayed for , the court
may issue an order restraining the defendant and
all other persons from removing or in any way in-
terfering with the liquor or fixtures , or other things
used in connection with the violation of this Act
constituting such nuisance . No bond shall be re-
quired in instituting such proceedings . It shall not
be necessary for the court to find the property in-
volved was being unlawful used as aforesaid at the
time of the hearing, but on finding that the material
allegations of the petition are true , the court shall
order that no liquors shall be sold , bartered , or

14 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
stored in such room , house , building , boat , vehicle ,
structure or place , or any part thereof . And upon
judgment of the court ordering such nuisance to
be abated , the court may order that the room , house ,
building , structure, boat , vehicle , or place shall not
be occupied or used for six months thereafter ; but
the court may , in its discretion , permit it to be
occupied or used if the owner , lessee , tenant , or
occupant thereof shall give bond with sufficient
surety , to be approved by the court making the
order , in the penal and liquidated sum of not less
than five hundred ( $ 500 ) dollars nor more than one
thousand dollars ( $ 1,000 ) , payable to the Common-
wealth of Kentucky , and conditioned that intoxicat-
ing liquor will not thereafter be sold , bartered , kept ,
or otherwise disposed of therein or thereon , and that
he will pay all fines , costs , and damages that may
be assessed for any violation of this Act upon said
property .
Section Twenty - Six ( 26 ) . Transportation of
liquor ; seizure of property . When any peace offi-
cer shall discover any person in the act of illegally
possessing or transporting intoxicating liquors in
any automobile , water or air craft , or other vehicle ,
it shall be his duty to seize at once any and all in-
toxicating liquors found therein ; and to seize said
automobile , craft or vehicle , and arrest any person
or persons in charge thereof . Upon conviction of
the person so arrested , the court shall order the
liquor destroyed and shall order a sale by public
auction of the property seized . The expenses of
keeping and selling the same , and of all valid re-
corded liens which are established by intervention
at any hearing therefor as being bona fide shall be
paid out of the proceeds of such sale . The burden
of establishing that the property was being used
without the knowledge , consent or approval of the

CHAPTER 1 15
owner or lienor of the property shall be upon such
owner or lienor . After the satisfaction of such ex-
penses and liens , the court shall direct that the bal-
ance of the proceeds be paid to the trustee of the
jury fund , who shall transmit same as in all other
fines and forfeitures . The court shall order all sales
under this Act to be made by the sheriff who shall
receive and be allowed the same fees as allowed for
sales under execution .
Section Twenty - Seven ( 27 ) . No property rights .
No property rights shall exist in any intoxicating
liquor unlawfully obtained , possessed , held or used
in violation of this Act . The court , upon conviction
of the person arrested , shall order the liquor de-
stroyed . A failure upon the part of the person so
ordered to destroy all of said liquor shall be deemed
an offense and punished as a violation of this Act .
Section Twenty -Eight ( 28 ) . Penalties for viola-
tions . On a first conviction for a violation of any
provision of this Act , except for a violation of a
provision for which a particular penalty is herein
provided , the defendant shall be fined not less than
Twenty Dollars ( $ 20 ) nor more than One Hundred
Dollars ($ 100 ) and , if a person , he shall in addition
be imprisoned in the county jail for not less than
thirty nor more than sixty days . Upon the second
conviction for a violation of any provisions of this
Act , except for a violation of a provision for which
a particular penalty is herein provided , the defend-
ant shall be fined not less than Forty Dollars ( $40 )
nor more than Two Hundred Dollars ($ 200 ) and ,
if a person, he shall in addition be confined in the
county jail for not less than sixty nor more than
one hundred and twenty days . Upon a third or sub-
sequent conviction for a violation of any provision
of this Act , except for a violation of a provision for
which a particular penalty is herein provided , the

16 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
defendant , if a person, shall be confined in the peni-
tentiary for not less than one nor more than two
years , and if a corporation , shall be fined not less
than One Thousand Dollars ( $ 1,000 ) nor more than
Two thousand ($2,000 ) .
Section Twenty -Nine ( 29 ) . Bond for good be-
havior . On a first or second conviction for violation
of any of the provisions of this Act , the court shall
require the defendant , in addition to the penalty
inflicted as provided in Section twenty -eight ( 28 )
hereof, to execute bond in a sum of not less than
Five Hundred Dollars ( $ 500 ) nor more than One
Thousand Dollars ( $ 1,000 ) , to be of good behavior
for twelve months and not violate any of the local
option laws of this State . If the bond be not exe-
cuted , the defendant shall be committed to the
county jail in default of such bond for a period of
sixty days . The order of the trial court , requiring
the execution of the peace bond herein provided
for, shall not be considered a part of the punish-
ment inflicted under this Act , but as a security
against future violations of its provisions . Said
order shall not be subject to appeal .
Section Thirty ( 30 ) . Evasion of law not allowed .
No trick , device , subterfuge , or pretense shall be
allowed to evade the operation or defeat the pur-
pose of this law .
Section Thirty -One ( 31 ) . Duty of courts , judges
and officers to enforce law ; penalty for failure to
enforce . It shall be the duty of the courts and judges
of this Commonwealth and all officers and conserva-
tors of the peace to enforce the provisions of this
Act. Any sheriff , deputy sheriff , policeman , or other
peace officer of this Commonwealth , who shall fail
to enforce any provision of this Act after receiving
information of a violation thereof, or having knowl-
edge of a violation thereof and failing to act thereon ,

CHAPTER 1 17
shall be subject to indictment for nonfeasance or
malfeasance in office , and upon conviction shall be
fined not less than Fifty Dollars ( $ 50 ) nor more
than Two Hundred Dollars ( $ 200 ) , and upon con-
viction the office held by such officer shall become
vacant and the judgment of conviction shall so re-
cite ; but such officer shall have the right to appeal
to the Court of Appeals .
Section Thirty -Two ( 32 ) . Courts ' concurrent
jurisdiction . Quarterly courts , and the judges
thereof, justices of the peace and police courts , shall
have concurrent jurisdiction with the circuit court
in the trial of all offenses committed in their respec-
tive jurisdictions under the provisions of this Act ,
where the penalty which may be inflicted does not
exceed a fine of One Hundred Dollars ($ 100 ) and
imprisonment in the county jail not exceeding sixty
days , and said courts shall always be open for the
trial of such offenses .
Section Thirty -Three ( 33 ) . Charge to grand
jury . It shall be the duty of the judges of the cir-
cuit courts to charge the grand juries of the coun-
ties within their jurisdiction at each term of court
especially concerning the provisions of this Act , and
direct said jury to inquire particularly of all viola-
tion of its provisions .
Section Thirty -Four ( 34 ) . Search and seizure ;
issuance ; disposition of property seized . Any cir-
cuit judge , county judge , police judge , or justice of
the peace , when the affidavit of an officer , or of any
reputable citizen , is filed with him describing the
house , building or premises , or vehicle , as nearly as
may be where intoxicating liquors are sold or dis-
posed of, or illegally possessed in violation of this
Act , shall by his warrant cause any house or build-
ing or other place or premises or vehicle to be
searched by night or day for the detection of any

18 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
intoxicating liquors which are kept there for sale
or other disposition in violation of this Act , or for
intoxicating liquors illegally possessed in violation
of this Act , and if admission be not given on demand
made the officers enforcing said warrant shall force
an entrance into such house , building or other place
and seize any intoxicating liquors and arrest the
keeper or person in charge of such house , building
or other place , or intoxicating liquors , and carry
such person before said judge issuing the warrant
to be dealt with according to law.
The officer shall carry said intoxicating liquors
before the judge issuing said warrant . Any officer
receiving a warrant as herein set out shall immedi-
ately proceed to execute same on the day received
and for a failure to do so shall be fined not less than
One Hundred Dollars ( $ 100 ) nor more than Five
Hundred Dollars ( $500 ) .
No search warrant as herein provided for shall
be quashed if said search warrant and affidavit on
which it is based are sufficient on the face thereof .
If the search warrant is quashed no property taken
by virtue thereof shall be ordered returned or re-
stored unless the person from whose possession
same was taken both alleges and proves that he was
in lawful possession of said property .
Section Thirty -Five ( 35 ) . Indictment ; allega-
tions of. It shall not be necessary to allege in any
indictment charging the commission of an offense
under this Act that a vote was taken or an election
held , or any other step relative thereto , in the terri-
tory where said offense is charged to have been com-
mitted , but it may be simply stated that the act or
acts charged were committed in local option terri-
tory . And in said indictment it shall be sufficient
to designate said act as in violation of the local op-
tion law .

CHAPTER 1 19
Section Thirty -Six ( 36 ) . Witness may not refuse
to answer. No witness before a grand jury , court
of inquiry or on a trial for any violation of this Act
shall be permitted to refuse to answer any question
because the answer will incriminate himself ; but
this evidence shall not be used against him in any
subsequent proceedings , and such witness shall not
be prosecuted for any offense disclosed in such tes-
timony .
Section Thirty - Seven ( 37 ) . Burden of proof
upon defendant . No person shall possess , or have
in possession , any intoxicating liquor unless same
shall have been lawfully acquired and is intended
to be used lawfully ; and in any suit, prosecution ,
proceeding or action or motion , the burden of prov-
ing that same was lawfully acquired and is intended
to be used lawfully shall be upon the defendant or
defendants .
Section Thirty -Eight ( 38 ) . General reputation
of defendant admissible in evidence . In any prose-
cution or proceeding for any violation of this Act ,
the general reputation of the defendant for boot-
legging or being engaged in selling , or trading in ,
or keeping for sale , or transporting for sale , shall
be admissible in evidence against said defendant or
defendants .
Section Thirty -Nine ( 39 ) . United States tax ;
effect of possession . The possession of a United
States special tax stamp , commonly called a United
State license , for carrying on the business of a re-
tail dealer in spirituous , vinous or malt liquors , or
having such tax stamp or license at the place of
business in such territory , shall be prima facie evi-
dence of guilt under this Act . An attested copy of
the record of the office of the United States Col-
lector of Internal Revenue or any other office issu-
ing such license showing its issuance shall be ad-

20 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
missible in evidence on any trial or proceeding
under this Act .
Section Forty (40 ) . Articles not subject to
provisions of Act ; wine for sacramental purposes .
The articles enumerated in this section shall not be
subject to the provisions of this Act if they corre-
spond to the following descriptions and limitations ,
namely :
(a ) Denatured alcohol or denatured rum pro-
duced and used as provided by laws and regulations
now or hereafter in force.
(b) Medicinal preparations manufactured in
accordance with formulas prescribed by the United
States Pharmacopoeia , national formulary or the
American Institute of Homeopathy , that are unfit
for use for beverage purposes .
(c ) Patented , patent and proprietary medicines
that are unfit for use for beverage purposes .
(d) Toilet , medicinal and antiseptic prepara-
tions and solutions that are unfit for use for bev-
erage purposes .
( e ) Flavoring extracts and syrups that are un-
fitfor use
as
abeverage
orfor intoxicating bev-
erage purposes
.
(f) Vinegar and preserved sweet cider
.
(g) Wine for sacramental purposes
.
(h) Alcohol medicated according to such
formulas as will render
itunfit for beverage pur-
poses and which
is
tobe sold for legitimate external
use
.(i) Alcohol for mechanical and scientific pur-
poses
ifunfit for
abeverage
.
(j) Any person who shall knowingly sell
orgive
any
ofthe articles mentioned herein for beverage
purposes or any extract or syrup for intoxicating
beverage purpose
,
orwho shall sell any
ofthe same
under circumstances from which the seller might

CHAPTER 2 21:
reasonably deduce the intention of the purchaser to
use them for such purpose shall be subject to the
penalties provided in this Act .
Section Forty - One ( 41 ) . Distillers and manufac-
turers ; when Act not applicable . The provisions of
this Act shall not apply to any distiller or manu-
facturer who in good faith and in the usual course
of trade sells intoxicating liquor of his own make ,
at his distillery or manufactory , in quantities of
not less than three gallons delivered at one time for
immediate transportation to a point or destination
in some other State , or to a point in this State where
intoxicating liquor may be lawfully sold .
Section Forty -Two (42 ) . Constitutionality . If
any section , paragraph , or part of this Act shall be
held to be unconstitutional , the remainder of this
Act shall continue effective and enforceable notwith-
standing such decision .
An emergency is hereby declared to exist , and
this Act shall be effective from and after its passage
and approval by the Governor .
Approved February 25 , 1936 .
CHAPTER 2 .
AN ACT authorizing, empowering and directing the Governor
to cause to be made an audit , study and survey of Depart-
ments , Institutions , Boards , Commissions and Agencies of
the Commonwealth ; empowering him to employ auditors ,
accountants , clerks , bookkeepers and any other class of skilled
and professional services which, in his judgment , may be nec-
essary and expedient to make said audit , study and survey ;
defining the scope or character of same and the powers and
duties of auditors and others employed for the aforesaid
purposes ; and appropriating the sum of one hundred and
twenty thousand dollars ( $120,000.00 ) to be used by the Gov-
ernor in carrying out the purposes of the Act .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :

22 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Section One ( 1 ) . The Governor of this Com-
monwealth hereby is authorized , empowered and
directed to cause to be made a full , thorough , com-
prehensive and complete audit , study and survey
of all Departments , Institutions , Boards , Commis-
sions and Agencies of this Commonwealth . To this
end he hereby is specifically authorized , empowered
and directed to employ and pay such reputable ,
qualified and experienced auditors , accountants ,
clerks , bookkeepers and any other class of skilled
and professional services which , in his judgment ,
may be necessary and expedient to carry out , as ex-
peditiously as possible , the duties imposed upon him
by this Act , and to authorize , incur and pay such ex-
penses as , in his judgment , may be necessary .
This audit, study and survey herein provided for
shall extend back over such period of time as neces-
sary to reveal the true financial condition of the
Commonwealth , and of every Department and
Agency thereof , and shall be of such character as
to ascertain waste , duplication of efforts , useless
offices , faulty or inadequate systems of bookkeep-
ing , accounting , improper budgeting , and any and
all other facts that may aid in placing the Common-
wealth upon a sound financial basis .
From time to time as the work is finished and
upon completion of said audit , study and survey ,
the Governor shall make a report thereof to the
General Assembly, together with his recommenda-
tions relating thereto .
Section Two ( 2 ) . Within a reasonable time after
the effective date of this Act , and as soon as prac-
tical after any Department , Board , Commission or
Agency of the State Government is reorganized , the
Governor shall cause to be installed and kept by
each Department, Board , Commission and Agency
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , a carefully sys-
4444

CHAPTER 2 23
tematized and well devised plan of filing , bookkeep-
ing and accounting . Said system shall be as nearly
uniform as practical and shall be as comprehensive
as necessary for the efficient administration and
business needs of the respective Departments . As a
part of the requirements to be imposed therein , the
records of each Department shall be so kept that a
full and complete report may be readily and eco-
nomically prepared and furnished to the Governor
monthly , showing in detail all receipts and disburse-
ments and other pertinent information which may
be required by the Governor , for the previous month .
Section Three ( 3 ) . Any auditor , accountant or
other person employed by the Governor under the
provisions of this Act shall have the right , when
authorized in writing by the Governor , to require
information , on oath , from any person or party
touching any matters relative to any account which
he is required to examine , state or audit . Any audi-
tor , accountant or other person having such written
authority from the Governor shall also have free
and full access to any and all books , files , records
and accounts of each and every Board , Commission ,
Department and Agency of this Commonwealth
necessary or useful for the full and complete per-
formance of the duties to which such person may
be assigned by the Governor .
Section Four (4 ) . There hereby is appropriated
out of any funds of the State Treasury not other-
wise appropriated the sum of one hundred and
twenty thousand dollars ( $ 120,000.00 ) to be used by
the Governor in performing the duties required
and/or authorized by this Act . Said appropriation
shall be paid out of the General Funds by the State
Treasurer on warrants drawn by the State Auditor
on proper certification by the Governor .
Section Five ( 5 ) . It is deemed by the General

24 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Assembly to be both advisable and necessary that
the audits , studies and surveys authorized and pro-
vided for by this Act should be made as promptly
as possible to aid in the proper and efficient re-
organization of the State Government and the
proper and efficient administration thereof . An
emergency hereby is declared to exist and this Act
shall become effective immediately upon its passage
and approval by the Governor .
Approved February 10, 1936 .
CHAPTER 3 .
AN ACT appropriating the sum of seventy -five ( $75.00 ) dollars
per month for the use of the widow and children of Powell
Hayes , deceased .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One ( 1 ) . Whereas , on the twenty-first
(21st ) day of December , one thousand nine hundred
thirty -five ( 1935 ) , Powell Hayes while serving as a
State Patrolman in the discharge of his duty, at-
tempted to arrest two men suspected of having vio-
lated the laws of this Commonwealth and the Fed- .
eral government , in the city of Franklin , Simpson
County, Kentucky , was shot by said suspects , from
which gunshot wound he died on the twenty-first
(21st ) day of December , and whereas , he left surviv-
ing him a widow and three infant children , leaving
said widow and children in destitute circumstances :
Section Two ( 2 ) . It is hereby appropriated out
of the general expenditure fund for the use and
benefit of the widow of Powell Hayes , deceased
(Mrs. Nell Hayes ) and children of said decedent the
sum of seventy -five ( $ 75.00 ) dollars per month , pro-
viding that payments stop at the twenty-fourth
monthly payment .

CHAPTER 4 25
Section Three ( 3 ) . The funds above appropri-
ated should be paid monthly by the State Treasurer
to a trustee appointed by the county court of Warren
County, and shall be disbursed by said trustee under
the order and direction of said court as may best
serve the interest of said widow and children of the
said Powell Hayes , deceased .
Section Four (4 ) . Whereas , said widow and chil-
dren are in destitute circumstances , and have abso-
lutely no funds with which to obtain food and cloth-
ing , and whereas said children are too young to
obtain gainful employment , now thereby an emer-
gency is declared to exist , and this action become
a law upon its passage and approval by the Gov-
ernor. Approved February 18 , 1936 .
CHAPTER 4 .
AN ACT appropriating the sum of seventy -five ( $75.00 ) dollars
per month for the use of the widow and children of Robert
Rowland, deceased .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One ( 1 ) . Whereas , on the twenty-first
(21st ) day of December , one thousand nine hundred
thirty -five ( 1935 ) , Robert Rowland while serving
as a State Patrolman and in the discharge of his
duty, attempted to arrest two men suspected of hav-
ing violated the laws of this Commonwealth and the
Federal Government , in the City of Franklin , Simp-
son County, Kentucky , was shot by said suspects ,
from which gunshot wound he died on the twenty-
second ( 22d ) day of December , and Whereas , he
left surviving him a widow and two infant children ,
leaving said widow and children in destitute circum-
stances :

26 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Section Two ( 2 ) . It is hereby appropriated out
of the General Expenditure Fund for the use and
benefit of the widow of Robert Rowland deceased ,
(Mrs. Hollis Rowland ) and children of said de-
cedent the sum of Seventy -Five ( $ 75.00 ) Dollars
per month , the payment to stop at the end of the
twenty-fourth ( 24th ) payment .
Section Three ( 3 ) . The funds above appropri-
ated should be paid monthly by the State Treasurer
to a Trustee appointed by the County Court of
Simpson County , and shall be disbursed by said
Trustee under the order and direction of said Court
as may best serve the interest of said widow and
children of the said Robert Rowland deceased .
Section Four (4 ) . Whereas, Said widow and
children are in destitute circumstances , and have
absolutely no funds with which to obtain food and
clothing, and Whereas said children are too young
to obtain gainful employment , Now thereby an
emergency is declared to exist , and this action be-
come a law upon its passage and approval by the
Governor . Approved February 18, 1936 .
CHAPTER 5 .
AN ACT relating to public roads and bridges ; providing State
aid for the improvement , reconstruction and maintenance of
county roads ; prescribing certain duties of the State High-
way Commission and the fiscal courts in relation thereto ;
making an annual appropriation from the State Road Fund ;
repealing all laws and parts of laws in conflict with this Act ,
and declaring an emergency .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One ( 1 ) . There is hereby appropriated
out of the State Road Fund the sum of two million
($2,000,000.00 ) dollars annually, beginning April

CHAPTER 5 27
first ( 1 ) , one thousand nine hundred thirty - six
(1936 ) , to be used by the State Highway Commis-
sion for the improvement , reconstruction and main-
tenance of county roads and bridges which have not
been accepted by the State Highway Commission for
maintenance .
Section Two ( 2 ) . The State Highway Commis-
sion , as soon as practical after April first ( 1 ) , one
thousand nine hundred thirty - six ( 1936 ) , shall allo-
cate , set apart , and include in its budget the sum of
two million ( $ 2,000,000.00 ) dollars for each fiscal
year to be used for the purposes set forth in Sec-
tion one ( 1 ) of this Act . Said sum shall be appor-
tioned to each of the several counties of this Com-
monwealth on the following basis : One -third of the
sum allocated shall be apportioned equally between
the one hundred and twenty counties . One -third of
the sum allocated shall be apportioned among the
several counties on the basis of the ratio which the
population of each county bears to the total popu-
lation of this Commonwealth and one-third of the
sum allocated shall be apportioned among the
several counties on the basis of the ratio which
the square mile area of the county bears to the total
square mile area of this Commonwealth . The amount
so apportioned to each county , subject to the con-
ditions and restrictions imposed by this Act , shall
be expended by the State Highway Commission in
that county for the maintenance , reconstruction , and
improvement of the county roads and bridges which
have not been accepted by the State Highway Com-
mission for maintenance as a part of the State Pri-
mary Road System .
Section Three ( 3 ) . The funds apportioned the
several counties by Section two ( 2 ) of this Act shall
be used for the purposes provided herein by the
State Highway Commission in accordance with

28 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
previously agreed to and accepted plans prepared
jointly by the Fiscal Courts of the several counties
and the State Highway Commission , or prepared
by one or the other and jointly approved . Said plans
shall take into consideration the mileage of county
roads in the particular county , the condition and
needs of such roads and the manner in which said
funds shall be expended by the State Highway Com-
mission for the fiscal year to which the agreement
applies. Said plan for each county shall be formu-
lated and agreed to as soon as practicable after the
effective date of this Act , for the then current fiscal
year and annually thereafter . If at the end of any
fiscal year of the State Highway Commission , any
county or counties have failed or refused to com-
ply with the provisions of this Act the funds ap-
portioned said county or counties for that year shall ,
to the extent that same has not been obligated by
the joint agreement between the Highway Commis-
sion and the Fiscal Court of the county , be released
and shall revert to the State Highway Fund for
such other uses as the State Highway Commission
may determine .
Section Four (4) . All of the funds appropriated
and made available for use under the provisions of
this Act shall be expended by or under the direction
of the State Highway Commission of Kentucky , and
no.part of the funds appropriated or made available
by the provisions of this Act in any event shall be
turned over to the Fiscal Court of or to any county .
Section Five ( 5 ) . In order to provide for the
simplification of the administration , and for as
nearly as practical the uniform operation of this
Act, the State Highway Commission is hereby
authorized to prescribe such reasonable rules and
regulations , not inconsistent with the provisions of
this Act , as it may deem necessary and proper for
113

CHAPTER 6 29
the information and guidance of the Fiscal Courts
of the several counties .
Section Six ( 6 ) . Nothing in this Act shall be con-
strued as prohibiting any county from spending
county road funds for the further and additional
maintenance , reconstruction and improvement of
county roads ;
Section Seven ( 7 ) . Whereas , the next ensuing
fiscal year of the State Highway Commission will
begin April first ( 1 ) , nineteen hundred thirty - six
(1936 ) , and
Whereas, it is deemed important by the General
Assembly that the benefits to the counties which this
Act provides should be made available promptly in
order to enable the State Highway Commission and
the Fiscal Courts of the respective counties to make
plans and agreements for the next ensuing fiscal
year, an emergency is hereby declared to exist and
this Act shall become effective immediately upon its
passage and approval by the Governor .
Approved February 17 , 1936 .
CHAPTER 6 .
AN ACT to amend and re -enact subsections ninety-eight
a-two (98a -2 ) and subsection ninety-eight a -five ( 98a -5) of
Section ninety -eight ( 98 ) of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes ,
same being an Act of the Kentucky Legislature at its one
thousand nine hundred eighteen ( 1918 ) session ; and being
Chapter one hundred thirty -one ( 131 ) , at page five hundred
fifty-nine ( 559 ) , of the Acts of said Legislature , regulating the
qualifications and admission to practice law of applicants in
the State of Kentucky .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That subsection a-two ( a-2 ) and subsection a-five
(a-5) of Section ninety -eight ( 98 ) of Carroll's

30 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Kentucky Statutes , same being subsection a-two
(a-2 ) and subsection a -five ( a-5 ) of the Acts of the
General Assembly of Kentucky , one thousand nine
hundred eighteen ( 1918 ) , regulating and prescribing
the rules for admission of applicants to practice
law in the courts of the State of Kentucky , be
amended and re -enacted so that said subsections
shall read as follows :
Ninety -Eight a-two (98a -2 ) : The Court of Ap-
peals of Kentucky shall from time to time make and
adopt such rules and regulations as the said court
may see proper , fixing the moral qualifications of
applicants and standard of acquirements , academic ,
legal and official service to entitle them to admission
to practice law in the courts of this Commonwealth .
No applicant , however , shall be under the age of
twenty- one years .
Ninety -Eight a-five ( 98a -5 ) : Applicants who may
appear before the board for license to practice law
shall be examined as to their qualifications . All such
examinations shall be in writing and be on such sub-
jects as shall be prescribed by said Court of Appeals
and the answers of the applicants to the questions
propounded to them shall be graded by said board
and no such applicant shall be granted a license un-
less he secures as much as seventy -five (75 ) per
cent on each subject . The board shall certify to the
said court the name of each applicant who shall have
passed the required examination before it . The said
court shall enter licensing and admitting to practice
as attorney and counselor -at-law in all courts of
this State , each person who complies with the rules
and regulations it shall adopt , which license , if pro-
cured by fraud may be revoked by the court at any
time within two years from the date of its issuance .
The applicant before beginning the practice of law
shall take the oath as now required . However , any

CHAPTER 7 31
applicant who shall have been a teacher in the com-
mon schools of this State for at least four years , and
shall possess the required moral qualification , and
have served as county judge or circuit court clerk
or clerk of United States District Court for at least
one full term of four years in any one of the coun-
ties of this State , and shall have served one full term
of two years as a member of the General Assembly
of this State , and in addition , have a certificate of
graduation from an accredited law college or ac-
credited correspondence law college showing two
years study on a prescribed course of law, and shall
file with the board of examiners of this State , certifi-
cates of the foregoing requirements , such applicant
shall be deemed to have all the academic and legal
requirements of this Act and shall be eligible to and
shall upon his application accompanied by the fore-
going , be admitted to examination before said board .
All laws and parts of laws in conflict herewith
are hereby repealed . Approved February 25 , 1936 .

AN ACT creating the office of Stenographer to the County At-
torney in counties containing cities of the third class or
having a population of fifty thousand ( 50,000 ) or over , pre-
scribing the method of appointment and removal , salary and
the method of payment of said salary .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
One . That in all counties in this Commonwealth
containing cities of the third class , or having a popu-
lation of fifty thousand ( 50,000 ) people or over ,
there is hereby created , subject to the discretion
of the Fiscal Court or County Commissioners of
said Counties , the office of stenographer or Clerk to

32 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
the County Attorney . Said stenographer or clerk
shall be appointed by the County Attorney for a
term of four years , but may be removed at any time
by the said County Attorney .
Two . Said stenographer shall receive a salary
of not less than six hundred ( $ 600.00 ) dollars , nor
more than twelve hundred ( $ 1,200.00 ) dollars per
annum , to be paid by the Fiscal Court in such
county , and shall be payable out of the County levy
in said county in equal monthly installments .
Three . That this Act shall be in force and effect
immediately upon its passage and approval .
Approved February 25 , 1936 .

hundred twenty-three b-two (2223b -2) , Kentucky Statutes ,

relating to the organization of loan and investment companies
in cities of the first and second class and extending the
terms thereof to cities of the fifth class .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
One (1 ) . That Section two ( 2 ) , of Chapter
twenty-eight (28 ) of the Acts of one thousand nine
hundred eighteen (1918 ) , being Section two thou .
sand two hundred twenty-three b-two ( 2223b -2 ) ,
Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's one thousand nine
hundred thirty ( 1930 ) Edition , be and the same is

follows :

may associate to establish a loan and investment
company in cities of the first , second and fifth class
upon the terms and conditions and subject to the
