CHAPTER 11 .
AN ACT to promote the objects of the National Housing Act by
authorizing banks , savings banks , trust companies , insurance
companies , building and loan associations , credit unions , and
trustees and other fiduciaries to make loans eligible for in-
surance under the National Housing Act , and by authorizing
banks , savings banks , trust companies , insurance companies ,

36 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
building and loan associations , credit unions , trustees and
other fiduciaries , the Commonwealth of Kentucky and any of
its political subdivisions and any agency or instrumentality
thereof, to invest in mortgages insured , and in debentures
issued , by the Federal Housing Administrator , and to invest
in securities of National Mortgage Associations, and to use
mortgages insured or debentures issued by the Federal Housing
Administrator , and debentures issued by National Mortgage
Associations as collateral or deposit security where required
by any statute of this State , and declaring an emergency .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One ( 1 ) . Banks , savings banks , trust
companies , insurance companies , building and loan
associations , credit unions , and trustees , guardians ,
executors , administrators and other fiduciaries , sub-
ject to the laws of this State , are authorized :
(a ) To make such loans and advances of credit
and purchases of obligations representing loans and
advances of credit as are insured by the Federal
Housing Administrator pursuant to the provisions
of Title one I of the National Housing Act , and
to obtain such insurance .
(b) To make such loans , secured by real property
or leasehold , as the Federal Housing Administrator
insures or makes a commitment to insure pursuant
to the provisions of Title II of the National Housing
Act, and to obtain such insurance .
Section Two ( 2 ) . It shall be lawful for banks ,
savings banks , trust companies , insurance com-
panies , building and loan associations , credit unions ,
trustees , guardians , executors , administrators and
other fiduciaries , subject to the laws of this State ,
the State of Kentucky and any of its political sub-
divisions , or any agency or instrumentality thereof,
to invest their funds and the moneys in their custody
or possession , eligible for investment , in notes or
bonds secured by mortgage or trust deed insured

CHAPTER 11 37
by the Federal Housing Administrator , and in de-
bentures issued by the Federal Housing Adminis-
trator pursuant to Title II of the National Housing
Act, and also in securities issued by National Mort-
gage Associations .
Section Three (3 ) . Wherever securities must be
furnished by any depository in the State of Ken-
tucky as security for the deposit of any funds what-
soever , or wherever securities must be deposited
with any official of the State of Kentucky pursuant
to any statute of this State , mortgages insured and ·
debentures issued by the Federal Housing Admin-
istrator and debentures issued by National Mort-
gage Associations shall be considered eligible
securities for such purposes .
Section Four (4 ) . No law of this State requir-
ing security upon which loans or investments may
be made , or prescribing the nature , amount or form
of such security , or prescribing or limiting interest
rates upon loans or investments , or limiting invest-
ments of capital or deposits , or prescribing or limit-
ing the period for which loans or investments may
be made , shall be deemed to apply to loans or in-
vestments made pursuant to the provisions of this
Act .
Section Five ( 5 ) . This Act shall be construed
as powers additional to and not in derrogation of
existing laws .
Section Six ( 6 ) . Whereas the Congress of the
United States has enacted a law entitled the " Na-
tional Housing Act " to relieve unemployment by
providing a system of mutual mortgage insurance
and encouraging the construction of new homes and
the improving or housing standards and conditions,
and to prevent a recurrence of a collapse of the
mortgage market and the distressed selling of
homes by providing for national mortgage associa-

38 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
•
tions to assist in refinancing mortgages and to
furnish a liquid market therefor , and whereas many
investing institutions organized under the laws of
this State are not authorized to invest in notes or
bonds secured by mortgage or trust deed insured
by the Federal Housing Administrator or in securi-
ties of national mortgage associations , and the
market for said notes , bonds and securities is limited
by the absence of such authority , and whereas the
said notes , bonds and other securities will provide
a safe and readily marketable investment for all
investors of this State by reason of the insurance
fund and the supervision provided for by the Na-
tional Housing Act, an emergency is hereby declared
to exist and this Act shall become effective immedi-
ately upon its passage and approval by the Gover-
nor . Approved February 18, 1936 .
CHAPTER 12 .
AN ACT to amend and re -enact Sections five hundred forty-
seven ( 547 ) and six hundred thirteen ( 613 ) of Carroll's Ken-
tucky Statutes , one thousand nine hundred thirty ( 1930 ) edi-
tion , and Section five hundred ninety-five ( 595 ) Carroll's
Kentucky Statutes , one thousand nine hundred thirty (1930 )
edition, as amended by Chapter one ( 1 ) of the Acts of the
Extraordinary Session of the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky of one thousand nine hundred thirty-
three ( 1933 ) , so as to provide for the elimination of double
liability of stockholders in banks , trust companies , and com-
bined banks and trust companies .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Sections five hundred forty - seven ( 547 ) and
six hundred thirteen ( 613 ) of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , one thousand nine hundred thirty ( 1930 )
edition , and Section five hundred ninety -five ( 595 ) ,

CHAPTER 12 39
Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , one thousand nine
hundred thirty ( 1930 ) edition , as amended by Chap-
ter one ( 1 ) of the Acts of the extraordinary session
of the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky of one thousand nine hundred thirty-
three ( 1933 ) , relating to the double liability of
stockholders in banks , trust companies , and com-
bined banks and trust companies , be and the same
are hereby amended and re -enacted so as to read
as follows , to wit:
"Section Five Hundred Forty -Seven ( 547 ) .
Stockholders ; liability of; action to enforce ; limita-
tion . The stockholders of each corporation shall
be liable to creditors for the full amount of the un-
paid part of stock subscribed for by them , and no
stockholder shall be liable because of being a stock-
holder, for any sum more than to the amount of the
unpaid part of stock held by such stockholder of
any company , except stockholder in guaranty com-
panies , investment companies and insurance com-
panies shall be liable equally and ratably , and not
one for the other , for all contracts and liabilities
of such corporation to the extent of the amount of
their stock at par value , in addition to the amount
of such stock ; but persons holding stock as
fiduciaries shall not be personally liable as stock-
holders , but the estates in their hands shall be liable ,
in the same manner and to the same extent as the
property of other stockholders , and no transfer of
stock shall operate as a release of any such lia-
bility existing at the time of such transfer ; pro-
vided, the action to enforce such liability shall be
commenced within two years from the time of
transfer . "
Section Six Hundred Thirteen ( 613 ) . Double
liability of stockholders ; action to enforce ; list of
stockholders to be filed . The stockholders of each

40 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
company organized under this article shall be in-
dividually responsible , equally and ratably , and not
one for the other , for all contracts and liabilities of
such corporation to the extent of the amount of their
stock at par value , in addition to the amount of
such stock ; but persons holding stock as fiduciaries
shall not be personally liable as stockholders ; but
the estates in their hands shall be liable in the same
manner , and to the same extent , as the property of
other stockholders ; and no transfer of stock shall
operate as a release of any such liaility existing at
the time of such transfer , provided the action to
enforce such liability shall be commenced within
two years after the time of the transfer ; and the
directors of each corporation shall, in January of
each year , file with the Secretary of State a cor-
rect list of the stockholders and officers of such
corporation . The additional liability herein im-
posed upon shareholders in trust companies and
combined banks and trust companies organized
under the laws of this State shall not apply with
respect to shares in any such trust companies or
combined banks and trust companies issued after
the date of the enactment of this Act . The addi-
tional liability herein provided for shall cease on
July one ( 1 ) one thousand nine hundred thirty - seven
(1937 ) , or on such date as double liability of stock-
holders in national banks ceases under the national
bank laws, with respect to all shares issued by any
such trust company or combined bank and trust
company in this State which shall be transacting
business on the above date ; provided , that not less
than six months prior to such date such trust com-
pany or combined bank and trust company shall
have caused notice of such prospective termination
of liability to be published in a newspaper published
in the city, town or county in which such trust

CHAPTER 12 41
company or combined bank and trust company is
located , and if no newspaper is published in such
city , town or county then in a newspaper of general
circulation therein . If such trust company or com-
bined bank and trust company fail to give such
notice as and when above provided , a termination
of such additional liability may thereafter be accom-
plished as of the date six months subsequent to pub-
lication in the manner above provided . "
Section Five Hundred Ninety -Five (595 ) . Double
liablity of stockholders ; nonassessable preferred
stock . The stockholders of each bank organized
under this article shall be individually responsible ,
equally and ratably , and not one for the other , for
all contracts and liabilities of such bank to the ex-
tent of the amount of their stock at part value in
addition to the amount of such stock ; but persons
holding stock as fiduciaries shall not be personally
liable as stockholders , but the estate in their hands
shall be liable in the same manner and to the same
extent as the property of other stockholders ; and
no transfer of stock shall operate as a release of
any such liability existing at the time of such trans-
fer , provided the action to enforce such liability shall
be commenced within two years from the time of the
transfer ; and the directors of each bank shall, in
January of each year , file with the Secretary of
State a correct list of the stockholders and officers
of such bank .
The additional liability herein imposed upon
shareholders in banks organized under the laws of
this State shall not apply with respect to shares
in any such banks issued after the date of the enact-
ment of this Act . Such additional liability shall
cease on July one (1 ) , one thousand nine hun-
dred thirty -seven ( 1937 ) , or at such date as the
double liability of stockholders in national banks

42 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
ceases under the national bank laws , with respect to
all shares issued by any such bank in this State
which shall be transacting the business of banking
on the above date ; Provided , that not less than six
months prior to such date such bank shall have caused
notice of such prospective termination of liability
to be published in a newspaper published in the
city , town or county in which such bank is located ,
and if no newspaper is published in such city , town
or county then in a newspaper of general circula-
tion therein . If such bank fail to give such notice as
and when above provided , a termination of such ad-
ditional liability may thereafter be accomplished as
of the date six months subsequent to publication in
the manner above provided .
Any bank or trust company , or combined bank
and trust company , heretofore or hereafter organ-
ized under the laws of this Commonwealth , may
issue nonassessable preferred capital stock , of one
or more classes , to which the double liability shall
not apply .
Such preferred capital stock shall not be con-
sidered as any part of the minimum capital stock
required by Section five hundred seventy - seven
(577 ) , Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's Edition one
thousand nine hundred thirty ( 1930 ) Acts of one
thousand eight hundred ninety-three ( 1893 ) , C - one
hundred seventy -one ( 171 ) , Section forty ( 40 ) but
shall be considered a part of the ' capital stock ' of
such bank or trust company , or combined bank and
trust company , for all other purposes , and in the
case of existing corporations shall be issued in the
manner now provided for increasing capital stock .
The holder of such preferred capital stock shall
be entitled to receive such cumulative dividends at
a rate not exceeding six per cent per annum , and
shall have such voting and conversion rights , and

CHAPTER 13 43
such control of management , and such preference
over the holders of common stock in the event of
liquidation , as may be provided in the original or
amended articles of incorporation under which said
stock is issued .
No issue of preferred stock shall be valid until the
par value of all stock so issued shall be paid in . "
Approved February 21 , 1936 .
CHAPTER 13 .
AN ACT to amend and re -enact five hundred ninety-six ( 596 ) ,
Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's Edition one thousand nine hun-
dred thirty ( 1930 ) , to increase the surplus of banks , trust
companies and combined banks and trust companies , organ-
ized under the laws of this State . "Harrison -Grogan Act .”
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That five hundred ninety-six ( 596 ) Kentucky
Statutes , Carroll's Edition one thousand nine hun-
dred thirty ( 1930 ) , which became a law in the Ses-
sion Acts of one thousand eight hundred ninety-
three ( 1893 ) , as amended in the Session Acts of one
thousand nine hundred sixteen ( 1916 ) , be amended
by striking out the words " twenty per cent of its
capital stock " , and adding the words " a sum to
equal the amount of its common capital stock " ; so
that said section , as amended , will read as follows :
" The board of directors of any bank or trust
company organized under the laws of this State
may declare a dividend of so much of the net profits
of the bank or trust company , after deducting there-
from all expenses , losses , bad or suspended debts ,
interest and taxes accrued or due from the bank, as
they may deem expedient . Provided , no bank or
trust company shall pay a dividend to the holders
of the common stock thereof until such bank or

44 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
trust company has a surplus fund equal to ten per
cent of its capital stock , and before any dividend is
declared , other than upon its preferred stock , if
any, not less than ten per cent of the net profits
of the bank for the period covered by the dividend
applicable to its common stock shall be carried to
its surplus fund until such surplus fund amounts
to a sum equal to the amount of its common capital
stock . All debts due to a bank or trust company on
which interest is due and unpaid for six months ,
unless the same be well secured or in the process
of collection , shall be construed bad or suspended
debts within the meaning of this section . "
Approved February 18 , 1936 .

so that said section when amended will read as fol-

Section Five Hundred Seventy -Nine ( 579 ) . Busi-

Any bank or trust company may accept for payment

upon it by its customers and issue letters of credits

ceeding one year, and may also accept drafts or bills

46 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
of exchange drawn upon it , having not more than
six months ' sight to run , growing out of transac-
tions involving the importation and exportation of

quired by law to be secured ; Provided that any
bank or trust company or combined bank and trust
company which is a stockholder in the Federal De-
posit Insurance Corporation , where the deposits in
such bank or trust company or combined bank and
trust company are insured according to the pro-
visions of the national banking law , shall only be

CHAPTER 15 .
AN ACT relating to the release of prisoners from penitentiaries ,
state reformatories and houses of reform in the Common-
wealth of Kentucky and providing for notice to county officials
and district attorneys , when said persons are released from
any of said institutions .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One . It shall be the duty of the warden
of the penitentiary , the superintendent of the State
reformatory and the superintendent of the houses
of reform in the Commonwealth of Kentucky , upon
the release of any prisoner or inmate from confine-
ment in any of said institutions to immediately
notify the Commonwealth's Attorney of the dis-
trict , the county attorney and sheriff of the county
and the chief of police of the city , to which the said
inmate is released , of said release and giving his
residence and the person's name to whom he was
released .
Section Two . All laws or part of laws in con-
flict herewith are hereby repealed .
Approved February 5 , 1936 .
CHAPTER 16.
AN ACT to amend Chapter sixteen ( 16 ) of the Acts of one
thousand nine hundred twenty -eight (1928 ) by adding after
Section seventy -seven ( 77 ) thereof and before Section seventy-
eight ( 78 ) thereof a new section to be entitled Section seven-
ty-seven a (77a ) and providing for the temporary commit-
ment for care and observation of persons whose mental con-

48 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
dition is so abnormal as to require their temporary commit-
ment for care and observation pending a determination
whether or not they are insane .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
If the certificate of the medical examiners men-
tioned in the preceding sections , instead of stating
that in the opinion of such examiners the person is
insane , shall state in substance that in the opinion.
of such examiners the mental condition of the per-
son is so abnormal that the safety of the person or
of the public requires that he be committed for a
period of care and further medical examination and
observation of his mental condition , upon the filing
of such certificate by the court may order such per-
son to a state hospital for the insane or to a city or
county hospital in the county having a psychopathic
ward or facilities suitable , in the court's opinion ,
for the care and medical examination and observa-
tion of mental diseases , for a period of care and
further medical examination and observation of the
mental condition of such person not to exceed thirty-
five ( 35 ) days . Within thirty ( 30 ) days after such
commitment , if the superintendent of the institu-
tion to which the person has been committed shall
be of the opinion that the person is sane , he shall
so certify to the judge who committed him , who
shall thereupon order the discharge of the person ,
and it shall be the duty of the clerk to mail an at-
tested copy of such order to the superintendent of
the institution to which the person was committed .
Within thirty ( 30 ) days after such commitment , if
the superintendent of the institution to which the
person has been committed be of the opinion that
the person is insane , he shall so certify to the judge
who committed him, whereupon , the judge shall
again appoint two medical examiners as provided

CHAPTER 17.
AN ACT relating to prisoners now or hereafter confined in
State prisons ; providing for the working of such prisoners
on prison farms , highways , and other public works ; providing
for the care , supervision , disciplining , maintenance , trans-
portation , and housing of such prisoners while so engaged
and for the payment of the costs and expenses thereof ;
authorizing payment of compensation to such prisoners and
their dependent families ; repealing all laws and parts of
laws in conflict with the Act ; and declaring an emergency .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One . The State Welfare Board is hereby
authorized and directed to classify for working pur-
poses all prisoners now or hereafter confined in
the State prisons . In making such classifications
the State Welfare Board shall take into account the
past record of each prisoner before and after con-
finement in prison , his age, physical fitness for vari-
ous types of work and such other conditions as the
Board may deem proper in order to classify each
and every prisoner as to his general fitness and
suitability for work within and without the prison.

50 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
walls or enclosures . Provided , however , that no
prisoner who has been sentenced for rape , attempted
rape , detaining a woman against her will , or who
has escaped or attempted to escape , or who has
been convicted and sentenced more than twice , or
who has been convicted of highway robbery or bank
robbery , shall be worked or released for work out-
side of the walls of the prison ; and no prisoner who
has been sentenced to life imprisonment shall be
worked or released for work outside of the walls of
the prison until he shall have actually served within
the walls of the prison five ( 5 ) years or more of
such sentence .
Section Two . The State Welfare Board shall
make and establish such rules and regulations as it
may deem necessary or proper in relation to : ( a )
The classification of prisoners as provided in Sec-
tion one. (b ) The terms and conditions under which
such prisoners may be assigned to work under the
direction of any other State Commission , Depart-
ment , Board or Agency . ( c ) For the adequate care ,
supervision , guarding , discipline , maintenance ,
transportation and housing of such prisoners when
so assigned to work outside of the prison walls or
enclosures . Provided however the State Welfare
Board shall ascertain from the jailor of the county
in which the work is being carried on under the pro-
visions of this Act and if accommodations are avail-
able , shall place as many prisoners as possible under
the care of said jailor . ( d ) For the payment of
money to such prisoners and their dependents for
work performed under the provisions of this Act ,
including the amount to be paid and the manner of
the payment and distribution thereof . ( e ) For such
other purposes as the Board may deem necessary
and proper for carrying out the intent of this Act
and not in coniflct with any of the provisions thereof .

CHAPTER 17 51
Section Three . The State Welfare Board is
hereby authorized to enter into contracts with any
other Commission , Board , Department or Agency
of this State for the use and employment by
such other Commissions , Boards , Departments or
Agencies of prisoners who may be eligible for such
assignments under the provisions of this Act and
the rules and regulations made and established by
the Welfare Board . Such contracts shall specifically
set forth the compensation to be paid to the Wel-
fare Board for the use and employment of such
prisoners , for the payment of the expenses of trans-
porting , guarding , housing , disciplining , and main-
taining such prisoners while so employed and the
amount so provided to be paid shall be certified by
the contracting parties to the Auditor of Public
Accounts at the end of each month and the same
shall be charged to the appropriation of the Com-
mission , Board , Department or Agency liable for
the payment thereof and credited to the budget of
the Department of Public Welfare to be disbursed
and expended as said Board shall direct . Provided ,
however , that any such contract may provide for a
fixed per diem compensation to be paid to the State
Welfare Board for each day's work performed by
any such prisoner and that the Welfare Board out
of the per diem compensation so fixed shall pay such
or all of the expenses of transportation , guarding ,
disciplining , housing and maintaining of the
prisoners as may be provided in such contracts .
Section Four . Prisoners confined in the State
prisons shall not be required by the State Welfare
Board , either by contract or otherwise , to work or
labor outside of the walls or enclosures of the prison
except : ( a ) On the construction , reconstruction or
maintenance of public roads and bridges . (b ) The
making and preparing materials for public roads

52 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
and bridges . ( c ) Work upon the public works of
the Commonwealth of Kentucky . ( d ) On a State
farm or farms , and (e ) Except during pestilence
or in the case of the destruction of prison buildings
when such prisoners cannot be confined within the
prison walls or enclosures . Such prisoners shall
not be used in any county for work either in the
construction , improvement or maintenance of any
highway without the consent of the Fiscal Court of
such county . Provided nothing in this Act shall be
construed to deprive any county of receiving its
portion of the two million dollars ( $ 2,000,000.00 )
road fund allocated to the counties by an Act passed
at the present session of the General Assembly and
designated as Senate Bill number three hundred
twenty -nine ( 329 ) nor shall any rule prepared under
the provisions of this or any Act require or per-
mit the working of convict labor in any county ,
without the consent of the fiscal court of said county .
Section Five . The rules and regulations to be
made and established by the Board of Welfare , as
hereinabove provided , shall provide some definite
and fixed compensation to be paid to the prisoner
or his dependents for each day such prisoner works
or labors outside of the prison walls or enclosures
under any contract made under the provisions of
this Act . It shall be the duty of said Board to col-
lect information concerning the number and require-
ments of the dependents of the prisoners ; and those
with families of greatest dependency shall , in the
discretion of said Board , be given preference in as-
signments to work outside of the prison walls or
enclosures under the provisions of this Act , if the
record , physical ability , conduct and behavior of
such prisoner warrants such preference . The rules
and regulations authorized by this Act may provide
that in the event of any infraction of such rules or

CHAPTER 17 53
of the rules governing the discipline of such
prisoner , either while confined within the walls of
the prison , or while engaged in employment as pro-
vided in this Act outside of said prison , by any
prisoner , shall work a forfeiture of the benefits
authorized in this Section , either in whole or in part ,
in the discretion of the State Welfare Board . Pay-
ments to the prisoners or their dependents as
authorized by this Section shall be made subject to
the aforesaid rules and regulations , as other ex-
penses of the Welfare Board , but only out of the
funds made available under the provisions of the
particular contract under which such prisoners have
been employed .
Section Six. Before any contract authorized by
this Act shall become effective , it must be approved
by the Governor in writing ; and , it is hereby de-
clared to be the legislative intent that each and
every contract entered into under this Act shall pro-
vide for the payment to the State Welfare Board
of an amount sufficient to pay the entire cost of the
care , housing , transporting , guarding , disciplining ,
and maintaining all of the prisoners assigned to
work under such contract for the period of time
during which they are so assigned .
Section Seven . Chapter one hundred fifty ( 150 )
of the Acts of the General Assembly of one thou-
sand nine hundred twenty -six ( 1926 ) and all other
Acts and parts of Acts , and laws and parts of laws
in conflict herewith, to the extent of such conflict ,
hereby are repealed .
Section Eight . If any clause , sentence , para-
graph , section or part of this Act shall for any
reason be adjudged by any court of competent juris-
diction to be invalid or unconstitutional , such judg-
ment shall not affect , impair or invalidate the re-
mainder of this Act , but the effect of such judgment

54 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ÁSSEMBLY
shall be limited to the clause , sentence , paragraph ,
section or part thereof directly involved in the con-
troversy in which such judgment shall have been
rendered ; and , it hereby is declared to be the intent
of the General Assembly to enact each clause ,
sentence , paragraph and section separately , and it
is further declared that if any clause , sentence ,
paragraph , section or part of the Act be held in-
valid the General Assembly nevertheless would have
enacted the remainder of this Act .
Section Nine . Whereas , a large percentage of
the number of the prisoners confined in State
prisons are now idle ; and , due to lack of employ-
ment there is a great danger of mutiny , discontent ;
and , whereas , said prisoners are being maintained
at great cost and loss to the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky , an emergency is hereby declared to exist and
this Act shall become effective immediately upon
its passage and approval by the Governor .
Approved February 18 , 1936 .
CHAPTER 18 .
AN ACT to permit J. W. Beams to sue the State of Kentucky .
WHEREAS , J. W. Beams was injured while working for the
Highway Department June fourteen ( 14 ) , one thousand nine
hundred thirty -four ( 1934 ) , by a truck , sustaining the fracture
of the bones of the leg between the knee and ankle and
mashed in the hip and from which he was caused to suffer
great pain , was confined to hospitals , bed and indoors for
eleven months , and all without any fault on his part, Now
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky
That the said Beams be permitted to , within six
months following the passage of this Act , institute
an action in the county in which said injury was
sustained for damages on account of said injury ;

CHAPTER 19 55
and , in the event injury shall be awarded , that the
Auditor of this State shall draw his draft upon the
Treasury of the State for the satisfaction of such
judgment . Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 19 .
AN ACT, proposing an amendment to the Constitution to be
submitted to the electorate ; which amendment authorizes the
General Assembly to reorganize government within the ter-
ritorial confines of counties under powers and limitations
therein set out . To be known as the Garnett -Mayer Act .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One . That upon the concurrence of three-
fifths of all members elected to each house of the
General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky , the ayes and nays being taken thereon and
entered in full in their respective journals , that
there be and hereby proposed an amendment to the
Constitution of the Commonwealth of Kentucky ,
which amendment reads as follows : Constitutional
Amendment within the territorial confines of coun-
ties (as now existing or as hereafter established )
the General Assembly may by legislation of state-
wide application , or of application according to
classification based on population , retain , modify ,
and consolidate any and/or all governmental bodies ,
offices , agencies , and institutions ; may enlarge , cur-
tail , modify , consolidate , redefine and redistribute
the powers , duties , functions , and jurisdiction of
governmental bodies , offices , agencies , institutions
and courts , and may provide the number of judges
for such courts ; and may abolish all governmental
bodies , offices , agencies and institutions , and may
abolish all courts inferior to the Circuit Court and

56 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
may set up new ones in their stead ; and may pro-
vide the method of selection of all officers , their
compensation and the means and method of its pay-
ment . Provided ; no legislation hereunder shall
affect any elected officer during his term of office .
The provisions of this Constitution are retained
limiting the powers of public corporations and
public quasi -corporations to tax , to incur debts , and
to impose pre-existing debts on territory thereto-
fore free therefrom or to release from pre -existing
debts territory theretofore liable therefor ; except
that the General Assembly, in creating any new
public corporation and/or quasi -corporation may
classify it be population in respect to taxing and
borrowing powers as cities are classified by this
Constitution .
Section Two . This amendment shall be published
and submitted to the voters of the State for their
ratification or rejection at the time and in the
manner provided under Sections two hundred fifty-
six ( 256 ) and two hundred fifty - seven ( 257 ) of the
Constitution of the Commonwealth of Kentucky
and under Section one thousand four hundred fifty-
nine ( 1459 ) of Baldwin's Revised Edition of Car-
roll's Kentucky Statutes of one thousand nine hun-
dred thirty ( 1930 ) , official edition ; Provided that
this amendment shall be submitted on a separate
ballot . Approved February 27 , 1936 .
CHAPTER 20 .
AN ACT to amend Section two hundred fifty -six of the Con-
stitution of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , prescribing the
mode of amendment of said Constitution .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :

CHAPTER 20 57
Section One . That upon the concurrence of three-
fifths of all members elected to each House , the yeas
and nays being taken thereon and entered in full in
their respective journals , Section two hundred fifty-
six (256 ) of the Constitution be and it is amended
and revised so that said section shall read as fol-
lows : Section two hundred fifty -six (256 ) . Amend-
ments to this Constitution may be proposed in either
house of the General Assembly at a regular or
special session thereof , and if such amendment or
amendments shall be agreed to by three -fifths of all
the members elected to each house , such proposed
amendment or amendments , with the yeas and nays
of all the members of each house taken thereon ,
shall be entered in full in their respective journals .
Such proposed amendment or amendments shall
then be submitted to the voters of the State , for
their ratification or rejection , on a separate ballot ,
without party designation of any kind , but under
the heading , " Constitutional Amendments , " printed
in bold face type , at either a special or general elec-
tion , as the General Assembly may prescribe , the
vote to be taken thereon and to be certified by the
officers of election to the Secretary of State in such
manner as shall be provided by law, which vote
shall be canvassed or compared and certified by the
same board authorized by law to compare the polls
and give the certificates of election to officers for
the State at Large . If it shall appear that a ma-
jority of the votes cast for and against the amend-
ment at said election was for the amendment , then
the same shall become a part of the Constitution of
this Commonwealth , and shall be so forthwith pro-
claimed by the Governor , and published in such
manner as the General Assembly may direct . None
of said proposed amendments shall be submitted
at an election which occurs less than ninety (90 )

58 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
days from the final passage of each proposed amend-
ment or amendments . Said amendments shall be so
submitted as to allow a separate vote on each , and
no amendment shall relate to more than one sub-
ject . The approval of the Governor shall not be
necessary to any bill, order, resolution or vote of
the General Assembly , proposing an amendment or
amendments to this Constitution .
Section Two . The foregoing proposed amend-
ment to the Constitution , relating to the mode of
amendment of said Constitution and dealing with
that one subject alone , shall be submitted to the
voters of this State for their ratification or rejec-
tion , at the next general election for members of
the House of Representatives of this Commonwealth
in accordance with the provisions of Section two
hundred fifty -six of the Constitution of Kentucky
and at the time and in the manner provided for
under said section of the Constitution and the stat-
utes enacted pursuant thereto to effectuate the same .
Approved February 27, 1936 .

Section One ( 1 ) . That section one hundred and
eighty -six c - seven ( 186c -7 ) of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , Baldwin's Revision , Supplement to one
thousand nine hundred and thirty -three ( 1933 ) , re-
lating to bonds or obligations of any county , city,
municipality or other taxing district , be, and the

ity or other taxing district , a copy of the judg-

ment for the issuance of bonds in conformity

ernor and be given a retroactive effect .
Section Three (3 ) . All Acts or parts of Acts in
conflict herewith are hereby repealed .
Approved February 18 , 1936 .

CHAPTER 22 .
AN ACT empowering and authorizing the Court of Appeals of
the Commonwealth of Kentucky to render advisory opinion
as to the constitutionality of any bill or measure pending
in either the House or Senate , during any regular or special
session of the General Assembly of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky , upon written request by the Governor .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
One ( 1 ) . The Governor , by a request in writing ,
or either house of the General Assembly by a reso-
lution of such house , may obtain the written opinion
of the justices of the Court of Appeals of Kentucky ,
or a majority thereof, on important constitutional
questions , and all such opinions shall be published
in volumes entitled " Advisory Opinions of the Court
of Appeals .
Two ( 2 ) . The General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky being now in regular ses-
sion and many important measures being intro-
duced at its daily sessions , an emergency is hereby
declared to exist and this Act shall take effect from
and after its passage and approval by the Governor .
Approved January 22 , 1936 .
CHAPTER 23 .
AN ACT to subject to execution and garnishment salaries of
State , county , school boards and municipal employees ; and
providing in which courts any such suits shall be filed .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That salaries or sum due State , county , school
boards , and municipal employees shall be subject
to attachment or garnishment the same as if such
salaries or sums were due from an individual ,

62 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
That all suits , whether actions at law or in
equity , wherein the salaries or sum due State ,
county , school boards and municipal employees are
garnisheed as provided herein , shall be filed in any
court of this State , which court has jurisdiction of
the subject matter of such suit or action as is now
provided by law . Approved February 27 , 1936 .
CHAPTER 24.
AN ACT relating to fiscal courts composed of County Judge
and County Commissioners , repealing and re -enacting Section
one thousand eight hundred fifty (1850 ) , Kentucky Statutes ,
Carroll's Edition , one thousand nine hundred thirty ( 1930 ) .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One . That Section one thousand eight
hundred fifty ( 1850 ) of Kentucky Statutes , Car-
roll's Edition , one thousand nine hundred thirty
( 1930 ) , be and the same is hereby repealed and re-
enacted , so that same , when thus re -enacted , shall
read as follows , to -wit :
All the provisions of this article governing fiscal
courts composed of the judge of the county court
and justices of the peace shall be applicable to and
govern fiscal courts , composed of three commis-
sioners and the judge of the county court , except
that when the county judge is unable to call a
special session , and it is necessary that one be called ,
then two commissioners may call the same , if , in
their opinion , the necessity exists therefor . Any
vacancy occurring in the office of commissioner shall
be filled in the same manner as vacancies in the
offices of justices of the peace are filled . And when-
ever there shall be a tie vote in said Board of Com-
missioners upon the question of the election of any

CHAPTER 25 63
officer or employee to be elected or employed by said
Commissioners , and a deadlock shall result and said
tie vote or deadlock shall continue for a period of
not less than fifteen ( 15 ) days , it shall thereupon
be and become the duty of the county judge to cause
to be entered upon the minutes of the Board of
Commissioners , an order reciting the facts as to
said deadlock or tie vote , and the question upon
which the same has occurred and exists , and there-
upon unless an election is immediately had by said
Board , it shall be and become the duty of the county
judge to appoint such officer or employe and such
appointee shall have all the qualifications required
by law of such officer or employee , and shall hold his
office for the full term provided by law , and until
his successor is elected and qualified .
Inasmuch as the Board of Commissioners con-
sists of four members and tied votes and deadlocks
will result , an emergency is hereby declared to exist
and this Act shall take effect from and after its ap-
proval by the Governor . Approved February 7, 1936 .
CHAPTER 25 .
AN ACT to amend and re -enact Chapter sixty -six ( 66 ) , Article
eleven ( 11 ) , Section two thousand one hundred seventeen
( 2117 ) , Carroll's Kentucky Statutes nineteen thirty ( 1930 ) ,
relating to divorce by adding thereto certain causes for
divorce to the husband .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Chapter sixty- six ( 66 ) , Article eleven ( 11 ) ,
Section two thousand one hundred seventeen ( 2117 ) ,
Carroll's Kentucky Statutes nineteen thirty ( 1930 )
relating to divorce be and the same is hereby

64 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
amended and re -enacted so that the same shall read
as follows :
Two thousand one hundred seventeen ( 2117 ) .
Equity Jurisdiction ; Cause for Divorce .-A jury
shall not be impaneled in any action for divorce ,
alimony or maintenance , but courts having general
equity jurisdiction may grant a divorce for any of
the following causes , to both husband and wife :
One ( 1 ) . To both parties . Such impotency or
malformation as prevents sexual intercourse .
Two ( 2 ) . Living apart without any cohabitation
for five consecutive years next before the applica-
tion .
To party not in fault . Also to the party not in
fault , for the following causes :
One ( 1 ) . Abandonment by one party or the other
for one year .
Two ( 2 ) . Living in adultery with another man
or woman .
Three ( 3 ) . Condemnation for felony in or out
of this State .
Four (4 ) . Concealment from the other party of
any loathsome disease existing at the time of mar-
riage , or contracting such afterward .
Five ( 5 ) . Force , duress or fraud in obtaining
the marriage.
Six ( 6 ) . Uniting with any religious society whose
creed and rules require a renunciation of the mar-
riage covenant , or forbid husband and wife from
cohabiting .
To the wife . Also to the wife , when not in like
fault , for the following causes :
One ( 1 ) . Confirmed habit of drunkenness on the
part of the husband of not less than one year's
durating accompanied with a wasting of his estate ,
and without any suitable provision for the main-
tenance of his wife or children .

CHAPTER 26 65
4
Two (2 ) . Habitually behaving toward her by
the husband , for not less than six months , in such
cruel and inhuman manner as to indicate a settled
aversion to her , or to destroy permanently her peace
or happiness .
Three ( 3 ) . Such cruel beating or injury, or at-
tempt at injury , of the wife by the husband as in-
dicates an outrageous temper in him, or probable
danger to her life, or great bodily injury from her
remaining with him .
To the husband . Also to the husband for the fol-
lowing causes :
One (1 ) . Where the wife is pregnant by another
man without the husband's knowledge at the time
of marriage .
Two ( 2 ) . When not in like fault , habitual
drunkenness on the part of the wife of not less than
one year's duration .
Three ( 3 ) . Adultery by the wife , or such lewd ,
lascivious behavior on her part as proves her to be
unchaste , without actual proof of an act of adultery .
Four (4) . Habitually behaving towards him by
the wife , for not less than six months , in such cruel
and inhuman manner as to indicate a settled aver-
sion to him or to destroy permanently his peace and
happiness .
Five ( 5 ) . Such cruel beating or injury , or at-
tempt at injury of the husband by the wife as indi-
cates an outrageous temper in her or probable
danger to his life or great bodily injury from his
remaining with her. Approved February 18, 1936 .
CHAPTER 26 .
AN ACT to amend Section two hundred and ninety-seven ( 297 ) ,
Civil Code of Practice , relating to application to a Judge of
Acts- 3

66 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
the Court of Appeals for the issue , reinstatement or dissolu-
tion of a temporary injunction .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Section two hundred ninety - seven ( 297 ) of
the Civil Code be amended so that when amended
it shall read as follows :
Section two hundred ninety-seven ( 297 ) : If a
party to an injunction suit desires to make applica-
tion to a judge of the Court of Appeals for an order
for the issue of a temporary injunction or for the
reinstatement or dissolution of such injunction as
provided in Section two hundred ninety -six ( 296 ) ,
such party shall give reasonable notice in writing
to the party affected thereby of the time and place
of such application , and of the motion to be made at
such time . Such application shall be made by pre-
senting to the Clerk of the Court of Appeals a copy
of the record , including the evidence read or offered
to be read , and a copy of the notice of such applica-
tion . Upon such presentation , such clerk shall at
once notify the Chief Justice of such application and
motion , and the Chief Justice , or the clerk in vaca-
tion , shall thereupon at once assign such applica-
tion and motion to one of the judges of the Court of
Appeals for disposition . The judge of the Court
of Appeals to whom such application and motion
shall have thus been assigned shall consider such
record and shall make such order as he shall deem
proper in the case , either directing that such tempo-
rary injunction shall issue or refusing the same or
directing a dissolution or modification thereof, as
he shall decide . Such order shall be returned by the
appellate judge to the clerk's office of the circuit
court , and , when filed in such clerk's office , shall

CHAPTER 27 67
have the same effect as an order made by such cir-
cuit court . Approved February 18 , 1936 .
CHAPTER 27.
AN ACT to repeal Section three hundred forty -one ( 341 ) and to
amend , and re-enact Section three hundred forty ( 340 ) of
Article five (5 ) , Chapter two (2 ) , Title nine ( 9 ) of the Ken-
tucky Code of Practice in civil cases , relating to new trials .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Section three hundred forty- one ( 341 ) of
Article five ( 5 ) , Chapter two ( 2 ) , Title nine ( 9 ) of
the Kentucky Code of Practice in civil cases , be re-
pealed , and that Section three hundred forty ( 340 )
be amended and re-enacted : by adding to subsec-
tion four ( 4 ) of Section three hundred forty ( 340 ) ,
after the word " excessive " the words " or inade-
quate " and adding thereto after the word " preju-
dice " the words " or in disregard of the evidence
or the instructions of the court " ; and by striking
out the last fourteen ( 14 ) words of subsection five
( 5 ) of Section three hundred forty ( 340 ) . So that
after Section three hundredthree hundred forty ( 340 ) is
so amended and re -enacted it shall read as follows :
" Section three hundred forty ( 340 ) . A new trial
is a re-examination in the same court of an issue of
fact after a verdict by a jury or a decision by the
court. The former verdict or decision may be
vacated and a new trial be granted, on the applica-
tion of the party aggrieved , for any of the follow-
ing causes affecting materially his substantial
rights .
" One ( 1 ) . Irregularity in the proceedings of the
court, jury or prevailing party , or an order of the

68 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
court, or abuse of discretion, by which the party was
prevented from having a fair trial .
" Two (2) . Misconduct of jury , of the prevail-
ing party , or of his attorney .
" Three ( 3 ) . Accident or surprise which ordi-
nary prudence could not have guarded against .
" Four (4 ) . Excessive or inadequate damages ,
appearing to have been given , under the influence
of passion or prejudice or in disregard of the evi-
dence or the instructions of the court .
" Five (5 ) . Error in the assessment of the amount
of recovery whether too large or too small .
" Six (6 ) . That the verdict or decision is not sus-
tained by sufficient evidence , or is contrary to law .
" Seven (7 ) . Newly discovered evidence , mate-
rial for the party applying , which he could not , with
reasonable diligence , have discovered and produced
at the trial .
" Eight ( 8 ) . Error of law occurring at the trial
and excepted to by the party making the applica-
tion . ' Approved February 18, 1936 .
" "
CHAPTER 28.
AN ACT amending and re -enacting Section two thousand two
hundred forty -one ( 2241 ) , Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's one
thousand nine hundred thirty ( 1930 ) Edition [ Baldwin's Re-
vision one thousand nine hundred thirty -three ( 1933 ) Supple-
ment ] .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Section two thousand two hundred forty - one
( 2241 ) Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's one thousand
nine hundred thirty ( 1930 ) Edition [ Baldwin's Re-
vision one thousand nine hundred thirty -three ( 1933 )
Supplement ] be amended and re - enacted so that

CHAPTER 28 69
when so amended and re -enacted it shall read as
follows :
The circuit judge of each county shall at the first
regular term of circuit court therein , after this Act
takes effect and annually thereafter , appoint three
intelligent and discreet housekeepers of the county
over twenty-one years of age, resident in different
portions of the county , and having no action in
court requiring the intervention of a jury , as jury
commissioners for one year , who shall be sworn in
open court , to faithfully discharge their duty. They
shall hold their meetings in some room designated
by the judge ; and , while engaged in making lists of
juries , and selecting the names , writing and deposit-
ing or drawing them from the drum or wheel case ,
no person shall be permitted in said room with
them except that in counties containnig a city of
the first class the commissioners may select a clerk
to aid them in the performance of their duty, who
shall be sworn in open court to faithfully discharge
his duties , and who shall be in attendance on the
commissioners in session ; and such counties by their
board of magistrates or fiscal court are hereby
authorized and empowered to allow and pay to the
clerk of the jury commissioners herein provided for,
such compensation as they may deem just and
proper , not exceeding , however , the sum of one hun-
dred dollars ($ 100.00 ) in any one year .
They shall take the last returned assessor's book
for the county and from it shall carefully select
from the intelligent , sober , discreet and impartial
citizens , resident housekeepers in different portions
of the county , over twenty-one years of age , the
following number of names of such persons , to wit :
In counties having a population of ten thousand
or less , not less than one hundred and sixty -two nor
more than two hundred ; in counties having a popu-

In counties having circuit courts of continuous

CHAPTER 30 .
AN ACT to provide for the postponement of the rendition of
judgment and the use of probation in criminal cases and em-
powering the Department of Public Welfare to appoint a
director of probation and parole , and such probation and
parole officers as are deemed necessary and defining their
powers and duties ; repealing page one hundred forty-eight
( 148 ) , Sections one , two , three and four of the Act of the
General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky for the
year one thousand nine hundred thirty -four ( 1934 ) being
Sections nine hundred seventy -nine b-one ( 979b -1 ) , nine hun-
dred seventy nine b -two ( 979b -2 ) , nine hundred seventy-
nine b -three ( 979b -3 ) and nine hundred seventy -nine b-four
(979b -4) of Baldwin's one thousand nine hundred thirty -four
( 1934 ) Edition of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes and all other
laws or parts of laws in conflict herewith .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One . Probation and Postponement of
Rendition of Judgment . Any Circuit Court of this
Commonwealth , subject to the provisions and con-
ditions hereinafter provided , may postpone the
entering of judgment and the imposing of sentence
and probate any person arraigned before it charged
with crime . The court shall have no power to post-
pone the entering of judgment or the imposing of

CHAPTER 30 75
sentence on any person who has been found guilty
by verdict of the jury and whose punishment is fixed
by the jury at life imprisonment or death . Except
in the cases next hereinabove provided, the afore-
said courts , after a plea of guilty or after the re-
turning of a verdict of guilty by the jury , may post-
pone the entering of judgment and the sentencing
of such defendant and place the defendant on pro-
bation , or may impose a fine and also place the de-
fendant on probation .
Section Two . Investigation . When directed by
the court the probation officer shall fully investigate
and report to the court in writing the circumstances
of the offense , criminal record , social history and
present condition of defendant . No defendant
charged with a felony , and , unless the court shall
direct otherwise in individual cases , no other de-
fendant shall be placed on probation or released
under the postponement of rendition of judgment
until the report of such investigation shall have
been presented to and considered by the court .
Whenever practicable , such investigation shall in-
clude physical and mental examinations of the de-
fendant. If such defendant is committed to an in-
stitution , a copy of the report of such investigation
shall be sent to the institution at the time of com-
mitment .
Section Three . Conditions of Probation . The
court shall determine and may at any time modify
the conditions of probation and may include among
them the following , or any other : That the proba-
tioner shall :
( a) avoid injurious or vicious habits ;
(b) avoid persons or places of disreputable or
harmful character ;
(c) report to the probation officer as directed ;

76 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
(d ) permit the probation officer to visit him at
his home or elsewhere ;
( e ) work faithfully at suitable employments as
far as possible ;
(f) remain within a specified place ;
(g ) pay a fine in one or several sums as directed
by the court ;
(h) make reparation or restitution to the ag-
grieved party for the damage or loss caused by his
offense ; in an amount to be determined by the court ;
(i ) support his dependants .
Section Four . Termination or Probation . Ar-
rest. Subsequent Disposition . The period of proba-
tion or postponement of rendition of judgment shall
be determined by the court and may be continued
or extended . Upon the satisfactory fulfillment of
the conditions of probation or postponement of
rendition of judgment, the court shall by order duly
entered discharge the defendant . At any time dur-
ing the period of probation or postponement of
judgment, the court may issue a warrant and cause
the defendant to be arrested for violating any of
the conditions of probation or postponement or
rendition of judgment . Any probation officer , police
officer or other officer with power of arrest , upon
the request of the probation officer, may arrest a
probationer without a warrant . In case of an ar-
rest without a warrant the arresting officer shall
have a written statement by said probation officer
setting forth that the probationer has , in his judg-
ment , violated the conditions of probation and said
statement shall be sufficient warrant for the deten-
tion of said probationer in the county jail , or other
appropriate place of detention , until such proba-
tioner shall be brought before the court . Such pro-
bation officer shall forthwith report such arrest and
detention to the court and submit in writing a re-

CHAPTER 30 77
port showing in what manner the probationer has
violated probation . Thereupon the court, after a
hearing , may revoke the probation or postponement
of rendition of judgment and shall proceed to deal
with the case as if there had been no probation or
postponement of rendition of judgment.
Section Five . Appointment of the Director of
Probation and Parole ; Duties . The Department of
Public Welfare shall appoint a Director of Proba-
tion and Parole , who shall receive a salary to be
fixed by the Department of Public Welfare not to
exceed Three Thousand Dollars per annum , and who
shall give his entire time to the work . The Director
of Probation and Parole shall appoint , subject to
the approval of the Department of Public Welfare ,
such probation and parole officers as are required
for service in the Circuit Courts , and for the super-
vision of persons paroled from the institutions
under the control of the Department of Public Wel-
fare . The Director shall from time to time conduct
competitive examinations to establish lists of per-
sons eligible for appointment as probation and
parole officers . He shall prescribe qualifications for
entrance into such examinations and the eligibility
of candidates for appointment . No person shall be
appointed under this Act as probation or parole
officer who has not been certified by the Director of
Probation and Parole as eligible in pursuance of
such rules and examinations . The Director of Pro-
bation and Parole shall supervise the work of the
Probation and Parole Officers appointed under this
Act . He shall keep informed of the work of all pro-
bation and parole officers ; he shall inspect the work
of such officers and shall have access to their
records ; he shall make an annual written report
with statistical and other information to the De-
partment of Public Welfare ; he shall compile and

78 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
publish annually a list of the probation and parole
officers of the State . He shall require reports of
their work at regular intervals from probation and
parole officers and juvenile courts ; shall prescribe
the form of records and reports to be used by pro-
bation and parole officers ; shall consult and coop-
erate with the courts and institutions in the develop-
ment of methods and procedure in the administra-
tion of probation and parole ; and shall arange con-
ferences of probation and parole officers and judges .
Section Six. Assignment and Compensation of
Probation and Parole Officers . Probation and parole
officers appointed under this Act shall be assigned
to serve in such courts or districts as the Director
of Probation and Parole may determine . They shall
be paid such salaries as the Department of Public
Welfare may prescribe not to exceed Eighteen Hun-
dred Dollars per annum and may also be paid
traveling and other necessary expenses incurred in
the performance of their duties . The number of
Probation and Parole Officers shall not exceed one
for each judicial district in the Commonwealth of
Kentucky .
Section Seven . Duties and Powers of Probation
and Parole Officers . A probation or parole officer
shall investigate all cases referred to him for in-
vestigation by any court or by the Department of
Public Welfare , and shall report in writing thereon .
He shall furnish to each person released on proba-
tion or parole under his supervision a written state-
ment of the conditions of probation or parole and
shall instruct him regarding the same . Such officer
shall keep informed concerning the conduct and
condition of each person on probation or parole
under his supervision by visiting , requiring reports
and in other ways and shall report thereon in writ-
ing as often as the court or Department of Public

CHAPTER 30 79
Welfare may require . Such officer shall use all prac-
ticable and suitable methods , not inconsistent with
the condition imposed by the court, or Department
of Public Welfare , to aid and encourage persons on
probation or parole and to bring about improvement
in their conduct and condition . Such officer shall
keep detailed records of his work ; shall keep accu-
rate and complete accounts of all monies collected
from persons under his supervision and shall keep
or give receipts therefor ; shall pay over all monies
collected from the defendant to the court having
jurisdiction of a person on probation ; and in the
event that such money is collected in restitution , or
for the support of neglected or abandoned depend-
ents , such money shall be paid to the aggrieved ,
neglected or abandoned parties and receipts therefor
taken and filed in the records of the case ; shall make
such reports in writing to the Director of Probation
and Parole as he may require ; and shall perform
such other duties as the Director of Probation and
Parole may require . A probation or parole officer
shall have , in the execution of his duties , the powers
of arrest and the same right to execute process as
is now given , or that may hereafter be given by
law , to the sheriffs of this Commonwealth .
Section Eight . Protection of Records . All in-
formation and data obtained in the discharge of
official duty by any probation or parole officer shall
be privileged information , shall not be received as
evidence in any court, and shall not be disclosed
directly or indirectly to any one other than to the
Judges or to others entitled under this Act to re-
ceive reports , unless and until otherwise ordered
by such Judge or the Department of Public Welfare .
Section Nine . Office Accommodations of Pro-
bation Officers . The fiscal court in each county in
which a probation officer serves shall provide in or

80 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
near the Court House suitable office space for such
officer .
Section Ten . Page one hundred forty -eight ( 148 ) ,
Sections one ( 1 ) , two ( 2 ) , three ( 3 ) and four ( 4 ) of
the Acts of the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky for the year one thousand nine
hundred thirty -four ( 1934 ) , being Sections nine
hundred seventy -nine b-one ( 979b -1 ) , nine hundred
seventy -nine b-two ( 979b - 2 ) , nine hundred seventy-
nine b-three and nine hundred seventy - nine b-four
( 979b -4 ) of Baldwin's one thousand nine hundred
thirty-four (1934 ) edition of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes and all other laws or parts of laws in con-
flict herewith are hereby repealed .
Approved February 21 , 1936 .
