CHAPTER 32 .
AN ACT making it a misdemeanor punishable by fine and im-
prisonment for anyone failing and refusing to extinguish any
fire that he may originate or start for any purpose within the
territory of any forest reserve or park or other woodland
within the confines of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , or
over which it or the Federal Government has power and
authority of regulatory control .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One ( 1 ) . It is hereby made the duty of
any person who starts or consents to starting , or
originates, any fire for any purpose within the
boundaries of any forest lands within this Common-
wealth , whether they be public forest reserves or
public parks or not , to totally extinguish such fires
before leaving them unprotected , and if he should

82 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
fail to do so and should abandon same whereby
damage and injury is produced to growing timber
on land upon which the fire was started , or to which
it may be communicated , he shall be guilty of a mis-
demeanor and upon conviction shall be fined not
less than ten ( $ 10.00 ) dollars nor more than one
hundred ($100.00 ) dollars , or imprisoned in the
county jail not less than ten ( 10 ) days nor more than
one hundred (100 ) days , or both fined and im-
prisoned at the discretion of the court or jury try-
ing the case . Approved February 18 , 1936 .
CHAPTER 33 .
AN ACT to prohibit marathons , danceathons , walkathons ,
skateathons , or any endurance contest of a like or similar
nature in the State of Kentucky , and providing penalties for
any person , form or corporation participating in, aiding in or
promoting such contests .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One . It shall be unlawful for any per-
son or persons , firm or corporation , to advertise ,
maintain , promote or to aid in the advertising , op-
erating, promoting or maintaining of any mental or
physical endurance contest in the nature of a dance ,
marathon , walkathon , skateathon or any such endur-
ance contests of a like or similar nature , whether
under that or similar names . Nothing in this bill
shall apply to the continuance of the ordinary ama-
teur or professional athletic events or contests of
high school , college or inter -collegiate sport , six-
day bicycle racing or similar contests which are
conducted for a definite length of time .
Section Two . Any person , firm or corporation
participating in or promoting any such contests and

CHAPTER 34 83
violating any of the provisions of this bill shall be
deemed guilty of a misdemeanor , and upon convic-
tion thereof shall be fined not less than fifty dollars
( $ 50 ) nor more than three hundred dollars ( $ 300 ) ,
to which may be added imprisonment not exceeding
one hundred and eighty ( 180 ) days , in the discretion
of the court . Each day on which violations continue
shall constitute a separate offense under this Act .
Approved February 27, 1936 .
������� 34 .
AN ACT to repeal , amend and re -enact Section two hundred
one c -one ( 201c -1 ) to two hundred one -twelve ( 201c -12 ) in-
clusive , Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's one thousand nine hun-
dred thirty ( 1930 ) edition , relating to the issual of free passes
and giving of reduced rates by common carriers , and repeal
ing all laws in conflict therewith .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Section two hundred one c-one ( 201c - 1 ) to
two hundred one c-twelve ( 201c - 12 ) inclusive , Car-
roll's Kentucky Statutes , one thousand nine hun-
dred and thirty ( 1930 ) being the whole of Chapter
one of the Acts of the General Assembly of one
thousand nine hundred and sixteen ( 1916 ) , relating
to the issual of free passes and the giving of re-
duced rates by common carriers , so when said Act
is repealed and re -enacted it will read as follows :
Section One ( 1 ) . It shall be unlawful for any
common carrier of passengers in this State or any
officer or agent of such common carrier to issue or
give to any person or persons any free pass or
free transportation or sell any ticket at reduced
rates not common to the public , for the transporta-
tion of any passenger or passengers within this
State , and it shall be unlawful for any person or

84 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
persons other than those designated in Section two
(2 ) of this Act , to accept or use, or attempt to use
any free pass or free transportation or ticket issued
or given by such common carrier .
Section Two ( 2 ) . No common carrier subject to
the provisions of this Act shall , directly or indi-
rectly , issue or give any free pass or free transporta-
tion or sell any ticket at reduced rates not common
to the public , for passengers , except to its officers ,
bona fide employees , agents , attorneys , physicians
and surgeons and the dependent members of their
immediate families : ministers of religion , persons
engaged exclusively in charitable and eleemosynary
work , inmates of homes , for the rescue and reform
of the unfortunate or vicious , including those about
to enter such institutions , and those returning home
after discharge , and boards of managers , including
officers and superintendents of such homes : in-
digent , destitute and homeless persons , and such
persons when transported by charitable societies or
hospitals , and the necessary agents employed in
such transportation : necessary caretakers of live-
stock , poultry , milk or fruit , including return trans-
portation to the forwarding points ; traveling sec-
retaries of Young Men's Christian Associations ,
and officers or regular accredited representatives of
labor organizations, composed wholly of em-
ployees of such common carriers ; employees of
sleeping cars , express cars and linemen of tele-
graph and telephone companies ; railway mail
service employees , post office inspectors , custom
inspectors and immigration inspectors ; newsboys
on trains and baggage agents ; witnesses attend-
ing any legal investigation or proceeding in
which such common carrier is interested ; per-
sons injured in wrecks or accidents and phy-
sicians and nurses attending such persons , and
11

CHAPTER 34 85
employees crippled or disabled in the service of
such common carrier ; to inmates of national or
state homes for soldiers and sailors ; policemen and
firemen of any city wearing the insignia of their
office within the limits of such city ; school children
going to and returning from public or parochial
schools , or students of the State university or State
normal schools , going to and returning from such
institutions ; regularly employed common school and
high school inspectors ; all persons traveling upon
a special train or car provided by such common
carrier for educational , health or agricultural pur-
poses , or any car for the distribution of fish or game
or the employees of the game and fish commisison
while engaged in the distribution of fish or game
and , provided further , that nothing contained in
this Act shall be construed or deemed to prohibit
the interchange of free passes or free transporta-
tion for officers , agents , attorneys and employees
of such common carriers and the dependent mem-
bers of their immediate families .
Section Three (3 ) . " Free pass " or " free trans-
portation " as used in this Act shall include any
ticket , passpass contract , permit or transportation
issued , furnished or given to any person by any
common carrier of passengers , or passage for any
other consideration than money paid in the usual
way at the same rate , fare or charge open to all
who desire to purchase , and shall include any ticket
sold at reduced rate not common to all the public ;
Provided , however that nothing herein contained
shall prevent the bona fide exchange of transporta-
tion for advertising matter actually contracted .
"Families " as used in this Act include the families
of those persons killed and widows during widow-
hood and minor children , during minority , of per-
sons who died while in the service of such carrier .

86 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
"Employees " as used in this Act , shall include fur-
loughed , pensioned supernnuated employees , and
persons who have become disabled or infirm in the
service of such carrier .
Section Four ( 4 ) . Any common carrier of pas-
sengers , or any officer , agent , or representative
thereof, violating any of the provisions of this Act ,
shall for each offense , be fined not less than five hun-
dred dollars , ( $500 ) , nor more than twenty -five hun-
dred dollars , ( $ 2,500 ) or confined in the county jail
for not less than thirty days , and not more than
ninety days , or may be both so fined and imprisoned
in the discretion of the jury ; and any person or
persons other than those excepted in this Act who
shall accept or use , or attempt to use any free pass
or free transportation for carriage or passage
within this State , shall be subject to a like penalty.
Section Five ( 5 ) . No person within the purview
of this Act shall be privileged from testifying in
relation to anything herein prohibited , but no per-
son having so testified shall be liable to any prose-
cution or punishment for any offense concerning
while he was required to give his testimony .
Section Six ( 6 ) . The circuit court or any court
through , or into while , any person shall be trans-
ported in violation of the provisions of this Act
shall have jurisdiction of the offense .
Section Seven ( 7 ) Every common carrier of
passengers in this State shall keep an accurate
record of each and every free pass , or free trans-
portation or ticket sold at reduced rate not common
to the public , while it has issued or given to any per-
son , except to its officers and regular employees ,
and their immediate families , within one year next
before the thirty -first day of December of each year,
was required to give his testimony .
1

CHAPTER 34 87
Section Six ( 6 ) . The circuit court or any court
through , or into which any person shall be trans-
ported in violation of the provisions of this Act
shall have jurisdiction of the offense .
Section Seven (7 ) . Every common carrier of
passengers in this State shall keep an accurate
record of each and every free pass , or free trans-
portation or ticket sold at reduced rate not common
to the public , which it has issued or given to any
person , except to its officers and regular employees ,
and their immediate families , within one year next
before the thirty-first day of December of each year,
which record shall state when and to whom each
free pass of free transportation was given , the
nature and character of the free pass , whether a
general pass , and for what length of time given , or
whether a limited pass , and to points from which
and to which it was issued . On or before March
first ( 1 ) one thousand nine hundred eighteen ( 1918 ) ,
every such common carrier shall file in the office of
the attorney general a true and correct report of
all such free passes or free transportation issued
between January first ( 1 ) , one thousand nine hun-
dred seventeen ( 1917 ) , and December thirty -first
( 31 ) , one thousand nine hundred seventeen ( 1917 ) ,
and thereafter every such common carrier shall , on
or before the first of March , file in the same manner
a statement of all such free passes , or free trans-
portation , or ticket sold at reduced rates not com-
mon to the public given or issued during the year
preceding such statement . Such report shall be
subscribed and sworn to by the president , general
passenger agent or secretary of such carrier and
when filed in the office of the attorney general shall
constitute a public record. Any common carrier of
passengers who shall fail to file such report on or
before the first ( 1 ) day of March as required by this

88 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
section , shall be fined one thousand dollars upon in-
dictment and conviction in the Franklin circuit
court, or other court of common jurisdiction . Any
officer of such carrier who shall make in such report
any false statement , upon indictment , trial and con-
viction in the Franklin circuit court or other court
of competent jurisdiction , shall be deemed guilty of
false swearing and shall suffer the penalty now
prescribed by law for that crime .
Section Eight ( 8 ) . It shall be the duty of every
circuit judge in this Commonwealth at every term
of court, in each county in his district , into or
through which the line or lines of such carrier is
operated , and of the Franklin circuit court , to give
the provisions of this Act in charge to the grand
jury .
Section Nine ( 9 ) . The attorney general at all
times shall have access to the original record of
such passes in the office of such carriers , which is
required by this Act to be kept . All laws or parts
of laws in conflict herewith are hereby repealed .
Approved February 25 , 1936 .
CHAPTER 35 .
AN ACT for the conservation of natural gas and prevention of
its waste .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That natural gas shall not be permitted to waste
or escape from any well or pipe line , when it is rea-
sonably possible to prevent such waste , after the
owner or operator of such gas , or well , or pipe line ,
has had a reasonable length of time to shut in such
gas in the well , or make the necessary repairs to

CHAPTER 35 89
66
such well or pipe line to prevent such waste . Pro-
vided, however , that ( a ) if , in the process of drill-
ing a well for oil or gas , or both , gas is found in
such well , and the owner or operator thereof desires
to continue to search for oil or gas , or both , by drill-
ing deeper in search of lower oil or gas bearing
strata , or ( b ) if it becomes necessary to make re-
pairs to any well producing gas , commonly known
as 'cleaning out , " and if in either event it is nec-
essary for the gas in such well to escape therefrom
during the process of drilling or making repairs , as
the case may be , then the owner or operator of such
well shall prosecute such drilling or repairs with
reasonable diligence , so that the waste of gas from
the well shall not continue longer than reasonably
necessary , and if, during the progress of such
deeper drilling or repairs , any temporary suspen-
sion thereof becomes necessary , the owner or op-
erator of such well shall use all reasonable means
to shut in the gas and prevent its waste during such
temporary suspension : Provided , further , that in
all cases where both oil and gas are found and pro-
duced from the same oil and gas bearing stratum ,
and where it is necessary for the gas therefrom to
waste in the process of producing the oil , the owner
or operator shall use all reasonable diligence to con-
serve and save from waste so much of such gas as
it is reasonably possible to save . In case of waste
arising under this Act , the presumption may be in-
dulged that such waste was due to the negligence
of the operator . Any violation of this Act or any
provision thereof shall be a misdemeanor and upon
conviction for such violation the offender shall be
fined not exceeding two hundred ($ 200.00 ) dollars .
If the offender be a natural person , he shall be im-
prisoned not less than ten ( 10 ) days nor more than

90 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
six (6 ) months at the discretion of the jury . Each
day's violation of this Act shall constitute a sepa-
rate offense . Approved February 25 , 1936 .
CHAPTER 36 .
AN ACT prohibiting the sale , disposition of , severance , cutting ,
or removal of standing timber ( other than a reasonable
amount for firewood and fencing ) , houses , buidings , barns ,
soil , minerals, and improvements in the nature of fixtures
real , from lands covered by mortgages or vendors ' liens ,
without the written consent of the record holders of said
mortgages or liens , and providing penalties therefor, and re-
pealing any and all Acts or parts of Acts , or laws or parts of
laws in conflict herewith .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One ( 1 ) . Any person , firm or corpora-
tion who shall, in person or by agent , fradulently
sell , dispose of, sever , or remove from his land , on
which there is at the time a recorded and unsatisfied
mortgage or vendor's lien , any standing timber,
houses , buildings , barns , soil , minerals , or improve-
ments in the nature of fixtures real , which are
covered by said mortgage or lien without the writ-
ten consent of the record holder of said mortgage
or lien, such person , firm or corporation shall , if
such disposition , sale , severance , cutting or removal
impairs the value of the mortgage or lien security
in an amount exceeding fifty ( $ 50.00 ) dollars , be
deemed guilty of a misdemeanor , and , upon convic-
tion, shall be punished by a fine of not less than ten
($10.00 ) dollarsdollars nor more than five hundred
($500.00 ) dollars or by confinement in the county
jail for a period of not less than ten (10 ) days nor
more than six ( 6 ) months , either or both , in the
discretion of the jury .

CHAPTER 37 91
Section Two ( 2 ) . The selling, disposing of ,
severance , cutting or removal of said property in
said amount and without the written consent of the
record holder of said mortgage or vendor's lien ,
shall constitute prima facie evidence of fraud .
Secton Three ( 3 ) . Nothing in this Act shall pre-
vent the person in possession of said land from
cutting a reasonable amount of said standing
timber for his own firewood or for the repair of
fences or buildings on said land , nor shall anything
in this Act prevent the person in possession of said
land from removing and using a reasonable amount
of coal or gas underlying said land , for the domestic
use of the family of the person in possession .
Section Four ( 4 ) . Any term used in the singular
may , as required by the context hereof, be read as
plural and vice versa . And any term in the mascu-
line gender shall include the feminine and neuter
genders as well .
Section Five ( 5 ) . If any provision of this Act
shall be held invalid , the remander of the Act shall
not be affected thereby .
Section Six ( 6 ) . All Acts or parts of Acts , and
all laws or parts of laws , in conflict herewith , are
hereby repealed . Approved February 6, 1936 .
CHAPTER 37.
AN ACT to make unlawful the fraudulent setting back or dis-
connecting or failure to connect or causing the fraudulent
setting back or disconnecting or failure to connect speed-
ometers on motor vehicles for the purpose of effecting a sale
of such vehicles .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
If any dealer in motor vehicles , or any other per-

92 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
son, shall fraudulently change , set back , or discon-
nect , or fail to connect , or cause to be changed , set
back or to be disconnected , or cause the failure to
connect the speedometer of any motor vehicle , for
the purpose of effecting the sale of such motor ve-
hicle or vehicles , he shall upon conviction thereof
be adjudged guilty of a misdemeanor and shall be
fined in the sum of not less than one hundred dol-
lars nor more than three hundred dollars for each
and every separate offense .
Approved February 18 , 1936 .
CHAPTER 38 .

of Kentucky and authorizing the refunding of the bonded in-

Section One ( 1 ) . Whenever the Board of Drain-

gate the amount of bonds refunded thereby and in-
terest accrued thereon to the date of said refunding
bonds . They shall bear interest at a rate not exceed-

Section Four ( 4 ) . All such taxes or assessments
levied as herein provided , for the payment of prin-
cipal and interest on refunding bonds of any such
district , shall be collected and collection thereof en-
forced, at the same time , in the same manner , and
by the same means as is provided by law for the
collection of other taxes levied by such district ,
and the holder of any such refunding bonds , in ad-
dition to the rights hereby conferred , shall have
the same rights as were possessed by the holders of
the bonds refunded thereby .

CHAPTER 38 97
Section Five ( 5 ) . Any landowner failing to avail
himself of the privilege conferred by this Act of
paying in full the unpaid principal tax or assess-
ment against his land , shall not be heard to com-
plain by reason of adidtional interest to be collected
from his lands on account of the issuance of refund-
ing bonds .
Section Six ( 6 ) . Taxes or assessments levied for
the payment of refunding bonds and interest thereon
shall be secured by the same lien as other taxes of
such districts heretofore provided by law .
Section Seven (7) . All powers necessary to be
exercised by any such district or any such board
of drainage commissioners or other governing
authority , in order to carry out the provisions of
this Act , are hereby conferred upon such district
and upon such board of drainage commissioners or
other governing authority as the case may be , and
no proceedings for the issuance of refunding bonds
other than those provided for and required herein ,
shall be necessary .
Section Eight ( 8 ) . All drainage , levee or rec-
lamation districts heretofore or that may be here-
after organized under any law of this State are
hereby authorized and empowered to do each and
every act necessary to be by them performed in
order to comply with or avail themselves of the pro-
visions of any legislation now enacted or that may
be hereafter enacted by the Congress of the United
States of America , having for its purpose the re-
funding or extending the time of payment of the
bonded indebtedness of any drainage , levee or rec-
lamation district or otherwise lightening the pres-
ent burdens of taxation resting on the lands and
property in such districts .
Section Nine ( 9 ) . Whereas , the depressed con-
ditions of agriculture during the past several years
Acts-4

98 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
have resulted in the inability of landowners in drain-
age , levee and reclamation districts in this State to
pay taxes levied by such districts ; and whereas ,
the inability and failure of landowners to pay taxes
to such districts has resulted in the default by such
districts in the payment of bonds issued thereby ;
and whereas , the defaults aforesaid are widespread
throughout the State and the continuance thereof
may result in the serious impairment of the public
faith and credit of the State of Kentucky and its
subdivisions ; and whereas , it is highly important
that the burden of taxation levied by such districts
upon the landowners therein be lightened through
the refunding of outstanding indebtedness and
through the extension of the time of payment
thereof, an emergency is hereby found and declared
to exist within the meaning of Section 55 of the
Constitution of the Commonwealth of Kentucky ,
and this Act shall be in force and effect from and
after its passage and approval .
Approved February 18 , 1936 .
CHAPTER 39 .
AN ACT creating and providing for the election of Boards of
Drainage Commissioners ; prescribing the powers and duties
of such Boards ; and repealing Section two thousand three
hundred eighty - seven ( 2380-7 ) of the Kentucky Statutes ,
Carroll's one thousand nine hundred thirty ( 1930 ) Edition ,
and all other laws and parts of laws in conflict with the Act ,
to the extent of such conflict , and declaring an emergency .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section two thousand three hundred eighty -seven
( 2380-7 ) , Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's one thousand
nine hundred thirty ( 1930 ) Edition , hereby is re-
pealed and the following enacted in lieu thereof :

CHAPTER 39 99
In all counties of this State where a public ditch ,
drain , levee or other improvement of similar char-
acter shall have been established under the pre-
existing laws and/or , may hereafter be established
under this Act , it shall be the duty of the County
Judge to call an election in each separate drainage
district , which election shall be held not later than
thirty days after the passage of this Act , and at the
County Seat and thereafter on some day in May of
each year . If for any reason the County Judge shall
refuse to call an election , or refuse to publish notice
of an election , or refuse to preside at an election ,
or in any other particular refuse or fail to carry out
any of the provisions of this Act , then any five per-
sons , being taxpayers in any drainage district , com-
ing under the provisions of this Act , shall have the
right to call an election , and shall upon the day of
said election , designate one of their number to act
in place of said County Judge , said person upon
being designated , shall have all of the rights granted
to the County Judge , under the provision of this
Act. At this election the taxpayers of each separate
drainage district shall elect a director to represent
said Drainage District on the Board herein created .
The County Judge shall preside at the election
of each of said Drainage Districts , which shall be
by viva voce vote of each member present at the
hour designated by said County Judge on the said
day . The Drainage Districts of the County shall
be numbered by the said County Judge upon the
passage of this Act and the various elections shall
be held in the courthouse and the election shall take
place in the numerical order of the District . If any
District is not represented at said election , it shall
be the duty of the County Judge to appoint a di-
rector representing said District , who must be a
taxpayer of said District and these elections shall

100 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
be held annually in the month of May upon the call
of the County Judge . He shall fill all vacancies dur-
ing any year by appointment . The election called
shall be made at least ten days before the election
by posting notice at the courthouse door and by
publishing same in one issue of some newspaper
of general circulation . The various directors from
the various districts of the county shall constitute
the Board of Drainage Commissioners of the coun-
ties wherein they are elected , with the right to
acquire , hold , encumber , sell and convey such real
estate and personal property as shall be necessary
to the conduct of its affairs as such drainage com-
mission, to sue and be sued , contract and be con-
tracted with , and shall possess such other powers
and rights as shall pertain to corporations , or as
may be necessary for carrying on its work under
the provisions of this Act , including the right of
Eminent Domain ; it shall have and use a corporate
seal and shall change same at its pleasure . Not
later than fifteen days after the election of these
directors , representing the various drainage dis-
tricts in the County , they shall meet at the County
Seat , elect a President from one of their number
and appoint a Secretary , who need not be a director ,
and if no secretary is appointed , the County Clerk
of the County shall act as such and be ex officio
secretary thereof . The President and Directors shall
receive no compensation for their services and shall
meet at such time or times as the President may
call them to meet , and the President shall call a
meeting when the request is made by two or more
directors which request shall be in writing . The
Board shall elect a Treasurer , who shall execute
bond as the board directs and they may pay said
Treasurer such salary as they deem proper . Said
commission shall have an office at the County Seat

CHAPTER 39 101
of the County which shall be provided with such
furniture , stationery and records as may be neces-
sary .
The Board shall keep or cause to be kept com-
plete records of its proceedings which records shall
be public records and at all times open to the in-
spection of the public . The Board of Drainage Com-
missioners may employ a superintendent of drain-
age if they deem such necessary , and for such time
as they deem necessary , and pay to him such salary
as they deem proper , not exceeding five dollars
( $5.00 ) per day.
It shall be the duty of the Board of Drainage
Commissioners on or before April 1st of each year
to make a complete report of its acts and doings
during the previous year , showing the amount of
money received and from what source , and the
amounts paid out and for what purposes , which re-
port shall be spread upon its records and open for
public inspection . It shall be the further duty of the
Board of Drainage Commissioners on or before
April 1st of each year to have published in the
county seat newspaper a financial statement of the
previous year , showing the amounts received , the
date of receipt , and from what source , and the
amounts paid out , the date of disbursement and
for what purposes . All expenses incurred under
this Act to be borne by the Board of Drainage Com-
missioners out of drainage funds and not from any
general funds of the county .
The Boards provided for herein shall succeed to
all the rights , powers and duties of each and every
Drainage Commissioner and/or Board of Drainage
Commissioners , heretofore appointed under the
provisions of Section two thousand three hundred
eighty -seven ( 2380-7 ) , Kentucky Statutes , Car-
roll's one thousand nine hundred thirty ( 1930 ) Edi-

102 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
tion ; and , in addition thereto said Board hereby is
expressly authorized to compromise and settle any
and all maintenance assessments heretofore made ,
levied or assessed , against any land or the owner
thereof, and the Board shall have the right to make
such settlements upon such terms and conditions
as it may deem proper , and to do and perform each
and every act necessary to release and discharge
any and all claims and demands , and all liens upon
lands or real estate heretofore established , made
or claimed , under or by reason of any and all such
maintenance assessments .
Whereas the time is fast approaching when as-
sessments may be made for the maintenance of
Drainage projects in Kentucky and this Board is
necessary to more justly represent the people ,
therefore , it is declared that an emergency exists
and this Act shall take effect immediately upon its
passage and approval by the Governor .
Approved February 17 , 1936 .
CHAPTER 40 .
AN ACT relating to ditches and drains in counties having more
than seventy -five ( 75 ) separate drainage districts ; providing
that in such counties the county judge shall appoint a drain-
age commissioner ; prescribing the powers and duties of such
commissioner ; repealing all laws and parts of laws in con-
flict with the Act , to the extent of such conflict only ; and de-
claring an emergency .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the.
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One ( 1 ) . That in all counties having
seventy -five (75 ) or more separate drainage dis-
tricts the County Judge shall appoint a drainage
commissioner who shall have and exercise all the
powers and duties vested in directors and Boards

CHAPTER 40 103
of Drainage Commissioners elected under the pro-
visions of Section two thousand three hundred
eighty -seven ( 2380-7 ) , Kentucky Statutes , Car-
roll's one thousand nine hundred thirty ( 1930 ) Ed-
ition , as amended . The person so appointed shall
hold this office for a term of four (4 ) years or until
his successor is appointed and qualified , shall exe-
cute bond for the faithful performance of his duties
in the penal sum of five thousand ( $ 5,000.00 ) dol-
lars , and shall be sworn to faithful performance of
duties of his position to the best of his ability . Any
vacancy in said office shall be filled by appointment
by the county judge . He shall have the right to
acquire , hold, encumber , sell and convey such real
estate and personal property as shall be necessary
to the conduct of his affairs as such Drainage Com-
missioner , to sue and be sued , contract and be con-
tracted with , and shall possess such other powers
and rights as may be reasonably necessary for car-
rying on his work under the provisions of this Act .
He may appoint a secretary and fix his compensa-
tion , and if no such secretary is appointed , the
county clerk of the county shall act as such and be
ex-officio secretary thereof .
Said Commissioner shall be paid as full compen-
sation for his services not exceeding the sum of five
( $ 5.00 ) dollars per day , while actually engaged in
the performance of his duties , to be fixed by order
of the county court at the time of his appointment ,
as well as all traveling expenses incurred in the
performance of his duties under this Act , to be paid
out of the drainage funds herein provided for .
Section Two ( 2 ) . The Drainage Commissioner
provided for in this Act shall succeed to and be
vested with all of the rights , powers , duties and ob-
ligations heretofore imposed upon the Drainage
Commissioner and/or Board of Drainage Commis-

104 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
sioners in office at the time of the effective date of
this Act .
Section Three ( 3 ) . This Act shall not be con-
strued with reference to , or as a part of , any pre-
vious Act of this session of the General Assembly.
It is the intention of the General Assembly that this
Act is and shall be construed as a separate and in-
dependent Act , relating only to counties having
seventy -five ( 75 ) or more separate drainage dis-
tricts ; and , that this Act provides the sole and ex-
clusive law for the selection , term of office and qual-
ifications of Drainage Commissioners in such coun-
ties .
Section Four ( 4 ) . Whereas , it is deemed by the
General Assembly to be impractical to have seventy-
five ( 75 ) or more directors for drainage districts ;
and , whereas , it is deemed necessary that this Act
be made effective immediately upon its passage and
approval by the Governor , an emergency hereby is
declared to exist and this Act shall become effective
immediately upon its passage and approval by the
Governor . Approved February 17 , 1936 .
CHAPTER 41 .
AN ACT to repeal and re -enact Sections one , two , four, six and
seven of Article nine (IX ) of the Acts of the General Assembly
of one thousand nine hundred and thirty -four ( 1934 ) , being
Sections four thousand five hundred and six-one ( 4506-1 ) ,
four thousand five hundred and six -two (4506-2 ) , four thousand
five hundred and six -four ( 4506-4 ) , four thousand five hundred
and six-six ( 4506-6 ) , four thousand five hundred and six-seven
(4506-7 ) , Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's Edition one thousand
nine hundred and thirty -four ( 1934 ) , Supp . pertaining to the
retirement of teachers from the common schools of the State
of Kentucky and extending the privileges of said statutes to
include cities of the third class as well as cities of the first
and second classes , and declaring the existence of an

CHAPTER 41 105
emergency and providing for the repeal of all Acts and parts
of Acts which may be inconsistent therewith .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Sections one, two , four, six and seven of
Article nine of the Acts of the General Assembly of
one thousand nine hundred and thirty -four ( 1934 ) ,
being Sections four thousand five hundred and six-
one (4506-1 ) , four thousand five hundred and
six-two (4506-2 ) , four thousand five hundred and
six-four ( 4506-4 ) , four thousand five hundred and
six-six ( 4506-6 ) , four thousand five hundred and
six-seven (4506-7 ) , Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's
Edition , one thousand nine hundred and thirty-four
( 1934 ) Supp . , be and the same are hereby repealed
and re-enacted so that as and when re -enacted said
sections shall read as follows : Section four thou-
sand five hundred and six-one (4506-1 ) . Boards of
Education of Independent School districts embrac-
ing cities of the first , second and third classes may
and are hereby empowered to establish retirement
systems for the purpose of providing retirement
allowances and other benefits for teachers and other
school employees . A retirement system so created
shall have the powers and privileges of a corpora-
tion and shall be known as " The Teachers Retire-
ment System of the Schools of
Kentucky , " and by such name all of its business.
shall be transacted , all of its funds invested , and all
of its cash and securities and other property held .
Section four thousand five hundred and six-two
(4506-2 ) . In Independent school districts embrac-
ing cities of the first , second and third classes which
are already operating insurance and annuity sys-
tems , for teachers or for teachers and other em-
ployees , full authority to adopt , modify , change or

106 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
revise the present systems , including pensions
already granted, is by this Act conferred upon the
Boards of Education of such school districts , sub-
ject to the approval of the board or governing
body of the existing system and subject also to the
provisions hereinafter stated . Section four thou-
sand five hundred and six-four (4506-4 ) . Each
board of trustees of retirement systems in inde-
pendent school districts embracing cities of the
first , second and third classes may adopt , subject
to the provisions of this Act , such regulations as
are necessary to the effective operation of the re-
tirement systems without regard to regulations
adopted by any other such board or trustees . Sec-
tion four thousand five hundred and six-six
(4506-6 ) . The participating teachers and other
employees , and the Board of Education in districts
in which such retirement system is continued , re-
organized or established may contribute to the re-
tirement fund and the rates of the contribution
shall be fixed by the board of trustees of the re-
tirement system on the basis of careful acturial in-
vestigations , and shall be adequate and sufficient to
support the benefits granted in the system set up :
provided that the total contribution of the board
of education shall be at least the equal of the total
contributions of the members of the system . The
board of trustees of the retirement system may
increase or decrease the rates of contribution when-
ever such action is deemed by them to be necessary
to preserve the solvency and equity of the system
as determined by acturial studies , provided that the
contribution of the board of education of a city of
the first class shall in no case exceed the returns
from a tax rate of four and one-half cents (42c )
on each one hundred dollars ( $ 100 ) of assessed val-
uation subject to local school tax in said district ,

CHAPTER 42 107
and that the contribution of a board of education
of a city of the second or third class shall in no
case exceed the returns from a tax rate of four
cents ( 4c ) on each one hundred dollars ( $100 ) of
assessed valuation subject to local school tax in
said district . Section four thousand five hundred
and six-seven ( 4506-7 ) . It shall be the duty of the
board of education in cities of the second and third
classes , the board of alderman in cities of the first
class , or such other agency to which the General
Assembly may in the future delegate the function
of levying taxes for local school purposes in inde-
pendent school districts embracing cities of the
first , second and third classes , to levy the tax rate
recommended by the board of trustees of the re-
tirement system ; provided that the raterate of
taxation in a city of the first class shall not exceed
four and one-half cents (42c ) on each one hundred
dollars ( $ 100 ) of assessed valuation subject to local
school tax in said district , and provided the rate of
taxation in a city of the second or third class shall
not exceed four cents ( 4c ) on each one hundred dol-
lars ( $ 100 ) of assessed valuation subject to local
school tax in said district . On account of the im-
mediate necessity for the inaugeration of retire-
ment systems to take care of infirm and incapac-
itated teachers , an emergency is declared to exist
and this Act shall become effective immediately
upon
itspassage by the General Assembly and its
approval by the Governor
. All laws or parts of
laws
inconflict herewith are hereby repealed
tothe
extent of such conflict
. Approved February 18
, 1936
.
CHAPTER 42
.
AN ACT amending Section three
(3) of Article five
(
5) of
Chapter sixty
-five
(65
) of the Acts of the General Assembly of

108 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
nineteen thirty -four ( 1934 ) , being Section four thousand three
hundred ninety -nine -three (4399-3 ) , Kentucky Statutes , Bald-
win's Kentucky Statutes Service of nineteen thirty -four ( 1934 ) ,
relating to independent school districts .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Section three ( 3 ) of Article five ( 5 ) of Chap-
ter sixty -five ( 65 ) of the Acts of the General As-
sembly of nineteen thirty -four ( 1934 ) being Section
four thousand three hundred ninety-nine -three
( 4399-3 ) , Kentucky Statutes , Baldwin's Kentucky
Statute Service nineteen thirty -four ( 1934 ) Edi-
tion , be and the same hereby is amended by repeal-
ing said Section in its entirety and re-enacting same
so that as amended and re -enacted said Section will
read as follows , to wit :
Independent school districts . All school districts
embracing cities of the first five classes together
with the territory within their limits , including any
territory which has heretofore been added for school
purposes outside of the limits of such cities or dis-
tricts , and any territory which may be included by
any future change in the limits of such cities , and
all independent graded common school districts
having a school census enumeration of two hundred
fifty ( 250 ) or more white children shall hereafter be
known and designated as independent school dis-
tricts , provided that no such district other than cities
of the first , second , third , fourth , and fifth classes ,
operating as independent districts at the time this
Act becomes effective , shall continue to operate
when its school census enumeration of white chil-
dren falls below two hundred fifty ( 250 ) pupils un-
less it appears to the State Board of Education that
the district can maintain a more efficient program
of school service by operating as an independent
district . The State Board of Education may per-

CHAPTER 42 109
mit an independent graded common school district
existing at the time of the passage of this Act with
a census enumeration of white children below two
hundred and fifty ( 250 ) to operate as a temporary
independent school district for four -year periods
beginning with July first ( 1 ) , nineteen thirty-four
(1934 ) , if it appears to the State Board of Educa-
tion that such district can maintain a more efficient
program of school service by operating as a tempo-
rary independent district , and the decision of the
State Board of Education shall be final ; provided ,
at the end of each four -year period the State Board
of Education may permit such independent district
to operate for an additional four -year period on the
condition that such inspection as the State Board
of Education may make shows that it is operating
a school in accordance with the school law and the
standards , rules , and regulations set up by the State
Board of Education . The State Board of Educa-
tion may by order make any temporary independ-
ent school district a part of the county district
whenever the same is (it ) not complying with the
school law and the standards , rules , and regulations
of the State Board of Education , and after it has
been given a reasonable time , to be fixed by the
State Board of Education , within which to so com-
ply . The first application of such district to operate
as a temporary independent . district shall be filed
with the State Board of Education not later than
July first ( 1 ) , nineteen thirty -four ( 1934 ) . Each
subsequent application of such independent district
to operate as a temporary independent district for
an additional four -year period shall be filed with
the State Board of Education not later than May
first ( 1 ) , of the last year of the four -year period .
Each independent school district shall maintain at
least an approved twelve -grade school service for

110 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
the white children residing in its district . Provided ,
however , any independent school district that main-
tains within the district at least an approved ten-
grade school service for the white children residing
in its district , may furnish free transportation for
white children residing within the district to attend
school in an adjoining district for completing the
eleventh and twelfth grade school work and pro-
vide by contract with an adjoining district , which
maintains at least an approved twelve -grade pro-
gram of school service , for such children procuring
eleventh and twelfth grade service in such adjoin-
ing district . Provided further that each independ-
ent school district shall provide by establishment
or by contract with another district for at least an
approved twelve -grade program of school service .
for its colored children .
All laws in conflict herewith are hereby repealed .
Approved February 25 , 1936 .
CHAPTER 43 .
AN ACT to provide State aid for education in certain courses
of study of State students pursuing certain courses of study
at educational institutions outside of the State for whom no
such courses are provided in the educational institutions of
the State , and prescribing the conditions under which such
State aid may be granted and authorizing and directing the
State Board of Education of Kentucky to administer pro-
visions of this Act , making an appropriation therefor and
declaring an emergency . This Act to be known as the "Ander
son -Myer State Aid Act ."
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One . That pending the full develop-
ment of the educational institutions of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky , all bona fide residents of this
State at the time of making written application for

CHAPTER 43 111
the benefits provided in this Act and have been such
residents continuously for five ( 5 ) years next pre-
ceding the time of filing said application , and who
are duly qualified for matriculation in courses of
study offered at the University of Kentucky , but
who , because of Section one hundred eighty - seven
(187 ) of the Constitution of Kentucky cannot pur-
sue such courses at the University of Kentucky or
other State institutions at which such courses are
offered , or who have otherwise qualified to pursue
such courses therein , and who are now pursuing or
may hereafter pursue such courses in educational
institutions outside of the State whereof no courses
of study are provided for such persons within this
State , shall have their tuition and fees paid at such
institutions by the Commonwealth of Kentucky .
Section Two . That such tuition and fees be as-
certained by the State Superintendent of Public
Instruction and paid upon requisition of him out of
funds not otherwise appropriated .
Section Three . That the State Board of Edu-
cation shall prescribe the rules and regulations gov-
erning the granting of State aid under this Act . In
event the funds appropriated for the purpose of
carrying out the provisions of this Act are insuf-
ficient for the purpose in any year , said Board of
Education shall have the right to prorate the same
among such persons whose applications are ap-
proved therefor pursuant to the provisions of this
Act ; and provided further , that not more than one
hundred and seventy -five ( $175.00 ) dollars shall be
allowed to any such person for the purposes and
under the provisions of this Act during any one
school year of nine ( 9 ) months .
Section Four . That for the purpose of carrying
out the provisions of this Act and for no other pur-
pose , there is hereby appropriated for the State

112 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Board of Education of Kentucky , out of funds in
the State Treasury not otherwise appropriated the
following sums : Five thousand ( $5,000.00 ) dollars
for the fiscal year ending June thirtieth ( 30th ) one
thousand nine hundred thirty - seven , and five thou-
sand ( $5,000.00 ) dollars for the fiscal year ending
June thirtieth ( 30th ) , one thousand nine hundred
thirty -eight .
Section Five . That if any part of this Act shall
be held unconstitutional , such holding shall not in-
validate any other portion thereof .
Section Six . It being immediately necessary for
educational opportunity to certain persons of this
Commonwealth , and for the preservation of the
public peace , health and safety , an emergency is
hereby declared to exist , by reason whereof this
Act shall take effect and be in full force from and
after passage and approval . Approved February 25 .
CHAPTER 44 .
AN ACT to amend and re -enact Section four thousand five hun-
dred twenty -seven -forty-four ( 4527-44 ) , Baldwin's Revision of
Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , being Chapter sixty-five ( 65 ) ,
page three hundred thirty -four ( 334 ) of the Acts of one thou-
sand nine thirty -four (1934 ) , providing for Powers of regula-
tion of Boards of Regents in governing teachers ' colleges .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Section four thousand five hundred twenty-
seven-forty-four (4527-44 ) Baldwin's Revision of
Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , be and the same is
hereby amended and re -enacted so that when so
amended and re -enacted it shall read as follows :
The government , administration , and control of
each of the said State teachers ' colleges is hereby

CHAPTER 44 113
vested in its respective board of regents which
when its members have been appointed and quali-
fied , shall constitute a body corporate and shall
have perpetual succession with power to contract
and be contracted with , to sue and be sued , to plead
and be impleaded , to receive by any legal mode of
conveyance property of any description , and to
have and to hold and enjoy the same ; to receive
grants of money and to expend the same for the
use and benefit of the said college ; also , to sell and
convey any real estate , buildings , or equipment be-
longing to it, but the proceeds from such sale shall
be reinvested in other real estate and/or build-
ings and equipment for the use and benefit of the
said college ; to make and use a corporate seal with
power to alter and enjoy the same ; to adopt by-
laws , rules and regulations for the government of
their members , officers , agents , and employees ,
and to enforce obedience to such rules ; to elect a
secretary and a treasurer and to fix the bond of the
treasurer which shall not be less than ten thou-
sand dollars ( $ 10,000 ) , and the treasurer shall not
be a member of the board of regents ; to designate
a depository or depositories for its funds and to
fix the bond thereof ; to require such reports from
the president , officers , faculty , and employees as it
deems necessary and proper from time to time ;
to determine the number of divisions , departments ,
bureaus , offices , and agencies needed for the suc-
cessful conduct of the affairs of the said college ;
to invest the faculty or a committee of the faculty
with the power to suspend or expel any student
for disobedience to its rules , or for any other con-
tumacy , insubordination , or immoral conduct ; to
appoint a president and , on the recommendation
of the president , appoint in its discretion , all offi-
cers , teachers and employees and fix their com-

114 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
pensation and tenure of service , provided no per-
son shall be employed for a longer period than
four years ; and provided further that no person
shall be employed who is related to any member of
said Board of Regents , as father , mother , brother ,
sister , husband , wife , son , daughter , aunt , uncle ,
sister-in -law or daughter -in-law , except that upon
written recommendation of the president of the
respective institution , one such relative of each
member of the Board of Regents may be appointed
upon confirmation by the remaining three members
of the said board ; to dismiss any president and upon.
the recommendation of the president , any officer ,
teacher , or employee for cause as hereinafter pro-
vided ; to grant diplomas and confer degrees upon
the recommendation of the president and the
faculty , and to possess all other immunities , rights ,
privileges , and franchises usually attaching to the
governing bodies of educational institutions .
Approved February 18 , 1936 .
CHAPTER 45 .
AN ACT relating to suffrage and elections and providing a
method of registering voters of this Commonwealth residing
outside of the corporate limits of cities of the first class ,
fixing penalties for violations of this Act and repealing Chap-
ter sixty-four ( 64 ) of the Acts of the General Assembly , one
thousand nine hundred twenty -four ( 1924 ) session , as
amended , being Sections one thousand four hundred eighty-
six -b1 ( 1486 -b1 ) to one thousand four eighty -six b -twenty-
seven ( 1486 -b27 ) , inclusive , of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes ,
one thousand nine hundred thirty ( 1930 ) Edition . This Act to
be known as the Rives -Robinson Registration Law .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One . This Act may be cited and referred
to as the " General Registration Act " . The term

CHAPTER 45 115
"initial registration " shall refer to the general
initial registration to be held in each precinct as
provided in this Act .
Any word importing the masculine gender , when
used herein , shall extend to and be deemed to in-
clude women as well as men .
Section Two . Every citizen of the United States
not otherwise disqualified by the laws of this Com-
monwealth , who is twenty - one years of age or who
will become twenty -one years of age on or before
the next regular election to be held in November
of any year, or on or before the date of any special
election , and who will have been a resident of the
State of Kentucky one year , and of the county six
months , and of the precinct , outside the corporate
limits of a city of the first class , in such county for
sixty days next preceding such election , and who is
a registered voter , and is otherwise qualified , shall
be entitled to vote for all officers elective by the
people , and on all measures or questions submitted
for determination by the voters at any regular or
special election ; provided , however , that only per-
sons who are duly and properly registered as herein
provided shall be permitted to vote at the primary
election to be held on the first Saturday in August ,
one thousand nine hundred thirty -six ( 1936 ) , and
at all primary , general and special elections held
thereafter .
Section Three . The initial registration held
under this Act shall be at the voting precincts in
each county at the primary election to be held on
the first Saturday in August , one thousand nine
hundred thirty -six ( 1936 ) . After said initial regis-
tration , all further registration or change of regis-
tration shall be made in the manner herein pro-
vided .
Section Four . Any person possessing the quali-

116 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
fications for registration as a legal voter as set out
in Section two ( 2 ) hereof, and who fails to register
on such primary election date , may appear before
the county court clerk of the county of the residence
of such voter , between the fifteenth day of August
and the tenth day of October , and between the
fifteenth day of November and the tenth day of
July of any year ( Sundays and legal holidays ex-
cepted ) at any hour of the day the office of the
County Court Clerk may be open for business . Any
voter who wishes to change his party affiliation may
appear before the clerk between said dates of any
year, and upon his request , the clerk shall make
said change on the registration book .
Section Five . No person shall be registered who
does not personally appear before the officers on
said primary election date , or who does not per-
sonally appear before the County Court Clerk at
and within the times hereinbefore set out , except
those hereinafter provided for , and the voter , if he
be not known to the clerk, or if any bystander shall
challenge the voter's right to register , shall be
sworn by the clerk and shall answer any and all
questions asked him by the clerk or bystander ,
touching his qualifications as a voter , or his right
to be then and there registered , and on the registra-
tion book opposite the name of the voter , the clerk
shall write the words, " sworn as to qualifications " .
Any voter otherwise qualified who , on account of age,
infirmity or illness , is not able to attend the County
Clerk's office of the county of his residence during
the time between the dates above set out , may be-
tween the first and thirtieth day of September ( both
inclusive ) be registered in the following manner :
He shall present to the clerk by mail or at the hands
of some friend , his affidavit stating that his failure
to appear was due to age, infirmity , or illness , and

CHAPTER 45 117
an affidavit of a physician or neighbor corroborating
the voter's affidavit . Upon compliance with the
foregoing the clerk shall enter his name on the regis-
tration book for the precinct of his residence , the
same as if he had registered before the clerk in
person .
If any such voter be unknown to the Clerk , or if
his right to register be challenged , it shall be the
duty of the Clerk to write opposite his name on the
registration book the word , " questioned " , and it
shall be the duty of the election officers , when said
voter applies for a ballot , if there be any doubt of
his qualification to vote , to swear said voter, who
shall, under oath , answer such questions as may be
asked him touching his qualifications .
Section Six . The Attorney General of Kentucky ,
and the two apointive members of the State Board
of Election Commissioners shall constitute a Board
to prepare proper blanks and record forms for the
registration of voters as herein provided . It shall
be the duty of said Board to have said blanks and
record forms properly prepared on or before the
tenth day of July , one thousand nine hundred thirty-
six ( 1936 ) , by which date the necessary blanks and
forms shall be in the hands of the county court
clerks of each county , for distribution to the pri-
mary election officers in each precinct of the State .
Said blanks and forms shall be printed , prepared
and furnished as other books are now printed and
prepared for the Commonwealth , and shall be paid
for out of the State Treasury . Separate card or
looseleaf registration record forms for the regis-
tration of the individual voters shall be prepared
in duplicate , upon durable paper and of convenient
size and shape as determined by the Board , the
original cards or forms being printed upon white
paper and marked " Original " , and the duplicates

118 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
printed upon paper of another color , and marked
" Copy", each of said cards or forms providing for
the entry thereon of the following information ,
signatures and affidavit of the voter :
Date of Registration
Serial Number
