CHAPTER 45 119
At the time of registering a voter , the Clerk shall
enter upon the original record form the name of the
voter and all other data and information called for
on said form ; after such entries have been made ,
the voter shall , in his own hand , write his signature
upon both the original and copy record forms . The
clerk , either at the time of registering the voter or
as soon thereafter as practicable , shall enter upon
the copy record form the name of the voter together
with all of the data and information appearing on
the original record form , so that the copy form shall
be an exact duplicate of the original , and shall at all
times keep in a safe place in his office both the
original and copy record forms except that the copy
forms shall be furnished to the election officers as
hereinafter provided .
In the event any applicant for registration states
under oath that he cannot write his name , the clerk
shall sign the registration record forms for such
person , and such person shall sign said records by
making his mark thereon and the clerk shall attest
the same .
The original and copy record forms shall be filed
in securely locked binders or card files , by pre-
cincts , in such systematic order as the Board shall
prescribe and shall constitute the precinct registers
to be used as hereinafter provided .
Section Seven . Upon each primary , special or
general election , the precinct register containing the
"copy" record forms signed by the voters , after
having been first securely locked in the binders or
containers provided therefor , shall be delivered by
the county court clerk to the precinct election clerk
of each precinct at the same time he delivers the
ballots , and the election clerk shall give the county
court clerk a receipt for same and it shall be the
duty of the precinct election clerk to produce such

120 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
precinct register so delivered to him (or see that
same is so produced ) at the proper polling place in
his precinct at the opening of the polls on such elec-
tion day. The county court clerk shall also at the
time of delivering the ballots , deliver to the pre-
cinct election clerk a complete and correct single-
spaced typewritten list , in triplicate , of all voters
then registered in said precinct and entitled to vote
in said election , giving the name and address of
each voter , each page being separately attested by
the county court clerk ; the election clerk shall , at
the opening of the polls , post one copy of the type-
written list outside the entrance to the voting place ,
and deliver one copy to the sheriff of the election .
retaining the third copy for the use of himself and
the judges of the election . At the close of the polls
the election clerk shall deposit one copy of said list
in the ballot box ; the election officers who return
the ballot boxes to the county court clerk's office
shall at the same time return said precinct register
to the county court clerk and secure a receipt
therefor .
Section Eight. If any person applying to vote at
any primary , special or general election is unknown
to the election officers , or is challenged , such person ,
before a ballot is issued to him, shall be required
to sign his name upon a comparative signature card ,
unless such person signed his registration record
by making his mark , and it shall be the duty of the
officers of election in that precinct to compare the
signature of such person with the signature of such
person in the registration record form in the pre-
cinct register. The challengers of the respective
political parties present shall also have the right to
compare said signature at the same time said com-
parison is made by the election officers . If such com-
parison of signatures shall create a doubt in the

CHAPTER 45 121
mind of two of the election officers , as to whether
the person applying to vote is the same person as
the registrant , no ballot shall be issued to him un-
less he shall otherwise satisfactorily establish his
identity as to the registrant .
In the event any person applying to vote at any
election shall state that he cannot sign his name
as above provided , the judges of the election in that
precinct shall immediately swear such person as
to his name and his inability to write , and there-
upon the judges of election and the challengers of
the respective political parties present shall ex-
amine the registration record in the precinct regis-
ter , and if it shown by said record that the person
of that same name signed his name on the registra-
tion record, a ballot shall not be issued to the per-
son so applying , unless the inability of the person
to sign his name at the date of such election is due
to some physical disability arising subsequent to
his registration .
It shall be the duty of the election officers to make ,
keep and return to the county court clerk , a record
or record sheets to be provided in the same manner
as other election paraphernalia are provided , of
each person so sworn , and showing the disposition
made of such case.
It shall be the duty of the clerk of each precinct ,
immediately after issuing the ballot to any voter ,
at the primary election held in August , one thou-
sand nine hundred thirty -six ( 1936 ) , and at every
primary and general election held thereafter , to in-
dicate at the place provided therefor on the regis-
tration record form that such person voted at such
primary or election . The fact that any person is
registered shall constitute prima facie evidence only
of his right to vote and shall not prevent the officers
of any primary or general election from refusing

122 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
to issue him a ballot for causes and in the manner
provided by the general election laws of this State .
Section Nine . Not less than ten days prior to the
primary election held on the first Saturday in
August , one thousand nine hundred thirty -six
( 1936 ) , the county court clerk of each county shall
appoint two special deputy clerks for each precinct
in his county , who shall serve for the day of such
election only , and who shall be appointed one from
each of the two political parties , having representa-
tion on the State Board of Election Commissioners .
These appointments to be made from a list of two
or more names for each precinct submitted to the
County Court Clerk by the County Executive Com-
mittee of each of such political parties , and who
shall be sworn to faithfully perform their duties .
At the time of delivering the ballots to the clerk of
election for each precinct , the county court clerk
shall also deliver to each election clerk two precinct
registers containing , respectively , a supply of orig-
inal and " copy " registration record forms , serially
numbered and equal in number to the total number
of votes cast in that precinct at the general election
held in November , one thousand nine hundred thirty-
five ( 1935 ) , such forms being securely locked in
binders or containers provided therefor , before de-
livery by the county court clerk, who shall take re-
ceipts therefor from the clerks of election . On the
day of said primary election , each such deputy clerk
so appointed shall be present at the opening of the
polls in the precinct for which he is appointed , in the
room in which such election is to be held , and the
clerk of the election shall there deliver to such
deputy clerk the original and the " copy " precinct
registration forms received from the county court
clerk . The sheriff of the election shall provide , at a
convenient place within the voting place , a table and

CHAPTER 45 123
)
at least three chairs for the use of the deputy clerks
and persons being registered . The deputy clerks
shall there register in the manner herein provided ,
every person who offers for registration during the
hours in which such election is held , and who has the
legal right to vote or who will have the legal right
to vote , in such precinct , at the next election to be
held therein . Every person registering shall be re-
quired to sign his name , in his own hand except as
herein after provided , upon both the original and the
"copy " registration record forms . The original
registration record form shall be entirely filled out
with the name and serial number of the person reg-
istering and all the information called for on said
form , before the signature of the registrant is made
thereon , but only the signature of the registrant and
the attestation of the deputy clerk shall be made
upon the " copy " registration record form . In the
event any applicant for registration states that he
cannot write his name , the deputy clerk , after swear-
ing the applicant to such statement , shall sign both
the original and " copy " registration record forms
for him, and such person shall sign said records by
making his mark thereon , attested by the deputy
clerk .
If the deputy clerk be in doubt as to the right of
any person to be registered , or if his right to regis-
tration be challenged by any officer of the election
or by any challenger of a political party present ,
the applicant shall be sworn by the deputy clerk
touching his right to be registered and may be inter-
rogated thereon by the deputy clerks or any officer
of the election ; not more than two citizens may also
be sworn and interrogated touching his right to
registration . The judges of the election shall de-
cide all questions relating to the qualifications of
the applicant for registration , except that in case

124 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
of a difference of opinion between the judges , the
clerk of the election shall have the deciding vote .
If any applicant whose right to be registered is
challenged is allowed to register , the word " ques-
tioned " shall be written by the deputy clerk oppo-
site his signature on both the original and the
"copy" registration record forms . At the close of
the polls at said primary election , the deputy clerks
shall endorse on reverse side of the last original
and " copy" registration record forms signed by a
registrant during such election , the notation " End
of precinct registration " , and such notation shall
be signed by the deputy clerks and by both judges
of the election . The election officers who return the
ballot boxes to the county court clerk's office shall
at the same time return said original and " copy"
registration record forms to the county court clerk ,
and secure a receipt therefor ; upon receipt of such
registration record forms from the election officers ,
the county court clerk shall promptly copy into the
"copy" registration record forms , in the corre-
sponding blanks provided therefor , all information ,
data and remarks which appear on the original
registration record forms relative to such voters .
Each deputy clerk conducting such precinct regis-
tration shall be paid as compensation for his serv-
ices , the sum of three dollars , to be paid out of the
county treasury .
Section Ten . In case a registered voter changes
his place of residence from one precinct to another
in the same county , or whenever the boundaries of
the precinct in which he is registered are changed
by law so as to remove him from that precinct , he
may appear before the county court clerk and , upon
his making oath as to such removal and indicating
the precinct into which he has moved or been trans-
ferred by such change of boundaries , the clerk shall ,

CHAPTER 45 125
under the heading of " Remarks " on his original
and " copy " registration record forms , write the
words " Removed to ..precinct . "
(Filling in the name of the precinct to which he
has so removed ) , and shall at the same time regis-
ter such voter in the proper precinct . Such trans-
fers shall be registered only on those days when
registrations are provided for under Section four
(4) hereof.
Section Eleven . If any original or " copy " regis-
tration record form is lost or destroyed , or be-
comes so mutilated , blotted , torn or defaced or
other wise in such condition as to render it difficult ,
troublesome or unsafe to use such record , it shall
be the duty of the county court clerk to cause a
new registration record form to be made and exe-
cuted in such manner as may be necessary , con-
sistently with the provisions of this Act , the cost
thereof to be paid out of the county treasury . If
the re-registration of the voters of an entire pre-
cinct shall so become necessary , it shall be held in
the precinct at the next ensuing primary or general
election , in the same manner as the initial registra-
tion herein provided for . If such re -registration is
made necessary by the loss or destruction of regis-
tration records through the negligence or willful
misconduct of any person , such person shall be
liable to the Commonwealth for the cost of such re-
registration of any precinct , to be recovered in a
civil action in the name of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky .
Section Twelve . The following methods shall be
used to purge the registration records and keep
them corrected up to date :
(a) It shall be the duty of the Registrar of Vital
Statistics for each county to furnish to the county
court clerk of his county , at least once a month , an

126 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
official and certified report containing the names
and addresses of all persons whose death , occurring
in said county during the month immediately pre-
ceding , has been reported to said registrar ; like-
wise , it shall be the duty of each Circuit Court Clerk
to furnish to the county court clerk, at least once
a month , an official and certified report containing
the names and addresses of all persons who have ,
during the month immediately preceding , been, by
the circuit court of such county , adjudged insane
or convicted of any offense which , under the laws
of this State , operates as an exclusion from suffrage
and the offense of which such persons have been
convicted . Immediately upon receipt of such lists
of deaths , adjudications of insanity , and convic-
tions , it shall be the duty of the Clerk to mail to
every person whose name is included in any of such
lists , at the address appearing on the registration
record of such person in the office of the County
Court Clerk , stating that the registration of such
person will , unless protest is filed within five days
from and including the date of such notice , be can-
celled , and the reason therefor . If no protest is
filed within said time , the registration record of such
person shall be cancelled . If protest is filed within
said time , the Clerk shall investigate the matter and
shall not cancel such record until after the prot-
estant has been given an opportunity to be heard .
(b ) The failure of any person registered as
herein provided to vote at any primary or general
election for two consecutive years as shown by the
voting record of such person on his registration
record shall be prima facie evidence that such per-
son is no longer entitled to registration . The County
Court Clerk shall , in the month of January of each
year , cause a check of all registrations to be made
and a list prepared of all persons whose registra-

CHAPTER 45 127
tion records indicate that such persons have failed
to vote at any primary or general election for two
consecutive years , and shall mail a notice to each
of such persons on said list , at the address of such
person appearing on the registration record , stating
that his registration will , unless protest is filed
within five days from the date of such notice , be
cancelled , and the reason therefor . If protest is
filed , said Clerk shall make an investigation , and
shall not cancel said registration if the person is
found to be legally entitled to registration . If no
protest is filed within said time , his registration
shall be cancelled , and to vote thereafter he shall be
required to register as if he had never registered .
Section Thirteen . When the county executive
committee of any political party having representa-
tion on the State Board and County Board of Elec-
tion Commissioners shall desire it , the County Board
of Election Commissioners shall appoint two offi-
cers for any precinct, whose duty it shall be to purge
the registration list in that precinct , and these offi-
cers need not reside in the precinct . If the county
committee of either of such political parties shall
desire to have the registration purged in any pre-
cinct , such committee shall file a request in writing
with the County Board of Election Commissioners
not later than two weeks before the next ensuing
primary or general election in any year ; such re-
quest shall state the name and address of each voter
whose name is challenged , and the ground upon
which challenged . It shall be the duty of the County
Board of Election Commissioners , within three
days after the filing with them of such request , to
appoint two purgation officers as aforesaid , one
from each of the political parties having represen-
tation on the State Board and County Board of
Election Commissioners , these appointments to be

128 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
made from a list of names submitted to the County
Board of Election Commissioners by the county ex-
ecutive committee of each of such political parties .
The list of challenged voters shall be turned over
to these two purgation officers , who shall be sworn
faithfully to discharge their duties as such , and
they shall meet in the precinct for which they are
to act , at some place designated and provided by
the sheriff . Said officers shall have the power to
summons and swear witnesses , and shall conduct a
hearing in the precinct at such time as they may
fix ; each voter who has been challenged shall , unless
upon investigation the purgation officers shall agree
that the challenge is unfounded and the voter law-
fully registered , be notified of the time and place of
the meeting at which the challenge against him will
be heard, such notice to be in writing and served
upon the challenged voter either by delivering or
offering , to deliver a copy of said notice to him in
person or , if said voter cannot be found at his place
of residence shown on his registration record , by
leaving a copy thereof with any person over the age
of sixteen residing in the same family with said
voter , or, if no such person be there , by affixing a
copy of said notice to the front door of such place
or residence . The person who serves such notice
shall return a copy thereof to the purgation officers ,
with an endorsement thereon stating when and how
it was served , and if a copy was not delivered to
the person to whom it was directed , the endorse-
ment must state the facts authorizing the manner
of service adopted , if the person serving the notice
be an officer , his endorsement must show his official
position ; if he be not an officer , he must make an
affidavit that his endorsement is true , that he is
over sixteen years of age , and that he is not a party
to nor interested in the proceeding . Such endorse-

CHAPTER 45 129
ment of an officer , or such endorsement and affidavit
of one who is not an officer , shall be proof of
service .
After investigation and hearing such evidence as
may be presented , the purgation officers shall deter-
mine whether the voter is properly and legally reg-
istered ; such hearing shall be conducted in a sum-
mary manner and the officers , in their discretion,
may reasonably limit the number of witnesses tes-
tifying for either party . If the purgation officers
find that the challenged voter is not properly and
legally registered , his registration shall thereupon
be canceled by them. The failure of any voter
against whom a notice of challenge has been served
as provided herein to appear at such hearing shall
be prima facie evidence that he is not entitled to
registration , and his registration record shall be
cancelled . Appeals from the action of the purga-
tion officers may be taken in the manner hereinafter
provided . If they find that he is legally and prop-
erly registered , nothing further shall be done with
his name . The County Court Clerk shall turn the
"copy " registration record of the precinct over to
such purgation officers when they are to hold their
meetings , taking their receipt therefor , and upon
the conclusion of their meetings , the purgation offi-
cers shall return such registration record to the
County Court Clerk and take his receipt therefor .
In the event the purgation officers disagree as to
whether the registration of any person should be
canceled , they or either of them shall reduce to
writing the facts as developed and certify such
statement of facts to the County Court Clerk , who
shall forthwith assign the case of each such voter
challenged to the judge of the County Court , who
shall promptly hear the case de novo , summarily
decide the question , and endorse on the statement
Acts- 5

130 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
an order disposing of the same , returning the state-
ment with his endorsement thereon to the clerk .
The clerk , upon receipt of such certification from
such purgation officers , shall immediately issue a
notice to the challenged voter to appear before the
judge of the County Court not later than three days
after the service of such notice , which notice shall
be served by any officer authorized to serve sub-
poenas or by any person authorized by law to serve
notices .
Section Fourteen . When any person is ag-
grieved by any act of the purgation officers in
respect to his registration , or when any person or
executive committee that has challenged the regis-
tration of any voter is aggrieved by any act of the
purgation officers in respect to the registration of
such person , such person or such committee so ag-
grieved shall have the right to apply to the judge
of the County Court , with a written statement of
the act of the purgation officers and the grounds of
his , or its complaint . Before such application is
made , written notice shall have been given to the
purgation officers setting out the time and place at
which such application will be made , and the
grounds therefor , so as to give the purgation officers
a reasonable opportunity to be present at such time
and place . The judge to whom such application is
made shall , at the time fixed in the notice , or a sub-
sequent time appointed by the said judge , summa-
rily decide the question presented and endorse on
the statement of complaint an order disposing of
the same. The action of said judge in such case
shall be final and upon return of the statement of
complaint, with the order of the judge disposing of
same endorsed thereon , to the County Court Clerk ,
the clerk shall correct his record in accordance with
such order .

CHAPTER 45 131
Section Fifteen . Any voter may, by giving five
days ' notice in writing , to any person whose name
has been registered, move the county court to strike
his name from the register , and both parties may
introduce witnesses , not exceeding two in number
on each side . Said notice must be served person-
ally . If at the hearing the court shall be of the
opinion that the person registered is not lawfully
entitled to register , it shall direct the clerk to strike
his name from the register , by inserting opposite to
it the words " Stricken off by order of the county
court." If the person upon whom notice is at-
tempted to be served cannot be found , the clerk
shall write opposite such name on the registration
book the word " doubtful " and when at an election
such person whose name is marked " doubtful "
shall offer to vote he shall be sworn , and his right
to vote investigated fully .
Section Sixteen . The Clerk shall preserve for a
period of not less than two years all cancelled
registration records in a separate file , and said
records shall be filed and arranged in alphabetical
order for the entire county according to the last
name of the person registered thereon . Said file
shall be designated the " Canceled Registration
File " . When the registration record is canceled ,
the original record and the copy thereof shall be
marked in large letters in ink " Canceled " , and
there shall also be noted thereon in ink the date of
and reason for such cancellation .
Section Seventeen . Any county court clerk or
deputy county court clerk who shall falsely or
fraudulently register the name of any person , or
permit any person to register , knowing that such
person it not entitled to register , or who shall fraud-
ulently alter, change , or make erasures in , or add
names to any registration record or who shall reg-
ןיד

132 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
ister the name of any person at any other time or
times , as fixed herein , or who shall fail or refuse to
deliver to the person entitled thereto , or to the
proper election officers , copies of the registration ,
or the copy of the records to be used in such elec-
tions as they are required to be used for , shall be
deemed guilty of a felony and , upon conviction , pun-
ished by confinement in the penitentiary for not less
than one nor more than three years , and shall there-
after be disqualified from holding any elective or
appointive office in this Commonwealth .
Section Eighteen . Any person knowingly or
fraudulently , shall cause himself to be regis-
tered in more than one precinct , or in a pre-
cinct other than that in which he is a legal voter ,
or shall register under any name than his real
name , or who shall give a false address , or who in
any manner or method shall cause himself to be
registered when he is not legally entitled to regis-
ter , or any person , or officer who knowingly or
fraudulently shall in any way aid , abet , counsel or
advise in the commission of any such act , or acts ,
shall be deemed guilty of a felony , and upon con-
viction shall be confined in the penitentiary for not
less than one year nor more than three years and
shall thereafter be disqualified from holding any
appointive office in the Commonwealth .
Section Nineteen . Any county court clerk or
election officer , or other person who shall un-
lawfully alter or change any registration book , or
fraudulently add any name thereto , or who shall
willfully secrete , suppress , mutilate or destroy , or
make , or use any false or fraudulent registration
book , shall be deemed guilty of a felony , and shall
be, upon conviction , confined in the penitentiary
for not less than one nor more than three years and

CHAPTER 45 133
shall thereafter be disqualified from holding any
appointive office in the Commonwealth .
Section Twenty . Any person who shall at-
tempt to or forcibly prevent any person from
registering , or shall in any way unlawfully inter-
fere with any person registering , or shall prevent
or interfere with a clerk in carrying out his duties
with relation to the registration of voters , shall be
deemed guilty of a misdemeanor , and shall be fined
not less than one hundred nor more than five hun-
dred dollars , or imprisonment for not less than six
months nor more than twelve months , or both , in
the discretion of the court or the jury .
Section Twenty - One . The clerk of the county
court shall permit any citizen to inspect at all
reasonable hours , or to make copies of any regis-
tration book , without any fee , and he shall also , upon
request , furnish to any person requesting same a
copy or copies of the registration book , or books ,
for which he shall receive compensation as pro-
vided in the succeeding Section .
Section Twenty -Two . The clerk for his serv-
ices in the carrying out of the provisions of
this law shall be entitled to collect the following
fees :
For original registration , except the initial reg-
istration in the precincts , two cents for each person
registered ; for copying from the original registry
into the "copy" book , or copying from the original
for the use of any person , two cents per name , in-
cluding data and remarks ; for making copies of
any registration books lost, mutiliated or destroyed ,
two cents per name , data and remarks included ; for
preparing the lists of registered voters for use of
election officers as required by Section seven (7)
hereof, two cents for each five names listed ; for all
services with relation to the purging of the regis-

134 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
tration as hereinafter required , five cents for every
name ordered stricken off, or canceled as provided
in Section twelve (12 ) hereof, all of which expenses
and fees shall be paid by the county , except where
the copying be done for an individual requesting
the copy.
Section Twenty -Three . Whenever in this Act a
duty is imposed on the county clerk, it may be per-
formed by any deputy county court clerk, who may
be subject to all penalties provided herein against
the county clerk , and for the registration held at the
primary election all penalties imposed upon the
county clerk in the violation of this law , when such
violations occur , shall be imposed upon the deputy
clerk, or the clerk of the election . All election of-
ficers shall be liable to the penalty provided in
Sections eighteen ( 18 ) and nineteen ( 19 ) hereof,
for any violation of law mentioned in such cases ,
and for any violation or refusal to permit the
deputy county court clerk to enter and remain in
the voting room, or for any interfenence of such
officer in the performance of his duties under this
law shall be punished as provided in Section twenty
( 20 ) hereof .
Nothing in this law shall be construed to prevent
any soldier, sailor , officer or employee of the United
States Government , or officer or employee of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky , or any person coming
within the meaning of the word , " absent voter " ,
from registering in the manner now or as may here-
after be provided by law .
Section Twenty -Four . If any section , para-
graph , clause or separable provision of this Act
should be adjudged to be unconstitutional , such de-
cision shall not affect , or render unconstitutional
any other section , paragraph , clause or separable
provision of the Act, it being the intention of the

CHAPTER 46 135
General Assembly in enacting this Act to enact
each section , paragraph , clause and provision sep-
arately .
Chapter sixty -four ( 64 ) of the Acts of the Gen-
eral Assembly one thousand nine hundred twenty-
four ( 1924 ) session as amended , being Sections one
thousand four hundred eighty-six b-one (1486b - 1 )
to one thousand four hundred eighty -six b-27
( 1486b -27 ) , both inclusive , of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , one thousand nine hundred thirty ( 1930 )
Edition , are hereby repealed . All other laws and
parts of laws relating to the registration of voters
residing outside of the corporate limits of cities of
the first class in conflict herewith are hereby re-
pealed to the extent of such conflict .
Approved February 17 , 1936 .
CHAPTER 46 .
AN ACT to repeal and re -enact Section one thousand four hun-
dred sixty ( 1460 ) of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , one thou-
sand nine hundred thirty -three ( 1933 ) Supplement , being
Chapter eighty -two ( 82 ) , Paragraph one ( 1) , of the Acts of
the General Assembly of one thousand nine hundred thirty-
two ( 1932 ) , relating to the form , arrangement and printing
of ballots in general elections .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Section one thousand four hundred sixty
( 1460 ) of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , one thou-
sand nine hundred thirty -three (1933 ) Supplement ,
being Chapter eighty -two ( 82 ) , Paragraph one ( 1 ) ,
of the Acts of the General Assembly of one thou-
sand nine hundred thirty-two ( 1932 ) , be repealed
and re -enacted to read as follows :
The county clerks of the several counties shall
cause the names of all candidates of their respec-

136 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
tive jurisdictions , where nominations for any office
specified in the ballot have been duly made and not
withdrawn , in accordance herewith, to be printed
on one ballot all nominations of any party or group
of petitioners , as designated by them in their cer-
tificate or petition ; or if none be designated , under
some suitable title and device . If the same device
for designating candidates be selected by two par-
ties or groups of petitioners it shall be given to the
one which first selected it, and the clerk shall select
a suitable device for the other . The arrangement
of the ballot shall in general conform , as nearly as
practicable , to the plan hereinafter given , and the
devices named and lists of the candidates of the
various parties shall be printed in parallel columns ,
in such order as the Secretary of State may direct ,
precedence , however , being given to the party which
polled the higher number of votes for the head of
the ticket in the last preceding election . The device
of each party shall be placed at the head of the list
of candidates of the party . The device shall not be
enclosed in a square , but immediately under the de-
vice shall be placed a circle of one inch in diameter .
Immediately under it shall be placed the name or
title of the party ticket , and immediately under the
name or title the list of candidates of the party , the
name of each candidate having immediately on its
right a small square large enough to contain the
cross -mark by which the voter is required to desig-
nate his vote . Underneath the name of each candi-
date shall be left a blank space large enough to
contain a written name .
At the top of the ballot and separated from it by
a perforated line shall be a stub , containing the con-
secutive number of the ballot and a space for the
name and residence of the voter . No other or sec-
ondary stub shall be attached to the ballot .

CHAPTER 47 137
" "
On the back shall be printed " Official Ballot , "
the date of the election and facsimile of the signa-
ture of the clerk, who has caused the ballot to be
printed . There shall also be printed on the back of
the ballot a blank line followed by the word
'Judge ' , and after the ballot has been detached
from the stub in the book of ballots and before the
ballot is delivered to the voter to be voted one of
the judges shall sign his name on such blank line
and no ballot not so signed by one of the judges
shall be counted by the canvassing board whose
duty it is to certify the results of the election .
Should an elector desire to vote for each and every
candidate of one party he shall make a cross -mark
(X ) in the circle under the device of said party and
the vote shall be counted for all candidates under
said device : Provided , however , if a cross -mark
( X) be made in the circle under the party device
and a cross -mark (X ) be also made after one or
more candidates of a different party , or parties ,
the vote shall be counted for the candidates so
marked , and not for the candidates for the same
offices of the party so marked , but the vote shall be
counted for the other candidates of said party .
The ballots to be used in any primary , general
or special election in any county shall be printed
by a printing establishment located within the
county , if any be so located and capable of furnish-
ing same , if not , by one located within the Common-
wealth and each county court clerk shall comply
with this section by having said printing done by
a printing establishment of the county , if one be
located therein , Approved February 27 , 1936 .
CHAPTER 47 .
AN ACT to repeal and re- enact Section one thousand five hun-
dred fifty -seventeen (1550-17 ) of Carroll's Kentucky Stat-

138 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
utes , one thousand nine hundred thirty -three ( 1933 ) Supple-
ment , being Chapter eighty -five ( 85 ) of the Acts of the
General Assembly of one thousand nine hundred thirty -two
( 1932 ) and relating to ballots in primary election .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Section one thousand five hundred fifty-
seventeen ( 1550-17 ) of Carroll's Kentucky Stat-
utes , one thousand nine thirty-three ( 1933 ) Supple-
ment , being Chapter eighty -five ( 85 ) of the Acts of
the General Assembly of one thousand nine hun-
dred thirty-two ( 1932 ) , be repealed and re - enacted
so as to read as follows :
There shall be a separate ballot for each political
party subject to this Act , at the primary election
provided for herein . Such ballots shall be printed
in substantially the same manner as now provided
by law in case of regular elections , except that on
the back thereof shall be printed the words , " Offi-
cial Primary Ballot , " and at the head thereof
shall be printed the words " Official Primary Bal-
lot , " together with proper party name and the
party emblem . The party emblem in each case
shall be the same as that used at the last preceding
regular election , unless sixty days before the pri-
mary election the proper party authority certify a
different emblem to the Secretary of State , in
which event he shall certify the new emblem to the
county clerks as herein provided .
All the official ballats designed to be voted in the
primary nominating elections shall be printed in
black ink upon a good quality of white ballot paper.
The arrangement of each ballot shall be exactly the
same for each political party , and the size and the
printing shall be the same for each political party .
Duplicate impressions of the ballots for each politi-
cal party voted for at every primary election shall

CHAPTER 48 139
be printed upon cheaper colored paper . These
colored ballots shall be used solely as sample ballots
for the information and convenience of voters , and
they shall not be voted or counted .
The ballots shall be printed so as to give each
elector a clear opportunity to designate his choice
of candidates for nomination by making with the
stencil a cross in the square after the name of each
candidate for whom he wishes to vote for nomina-
tion ; and on the ballot may be printed such words
as will aid the elector to do this , such as , " Vote for
one , " " Vote for two , " and the like , to inform the
elector of the number of candidates for whom he is
entitled to vote for each office .
Approved February 25, 1936 .
CHAPTER 48 .
AN ACT to repeal and re -enact Kentucky Statutes , Section one
thousand four hundred seventy -five (1475 ) changing the ways
and the rights of blind voters .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That the Kentucky Statutes Section one thousand
four hundred seventy -five ( 1475 ) be now and same
is hereby repealed and re -enacted .
So that said Statute when repealed and re - enacted
shall read as follows :
Paragraph One . "Any elector who declares , on
oath , that by reason of inability to read the English
language , he is unable to mark his ballot, may de-
clare his choice of candidates or party ticket to the
clerk, who , in the presence of the judges , sheriff
and challengers and the elector , shall , with his pencil ,
mark a dot in the appropriate place for the cross ,
to indicate the choice of the elector . The clerk shall

140 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
then fold and deliver the bollot by making a cross-
mark either in the squares showing dots or any
other squares he may desire . In all other respects
he shall vote as is required of other electors . In
case any person applying to vote is blind , and shall
so declare on oath , the clerk shall swear the person
accompanying the said blind voter to mark his bal-
lot for him in accordance with the directions of the
blind voter , and then the blind voter and the person
sworn shall enter the voting booth and the one so
sworn shall mark the ballot for the blind voter as he
is directed , the ballot shall then be folded by the
person marking the same and placed in the ballot
box ; or, in case any person shall be so physically dis-
abled as to be unable to mark his ballot , and shall so
declare , on oath , the clerk shall have the right to
mark his ballot . Any one making a false declara-
tion under this provision of this section shall , upon
conviction , be fined in any sum not exceeding fifty
dollars , and be disfranchised for a period of two
years ; and any clerk who shall willfully deceive any
elector in marking any ballot , or willfully mark the
same in any other way than as requested by said
elector , shall be guilty of felony, and upon convic-
tion , shall be imprisoned in the penitentiary for not
less than two nor more than five years .
Approved February 26 , 1936 .
CHAPTER 49 .
AN ACT making it a misdemeanor to obtain money by assess-
ment , intimidation or coercion from any State employee or
Federal employee or employees , for any campaign or politi-
cal purpose or purposes ; defining the terms "assessment , in-
timidation , coercion , and State or Federal employee or em-
ployees ", and providing penalties for violation .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky .

CHAPTER 49 141
One . That it shall be unlawful for any per-
son, or persons , to obtain or to attempt to obtain
money by assessments , intimidation or coercion
from any State or Federal employee or employees ;
for the purpose of using such money to promote or
aid the candidacy of any person , or persons , or po-
litical party , or any cause to be voted upon by the
voters of this State or any section or portion of same ,
in any State , National , District , County , Municipal
or Precinct Election , or Primary Election ; and any
person or persons who shall assess , intimidate or
coerce , or cause same to be done , either for himself
or for another , or any Political Party or questions
to be voted upon, at any Primary Election , General
Election , or in securing delegates , or in any manner
where nominations are to be made by a convention ;
shall be guilty of a misdemeanor and shall , upon
conviction , be punished by imprisonment of not less
than ten ( 10 ) days or more than thirty ( 30 ) days , or
by fine of not less than fifty ( $ 50.00 ) dollars , or
more than five hundred ( $500.00 ) dollars , or by both
such imprisonment and fine .
Two . The Term " State or Federal Employee , '
as used herein means any person who holds any
appointive office in any department of the State
or Federal Government , and who receives wages
or salary for their work from the funds of the
State of Kentucky or the United States and shall
include all members of the State Highway Commis-
sion of Kentucky and all persons who are employed
by the State Highway Commission of Kentucky in
any capacity , and receive any compensation there-
for .
Three . The term " assessment " as used herein
means the fixing of any amount , or amounts ,
to be given in money by any employee or em-
ployees , and the soliciting of such or any

142 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
amounts in money from a person or persons so as-
sessed . The term "intimidation " and the term
" coercion " as used herein means any threats of dis-
charging any employee or employees for failure to
contribute any amounts of money for campaign or
political purposes , or any attempt to force contribu-
tions of any amounts of money for political or cam-
paign purposes by any influence , or the discharging
or demoting or reducing the salary or wages of any
employee or employees for failure to contribute
portions of their salary or wages , or by putting such
employee or employees in fear in any manner .
Four . Every assessment and each act of in-
timidation or coercion shall constitute a separate
offense .
Five . It shall be the duty of all Circuit Court
Judges of the State of Kentucky to instruct the
grand juries of their Courts regarding this Act each
time that a Grand Jury is instructed .
Six . This Act shall become effective upon its
passage and approval as required by law .
Seven . All laws or parts of laws in conflict here-
with are hereby repealed .
Approved February 25 , 1936 .
CHAPTER 50.
AN ACT repealing and re -enacting Section one thousand five
hundred ninety -six a -fourteen ( 1596a -14 ) , Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , one thousand nine hundred thirty ( 1930 ) Revised
Edition , providing for the compensation of county election
commissioners in the several counties of the Commonwealth .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Section one thousand five hundred ninety-
six a-fourteen ( 1596 a-14 ) Carroll's Kentucky Stat-

CHAPTER 51 143
utes , one thousand nine hundred thirty ( 1930 ) Re-
vised Edition , is hereby repealed and re-enacted so
that, when re -enacted , said section shall read as
follows :
" The compensation to be paid members of the
County Boards of Election Commissioners in the
several Counties of this Commonwealth for their
services in the holding of elections , and in determin-
ing the results thereof, shall be fixed by the Fiscal
Courts of such counties , and in determining a proper
and reasonable allowance for the members of said
County Boards of Election Commissioners , the Fis-
cal Court may take into consideration the number of
votes counted by said Board or its assistants , the
cost of counting said votes , and the number of days
necessary for said Board to be in session in the per-
formance of its duties . Approved February 18 , 1936 .
CHAPTER 51.
AN ACT to amend Section one thousand four hundred eighty -two
( 1482 ) , Carroll's Revised Kentucky Statutes , one thousand
nine hundred thirty -three ( 1933 ) Supplement Edition , being
part of Chapter eighty -three ( 83 ) of the Acts of the General
Assembly of one thousand nine hundred thirty -two ( 1932 )
Regular Session , relating to primary and general elections
and to the time of closing the polls , procedure ; duty of officers
respecting ballot boxes , penalties for violating duty , time of
counting the ballots and announcing results , election com-
missioners ' duty in regard to counting ballots and issuing
certificates of elections or nominations in regard to said elec-
tions and repealing all conflicting laws .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
When the hour of four p . m . has arrived the of-
ficers shall announce the same at the door of the
voting room and close the polls . They shall take
charge of the ballot box , at the booth , and in the

144 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
presence of each other shall place the lock on the
small vent in lid and securely lock it, and return
the key to the county clerk. Said election officers
shall then stamp upon all unused stubs and ballots ,
with rubber stamp or by writing on the stub and the
ballot attached thereto , the word , " unused " , and
place said stub or ballot book and all other utensils
or election material into a linen envelope , to be pro-
vided by the county clerk for that purpose , and shall
seal with sealing wax across the seal of the enve-
lope and place the county election seal upon said wax ,
so as to make the letters plainly visible in three dif-
ferent places on said envelope . The officers of elec-
tion shall immediately deliver the ballot box , stub
book and the envelope containing the unused ballots
to the county clerk . The judge and the sheriff of
election , of opposite faith , shall forthwith convey
said ballot box , stub book and envelope to the county
clerk's office , taking his receipt therefor . Said of-
ficers shall see that no person other than themselves
has access to , or custody of, said ballot box , and
they shall each remain in the presence of the other ,
until said box and envelope is delivered to the county
clerk .
And any violation by them of any duty imposed
upon them by this section with reference to such con-
veyance shall render them guilty of a misdemeanor
and upon conviction thereof they shall be punished
by a fine of not less than five hundred dollars
($500.00 ) , nor more than one thousand dollars ( $ 1 , -
000.00 ) or imprisonment in the county jail for not
less than sixty nor more than one hundred days , or
both so fined and imprisoned within the discretion
of the jury trying the case . Said officers shall also
upon delivery to the county court clerk of the ballot
box and the poll books , certificates and the envelope
containing the spoiled , mutilated and questioned

CHAPTER 51 145
ballots and the keys to the ballot box , together with
the county election seal , each make an affidavit and
file it with the county court clerk stating in sub-
stance that neither they nor anyone else took from
the ballot box while in their custody any ballot or put
into it any ballot , or in any manner whatever tam-
pered with the contents of the ballot box and that it
and its contents as well as all of the articles delivered
by them to the county court clerk were and are in
the same identical condition as when taken charge of
by them at the closing of the polls , and for any false
statement made in such affidavit the one so making
it shall be subject to indictment and prosecution for
false swearing and punished accordingly.
The ballot box shall remain locked , from the time
it leaves the county clerk's office and is returned to
said office , and until the county election commission .
ers shall unlock the same for the purpose of count-
ing the ballots as herein provided .
The county court clerk, upon receiving said ballot
box and envelope from the election officers shall re-
ceipt for the same and shall place said ballot boxes
and envelopes in a secure and substantial place and
shall keep a sufficient guard over said boxes and en-
velopes in a careful and painstaking manner until all
the ballots have been counted by the election com-
missioners and those acting with them . Provided ,
that the chairman of each of the two dominant po-
litical parties , shall have the right , and each candi-
date to be voted for, at any election , may designate
one or more persons to assist the county clerk and
other guards , to guard the safety of the ballots
in said clerk's office . The county clerk shall assist
the county clection commission , and shall act as its
clerk in the counting and certifying of the ballots
and the results of said election , for which he shall

146 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
receive compensation to be allowed by the fiscal
court .
The County election commission for the purpose
of counting the ballots in any primary or general
election and the issuing certificates and making a
report shall meet at five ( 5 ) o'clock p . m . on the
same day of any primary or general election in the
office of the county clerk or in any other room desig-
nated by the election commissioners and proceed
as speedily as practical to count the ballots of every
precinct in such county and at the finish of each and
every precinct they shall announce the results at the
door of the room where the count is being conducted
and they shall fill out the blank in the back of the
stub book provided for that purpose and each com-
missioner shall sign the same on a blank provided
for that purpose .
The election commission is authorized to employ
at county expense , such clerical help as said commis-
sion may deem necessary to make said count and
at the meeting of said commission on the day of
election at five ( 5 ) o'clock p . m. on said day, as
above set out , the commission shall stay in session
for the purpose of ascertaining the results of said.
election until twelve ( 12 ) o'clock mid-night of said
day and that they may at their discretion adjourn
the said tabulation or count of the said ballots until
the following day at eight ( 8 ) o'clock a . m . , except
if the said following day be Sunday or a legal holi-
day, they may adjourn the said counting and tabu-
lation until the following day succeeding the said
Sunday or legal holidays , at their discretion and
continue in session , Sundays and legal holidays ex-
cepted , at their discretion , until the final result of
said election is finally completed .
The election commission , if unable to ascertain the
result of the election in any county or fail to com-

CHAPTER 51 147
plete the count of all the precincts in said county by
twelve ( 12 ) o'clock mid-night on the day of said
election , may adjourn the said meeting to another
day , the reconvening of said commission shall be
at eight ( 8 ) o'clock on the day set for reconvening
and the count shall proceed during said day until
six (6) o'clock p . m. , or longer at the discretion of
the said commission and each additional day they
may be required to complete the said count , shall
be at such hours as above set out , provided however ,
they shall not meet nor tabulate during the Sabbath
day .
There shall be provided by the county clerk a
large sheet upon which to tabulate the vote of the
whole county , district or municipality , so that each
candidate can ascertain the number of votes in each
precinct and the total number of votes in each pre-
cinct and the total number in the election unit , cast
for him . If any ballots are found mutilated , defaced ,
marked or spoiled or otherwise questionable so that
they are of doubtful integrity , the commission shall
put all such ballots in a sealed envelope after writ-
ing on them and each of them , whether they have
been counted , and for whom .
In any general or primary election held in this
state or any county , district or municipality , the
same procedure shall prevail and the county election
commission shall court and certify the result in the
manner herein provided .
The said election commission shall issue to suc-
cessful candidates for office or nominations , their
certifictaes of election or nominations in all county ,
magisterial districts or any subdivision of any coun-
ty and shall certify the result of the election in their
said county , the Secretary of State in all elections
or nominations , to be made for state and district
officers and nominations .

148 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
In all general elections the governing authority of
each political party , and in all primary elections ,
each candidate or group of candidates for an office ,
may designate to the county election commission , one
representative each to meet with the county election
commission , as herein provided , and it shall be the
duty of said commission to permit said representa-
tive to assist in , and witness the count of the ballot in
all the precincts of such county and each and all of
said commissioners and said representatives , so
designated , shall have equal and full opportunity to
inspect and count said ballots , and it shall be unlaw-
ful for any county election commission or a majority
thereof, to refuse to permit said representative to
exercise full and free action in witnessing the count
of said ballots and any interference with , or refusal ,
on the part of said election commission , or either of
them , to permit said representatives , either of a po-
litical party , or of a candidate , or group of candi-
dates , to have a free and full opportunity to witness
and observe the counting of said ballots , shall be
guilty of a high misdemeanor , and upon conviction
thereof shall be fined not less than one thousand
dollars nor more than two thousand dollars or im-
prisoned in the county jail not less than six months
nor more than twelve months in the discretion of
the jury .
The county election commission after fifteen days ,
provided no contest is filed in any race against any
candidate , shall return the keys to said ballot boxes
to the county court clerk, but if any such contest ,
either of nominations or elections , has been filed ,
said commissioners shall hold said keys subject to
the orders of the court trying such contest .
This act , shall govern both the conducting , hold-
ing , counting of ballots , and certifying of returns , in
general and primary elections .

CHAPTER 52 149
Nothing herein contained shall be construed to
prevent the election commissioners in counties hav-
ing cities of the first , second and third class from em-
ploying extra tabulators , who under their supervi-
sion and responsibility may expedite the counting
of the ballots .
All laws in conflict herewith , are hereby repealed .
Approved February 27 , 1936 .
CHAPTER 52 .
AN ACT relating to primary elections .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One ( 1 ) . Each and every political party ,
except as hereinafter provided, shall nominate all
of its candidates for elective offices to be voted for in
the general elections in a primary election to be held
at the time and in the manner provided by law . No
governing authority of any politcal party shall have
the power to nominate any candidate for any elective
office in this Commonwealth or to provide any other
means or method of nominating candidates for any
elective office other than by primary elections as
provided in this Act .
Section Two ( 2 ) . Any qualified elector who is a
member of a political party within the meaning of
this Act, and who has affiliated with and supported
the nominees of the party at the last general election
at whose hands he seeks the nomination , shall have
his name printed on the official ballot of his party
for an office to which he is eligible in any primary
election held under the provisions of this Act and/or
the provisions of the general primary election laws
of this State upon filing with the proper officer at
the proper time a notification and declaration and

150 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
in the manner and form now prescribed by law . The
Secretary of State shall have no power or authority
to print or cause or permit to be printed upon the
ballots for any general election the name or names
of any candidate or candidates as the nominee or
nominees of any political party or parties , as the
term "political party " is defined in Section one
thousand five hundred fifty -five ( 1550-5 ) , Carroll's
Kentucky Statutes , one thousand nine hundred thir-
ty ( 1930 ) Edition , or the name or names of any
candidate or candidates under the emblem of any
political party or parties for any of the offices here-
inabove referred to , except the names of such can-
didates as shall have been duly and regularly nom-
inated at a primary election held as provided in
this Act .
Section Three ( 3 ) . Nothing in this Act shall be
construed to prevent any political organization or
party , not constituting a political party within the
definition embraced in Section one thousand five
hundred fifty-five ( 1550-5 ) , Kentucky Statutes , Car-
roll's one thousand nine hundred thirty ( 1930 ) Edi-
tion , from making their nominations for any of the
offices mentioned in this Act as they are now per-
mitted by law to make same .
Section Four ( 4 ) . Hereafter all candidates for
elective offices to be voted for at any general elec-
tion shall be nominated by a primary held in accord-
ance with the provisions of this Act . Provided
that if a vacancy occurs after any such nomination ,
by death or otherwise , the governing authority of
such party may provide for filling such vacancy or
vacancies and the making of such nominations , and
when such nominations have been so made the cer-
tificates of nomination shall be signed by the chair-
man and secretary of the governing authority of the
party making same and shall be filed in the same

CHAPTER 53 151
manner as certificates of nomination at a primary
election . The provisions of this Act shall not apply
to candidates for trustees of common schools or
members of school boards nor to trustees in towns
of the fifth and sixth class nor to candidates for
presidential electors , but such candidates for such
offices shall be nominated and have their nominations
certified as now, or may be hereafter provided by
law .
Section Five (5) . In any primary election the
candidate receiving the highest number of votes for
the particular office for which he is a candidate shall
be the nominee of his party for that office and shall
receive the certificate of nomination .
Section Six ( 6 ) . All acts and parts of acts in con-
flict herewith, to the extent of such conflict , hereby
are repealed . Chapter One (1 ) of the Acts of the
Extraordinary Session of the General Assembly of
Kentucky.of one thousand nine hundred thirty -five
( 1935 ) , being Sections one thousand five hundred
forty-nine a-two ( 1549a -2 ) to one thousand five hun-
dred fifty -one b ( 1550-1b ) , Kentucky Statutes , Bald-
win's one thousand nine hundred thirty -five ( 1935 )
Kentucky Statutes Service , hereby is expressly re-
pealed . Approved February 6, 1936 .
CHAPTER 53 .
AN ACT to amend and re -enact Section four thousand seven
hundred and six (4706 ) of the Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's
Edition one thousand nine hundred thirty ( 1930 ) , as amended
by Chapter ninety -one ( 91 ) of the Acts of one thousand nine
hundred thirty -two ( 1932 ) , relating to the investment of funds
by fiduciaries .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Section four thousand seven hundred and

152 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
six (4706 ) of the Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's Edi-
tion one thousand nine hundred thirty ( 1930 ) , as
amended by Chapter ninety - one ( 91 ) of the Acts of
one thousand nine hundred thirty -two ( 1932 ) , re-
lating to the investment of funds by fiduciaries , be
amended and re -enacted so as to read as follows :
"It shall be lawful for persons or corporations
holding funds in a fiduciary capacity for loan or
investment , to invest the same in bonds or other
interest bearing obligations of the Government of
the United States of America ; bonds and State War-
rants and other interest bearing obligations of the
State of Kentucky , including Bridge Revenue bonds
issued by the Kentucky State Highway Commission
as an agency of the Commonwealth of Kentucky ;
securities authorized by an Act of Congress entitled
'The Federal Farm Loan Act ' ; bonds and obliga-
tions of the Home Owners Loan Corporation , Fed-
eral Farm Mortgage Corporation , Federal Inter-
mediate Credit Bank , and bonds , mortgages , and
obligations isued or insured by the Federal Hous-
ing Administrator ; and real estate mortgage notes ,
bonds , and other interst bearing or dividend paying
securities which would be regarded by prudent busi-
ness men as safe investments ; and to make loans
with such securities as collateral ; and a fiduciary
shall account for all interest or profit received , ex-
cept that a fiduciary , in making real estate mortgage
loans for the purpose of investing trust funds , may
charge the borrower not exceeding two ( 2 ) per
centum of the principal of the loan and retain such
amount to cover the cost incurred and services ren-
dered by the fiduciary in making the loan.
Where securities originally received by a fiduciary
do not meet the requirements for the investment of
trust funds as provided in this statute , or where
securities in which trust funds have been properly

CHAPTER 53 153
invested thereafter become ineligible for investment
of trust funds as provided in this statute , then such
ineligible securities shall be sold at such time , and
upon such terms and conditions , or shall be held
or otherwise handled in such manner , as prudent
business men would , under the particular circum-
stances , consider proper ; and a fiduciary shall be
responsible for otherwise acting .
In clarification of the foregoing provisions au-
thorizing the investment of trust funds in real estate
mortgages , a trust company , or a combined bank
and trust company (hereinafter called ' trust com-
pany ' ) , empowered to act as a fiduciary under the
laws of the State of Kentucky , and subject to ex-
amination by either Kentucky State or Federal
banking authorities , is authorized , one ( 1 ) to set
apart , in a separate real estate mortgage in-
vestment fund , real estate mortgages , which in
each instance would conform to the Kentucky
requirements as to trust investments ; two ( 2 )
to issue against such investment fund partici-
pation certificates covering fractional interests
therein ; and three ( 3 ) to invest trust funds in
its hands for investment in such participation cer-
tificates ; provided , however , that such trust company
shall at all times maintain definite records , fully and
accurately setting forty one ( 1 ) all mortgages held
in such investment fund , including all cash collec-
tions and any real estate acquired in foreclosure ;
and two ( 2 ) all participation certificates issued
against the investment fund ; and in no event shall
participation certificates be issued in the aggregate
so as to exceed the aggregate principal amount of the
investment fund . Said participation certificates
shall be substantially in the following form :
(Insert place and date :)
'The undersigned hereby certifies : That ( insert

154 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
description of trust estate of name of beneficial own-
er) is the owner of an interest in a real estate mort-
gage investment fund , which fund is shown on the
records and accounts of the undersigned , and which
interest is equivalent to the fraction which would
result from using (insert amount ) Dollars ( $………………………. )
as the numerator and the aggregate principal amount
of said investment fund as the denominator .
This Certificate is issued in accordance with , and
under the authority of, an Act of the General As-
sembly of the State of Kentucky , relating to the
investment of funds by fiduciaries , and enacted at
the regular session of one thousand nine hundred
thirty -six (1936 ) ; and is subject to all the terms and
conditions of said Act.
( Signature of trust company ) '
To provide for possible losses which might occur
in an investment fund established by a trust com-
pany under the foregoing provisions , such trust com-
pany is authorized to reserve from the interest col-
lected on the mortgages held in the fund , not ex-
ceeding one-half of one per centum per annum on
the principal of such mortgages , which reserved
amount shall be set aside in a separate reserve ac-
count , and used primarily for the purpose of cover-
ing any losses that might be sustained in connection
with any of said mortgages , or in foreclosing any of
said mortgages , or from actual outlays in connec-
tion with any mortgaged property or property ac-
quired under any such foreclosure proceedings . Such
reserve account , or the balance thereof after the pay-
ment of any such losses , if any , shall belong entirely
to the beneficial owners of the investment fund .
All income earned by the investment fund , except
that amount reserved in the manner and for the pur-
poses aforesaid , shall be periodically , and at least

CHAPTER 53 155
semi-annually , distributed ratably to the holders of
participation certificates issued against the invest-
ment fund . Principal cash which may accumulate
in the investment fund by reason of payments on
mortgages may be used in the redemption and can-
cellation of participation certificates ; or may be
used in making further mortgage loans ; and such
trust company may advance cash to the investment
fund to be used in making additional desirable mort-
gage loans in anticipation of trust funds becoming
available for investment in participation certificates ,
but in no event shall such trust company be entitled
to any profit on any such transactions other than the
interest earned on its advancements . If any partici-
pation certificates issued by a trust company under
the foregoing provisions should become distributable
by reason of the termination or removal of a trust ,
such trust company may at its option either dis-
tribute such certificates in kind , or redeem and can-
cel such certificates for the account of the invest-
ment , fund , or purchase such certificates for the ac-
count of other trust estates in its hands .
Should any trust company , having established an
investment fund as herein authorized , thereafter at
any time , in its judgment fairly and reasonably ex-
ercised , determine that there is an apparent loss in
such fund , over and above the account to be reserved
to cover such loss , then such trust company shall
at once proceed to liquidate the investment fund . In
such liquidation such trust company shall, except as
hereinafter provided, distribute pro rata on ac-
count of the principal of the outstanding participa-
tion certificates , all principal realized from the in-
vestment fund , and shall likewise distribute on ac-
count of interest all income earned by the fund .
Where the principal and interest on a particular
mortgage is not collected in full , then the amount

156 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
realized shall , after the payment of any costs in-
curred in its collection , be first used in making whole
the principal of the mortgage debt , and any balance
remaining shall be applied to interest . In such
liquidation such trust company , instead of distribut-
ing pro rata on account of participation certificates
all cash realized from the investment fund , may use
a portion of such cash in retiring and canceling par-
ticipation certificates , provided , however , that in re-
tiring and canceling participation certificates under
these conditions , such trust company shall carefully
estimate and determine the apparent loss in prin-
cipal and interest in the investment fund , over and
above the reserve account to cover such loss , and
shall pay for the certificates to be retired and can-
celed , the amount invested in such certificates , less
the loss as to principal and interest determined in
the manner aforesaid, provided , however , that any
person who might be entitled to the distribution in
kind of such certificates and who is not satisfied
with such trust company's determination of said
loss , may demand and receive distribution in kind
of such certificates .
In making real estate mortgage loans for the pur-
pose of investing trust funds , a trust company em-
powered to act as a fiduciary under the laws of the
State of Kentucky and subject to examination either
by Kentucky State or Federal banking authorities ,
may make such mortgage loans in its individual
name ; and in connection with such loans may prose-
cute all suits and foreclosure actions , and may buy
in , manage , lease , repair , and sell foreclosed prop-
erty in its individual name , without reference to any
trust or beneficial ownership ; provided , however ,
that such trust company shall at all times keep defi-
nite records showing fully and accurately all bene-
ficial ownership in such loans , suits , or property ,

CHAPTER 54 157
which records shall at all times be subject to the
inspection and examination of either Kentucky State
or Federal banking authorities ; and provided , fur-
ther that such mortgage loans and such foreclosed
property shall not be subject to the individual debts
of the trust company , but shall belong to the bene-
ficial owners thereof as shown by said records free
from any and all claims against the trust company .
The various provisions of this Act are severable
and if any of its provisions shall be held to be uncon-
stitutional , the decision or decisions of the Courts
so holding shall not affect or impair any of the re-
maining provisions of the Act .
All Acts and parts of Acts in conflict herewith
are hereby repealed . ' Approved February 25 , 1936 .
