CHAPTER 54 .
AN ACT to repeal and re -enact Section two thousand forty-
three-twelve (2043-12 ) , Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , one
thousand nine hundred thirty ( 1930 ) Edition , the same being
Section twelve ( 12 ) , of Chapter sixty -eight ( 68 ) of the Acts
of the General Assembly of one thousand nine hundred thirty
( 1930 ) and entitled , " An Act concerning the manner of com-
mitment of incompetent veterans of the World War who are
beneficiaries of World War Veterans ' Act as amended ; and
regulating the appointment , defining the duties and governing
the actions of guardians and committees for beneficiaries of
the World War Veterans ' Act , as amended , and the World
War Adjusted Compensation Act , as amended ," and declar-
ing an emergency to exist .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Section two thousand forty-three -twelve
( 2043-12 ) , Supplement Carroll's Kentucky Statutes ,
one thousand nine hundred thirty , ( 1930 ) Edition ,
being Section twelve ( 12 ) of Chapter sixty - eight
( 68 ) of the Acts of the General Assembly, one thou-

158 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
sand nine hundred thirty ( 1930 ) , relating to the
manner of commitment of incompetent veterans of
the World War who are beneficiaries of the World
War Veterans ' Act as amended ; and regulating the
appointment , defining the duties and governing the
actions of guardians and committees for beneficiaries
of the World War Veterans ' Act , as amended , and
the World War Adjusted Compensation Act , as
amended , be and the same is hereby repealed , amend-
ed and re -enacted , so that such section when thus
repealed , amended and re - enacted shall read as fol-
lows :
Section Twelve ( 12 ) Every guardian or commit-
tee, who has or is receiving funds from the Veter-
ans ' Administration , shall , before investing such
funds of the estate , obtain the prior order of the
court and with such prior order shall invest the
funds of the estate in such manner and in such se-
curities or annuities , in which the guardian or com-
mittee has no interest , as allowed by law .
All laws or parts of laws in conflict herewith are
hereby repealed .
Whereas, any delay in the privilege of so invest-
ing the funds of a veteran may result in a loss of
income to such veteran , an emergency is hereby de-
clared to exist , and this Act shall take effect upon
its passage and approval by the Governor.
Approved February 18 , 1936 .
CHAPTER 55 .
AN ACT for the protection of Deer and Elk , amending and re-
enacting Section one thousand nine hundred thirty -eight
a-sixteen (1938a -16 ) , Kentucky Statutes .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :

CHAPTER 56 159
That Chapter eighty -three ( 83 ) of the Acts of the
General Assembly of one thousand nine hundred
thirty-four ( 1934 ) , being Section one thousand nine
hundred thirty-eight a -sixteen ( 1938a -16 ) , Ken-
tucky Statutes , Carroll's one thousand nine hundred
thirty -four ( 1934 ) Supplement , be amended and re-
enacted , and when so amended and re -enacted shall
read as follows , to wit :
No person shall , in the state of Kentucky , hunt ,
pursue , chase, catch , kill , injure or molest any elk
or deer . Any person violating this section , or any
feature thereof , shall upon conviction , be fined not
less than fifty dollars ( $ 50.00 ) or more than one
hundred dollars ( $ 100.00 ) for each offense .
Approved February 18 , 1936 .
CHAPTER 56 .
AN ACT to create a Game and Fish Commission , to define its
powers and duties , and to provide for the protection , restora-
tion , propagation , and increase of game , birds , fish and fur-
bearing animals ; and transferring duties and funds .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
GAME AND FISH COMMISSION
Section One ( 1 ) . ( a ) Commission Created . That
the Game and Fish Department is hereby placed
under a Commission to be known as the " Game and
Fish Commission " , hereinafter referred to as the
Commission , which is hereby created .
(b) Appointment and Terms . The Commission
shall consist of five members , who shall fairly repre-
sent the several geographical sections of the State
(and not more than three of whom shall be of the
same political party ) .

160 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
The members of the Commission shall be appoint-
ed by the Governor within sixty days after the ap-
proval of this Act , by and with the advice and con-
sent of the Senate , one to serve one year , one to
serve two years , one to serve three years , two to
serve four years each , or until their successors are
appointed and qualified ; and thereafter as such
terms expire their successors shall be appointed for
terms of four years.
No person shall be appointed a member of the
Commission unless he shall be well informed on the
subject of wildlife conservation and restoration .
( c ) Removals . The Governor may remove a com-
missioner for inefficiency , neglect of duty, or mis-
conduct in office , or for any cause deemed sufficient
by the Governor of the Commonwealth , by the entry
of an executive order dismissing said Commissioner .
If such Commissioner shall be removed , the Gov-
ernor shall file in the office of the Secretary of State
a complete statement of all charges made aganist
such Commissioner and his findings thereon , to-
gether with a complete record of the proceedings .
The Governor shall fill vacancies by appointment
for any unexpired term , subject to confirmation by
the Senate at the next regular or special session of
the State Legislature .
( d ) Reimbursement for Expenses . The members
of the Commission shall receive no compensation for
their services as members thereof , except that each
commissioner shall be entitled to reimbursement for
actual and necessary traveling and other expenses
and disbursements incurred or made by him in the
discharge of his official duties to be paid from the
Game and Fish Fund .
(e ) Quorum. A majority of the commissioners.
shall constitute a quorum for the transaction of any

CHAPTER 56 161
business , for the performance of any duty, or for
the exercise of any power .
(f) Office and Supplies . The Commission shall
have its principal office in the State Capital or such
other place as may be designated in this Act . The
Commission is authorized to purchase all supplies ,
equipment , printed forms , and notices , and to issue
such publications as it may deem necessary to carry
out the purposes of this Act .
GENERAL POWERS AND DUTIES OF THE COMMISSION
Section Two ( 2 ) . ( a) Chairman and Meeting .
The members of the Commission shall meet at the
Capital within thirty days after their appointment
and shall organize by electing from their member-
ship a Chairman and a Secretary, and thereafter
regular meetings shall be held by the Commission
at its offices quarterly , and at such other times and
places within the State as the Commission shall se-
lect for the transaction of business .
(b) Powers and Duties . It shall be unlawful to
take or pursue any of the wildlife of the State at any
time or in any manner , except at such times and in
such manner as the supply of said wildlife may
justify , and the said Commission is hereby directed
to make adequate investigation as to the said supply
and thereupon shall , by appropriate rules and regu-
lations :
-One (1 ) Establish and close to hunting , trapping ,
or fishing, game , bird or fish refuges on public lands
and , with the consent of the owner , on private lands ;
Two (2 ) -Acquire by purchase , condemnation ,
lease , agreement , gift, or devise , lands or waters
suitable for the purposes hereinafter enumerated ,
and develop , operate and maintain the same for said
purposes :
Acts-6

162 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
(a ) For fish hatcheries , nursery ponds , or game
farms ;
(b ) Lands or waters suitable for game , bird , fish ,
or fur -bearing animal restoration , propagation , or
protection ;
(c) For public hunting , fishing, or trapping areas
to provide places where the public may hunt , trap ,
or fish in accordance with the provisions of law or
the regulations of the Commission ;
(d) To extend and consolidate by exchange lands
or waters suitable for the above purposes ;
( e ) To capture , propagate , transport , buy , sell , or
exchange , any species of game , bird , fish , or fur
bearing animal needed for propagation or stocking
purposes , or to exercise control measure of unde-
sirable species .
Three ( 3 )-Enter into cooperative agreements
with educational institutions and State , Federal , or
other agencies , to promote wildlife research and
to train men for wildlife management .
Four ( 4) -Enter into cooperative agreement with
Federal agencies , municipalities , corporations , or-
ganized groups of land-owners , associations and in-
dividuals for the development of game , bird , fish , or
fur -bearing animal management and demonstration
projects .
( c ) Limitation on Powers . Nothing in this Act ,
however , shall be construed to authorize the Com-
mission to change any penalty prescribed by law
for a violation of its provisions , or to change the
amount of license fees or the authority conferred by
licenses prescribed by law .
(d ) Organiztion of Work . The Commision shall
establish such departmental bureaus or divisions
and shall authorize the Director to employ such ex-
perts , clerks , or other employees as it may deem

CHAPTER 56 163
necessary for the conduct of the work of the Com-
mission, and it shall fix their salaries or other com-
pensation , which shall be paid out of the Game and
Fish Fund .
The Commission shall authorize such scientific and
other studies as may be deemed necessary to its
work , and shall collect , classify , and disseminate such ·
statistics , data , and information as in its discretion
will tend to promote the objects of this Act .
OFFICE OF DIRECTOR
Secion Three ( 3 ) . ( a ) Director to be Selected .
The office of Director of the Game and Fish Depart-
ment is hereby created . Upon the organization of
the Commission hereinbefore provided for , said
Commission shall appoint a Director of the Game
and Fish Department , who shall be a man with
knowledge of , and experience in , the requirements
for the protection , conservation , and restoration of
the wildlife resources of the State . The Director
shall serve for an indefinite term . The Director
shall not hold any other public office and shall devote
his entire time to the service of the State in the dis-
charge of his official duties .
(b) Compensation and Expenses . The Director
shall receive such compensation as the Commission
may determine , and shall be reimbursed for all
actual and necessary traveling and other expenses
incurred by him in the discharge of his official duties .
(c) Oath and Bond . Before entering upon the
duties of his office , the Director shall take , and sub-
scribe to , the constitutional oath of office and shall ,
in addition thereto , swear or affirm that he holds
no other public office , nor any position under any
political committee or party . Such oath , or affirma-
tion , shall be filed in the office of the Secretary of
State .

164 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
The Director shall execute and file with the State
Treasurer a bond to the people of the State in the
sum of Five Thousand ( $ 5,000.00 ) Dollars , condi-
tioned on the faithful performance of his duties , and
that he will account for and pay over , pursuant to
law , all State moneys received by him under law for
the protection of wild animals , birds , and fish . The
premium on such bond shall be paid from the Game
and Fish Fund .
(d ) Duties and Powers . The Director shall have
general supervision and control of all activities ,
functions , and employees of the Game and Fish De-
partment , under the supervision and direction of
the Commission , and shall enforce all the provisions
of the laws of this State relating to wild animals ,
birds , and fish , and shall exercise all necessary
powers incident thereto not specifically conferred
on the Commission .
Director shall make to League of Kentucky Sports-
men ( as long as League remains intact ) a quarterly
report of all receipts and disbursements .
CONSERVATION OFFICERS
Section Four ( 4 ) . ( a ) Appointment and Selec-
tion . The Director is hereby authorized to appoint ,
with the approval of the Commission , as many Con-
servation Officers as may be required efficiently to
enforce the laws for the protection of wild animals ,
birds , and fish .
All appointments of Conservation Officers here-
after made shall be under Civil Service rules adopt-
ed and promulgated by the Commission . Such ex-
amination shall embrace an investigation of the
character , habits , and qualifications of each appli-
cant , as well as his knowledge of the State game and
fish laws , and the duties and responsibilities ap-
pertaining to the position of Conservation Officer ,

CHAPTER 56 165
(b) Compensation and Advancement . Each Con-
servation Officer shall receive a salary of not less
than one thousand five hundred ($ 1,500 ) dollars per
annum , payable in monthly installments ; provided ,
however , that each officer who shall have been rated
in the first grade for a full year shall receive in-
creased salary at the rate of One Hundred Dollars
($ 100.00 ) per annum , and for each year thereafter
in which he shall qualify he shall receive a like in-
crease until he receives the sum of One Thousand
Eight Hundred ($ 1,800.00 ) Dollars per annum ; and
that each officer who has been rated in the second
grade for a full year shall receive increased salary
at the rate of Fifty Dollars ( $ 50.00 ) per annum , and
for each year thereafter in which he shall so qualify
he shall receive a like increase until he receives the
sum of One Thousand Five Hundred ( $ 1,500.00 )
Dollars per annum ; but the Commission shall have
power in its discretion, for cause shown , to cancel
such increase , or any part thereof , on the failure of
any officer receiving such increase to qualify for the
first or second grade in any years.
(c ) Political Activity Prohibited . While retain-
ing the right to vote as he may please and to express
privately his opinion on all political subjects , no em-
ployee or officer of the Commission shall take any
active part in political management or in political
campaigns , nor shall he use his official authority or
influence for the purpose of interfering with an elec-
tion or affecting the results thereof , or for the pur-
pose of coercing the political action of any person
or body .
ORDERS AND REGULATIONS
Section Five ( 5 ) . ( a ) Publication . Rules , regu-
lations and order of the Commission shall be pub-
lished in the following manner : Those having gen-

166 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
eral application throughout the State shall be pub-
lished at least once in some newspaper published
in and having general circulation throughout the
State ; those of special character having local appli-
cation only shall be published at least once in some
newspaper published in and having general circula-
tion in the locality wherein such rules , regulations
and orders are applicable ; but , if no such news-
paper is so published and circulated , copies of such
rules , regulations and orders shall be posted in at
least three conspicuous places in the locality in
which they are applicable . Such rules , regulations
and orders may also be given such other publicity
as the Commission may deem desirable .
(b) Violation of Rules and Regulations . Any per-
son violating any rules, regulation , or order of the
Commission shall be guilty of a misdemeanor and
upon conviction thereof shall be punished by a fine
or not less than Ten Dollars ($10.00 ) nor more than
Fifty Dollars ( $ 50.00 ) for each offense , or by im-
prisonment in the county jail for a period of not
less than ten ( 10 ) days nor more than thirty ( 30 )
days , or by both such fine and imprisonment .
GAME AND FISH FUND
Section Six (6 ) . ( a ) Creation of Fund . The Com-
missioners and the Director of the Game and Fish
Department shall promptly pay to the State Treas-
urer all moneys received by them , respectively , from
the sale of hunting licenses or from any other source
connected with the administration of the provisions
of this Act , or any law or regulation for the protec-
tion of wild animals , birds , or fish , and the State
Treasurer shall deposit all such moneys in a special
fund to be known as the " Game and Fish Fund ".
which is hereby reserved , set aside , appropriated
and made available until expended as may be di-
9

CHAPTER 56 167
rected by the Commission in carrying out the pur-
poses of this Act , or any law or regulation for the
protection of wild animals , birds , or fish , and shall
be used for no other purpose . All warrants shall
be drawn on said fund in the manner provided by
the Constitution or by statute .
(b) Transfer of Funds . All funds , appropria-
tions , and moneys provided for the purpose of ad-
ministering or enforcing the game and fish laws of
this State , and all funds , appropriations , property ,
and moneys under the control of the present Game
and Fish Department , or other funds of the State
in the custody or control of any other officer or per-
son connected with the office of the present Game and
Fish Department , or under the supervision of the
said Department, shall be and hereby are trans-
ferred to the Commission or the Game and Fish
Fund , and made available to and placed under the
control of the Commission herein created .
(c ) Control of Expenditures . The Commission
shall govern the financial policies of the Depart-
ment and shall fix the budget for the operation and
maintenance of its work for each fiscal year , which
budget shall not be exceeded by the Director .
Funds to the credit of the Commission shall be
withdrawn by the Director by warrant against the
Game and Fish Fund for such purposes as the Com-
mission may direct .
All such warrants shall have attached thereto or
contained therein an itemized statement showing the
purposes of said expenditure .
The Commission shall not contract any indebted-
ness or obligation beyond the funds available , or
presently to be available , for its use.
(d) Purposes of Expenditures . The Commission
shall have the exclusive power to expend for the pro-
tection , conservation , propagation , and restoration

168 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
of game , birds , fish , and fur -bearing animals , all
funds of the State acquired for the protection , con-
servation, propagation , or restoration of birds , ani-
mals , or fish , arising from State appropriations ,
licenses , gifts , or otherwise ..
FINES AND APPEALS
Section Seven (7) . Payment and Reports . It
shall be the duty of each Justice of the Peace or
Clerk of the Court , before whom any prosecution
under this Act or any law or regulation for the pro-
tection of wild animals , birds , or fish may be com-
menced , or shall go on appeal , and within twenty
days after a decision has been rendered therein, to
report in writing to the Director the result of the
prosecution , the amount of fine collected or penalty
imposed , if any, therein.
All moneys collected as fines or penalties for viola-
tions of the provisions of this Act or any law or
regulation for the protection of wild animals , birds ,
or fish shall be , within thirty ( 30 ) days , paid over
to the Commission by the court , Justice of the Peace ,
or other officer collecting or receiving the same .
SAVING CLAUSES
Section Eight ( 8 ) . ( a ) Employees Continued in
Service . All persons in the employ of the Game and
Fish Department shall continue in service under the
direction of the Commission hereby created until
such time as transfers or changes in personnel are
deemed necessary for the good of the service .
(b) Present Statutes and Orders . Except as here-
in, specifically or otherwise provided , all laws relat-
ing to the collection , carrying , transfer , custody , or
disbursement of funds , appropriations , and moneys ,
or relating to any clerical or ministerial act involved

CHAPTER 56 169
in the collecting , carrying , transfer , custody , or dis-
bursement of funds appropriated or made available
for the administration of the game and fish laws of
the State , or in the custody or control of the Game
and Fish Department or any other officer or person
connected with said Department , or under the juris-
diction of said Department , are applied to the col-
lecting, carrying , transfer , custody and disburse-
ment of said funds when under the control of the
Commission herein created .
All existing rules , orders , and regulations of the
State Game and Fish Commission shall continue in
full force and effect as rules , orders , and regulations
of the Commission hereby created , until amended
or rescinded by it .
(c) Invalidity of Provisions . If any clause , sen-
tence, paragraph , subdivision , section , or part of
this Act , or any order or rule of the Commission
shall, for any reason , be adjudged by any court of
competent jurisdiction to be unconstitutional or in-
valid , such judgment shall not affect , impair , or in-
validate the remainder thereof , but shall be con-
fined in its operation to such clause , sentence , para-
graph , subdivision , section , or part thereof directly
involved in the controversy in which said judgment
shall have been rendered .
REPEALS
Section Nine ( 9 ) . All Acts , whether general , lo-
cal , special , or private , or parts of such acts , in con-
flict with or which are inconsistent with the provi-
sions of this Act are hereby repealed .
DATE EFFECTIVE
Section Ten ( 10 ) . This Act shall take effect im-
mediately on its passage and approval by the Gov-
ernor ,
Approved February 21 , 1936 .

170 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
CHAPTER 57 .
AN ACT for the protection of Wild Turkey , Imported Pheasant
and Hungarian Patridge , amending and re -enacting Section
one thousand nine hundred thirty -eight a-six ( 1938a -6 ) , Ken-
tucky Statutes .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Chapter eighty -two ( 82 ) of the Acts of the
General Assembly of one thousand nine hundred
thirty -four ( 1934 ) , being Section one thousand nine
hundred thirty -eight a- 6 ( 1938a - 6 ) , Kentucky Stat-
utes , Carroll's one thousand nine hundred thirty-
four ( 1934 ) Supplement , be amended and re-
enacted , and when so amended and re -enacted shall
read as follows , to -wit :
No person shall catch , kill or pursue with such in-
tent , or have the same in his possession after it has
been caught or killed , any wild turkey, imported
pheasant and Hungarian partridge . Any person
violating this section , or any feature thereof, shall
be guilty of a misdemeanor and shall , upon convic-
tion be fined not less than fifteen dollars ($15.00 )
nor more than fifty dollars ( $50.00 ) for each offense .
Approved February 18 , 1936 .
CHAPTER 58 .
AN ACT to repeal , amend and re-enact Section one thousand
nine hundred thirty -eight a-five ( 1938a -5) , Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , pertaining to and fixing the time and manner for
hunting rabbits .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Section one thousand nine hundred thirty-
eight a-five ( 1938a -5 ) , Carroll's Kentucky Stat-
utes , be repealed , amended and re- enacted so that

CHAPTER 59 171
when repealed , amended and re -enacted said Sec-
tion shall read as follows .
Rabbits . No person shall between tenth ( 10th )
day of January and the twenty -third ( 23rd ) day of
November , both days inclusive , in any year , catch ,
kill , injure pursue with such intent, any rabbit ;
provided, however , anyone may catch rabbits with
dogs or in traps , upon their own property for their
own use , but said rabbits or parts thereof , cannot
be bartered or traded , or offered or exposed for
sale , barter or trade , between the dates above men-
tioned : Provided , however , that it shall be unlaw-
ful to catch or take by means of snares any rabbit
at any time . And it shall be unlawful to hunt with
carbide lights or other means used to blind animals ,
at night . Approved February 27, 1936 .
CHAPTER 59 .
AN ACT to repeal , amend and re -enact Section two thousand
eighty -seven ( 2087 ) of Carroll's Revised Statutes of Kentucky ,
one thousand nine hundred thirty (1930 ) Edition , relating to
heirs and devisees .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One . That Section two thousand
eighty- seven ( 2087 ) of the Carroll's Revised Stat-
utes of Kentucky , one thousand nine hundred thirty
( 1930 ) Edition , be and the same is hereby repealed ,
amended and re -enacted so when re -enacted , it
will read as follows :
When the heir or devisee shall alien , before suit
brought, the estate descended or revised , he shall
be liable for the value thereof , with legal interest
from the time of alienation, to the creditors of the
descendent or testator ; but the estate so alienated

172 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
shall not be liable to the creditors in the hands of a
bona fide purchaser for valuable consideration ,
unless action is instituted within twelve months
after the estate is devised or descended to subject
the same . Approved February 27 , 1936 .
CHAPTER 60.
AN ACT prohibiting the expenditure of public moneys on ac-
count of insurance against fire or tornado on State property ,
providing for the valuation of State property and the setting
of premiums thereon , for the establishment of an account
known as the State Fire and Tornado Insurance Fund , for
the crediting to such fund of premiums debited against the
accounts of agencies having control over public property , for
the reimbursement from such fund to such agencies of dam-
ages due to fire and tornado , for the proper investment of
moneys in such fund , for the inspection of public buildings ,
for reinsurance of excessive risks by such fund , for the ad-
ministration of such fund , fixing the duties of the Department
of Insurance , the State Treasurer , and other State officers ,
making needed appropriations , and for other purposes inci-
dental and complimentary to the above .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One . No officer or agent of the State ,
and no person , persons , department , or other
agency of the State having control or custody of
any buildings or other property belonging to or
under the control and use of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky , or of any department , institution , board,
or other agency of the Commonwealth of Kentucky ,
shall pay out any public moneys of funds on account
or any insurance against loss by fire or tor-
nado , intended to extend beyond July one ( 1 ) , one
thousand nine hundred thirty -six ( 1936 ) , or shall
in any manner contract for or incur any indebted-
ness against the State on account of any such in-

CHAPTER 60 173
surance upon any such public property . Provided ,
that contracts of insurance in force at the effective
date of this Act which cannot legally be canceled ,
and any insurance on property financed under a
statutory amortization plan are exempt from this
prohibition during the course of their effectiveness ,
and provided further , that the Department of In-
surance may reinsure public property in the manner
hereinafter set forth .
Section Two . Before July first following the
effective date of this Act , the Department of In-
surance shall make or cause to be made an accurate
survey , appraisal , and valuation of each public
building and all personal property belonging to or
under the control and use of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky , or any department or other agency
thereof, and in addition thereto shall certify the
value of such building and personal property to the
department or other agency of the State which has
the custody or control of said property belonging
to the State of Kentucky or agency thereof . Such
appraisal and valuation shall equal the amount
which the Department of Insurance deems necessary
to replace the said property at the time of the ap-
praisal , less actual depreciation .
Section Three . Before July first of each fol-
lowing year, the Department of Insurance shall
reconsider each such appraisal and valuation , and
shall certify any changes in value to the depart-
ment or other agency of the State which shall have
control or custody of said property .
Section Four . Before July first of each year
the Department of Insurance shall fix the rate
of premium upon each public building and upon
each class of personal property belonging to or
under the control and use of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky , or any department or other agency

174 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
thereof, which the Department of Insurance deems
to be the average rate charged by responsible in-
surance companies doing business in this State , for
insurance against damage by fire and tornado , upon
property of like kind and degree of exposure to
risk .
Section Five . Before July first of each year
the Department of Insurance shall certify to the
State Treasurer the premium to be charged against
each department or other agency of the State hav-
ing control or custody of property of the State
or agency thereof , which premium shall be at the
rates determined under the procedure of Section
four ( 4) of this Act , calculated upon ninety per cent
(90 % ) of the valuation of all such property , as de-
termined under the procedure of Section two ( 2 ) of
this Act .
Section Six . During each fiscal year, the State
Treasurer shall deduct from any funds which
may be in his hands and payable to the department
or other agency for the care and maintenance of
such public buildings or other property , an amount
equal to the premiums certified to him by the De-
partment of Insurance as chargeable against such
department or other agency . The amount so debit-
ed shall be credited by the State Treasurer to
an account which shall be kept by the State Treas-
urer and known as the State Fire and Tornado In-
surance Fund . Provided , that no premium shall be
charged on any single building including the con-
tents thereof , upon a valuation in excess of five
hundred thousand dollars ( $ 500,000.00 ) unless the
Department of Insurance shall have contracted for
reinsurance which limits the liability of the State
Fire and Tornado Insurance Fund to five hundred
thousand dollars ( $ 500,000.00 ) upon such building
and contents .

CHAPTER 60 175
Section Seven . In case any building or other
property belonging to the State or agency thereof
shall be damaged by fire or tornado , except as
herein otherwise provided , it shall be the duty of
the department or agency having control or cus-
tody over such property to certify within thirty
days such event to the Department of Insurance .
Upon such notification , or upon receipt of knowl-
edge in any manner of such event , the Department
of Insurance shall within thirty days ascertain and
fix the amount of such damage and shall file with
the State Treasurer a statement of the same . In
case the department or other agency having control
or custody of property so damaged shall disagree
with the estimate of damage so fixed , the Depart-
ment of Insurance and the said department or other
agency shall each appoint one member of a board
of appraisers , who in turn shall select a third mem-
ber of such board of appraisers . An award in writ-
ing , submitted by such board of appraisers to the
State Treasurer , shall determine the amount of
damage .
Section Eight . When the amount of damage
has been determined , the State Treasurer shall
debit the account of the State Fire and Tornado
Insurance Fund by such an amount , and shall credit
with an equal amount the account of the depart-
ment or other agency which shall have control or
custody of the State property damaged by fire or
tornado. Upon warrant from the State Auditor ,
the State Treasurer shall pay to such department
or other agency the amount so credited to it , for
the purpose of repairing such damage or recon-
structing or replacing such damaged property .
Provided , that no such debit , credit, or payment
made on account of the damage to any one building
and its contents , by any one fire or tornado , or

176 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
during any fiscal year , shall be in excess of five hun-
dred thousand dollars ( $ 500,000.00 ) , unless the De-
partment of Insurance shall have effected reinsur-
ance upon such buliding and its contents such as to
limit the liability of the State Fire and Tornado
Insurance Fund to five hundred thousand dollars
( $500,000.00 ) , and unless such excess over this
amount shall have actually been paid into the State
Fire and Tornado Insurance Fund by the reinsur-
ing company or companies .
Section Nine . If there shall not be in the
account of the State Fire and Tornado Insur-
ance Fund an amount sufficient to cover the damage
to any State building , buildings , or other property ,
the State Treasurer shall debit the said fund only
with the amount actually to its credit at the time ,
and shall continue , subject to prior claims , to debit
said fund by the amount of each ensuing credit to
such fund , until the total damage shall have been
covered . No payments shall be made on account
of such damage to any department or other agency
of the State in excess of the amount debited against
the said fund at the time of payment .
Section Ten . The State Treasurer , with the ap-
proval of every investment by the department of
Finance and Budgetary Control , shall invest the
amounts credited to the State Fire and Tornado
Insurance Fund from time to time in obligation of
the United States government , its agencies , and/ or
Kentucky cities of the first , second , third and fourth
classes . The securities purchased shall be kept by
the State Treasurer in the safety deposit vault of an
approved depository bank , approved for this pur-
pose by the Department of Finance and Budgetary
Control .
Section Eleven . All interest , earnings , profits ,
or other income from investments credited to

CHAPTER 60 177
the State Fire and Tornado Insurance Fund shall
be credited to such fund , and shall form part of the
moneys available to cover damages by fire and tor-
nado to State property .
Section Twelve . The Department of Insur-
ance and the State Treasurer are hereby author-
ized to employ such clerical , actuarial , inspectional,
appraisal , and other assistance , and to incur such
expense for supplies , for membership in the Ken-
tucky Actuarial Bureau , and for other purposes , as
may be necessary to carry out the purposes of this
Act , subject to approval in advance by the Depart-
ment of Finance and Budgetary Control . All such
expenses of the Department of Insurance and the
State Treasurer shall be debitable against the State
Fire and Tornado Insurance Fund , and shall be
payable on warrant by the State Auditor . Pro-
vided , that the total of such expenses during any
fiscal year shall not exceed ten per cent (10 %)
of the total receipts of the said fund during the
same fiscal year , and provided further , that if such
expenses are incurred at a time when there is not a
sufficient amount to the credit of the fund to pay
them , they shall constitute a prior claim to be paid
out of the first receipts of the fund thereafter be-
fore any damages on account of fire or tornado .
Section Thirteen . With the approval of the De-
partment of Finance and Budgetary Control , the
Department of Insurance may contract with any
responsible fire and tornado insurance or reinsur-
ance company authorized to do business in this
State to reinsure any building and contents thereof
which the total valuation has been fixed at over five
hundred thousand dollars ( $500,000.00 ) in such a
way as to limit the net liability of the State Fire and
Tornado Insurance Fund with respect to such build-
ing and the contents thereof to five hundred thous-

178 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
and dollars ( $ 500,000.00 ) . And the Department of
Insurance shall insure property financed under a
statutory amortization plan to the extent of the lien
indebtedness upon said property or to the extent of
the reasonable value of said property , whichever
is the lesser . The premium payable by reason of
such contract of reinsurance shall be debitable
against the State Fire and Tornado Insurance
Fund, and shall be payable on warrant from State
Auditor . Provided , however , that during the three
calendar years immediately following adoption of
this Act , and during any subsequent fiscal year, in
which the State Fire and Tornado Insurance Fund
amounts to less than Three Hundred Thousand
($300,000.00 ) Dollars , the Department of Insurance
with the approval of the Department of Finance
and Budgetary Control may reinsure in the manner
provided in this section any building and contents
thereof of which the total valuation has been fixed at
over Two Hundred Thousand ( $ 200,000.00 ) Dollars ,
in such way as to limit the net liability of the State
Fire and Tornado Insurance Fund with respect to
such building and the contents thereof to Two Hun-
dred Thousand ( $ 200,000.00 ) Dollars .
Section Fourteen . The Department of Insurance
shall annually cause an inspection to be made of
each building and contents thereof owned by the
State or agency thereof , for the purpose of de-
termining the unnecessary causes of fire hazard
therein , and shall make such recommendations as
it deems necessary to the department or other
agency having control or custody of such building
relative to the removal or correction of such un-
necessary hazard . Reasonable differences in the
premium chargeable against such department or
other agency on account of such building and the

CHAPTER 60 179
contents thereof may be made contingent upon com-
pliance with such recommendations .
Section Fifteen . If at the end of any fiscal year
the moneys and securities to the credit of the State
Fire and Tornado Insurance Fund shall equal or
exceed one million dollars ( $ 1,000,000.00 ) , the De-
partment of Insurance shall certify to the State
Treasurer a credit to each department or other
agency of the State having control or custody of
State property . Such credit shall operate to offset
and reduce premium to be debited against such de-
partment or other agency and credited to the State
Fire and Tornado Insurance Fund during the next
fiscal year . Such credit shall be calculated by taking
the total amount of the premiums as determined
under the procedure of Section four ( 4 ) of this Act ,
before and not in consideration of any credit or de-
duction from the same , by subtracting from such
premiums the total amount of the losses and damage
debited against the fund and subtracting also the
total amount of expenses debited against the said
fund as authorized under the Sections twelve ( 12 )
and thirteen (13 ) of this Act , and by dividing the
result by the total amount of premiums as deter-
mined under the procedure of Section four (4 ) of
this Act . Such proportionate credit shall be applied
equally against each premium debitable against
each department or other agency having control or
custody over State property and shall revert to the
account of the said department or other agency
without prejudice as to its use .
Section Sixteen . All policies whch have been con-
tracted by departments or other agences of the
State , insuring State buildings or other State prop-
erty against fire or tornado , except as herein other-
wise provided , shall he canceled by such depart-
ment or other agency as of July first following the

180 AOTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
effective date of this Act , or as soon thereafter as
such cancellation can lawfully be effected . In case
of policies which cannot lawfully be canceled by the
said date , the department or agency concerned shall
be relieved of obligation to be debited with the cor-
responding premium for the credit of the State Fire
and Tornado Insurance Fund , and said fund shall
be relieved of obligation to pay the corresponding
damages upon the property concerned , until such
time as such policies cease to be of effect .
Section Seventeen . There is hereby appropriated
from the General Expenditure Fund to the Depart-
ment of Insurance the sum of one thousand dollars
($1,000.00 ) to be used during the present fiscal year
in carrying out the duties imposed by this Act.
Section Eighteen . There is hereby appropriated
to the State Fire and Tornado Insurance Fund from
the General Expenditure Fund the sum of one hun-
dred thousand dollars ( $ 100,000.00 ) to assure safety
of operation while the former fund is being built
up from premiums and income from investments .
Approved February 20 , 1936 .
CHAPTER 61.
AN ACT to provide State Insurance for public buildings and
property , creating the State Insurance Commission , and de-
fining their powers and duties , and providing a penalty for
the violation, setting up the machinery for State Insurance ,
and creating an emergency insurance fund and providing the
investment thereof , for the purpose of meeting losses by fire ,
wind and explosion , and providing for reinsurance by said
Commission , and for such purposes incidental to the adminis-
tration thereof, and the acceptance by said Commission of
funds appropriated by the State , and repealing all laws in
conflict therewith .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One ( 1 ) . That after the passage and ap-

CHAPTER 61 181
proval of this Act no insurance of any kind shall be
taken upon any of the public buildings , furniture ,
fixtures or property of any kind whatever belong-
ing to the State , except in the manner herein pro-
vided ; except that this Act shall not apply to any
building , buildings or appurtenances thereto , which
have been , or may hereafter be , constructed or
acquired by the University of Kentucky , any of the
State Teachers Colleges , or other State educational
institutions , or the governing bodies thereof , from
funds obtained by loan and/or grant from the Pub-
lic Works Administraton or other agency of the
Federal Government as authorized and provided by
Chapter seventy -two ( 72 ) of the Acts of the General
Assembly of the one thousand nine hundred thirty-
four ( 1934 ) Regular Session , so long as any of said
bonds evidencing said indebtedness are outstanding,
but that said buildings and appurtenances thereto
shall be kept insured in insurance companies author-
ized to do business in the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky in an amount not in excess of the amount of
the existing indebtedness against said buildings .
Section Two ( 2 ) . That there is hereby created
the State Insurance Commission , consisting of the
Governor , Secretary of State , Auditor , State Treas-
urer , and Attorney General. The Governor shall
be chairman and the Treasurer shall be Secretary .
Section Three (3 ) . The amount of insurance to
be carried on all buildings and property owned by
the State , or any department thereof, as provided
herein , shall be fixed by the State Insurance Com-
mission, after consultation with the officials having
such buildings or property in their care or custody .
That the amount of insurance so carried shall in no
event exceed the actual value of the property to be
insured after reasonable deduction for depreciation .
Section Four (4 ) . That the value of all public

182 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
buildings hereafter built shall be the actual cost
thereof, and the insurance to be carried shall not
exceed three -fourths of such value .
Section Five ( 5 ) . That the State Insurance Com-
mission is hereby vested with the discretion and
authority to say or determine whether , under the
provisions hereof, any or all of the State's property
shall or not be insured and their determination shall
be final .
Section Six ( 6 ) . For carrying out the provisions
of this Act the Commission may employ such assist-
ants as are necessary and fix their compensation ,
together with the expenses of such assistants .
Section Seven (7) . A " State Insurance Fund "
is hereby created . All expenses of conducting the
work and carrying out the provisions of this Act
shall be paid out of the State Insurance Fund , upon
the certificate of the State Treasurer , audited by
the Auditor of Public Accounts .
Section Eight ( 8 ) . Within thirty days after the
passage and approval of this Act each officer , Board
of Control , Board of Trustees , Board of Regents ,
Agent or Agency of this State , having in charge any
public buildings or property of any kind whatsoever
belonging to the State , or any department thereof,
shall report to the State Insurance Commission ,
showing in said report , ( a ) each policy of insurance
which shall be then in force upon any property of
any kind belonging to the State , and the date issued ;
(b) the property covered by such policy or policies ;
(c) date of expiration of said policy ; (d ) rate of
such insurance ; and ( e ) the amount of premium
paid thereon , and whether any of said premium re-
mains unpaid and if so how much .
Section Nine ( 9 ) . The Commission shall then
first proceed to determine and fix the insurable value

CHAPTER 61 183
of each building , and item of property , belonging
to the State ; and shall next determine which build-
ings or items that need protection from fire, wind
or explosion or other insurance safety , and shall
say in writing in its permanent minutes , to be pro-
vided for the purpose of recording all business of
the Commission , what insurance , the amount , kind
and nature , should be carried , written , or provided
on each building or item of property , and the Com-
mission shall provide for said insurance coverage
or protection so determined and fixed on each build-
ing or item of public property , as herein authorized .
Section Ten ( 10 ) . The State Insurance Commis-
sion shall cause to be made a Biennial Report of its
activities to the General Assembly .
Section Eleven ( 11 ) . The State Insurance Com-
mission, herein created , shall have and it is hereby
vested with complete and final control and super-
vision of all insurance of every kind upon State
owned property of every kind , and it shall have the
authority to cancel , renew and in every way control
the said insurance of every kind upon property be-
longing to the State .
Section Twelve ( 12 ) . It is hereby made the duty
of the State Inspector and Examiner , and his as-
sistants , to render such assistance to the State In-
surance Commission as may be requested by it.
Section Thirteen ( 13 ) . The Commission shall
have the right , and it is hereby made its duty , to fix
a reasonable premium rate, not to exceed the rate
usually charged by responsible fire and tornado in-
surance companies , doing business in this State ,
and issuing policies upon property of similar kind
and exposed to risks in like manner , to be charged
for insurance and the premium rate so fixed shall
be paid upon the amount of insurance so determined ,
as herein set out , by the department or agency ,

184 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
having said property in charge , and the sum or
amounts of premiums thereof , on each building or
item of property , shall be paid into the State In-
surance Fund herein created . This fund shall be
used to pay expenses , fire , tornado and explosion
losses , and reinsurance , as herein provided . The
amount of premiums so paid in shall be held and
invested and shall constitute an insurance sinking
fund to be used as herein provided .
Section Fourteen ( 14 ) . Any sum or amount an-
nually appropriated by the General Assembly for
the purpose of creating , supplementing or aiding the
purpose of this Act shall be received and paid into
the State Insurance Fund , and shall be held , invested
and used as herein before provided .
Section Fifteen ( 15 ) . That when the insurance
fund herein provided for reaches the sum of five
hundred thousand dollars ( $ 500,000.00 ) , no further
premiums shall be paid until a part of such fund
has been used in the payment of losses , and in that
event the premiums of insurance shall be again
paid as herein provided until the fund again reaches
the same sum aforesaid .
Section Sixteen ( 16 ) . The State Insurance Com-
mission may reinsure upon terms which the Com-
mission may deem wise and expedient and most
advantageous in reliable insurance companies such
portion of their insurance liability as is commen-
surate with the principles of safe underwriting , and
as it may deem necessary and may prescribe such
rules and regulations as may be necessary or ex-
pedient in placing and handling this reinsurance ,
and the carrying out of the purposes of this Act .
Section Seventeen ( 17 ) . All monies paid into
the State Insurance Fund , as premiums on policies
of insurance , and all money received from interest
on loans and investments and from any and all other

CHAPTER 61 185
sources , connected with the insurance of public
property , as herein provided , shall be held by the
Commission as an insurance sinking fund for the
purpose of paying fire losses , expenses and rein-
surance costs . Provided no further premiums shall
be paid when the fund reaches the sum stated in
Section ( 15 ) , except as therein set out ; provided
further that no building or property insured or
that shall hereafter be insured or protected by the
fund herein created shall cease to pay premiums
until five ( 5 ) annual premiums shall have been paid ,
even though such payments may increase the fund
beyond said sum aforesaid .
Section Eighteen ( 18 ) . That in event of loss or
damage by fire or lightning the amount of such loss
or damage to be paid by the Commission shall be
determined by three appraisers , one to be named
by the Commission , one by the officer , official or
board, having the damaged or destroyed building
in charge , and the two so appointed , in the event of
disagreement , shall select a third . These appraisers
shall file their written report with the Commission .
Provided the funds on hand are sufficient , and in
their discretion , the amount of the loss or such por-
tion thereof as the Commission shall determine it is
able at the time to pay, shall be paid over to the
officer or board having the property in care and
custody , to be expended for repair , restoration or
rebuilding of the property damaged or destroyed .
If at the time of any such report or award of loss
or damage there shall not be in the State Insurance
Fund sufficient amount to pay said loss or damage ,
as reported to the Commission by the appraisers ,
the Commission shall pay such portion thereof as
it shall deem and determine the funds on hand will
warrant , and shall report to the General Assembly
for its consideration and action the facts with refer-

186 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
ence to the deficit , or balance necessary for replace-
ment .
Section Nineteen ( 19 ) . The State Insurance
Commission shall at all times keep the funds in its
hands invested in such securities as may be readily
sold for cash for the purpose of paying any loss
which may occur to any of the buildings or property
so insured or protected by this Act . It shall be
lawful for the Commission to invest the funds in its
hands in bonds or other interest bearing obligations
of the Government of the United States ; bonds or
State warrants , or other interest bearing obligations
of the State of Kentucky ; securities authorized by
the Congress , and known as the Federal Farm Loan
Act, and Federal Home Loan Bank , and Home
Owners Loan Corporation ; real estate , real estate
mortgage notes , bonds and other interest bearing
or dividend paying securities which would be re-
garded by prudent business men as safe investments
and to make loans with such securities as collateral .
And all interest collected on such investments shall
become a part of the State Insurance fund and held ,
used , reinvested and disbursed as herein provided .
Section Twenty ( 20 ) . That any officer or official
upon whom the duties provided in this Act devolves ,
who fails or refuses to carry out the provisions of
this Act shall be guilty of a misdemeanor , and upon
conviction thereof, shall be fined in a sum not less
than two hundred ( $ 200.00 ) dollars , nor more than
one thousand ( $ 1,000.00 ) dollars , or imprisonment
not less than fifty ( 50 ) nor more than one hundred
( 100 ) days .
Section Twenty -One ( 21 ) . All Acts and parts of
Acts inconsistent with the provisions of this Act
are hereby repealed .
Section Twenty - Two ( 22 ) . The State Treasurer
of Kentucky and Secretary of the Insurance Com-

CHAPTER 62 187
mission herein set up shall be custodian and trustee
of the monies and funds paid in to and belonging
and constituting a part of the State Insurance Fund .
He shall give additional bond to be set by the State
Insurance Commission and his surety to be approved
by said Commission .
Section Twenty -Three ( 23 ) . Nothing in this Act
shall be construed as lessening or altering the duties
of the State Insurance Department now set up with
reference to inspecting, investigating and pressing
incendiary fires under the present law of Kentucky .
Section Twenty -Four ( 24 ) . Should any section ,
paragraph , clause or line of this Act be construed
as being unconstitutional or invalid for any reason ,
this shall not effect the remainder of this Act .
Vetoed February 20 , 1936 .
CHAPTER 62 .
AN ACT relating to the regulation of road houses , eating and
drinking stands , and other places of public entertainment
lying outside of the limits of incorporated towns and cities :
and declaring an emergency to exist .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One . That no road house , eating place ,
drinking place , tourist camp , or place of public
entertainment at which people assemble to eat ,
drink , sing, dance , bathe , or engage in any game or
entertainment for either profit or amusement , where
intoxicating liquor is sold , shall be permitted to
operate in the Commonwealth of Kentucky outside
of the corporate limits of any incorporated city or
town unless its owner or proprietor shall have first
made application to the County Court in which it
is located for a permit to operate and shall have
procured from the Court such a permit , and the ap-

188 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
plication shall be addressed to the Court and it shall
state the location of the business , the name of the
owner and proprietor and the nature of the busi-
ness , and any permit granted by the Court shall be
by an order duly entered of record in the order book
of the Court , and it shall not extend beyond the first
day of the following January . The order shall
specifically state such facts as are required in the
application , and it shall provide for reasonable
hours of operation by a provision which shall be
uniform for places of business of like nature
throughout the county , and the Court may at any
time following its granting such permit revoke
same . Before revoking the permit the County At-
torney or the Court shall cause the hearing for the
revocation of the permit to be placed on the docket
of the County Court in the name of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky against the name of the owner
and proprietor stated in the permit , and the County
Attorney shall give the owner or proprietor named
in the permit five days ' notice in writing of the time ,
place and purpose of the hearing , provided the
notice may be executed by posting a copy of same
on either the front door or at a conspicuous place
on the premises in the event it shall appear by the
return of the officer serving the notice that he
visited the premises and failed to find at the prem-
ises the owner or proprietor named in the permit ,
and upon the trial of the cause , which shall be be-
fore the Court without the intervention of a jury ,
such orders as appear to the Court as right and
proper shall be entered . No application for a per-
mit shall be considered unless the same shall be
accompanied by a fee of five dollars ( $5.00 ) , pay-
able to the Treasurer of the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky, but no fees shall be collected in the event the
permit shall not be granted,

CHAPTER 62 189
Section Two . No person shall be granted a per-
mit who is not of good moral character or who will
not in the judgment of the Court obey the laws of
the Commonwealth of Kentucky in the carrying on
of the business , and any one or more of the follow-
ing reasons shall be sufficient grounds for revoking
the permit :
( One ) To suffer or permit drunken , disorderly
or boisterous persons , or persons of lewd and las-
civious reputation , to congregate in , near or about
the premises .
( Two ) To suffer or permit people to congregate
there for immoral or unlawful purposes and to
occupy any hidden or secreted room or place from
which the view of the public is obstructed ;
( Three ) To suffer or permit the premises to be
occupied or used as a place of assemblage or enter-
tainment at later hours than those stated in the
permit .
Section Two -A. Provided however that before
a permit is either granted or revoked the Court
must have the concurrence of the County Attorney
entered of record on the order books of the court .
Section Three . It shall be a misdemeanor for
any owner or proprietor coming within the purview
of this Act to suffer or permit his premises to be
used in the manner set out under either ( one ) ,
(two ) or (three ) , of the preceding section , and the
penalty on a conviction for such violation shall be
a fine of not exceeding two hundred dollars
($200.00 ) or imprisonment in the County Jail not
exceeding ninety days or both fined and imprison-
ment in the discretion of the Court or Jury trying
the case, and in the event of a conviction the judg-
ment shall include a revocation of the permit , and

190 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
the Quarterly Court and Circuit Court shall have
concurrent jurisdiction to try the charge .
Section Four . It shall be the duty of all peace
officers and County Patrolmen to visit regularly
such premises , and on their observing any violation
of this Act by the owner or proprietor or manager
or employee , they shall make arrests without war-
rants for violation committed within their presence .
Section Five . Nothing in this Act shall apply
to any private home nor to the entertainment of the
bona fide guests in a private home .
Section Six . From the judgment of the county
court the applicant for or the holder of a permit
may prosecute an appeal to the Circuit Court and
from that court to the Court of Appeals in the
manner now provide by law. From the judgment
of the county court the Commonwealth may prose-
cute an appeal to the Circuit Court and from that
court to the Court of Appeals in the manner now
provided by law.
Section Seven . Whereas , in many counties in
Kentucky numerous road houses are being operated
in such a manner as to constitute an uncontrollable
public nuisance under the law now in force and are
breeding places for crime and are resulting in
numerous law violations throughout the State , and
emergency is declared to exist and this Act shall
become effective upon its adoption by the General
Assembly and approved by the Governor .
Section Eight . This Act will not interfere with
places of business other than specified in Section
one of the printed bill and nothing in this Act shall
apply to filling stations , grocery stores , general
stores or other places of business which are not con-
ducted for entertainment purposes .
Approved February 27 , 1936 .

CHAPTER 65 .
AN ACT repealing Chapter 19 of the Acts of the Extraordinary
Session of the General Assembly of 1934 pertaining to pas-
senger automobiles and providing for registration fees of such
motor vehicles and amending and reenacting same and de-
claring an emergency to exist .

CHAPTER 65 195
-
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One 1. That Chapter 19 of the Acts
of the Extraordinary Session of the General As-
sembly of 1934 be repealed , amended and reenact-
ed so that when said Act is reenacted it shall read
as follows :
Automobiles primarily designed for carrying pas-
sengers and having provisions for not more than
seven passengers , including the operator , shall be
classed as passenger automobiles and the annual
registration fees for same shall be as follows :
For each passenger car having less than 22 horse-
power, Six Dollars ( $ 6.00 ) ; for each such car hav-
ing 22 horsepower and less than 32 horsepower ,
Seven Dollars and twenty cents ( $7.20 ) ; for each
such car having 32 horsepower or more , Twelve
Dollars ($ 12.00 ) , and " horsepower " within the
meaning of this Act shall be calculated on the rating
fixed by the Society of Automobile Engineers .
Section Two 2. The registration fee for each
motorcycle shall be Three Dollars ($ 3.00 ) , and for
each sidecar attached thereto , One Dollar and Fifty
Cents ($1.50 ) .
Section Three 3. Whereas the people of Ken-
tucky have expressed themselves as predomi-
nately in favor of the repeal of the " gross receipts
Act of 1934 " and whereas Chapter 19 of the Acts
of the Extraordinary Session of the General As-
sembly of 1934 was enacted by reason of the enact-
ment of the said gross receipts Act of 1934 and
whereas said gross receipts Act and Chapter 19 of
the Acts of the Extraordinary Session of 1934 con-
stitutes an unfair and unreasonable burden on cer-
tain classes of taxpayers, an emergency is hereby
declared to exist and this Act shall become effective

196 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
immediately upon its passage and approval by the
Governor . Approved January 24 , 1936 .
CHAPTER 66.
AN ACT defining passenger automobiles and providing for the
registration fees of such motor vehicles and for motorcycles
and side car attachments , and repealing all laws or parts of
laws in conflict herewith and repealing so much of the Act
of the General Assembly of one thousand nine hundred
thirty -six (1936 ) heretofore enacted as fixes the amount of
said registration fees .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One . Automobiles primarily designed
for carrying passengers and having provisions for
not more than seven (7 ) passengers including the
operator , shall be classed as passenger automobiles ,
and the annual registration fee for the same shall
be four dollars and fifty cents ($4.50 ) per year, and
the County Clerk who issues said registration fee
shall receive the fee now allowed by law .
Section Two . The registration fee for each
motorcycle shall be two dollars ( $ 2.00 ) per year
and for each side car attachment thereto , one dol-
lar ($1.00 ) per year.
Section Three . All laws or parts of laws in
conflict herewith are hereby repealed and so much
of the Act of the General Assembly of one thou-
sand nine hundred thirty- six (1936 ) heretofore
adopted , which fixes the amount of the registration
fees of said automobiles or motorcycles or sidecars
is hereby repealed .
Section Four . This law shall not become ef-
fective until January first ( 1st ) , one thousand nine
hundred thirty - seven ( 1937 ) .
Approved February 25 , 1936 .

thousand nine hundred thirty ( 1930 ) Edition , relating to the
registration of automobiles and trucks and providing for the
change of the registration day from the first day of January
of each year to the first day of March of each year .

Commonwealth of Kentucky :

thirty -nine g-2 ( 2739g -2 ) ( A ) be amended by strik-
ing therefrom the words " thereafter he shall regis-

uary of each and every year " and adding in lieu

CHAPTER 70.
AN ACT to promote safe driving and to remove the reckless
and financially irresponsible drivers from the highways ; to
provide regulations therefor and providing for suspension of

CHAPTER 70 203
operators ' license and registration certificate for certain
offenses ; providing for operator to offer proof of ability to
respond in damages as condition precedent to future licensing
or registration , making the provisions of this Act applicable
to non -residents ; providing for suspension of operator's
license upon failure to satisfy judgment ; providing for the
acceptance of insurance carrier's certificate as proof of ability
to respond in damages or a bond with good and valuable
surety as proof of such ability ; providing for the court pro-
cedure in regard to the regulations herein provided ; pro-
viding for the essential requisites to be written in each motor
vehicle liability policy issued in the Commonwealth and pro-
viding for the penalties for violation of this Act .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Definitions .-Section One . The following words ,
as used in this Act , shall have the following mean-
ings :
(a) Commissioner .- Shall mean the Commission-
er of Motor Transportation of this State or other
officer in charge of the issuance of motor vehicle
operators ' licenses , chauffeurs ' licenses , and regis-
tration certificates .
(b ) Person .-Every natural person , firm , copart-
nership , association , or corporation .
(c) Operator .- Every person , other than a chauf-
feur , who is in actual control of a motor vehicle upon
a highway .
(d ) Chauffeur .-Every person who is employed
for the principal purpose of operating a motor ve-
hicle and every person who drives a motor vehicle
while in use as a public or common carrier of per-
sons or property .
( e ) Owner.-A person who holds the legal title of
a vehicle or in the event a vehicle is the subject of
an agreement for the conditional sale or lease there-
of with the right of purchase upon performance of

204 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
the conditions stated in the agreement and with an
immediate right of possession vested in the condi-
tional vendee or lessee, or in the event a mortgagor
of a vehicle is entitled to possesion , then such con-
ditional vendee or lessee or mortgagor shall be
deemed the owner for the purpose of this Act .
(f) Nonresident .-Every person who is not a resi-
dent of this State .
(g) Motor Vehicle .- Every vehicle propolled by
an internal -combustion engine or by electricity or
steam , except traction engines , road rollers , and ve-
hicles propelled only upon rails and tracks .
(h) Highway .-The entire width between proper-
ty lines of every way or place of whatever nature
when any part thereof is open to the use of the pub-
lic , as a matter of right , for purposes of vehicular
traffic .
(i) State .- Any State of the United States , the
District of Columbia , or any province of the Do-
minion of Canada .
Section Two . This Act shall in no respect be con-
sidered as a repeal of any of the provisions of the
State Motor Vehicle Code or Laws , but shall be
construed as supplemental thereto .
SUSPENSION OF OPERATOR'S LICENSE AND REGISTRA-
TION CERTIFICATE FOR CERTAIN OFFENSES
Section Three. (a ) The motor vehicle operator's
license , chauffeur's license , and all of the rigstra-
tion certificates of any person shall be suspended
forthwith without notice or hearing by the Commis-
sioner whenever such person shall by final order or
judgment have been convicted of or shall have plead-
ed guilty to or shall have forfeited any bail or col-
lateral deposited to secure his appearance for trial
as defendant (where such forfeiture shall not have
been vacated ) for any offense hereafter committed

CHAPTER 70 205
which requires suspension or revocation of the
licenses of such person in this State , or any offense
in any other State which , if committed in this State ,
would require suspension or revocation of the
licenses of such person in this State .
PROOF OF ABILITY TO RESPOND IN DAMAGES CONDITION
PRECEDENT TO FUTURE LICENSING OR REGISTRATION
(b) Such operator's license , chauffeur's license ,
and registration certificates shall remain suspended
and shall not at any time thereafter be renewed , nor
shall any such license be thereafter issued to such
person [ including any such person not previously
licensed who shall by final order or judgment
have been convicted of , pleaded guilty to or
forfeited any bail or collateral deposited to secure
his appearance for trial (where such forfeiture
shall not have been vacated ) , for any such offense
or for operating a motor vehicle upon the highways
without being licensed to do so , or for operating an
unregistered motor vehicle upon the highways ] , nor
shall any motor vehicle be thereafter registered in
the name of such person until he shall have given
proof of his ability to respond in damages for any
liability thereafter incurred , resulting from the
ownership , maintenance , use or operation thereafter
of a motor vehicle , for bodily injury to or death of
any one person in the amount of at least Five Thou-
sand Dollars ( $ 5,000 ) , and , subject to the aforesaid
limit for any one person injured or killed , of at
least Ten Thousand Dollars ( $ 10,000 ) for bodily in-
jury to or death of two or more persons in any one
accident , and for damage to property in the amount
of at least One Thousand Dollars ( $ 1,000 ) resulting
from any one accident . Such proof in said amounts
shall be furnished for each motor vehicle registered
by such person.

206 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLŸ
APPLICABLE TO NON - RESIDENTS
(c ) If such person shall not be a resident of this
State , he shall not operate any motor vehicle in this
State nor shall any motor vehicle owned by him be
operated within this State by any person , and no
operator's or chauffeur's license shall be isued to
such person and no motor vehicle shall be registered
in his name until he shall have given proof of his
abilty to respond in damages as herein required .
CERTIFIED COPIES OF COURT RECORD TO BE FURNISHED
(d ) It shall be the duty of the clerk of the court ,
or of the judge of a court which has no clerk , in
which any such judgment or order is rendered or
other such action taken , to forward immediately to
the Commissioner a certified copy of such judgment,
order or record of other action of the court which
shall be prima facie evidence of the conviction , plea
or other action therein stated . In the event that the
person so shown to have been convicted , pleaded
guilty or forfeited bail or securities appears to be
a non -resident of this State , the Commisioner shall
transmit a certified copy of the judgment to the of-
ficial in charge of the issuance of operator's licenses
and registration certificates of the State of which
the defendant is a resident .
OPERATOR OR CHAUFFEUR RELIEVED OF NECESSITY OF
GIVING PROOF IN OWN BEHALF , UNDER
CERTAIN CONDITIONS
( e ) If it shall be duly established to the satisfac-
tion of the Commissioner ( one ) that any person ,
whether a resident or non -resident of , this State ,
who shall have been convicted , pleaded guilty or
forfeited bail or securities , as aforesaid , was ,
upon the occasion of the offense upon which
such conviction , plea or forfeiture was based ,

CHAPTER 70 207
a chauffeur or motor vehicle operator , however
designated , in the employ of an owner of a
motor vehicle or a member of the immediate
family or household of an owner of a motor
vehicle , and (two ) that there was not , at the time
of such offense or subsequent thereto up to the date
of such finding any motor vehicle registered in this
State , ( or if a non - resident , in the State of his resi-
dence ) in the name of the person who has been con-
victed , pleaded guilty or forfeited bail or collateral ,
as aforesaid , then and in that event , if the person
in whose name such motor vehicle is registered shall
give proof of ability to respond in damages accord-
ing to the provisions of this Act , which proof the
Commissioner shall accept , such chauffeur or other
person , as aforesaid , shall be relieved of the neces-
sity of giving such proof in his own behalf so long
as such chauffeur or other person is operating a
motor vehicle for which the owner has given proof
of his ability to respond in damages .
SUSPENSION OF OPERATOR'S LICENSE AND REGISTRA-
TION CERTIFICATE UPON FAILURE TO SATISFY
JUDGMENT
Section Four . (a ) The operator's license , chauf-
feur's license , and all of the registration certificates
of any person shall ( except as provided in Section
5 ) be forthwith suspended by the Commissioner
upon receiving from the court in which rendered a
certificate , in the form prescribed by the Commis-
sioner , showing that such person failed to satisfy
within thirty ( 30 ) days any judgment which shall
have become final by expiration without appeal with-
in the time in which appeal might have been per-
fected or by final affirmance on appeal , rendered
against him by a court of competent jurisdiction in
this State or in any other State , or in any District
Court of the United States , for damages on account

208 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
of bodily injury, including death , or damage to
property resulting from the ownership , maintenance
use or operation hereafter of a motor vehicle .
SATISFACTION OF JUDGMENT AND PROOF OF ABILITY TO
RESPOND IN DAMAGES CONDITION PRECEDENT TO
FUTURE LICENSING OR REGISTRATION
(b) Such operator's license , chauffeur's license ,
and registration certificates shall (except as pro-
vided in Section 5 ) remain so suspended and shall
not be renewed , or shall any such license be issued
to such person nor shall any motor vehicle be there-
after registered in the name of such person ( includ-
ing any such person not previously licensed ) while
any such judgment remains unstayed , unsatisfied
and subsisting nor until every such judgment is sat-
isfied or discharged , except that a discharge in bank-
ruptcy shall not be deemed a satisfaction of such
judgment, and until the said person gives proof of
his ability to respond in damages as required in
Section Three ( 3 ) of this Act , for future accidents .
If after such proof has been given, any other such
judgment shall be recovered against such person
resulting from an event occurring before such proof
was given but after this Act shall take effect , such
licenses and certificates shall again be and remain
suspended , and no other such license or certificates
shall be issued to such person while any such judg-
ment remains unsatisfied and subsisting , as afore-
said .
APPLICABLE TO NON - RESIDENTS
( c) If such person who has failed to satisfy with-
in thirty ( 30 ) days any final judgment , as aforesaid ,
shall not be a resident of this State , he shall not op-
erate any motor vehicle in this State , nor shall any
motor vehicle owned by him be operated in this State
by any person , nor shall any operator's or chauf-
<
"

CHAPTER 70 209
feur's license be issued to such person or any motor
vehicle registered in his name , until every such judg-
ment shall be stayed , satisfied or discharged as here-
in provided , and until such person shall have given
proof of his ability to respond in damages for future
accidents as required in Section Three ( 3 ) of this
Act .
CERTIFIED COPIES OF JUDGMENT TO BE FURNISHED
(d ) It shall be the duty of the clerk of the court ,
or of the judge of a court which has no clerk, in
which any such judgment is rendered , to forward to
the Commissioner immediately after the expiration
of said thirty ( 30 ) days , a certified copy of such
judgment . If the defendant is a non -resident , it
shall be the duty of the Commissioner to transmit a
certified copy of the judgment to the official in charge
of the issuance of opertor's licenses and registration
certificates of the State of which the defendant is a
resident .
PAYMENTS SUFFICIENT TO SATISFY REQUIREMENTS OF
Аст
( e ) Provided , however , anything in this Act to
the contrary notwithstanding , that
( 1 ) When five thousand dollars ( $5,000 ) has been
credited upon any judgment or judgments rendered
in excess of that amount for or on account of bodily
injury to or the death of one person as the result of
any one accident ; or
(Two ) When , subject to the limit of five thousand
dollars ($5,000 ) for any one person so injured or
killed , the sum of ten thousand dollars ( $10,000 )
has been credited upon any judgment or judgments
rendered in excess of that amount for or on account
of bodily injury to or the death of more than one
person as the result of any one accident ; or

210 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
( Three ) When one thousand dollars ( $ 1,000 ) has
been credited upon any judgment or judgments
rendered in excess of that amount for damage to
property as the result of any one accident ; resulting
from the ownership , maintenance , use or operation
of a motor vehicle , credit for such amounts shall be
deemed a satisfaction of such judgment or judg-
ments for the purposes of this Section only.
LICENSE OR REGISTRATION MAY BE RESTORED UPON
PAYMENT OF JUDGMENT IN INSTALLMENTS
Section Five . A judgment debtor to whom this
Act applies may , for the sole purpose of giving au-
thority to the Commissioner to authorize the judg-
ment debtor to operate a motor vehicle thereafter ,
on due notice to the judgment creditor , apply to the
court in which the trial judgment was obtained for
the privilege of paying such judgment in install-
ments , and the court , in its discretion and without
prejudice to any other legal remedies which the judg-
ment creditor may have , may so order , fixing the
amounts and times of payment of the installments .
While the judgment debtor is not in default in pay-
ment of such installments , the Commisioner, upon
his giving proof of ability to respond in damages
for future accidents , as hereinbefore provided , may ,
in his discretion , restore or refrain from suspending
his license and registration certificates ; but such
license and certificates shall be suspended as herein-
before provided if and when the Commissioner is
satisfied that the judgment debtor has failed to com-
ply with the terms of the court order .
ESTABLISHMENT OF PROOF OF ABILITY TO RESPOND
IN DAMAGES
Section Six . Proof of ability to respond in dam-
ages , when required under this Act , may be evi-
denced by any of the following :

212 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
as aforesaid , of any insurance carrier authorized
to transact business in the State in which the motor
