in the State in which the insured resides , shall
be accepted if such carrier shall ( one ) execute
a power of attorney authorizing the Commis-
sioner to accept service of notice or process in
any action arising out of a motor vehicle accident
in this State , and ( two ) duly adopt a resolution pro-
viding that its policies shall be deemed to be varied
to comply with the law of this State relating to the
terms of motor vehicle liability policies issued there-
in , and ( three ) agree to accept as final and binding
any final judgment duly rendered in any action aris-
ing out of a motor vehicle accident in any court of
competent jurisdiction in this State . If any foreign
insurance carrier which has qualified to furnish
proof of ability to respond in damages as herein-
before required defaults in any of said undertakings
or agreements , the Commissioner shall not thereafter
accept any certificate of said carrier , whether there-
tofore filed or thereafter tendered , as proof of abil-
ity to respond in damages so long as such default
continues .
COMMISSIONER TO BE NOTIFIED OF CANCELLATION
(c) When an insurance carrier has certified a mo-
tor vehicle liability policy under this Act , it shall
give ten ( 10 ) days ' written notice to the Commis-
sioner before cancellation of such policy and the
policy shall continue in full force and effect until
the date of cancellation specified in such notice , un-
less it expires before that date .
BOND AS PROOF
(d) The bond of a surety company , duly author-
ized to transact business within the State , or a bond ,

CHAPTER 70 213
with at least two ( 2 ) individual sureties , each own-
ing real estate within this State , which real estate
shall be scheduled in the bond approved by a judge
of a court of record which said bond shall be con-
ditioned for payment of the amounts specified in
Section (three ) of this Act ; and such bond shall
be filed with the Commisioner and shall not be can-
cellable except after ten ( 10 ) days ' written notice
to the Commissioner . Such bond shall constitute a
lien in favor of the State upon the real estate so
scheduled of any surety , which lien shall exist in
favor of any holder of a final judgment against the
person who has filed such proof on account of dam-
age to property or injury to , including death of a
person or persons resulting from the ownership ,
maintenance , use or operation hereafter of a motor
vehicle , upon the filing of notice to that effect by the
Commissioner in the office of the county clerk of the
county where such real estate shall be located .
One ( 1 ) The clerk shall record said bond or lien
in a book to be kept for that purpose and styled ,
"Motor Vehicle Responsibility Liens " and shall re-
ceive for his services a fee of one dollar ($ 1.00 ) , the
same to be paid by the person furnishing said bond .
MONEY OR SECURITIES AS PROOF
(e) The certificate of the State Treasurer that
the person named therein has deposited with him
a sum of money or securities approved by him in
the amounts as specified in Section three of this Act .
The State Treasurer shall accept any such deposits
and issue a certificate therefor , and the Commis-
sioner shall accept such certificate if accompanied
by evidence that there are no unsatisfied judgments
against the depositor registered in the office of the
county clerk of the county where the depositor re-
sides .

214 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
WHEN COMMISSIONER MAY REQUIRE OTHER EVIDENCE
(f) Whenever any evidence of proof of ability to
respond in damages filed under the provisions of
this Act no longer fulfills the purposes for which re-
quired , the Commissioner shall , for the purpose of
this Act, require other evidence of ability to re-
spond in damages as required by this Act , and shall
suspend the operator's license , chauffeur's license ,
and registrtation certificates , pending such proof .
RIGHTS OF A JUDGMENT CREDITOR AGAINST BOND ,
MONEY OR SECURITIES DEPOSITED WITH
THE TREASURER
Section Seven . A bond , money or securities filed
or deposited by or on behalf of any person under the
provisions of the preceding section , shall be held by
the Commissioner or said Treasurer to satisfy , in
accordance with the provisions of this Act , any exe-
cution issued against such person on a judgment for
damages as aforesaid arising out of the ownership ,
maintenance , use or operation of a motor vehicle as
aforesaid . Money or securities so deposited shal
not be subject to attachment of execution unless such
attachment or execution shall arise out of a suit for
damages as aforesaid . If such a judgment ren-
dered against the principal on the surety company
or real estate individual bond given under the pro-
visions of this Act shall not be satisfied within thirty
(30 ) days after it has become final as herein before
provided , the judgment creditor may, for his own
use and benefit and at his sole expense , bring an
action or actions in the name of the State against
the company or persons executing such bond , in-
cluding an action proceeding to foreclose any lien
that may exist upon the real estate of a person who
has executed such bond .

CHAPTER 70 215
FURNISHING OF OPERATOR'S RECORD BY THE
COMMISSIONER
Section Eight . The Commissioner shall upon re-
quest furnish any person a certified abstract of the
operating record of any person subject to the pro-
visions of this Act , which abstract shall fully desig-
nate the motor vehicles (if any ) registered in the
name of such person , and if there shall be no record
of any conviction of such person of violating any
law relating to the operating of a motor vehicle or of
any judgment rendered against such person , the
Commissioner shall so certify and shall collect for
each such certificate the sum of one dollar .
FURNISHING OF INFORMATION OF ABILITY TO RESPOND
IN DAMAGES
Section Nine . The Commissioner shall furnish
any person who may have been injured in person
or property by any motor vehicle , upon written re-
quest , with all information of record in his office per-
taining to the evidence of the ability of any opera-
tor or owner of any motor vehicle to respond in dam-
ages . The records provided for in Sections eight ( 8 )
and nine ( 9 ) shall not be admissible as evidence in
any action for damages or criminal proceedings
arising out of a motor vehicle accident .
LICENSE , REGISTRATION CERTIFICATE AND NUMBER
PLATES TO BE RETURNED TO COMMISSIONER
Section Ten . Any operator or any owner , whose
operator's or chauffeur's license or certificate of
registration shall have been suspended as herein
provided , or whose policy of insurance or surety
bond when same is required under this Act , shall
have been cancelled or terminated , or who shall neg-
lect to furnish other evidence of ability to respond
in damages upon request of the Commissioner shall

216 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
immediately return to the Commissioner his opera-
tor's or chauffeur's license , certificate of registra-
tion and the number plates issued thereunder . If
any person shall wilfully fail to return to the Com-
missioner the operator's or chauffeur's license , cer-
tificate or certificates of registration and the number
plates issued thereunder as provided herein, the
Commissioner, the operator's or chauffeur's license ,
certificate or certificates of registration and the num-
ber plates issued thereunder as provided herein ,
the Commisioner shall forthwith direct any peace
officer to secure possession thereof and to return the
same to the office of the Commissioner . Any person
wilfully failing to return such operator's or chauf-
feur's license or such certificate or certificates and
number plates shall be guilty of a misdemeanor and
upon conviction shall be fined not less than One
Hundred Dollars ( $ 100.00 ) , nor more than One
Thousand Dollars ( $ 1,000.00 ) , or imprisoned in the
county jail for not to exceed ninety (90 ) days , and
such penalty shall be in addition to any penalty im-
posed for any violation of the Motor Vehicle Laws
of this State.
PERIOD DURING WHICH PROOF OF ABILITY TO RESPOND
IN DAMAGES MUST BE MAINTAINED
Section Eleven . The Commissioner shall upon
request cancel any bond or return any certificate of
insurance , or the Commissioner shall direct and the
State Treasurer shall return to the person entitled
thereto any money or securities deposited pursuant
to this Act as proof of ability to respond in dam-
ages , or waive the requirement of filing proof of
ability to respond in damages in any of the follow-
ing events :
(a) At any time after three ( 3 ) years from the
date such proof was required when during the

CHAPTER 70 217
three (3 ) -year period preceding the request the per-
son furnishing such proof has not been convicted of
any offense referred to in Section two ( 2 ) of this
Act ; or
(b) In the event of the death of the person on
whose behalf such proof was filed , or the permanent
incapacity of such person to operate a motor ve-
hicle ; or
( c ) In the event the person who has given proof
of financial responsibility surrenders his operator's
or chauffeur's license , registration certificates , and
registration plates to the Commissioner , but the
Commissioner shall not release such proof in the
event any action for damages upon a liability re-
ferred to in this Act is then pending or any judg-
ment upon any such liability then outstanding and
unsatisfied or in the event the Commisisoner has re-
ceived notice that such person has within the period
of three ( 3 ) months immediately preceding been in-
volved as a driver in any motor vehicle accident . An
affidavit of the applicant of the nonexistence of such
facts shall be sufficient evidence thereof in the ab-
sence of evidence to the contrary in the records of
the department .
Whenever any person to whom proof has been
surrendered , as provided in the foregoing para-
graph , applies for an operator's or chauffeur's
li-
cense or the registration of

or

ofinsurance
,
orthe Commis-
sioner shall direct and the said Treasurer shall re-

218 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
turn any money or securities to the person entitled
thereto , upon the acceptance and substitution of
other adequate proof of ability to respond in dam-
ages pursuant to this Act.
PROHIBITING TRANSFER OF REGISTRATION TO DEFEAT
PURPOSE OF THIS ACT
Section Twelve . If an owner's certificate of regis-
tration has been suspended under the provisions of
this Act, such certificate shall not be transferred nor
the motor vehicle in respect of which such certifi-
cate was issued , registered in another name , where
the Commissioner has reasonable grounds to believe
that such transfer or registration is proposed for the
purpose or will have the effect of defeating the pur-
pose of this Act . Provided , however , that such
transfer of registration shall be permitted upon the
furnishing of proof of ability to respond in damages
to the Commissioner by such transferee whenever
the Commissioner shall deem it necessary in further-
ance of the purposes of this Section .
ACT NOT APPLICABLE TO CERTAIN POLICIES OF
AUTOMOBILE INSURANCE
Section Thirteen . Nothing in this Act contained
shall be held to apply to or affect policies of auto-
mobile insurance against liability which may now or
hereafter be required by any other law of this State ,
and such policies , if endorsed to conform to the re-
quirements of this Act shall be accepted as proof of
ability to respond in damages when required under
this Act.
PENALTIES
Section Fourteen . Any person who shall forge ,
or without authority sign any evidence of ability
to respond in damages as required by the Commis-
sioner in the administration of this Act and any

CHAPTER 70 219
person who shall violate any provisions of this Act
for which no penalty is otherwise provided , shall be
fined not less than One Hundred Dollars ( $ 100.00 )
nor more than One Thousand Dollars ( $1,000.00 ) ,
or imprisoned not more than ninety ( 90 ) days or
both .
OPERATING WITHOUT GIVING PROOF WHEN PROOF
REQUIRED
Section Fifteen . Any person whose operator's
or chauffeur's license or registration card or other
privilege to operate a motor vehicle has been sus-
pended or revoked and restoration thereof or is-
suance of a new license or registration is contingent
upon the furnishing of proof of ability to respond in
damages and who during such suspension or revoca-
tion or in the absence of full authorization from the
Commissioner drives any motor vehicle upon any
highway or knowingly permits any motor vehicle
owned by such person to be operated by another
upon any highway except as permitted hereunder
shall be punished by a fine of not more than five
hundred dollars ( $ 500 ) or by imprisonment not ex-
ceeding six ( 6 ) months or by both such fine and im-
prisonment .
RESTRICTIONS IN OPERATING MOTOR VEHICLES WHEN
CERTAIN TYPE OF POLICY ISSUED
Section Sixteen . (a ) When a certificate is filed
showing that a policy or policies have been issued
covering all motor vehicles owned by the insured but
not insuring such person when operating any motor
vehicle not owned by him it shall be unlawful for
such person to operate any motor vehicle not owned
by him or not covered by such certificate . In such
event the Commissioner shall designate the above
restriction upon the operator's or chauffeur's license
of such person.

220 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
(b) In the event the owner of a motor vehicle or
motor vehicles desires to be relieved of the fore-
going restriction and to be permitted to drive any
other motor vehicle he may have such restrictions
removed upon filing a certificate showing that there
has been issued to him a policy of insurance insur-
ing him as insured against liability imposed by law
upon such insured for bodily injury to or death of
any person or damage to property to the amounts
and limits as provided under Section seventeen of
this Act with respect to any motor vehicle operated
by him and which otherwise complies with the re-
quirements of this Act with respect to such type of
policy . Such policy is hereinafter referred to as
operator's policy .
(c) When the person required to give proof of
ability to respond in damages is not the owner of
a motor vehicle then an operator's policy of the
type and coverage described in the preceding para-
graph shall be sufficient under this Act.
MOTOR VEHICLE LIABILITY POLICY
Section Seventeen . (a ) A motor vehicle liability
policy as said term is used in this Act shall mean a
policy of liability insurance issued by an insuranc
carrier authorized to transact business in this State
to or for the benefit of the person named therein as
insured which policy shall meet the following re-
quirements :
One . Said policy shall designate by explicit de-
scription or by appropriate reference all motor ve-
hicles with respect to which coverage is thereby in-
tended to be granted .
Two . Said policy shall insure the person named
therein and any other person using or responsible
for the use of said motor vehicle or motor vehicles

CHAPTER 70 221
with the express or implied permission of said in-
sured .
Three . Said policy shall insure every said per-
son on account of the maintenance use or operation
of said motor vehicle or motor vehicles within the
continental limits of the United States or the Do-
minion of Canada against loss from the liability im-
posed by law arising from such maintenance , use
or operation to the extent and aggregate amount , ex-
clusive of interest and costs , with respect to each
such motor vehicle , of five thousand dollars ( $ 5,000 )
for bodily injury to or death of one person as a re-
sult of any one accident and , subject to said limit as
to one person , the amount of ten thousand dollars
( $ 10,000 ) for bodily injury to or death of all persons
as a result of any one accident and the amount of one
thousand dollars ( $ 1,000 ) for damage to property of
others as a result of any one accident .
(b) When an operator's policy is required it shall
insure the person named therein as insured against
the liability imposed by law upon the insured for
bodily injury to or death of any person or damage
to property to the amounts and limits above set
forth and growing out of the use or operation by the
insured within the continental limits of the United
States or the Dominion of Canada of any motor ve-
hicle not owned by him.
( c ) Any liability policy or policies issued here-
under need not cover any liability of the insured as-
sumed by or imposed upon said insured under any
workman's compensation law nor any liability for
damage to property in charge of the insured's em-
ployees .
(d) Any such policy may , however , grant any law-
ful coverage in excess of or in addition to the cover-
age herein specified or contain any agreements , pro-

222 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
visions or stipulations not in conflict with the provi-
sions of this Act and not otherwise contrary to law .
( e ) Any motor vehicle liability policy which by
endorsement contains the provisions required here-
under shall be sufficient proof of ability to respond
in damages .
(f) The department may accept several policies of
one or more such carriers which together meet the
requirements of this Section .
(g) Any binder pending the issuance of any pol-
icy , which binder contains or by reference includes
the provisions hereunder shall be sufficient proof of
ability to respond in damages .
ADDITIONAL REQUISITES OF MOTOR VEHICLE LIABILITY
POLICY
Section Eighteen . No motor vehicle liability
policy or operator's policy shall be accepted as proof
of ability to respond in damages hereunder unless
and until all of the following requirements of this
Section shall be complied with .
( a ) Any said form of policy shall specify the
name , address and business , if any , of the insured ,
the coverage afforded by the policy , the premium.
charged therefor , the policy period , and the limits
of liability and shall contain an agreement that the
insurance thereunder is provided in accordance
with the coverage defined in this Act as respects
bodily injury and death or property damage or both
and is subject to all the provisions of this Act .
(b) Every motor vehicle liability policy and every
operator's policy accepted as proof under this Act
shall be subject to the following provisions whether
or not contained therein .
One . The liability of the insurance carrier under
any such policy shall become absolute whenever loss

CHAPTER 70 223
or damage covered by such policy occurs and the
satisfaction by the insured of a final judgment for
such loss or damage shall not be a condition pre-
cedent to the right or obligation of the carrier to
make payment on account of such loss or damage .
Two . The insurance carrier shall , however , have
the right to settle any claim covered by the policy ,
and if such settlement is made in good faith , the
amount thereof shall be deductible from the limits
of liability specified in the policy .
Three . No such policy shall be cancelled or an-
nulled as respects any loss or damage by any agree-
ment between the carrier and the insured after the
said insured has become responsible for such loss
or damage and any such cancellation or annulment
shall be void .
Four . The policy may provide that the insured ,
or any other person covered by the policy , shall re-
imburse the insurance carrier for payment made on
account of any loss or damage claim or suit involv-
ing a breach of the terms , provisions , or conditions
of the policy ; and further , if the policy shall provide
for limits in excess of the limits specified in this Act ,
the insurance carrier may plead against any plain-
tiff , with respect to the amount of such excess limits
of liability , any defenses which it may be entitled to
plead against the insured , and any such policy may
further provide for the pro -rating of the insurance
thereunder with other applicable valid and collectible
insurance .
Five . The policy , the written application , there-
for, if any, and any rider or endorsement which does
not conflict with the provisions of this Act shall con-
stitute the entire contract between the parties .
WHEN INSURANCE CARRIER TO ISSUE CERTIFICATE
Section Nineteen . An insurance carrier who has

224 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
issued a motor vehicle liability policy or policies or
an operators ' policy meeting the requirements of
this Act shall upon request of the insured therein
deliver to the insured for filing , or at the request
of the insured shall file direct with the Commission-
er, an appropriate certificate showing that such pol-
icy or policies have been issued , which certificate
shall meet the requirements of this Act .
Section Twenty . The Commissioner shall make
rules and regulations necessary for the administra-
tion of this Act .
Section Twenty -One . Nothing herein shall be con-
strued as preventing the plaintiff in any action at
law from relying for security upon the other proc-
esses provided by law .
Section Twenty -Two . If any part , subdivision or
section of the Act shall be deemed unconstitutional ,
the validity of its remaining provisions shall not be
affected thereby. Approved February 26 , 1936 .

CHAPTER 72.

dred thirty -five DD -thirty -five ( 3235 ) DD -thirty -five ( 35 ) so as
to limit the power of the Board of Commissioners to ordain and
put into execution , by -laws , rules and regulations for the gov-
ernment of the fire department of cities of the Second Class .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Section three thousand two hundred thirty-

228 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
five DD-thirty -five ( 3235 ) DD-thirty -five ( 35 ) Car-
roll's Kentucky Statutes , one thousand nine hundred
thirty -three ( 1933 ) Edition , be and the same is here-
by amended by inserting in the second paragraph
thereof, after the words " may be deemed expedi-
ent " the following : " except that no member of a
Fire Department herein authorized to be created ,
shall be compelled to remain on duty for more than
twenty-four ( 24 ) hours in each consecutive period
of forty -eight ( 48 ) hours unless such duty may be-
come necessary by reason of fire , casualty , or other
emergency ' and that the second paragraph of said
section , as so amended , be re -enacted so as to read
as follows :
The City Manager shall have full control over the

with all the property and paraphernalia belonging

and grade of the members and officers of each de-
partment . Approved February 18 , 1936 .

the Acts of one thousand nine hundred twelve ( 1912 ) , as

CHAPTER 73 229
amended by Chapter one hundrd twenty-seven ( 127 ) of the

six ( 126 ) , one hundred twenty-seven ( 127 ) , one hundred

Chapter seventy -three ( 73 ) of the Acts of one thousand nine

Sections two thousand eight hundred ninety-six a-sixteen
(2896a -16) to two thousand eight hundred ninety-six two -thirty-
three ( 2896 2-33 ) , inclusive , Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , one
thousand nine hundred thirty ( 1930 ) ; and enacting in lieu
thereof an Act to authorize and empower the proper legisla-
tive authorities of cities of the first class to adopt and provide
for a system of pensions for retired , injured and disabled
firemen and their dependents ; and to empower the assess-
ment and collection of taxes , fines , penalties and donations to
provide therefor ; and exempting pensions from process ;
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One ( 1 ) . That Chapter one hundred
twenty two (122 ) of the Acts of one thousand nine
hundred twelve ( 1912 ) as amended by Chapter one
hundred twenty- seven ( 127 ) of the Acts of one
thousand nine hundred twenty ( 1920 ) , Sections one
hundred twenty three ( 123 ) , one hundred twenty
four (124 ) , one hundred twenty five (125 ) , one hun-
dred twenty six ( 126 ) , one hundred twenty seven
(127 ) , one hundred twenty eight ( 128 ) , of Chapter
one hundred fifteen ( 115 ) of the Acts of one thou-
sand nine hundred twenty six ( 1926 ) and Chapter
seventy three ( 73 ) of the Acts of one thousand nine
hundred twenty eight ( 1928 ) of the General Assem-
bly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , being Sec-
tions two thousand eight hundred ninety six two-
sixteen ( 2896 2-16 ) to two thousand eight hundred
ninety six a-thirty three ( 2896a -33 ) , inclusive , Car-
roll's Kentucky Statutes , one thousand nine hun-

230 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
dred thirty ( 1930 ) , be and the same hereby are re-
pealed as of August thirty one ( 31 ) , one thousand
nine hundred thirty six ( 1936 ) .
Section Two ( 2 ) . That the proper legislative
authorities of all cities of the first class be and they
hereby are authorized and empowered to enact
laws and ordinances , effective on and after the first
day of September , one thousand nine hundred
thirty six ( 1936 ) , adopting and providing for a sys-
tem of pensions for injured , retired and disabled
firemen and their dependents .
Section Three (3) . That the proper legislative
authorities of all cities of the first class be and they
hereby are authorized and empowered to levy ,
assess and collect taxes for the purpose of paying
such pensions .
Section Four (4) That such legislative author-
ities be and they hereby are empowered to allot ,
assign , and pay into any such pension fund all
monies credited upon the payroll of the fire depart-
ment by reason of fines or penalties imposed upon
officers , members and employees of said depart-
ment , and to provide for , assess and collect contri-
butions from such officers , members and employees
for the benefit of such fund .
Section Five ( 5 ) . No portion of any pension
fund , created by virtue of this Act , shall , before or
after its order for distribution to the persons en-
titled thereto , be held, seized , taken , subjected to ,
detained or levied , by virtue of any attachment ,
execution , injunction , writ , interlocutory , or any
other order or decree or any process or proceeding
whatever , issued out of or by any court whatsoever ,
for the payment or satisfaction , in whole or in part ,
of any debt , damage , claim, demand or judgment
against the beneficiary of said fund ; but said fund
shall be held and distributed for the purpose of

CHAPTER 74 231
paying pensions , and for no other purpose what-
ever .
Section Six ( 6 ) All laws and parts of laws in
conflict with this Act are hereby expressly repealed .
Approved February 18 , 1936 .

CHAPTER 75 .
AN ACT to repeal , amend , and re -enact Section three thousand
thirty -seven f-ten ( 3037f -10 ) concerning supervision of the
Plumbing Departments of cities of the first class ; reports by
and to whom .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Section three thousand thirty- seven f-ten
( 3037f- 10 ) , Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's one thou-
sand nine hundred thirty Edition , be and the same is
hereby repealed and re -enacted as follows :
Section Three Thousand Thirty - Seven f -Ten
(3037f -10 ) . Supervision by Director of Depart-
ment of Public Works or Boards of Health ; Reports
to . The Plumbing Department of every city of the
first class in this State , consisting of the chief in-
spector and his deputies , shall be under the super-
vision of the Director of the Department of Public
Works of said city, and the Chief Inspector shall
make a complete report of this department to said
Director of the Department of Public Works at the
end of each year.
Approved February 18 , 1936 .

( 115 ) , Section sixteen ( 16 ) of an Act enacted by the General

Section Two Thousand Seven Hundred and Sev-
enty -Seven (2777 ) . Ordinances , How Passed , How

CHAPTER 77 235

CHAPTER 77 .
AN ACT to amend and re -enact the Title and Section one ( 1)
of an Act of the General Assembly which was adopted at its
regular session in one thousand nine hundred thirty -two
(1932 ) , and entitled "An Act enabling cities of the third class
to acquire , construct , operate and maintain electric light , heat
and power plants and to issue revenue bonds of such cities
to pay the cost thereof, payable solely .from the revenues of
such works without the incurrence of indebtedness of said
cities ," so as to extend the provisions thereof to cities of the
second , fourth , fifth and six classes .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One . That the Title and Section one
( 1 ) of an Act of the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky , and being Chapter
one hundred nineteen ( 119 ) of the Acts of the
General Assembly for the year one thousand nine
hundred thirty-two ( 1932 ) , be amended , extended
and re-enacted , so that said Title of said Act and
said Section one ( 1 ) , thereof as so amended , ex-
tended and re -enacted will read as follows :
“ An Act enabling cities of the second , third ,

236 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
fourth , fifth and sixth classes to acquire , construct ,
operate and maintain electric light , heat and power
plants and to issue revenue bonds of such cities to
pay the cost thereof, payable solely from the rev-
enues of such works without the incurrence of in-
debtedness of said cities . "
And Section one ( 1 ) , of said Act as so amended ,
extended and re -enacted will read as follows :
"Section One ( 1 ) . That cities of the second ,
third , fourth , fifth and sixth classes , whether organ-
ized under the general law or special charter law ,
and including such cities where the commission
form of government has been adopted , are hereby
authorized and empowered to purchase , establish ,
erect , maintain and operate electric light , heat and
power plants, together with extensions and neces-
sary appurtenances thereto , within or without the
corporate limits under the provisions of this Act
for the purpose of supplying such city and the in-
habitants thereof with electric light , heat and
power. Provided , however , that before any such
city shall be authorized or empowered to purchase
or establish and operate an electric light , heat and
power plant or to issue revenue bonds therefor , the
legislative body of such city shall pass an ordinance
declaring it desirable that the municipality shall
purchase or construct and operate a municipal
light , heat and power plant and shall prepare or
cause to be prepared the necessary and proper
plans and specifications for the construction of the
municipal light , heat and power plant , select the lo-
cation therefor , and determine the size , type and
method of construction thereof, and make the nec-
essary estimates of the probable cost of construc-
tion and the acquisition of the land and right of
way or to buy or take over any existing plant that
may be furnishing electric current to the city, and

CHAPTER 77 237
cause a survey to be made of all the lands , struc-
tures , rights of way, franchises and easements , the
acquisition of which may be deemed necessary for
the construction and operation of such municipal
plants ; and , provided further that the question of
whether or not revenue bonds shall be issued to
provide for the payment of the cost thereof shall
be submitted to the qualified voters of such munici-
pality at the next regular election to be held therein .
The mayor of the municipality shall certify the or-
dinance to the County Court Clerk of the county
who shall , on the ballots provided for use in the mu-
nicipality in which it is proposed to purchase or

cordance with the plans and specifications adopted
by the city of (here insert

the issuance of revenue bonds in the amount of

CHAPTER 78 .
AN ACT amending Section three thousand five hundred forty-
five ( 3545 ) of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , one thousand nine
hundred thirty ( 1930 ) Edition , relating to cities of the fourth
class , by repealing and re -enacting said section in amended
form ; said Section three thousand five hundred forty-five
( 3545 ) , as re -enacted , providing for the appointment of a tax
collector for cities of the fourth class , fixing his qualifications ,
oath , bond , and providing for his compensation , and authoriz-
ing the city council of cities of the fourth class to impose
upon the city treasurer or the clerk of the council the duties
of tax collector , and , upon the clerk , the further duty of col-
lecting street improvement taxes .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Section three thousand five hundred forty-
five ( 3545 ) of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , nineteen
hundred thirty ( 1930 ) edition , relating to the char-
ter of cities of the fourth class , be , and the same is
hereby , repealed and re - enacted in amended form
as Section three thousand five hundred forty-five
( 3545 ) , sub -sections one ( 1 ) , two ( 2 ) , three ( 3 ) , so
as to authorize the board of council of cities of the
fourth class to provide by ordinance that the clerk
of the council shall perform the duties of city tax
collector , and to further provide by ordinance , if
the council see fit , that the clerk of the council shall

CHAPTER 78 239
be charged with the collection of local taxes for
street improvements ; so that said Section , as re-
enacted , shall read as follows :
" Section Three Thousand Five Hundred Forty-
Five-One ( 3545-1 ) . Appointment of collector ;
qualifications , oath and bond ; compensation ; coun-
cil may make either treasurer or clerk of council
collector ; may make clerk collector of street im-
provement taxes . At any regular meeting of the
council they may appoint a collector , who shall hold
his office for two years from his appointment , and
until his successor is appointed and qualified . The
collector shall be over twenty-four years of age,
and a resident of the city for six months before his
appointment . Before he enters upon his duties he
shall take an oath to faithfully perform his duties
as collector , and execute bond , with security, to be
approved by the council , conditioned for the faith-
ful performance of his duties , and to pay over to
the person or persons entitled thereto all moneys
that may come into his hands as such collector . It
shall be the duty of said collector to collect all dues
owing to the city placed in his hands . The council
shall fix by ordinance , previous to his appointment ,
the compensation for his services . All dues owing
to the city may be collected by the chief of police .
The board of council , however , may by ordinance
provide that the treasurer shall perform the duties
of collector , or it may by ordinance provide that the
clerk of the council shall possess the qualifications
of collector and in such case may further provide
by ordinance that the clerk of the council shall per-
form the duties of collector . No demand shall be
necessary for the collection of said taxes , but the
same shall be payable at the office of the town treas-
urer , or at the office of the clerk of the council as
may by ordinance be provided .

240 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Two . In all cases in which the council shall
require the clerk of the council to perform the
duties of collector , as herein authorized , it shall
also by ordinance provide that he shall take the
same oath and execute the same bond , before enter-
ing upon the duties of his office , as is now required
by law of the city treasurer , except that the words
" clerk of the council " shall be substituted for
"treasurer " in said bond . It shall also provide in
said ordinance for the appointment by the mayor
of a special auditing committee to be composed of
three members of the council , of which the chair-
man of the finance committee shall be ex-officio
chairman, and it shall be the duty of such commit-
tee, after the city tax bills shall have been made
out by the clerk from the schedules as at present
required , carefully to check said bills against said
schedules and correct any errors which may have
been made , and for the performance of this task
said committee may engage such clerical help as
the council may think proper , and thereupon the
chairman of said committee shall sign said tax bills
as "Finance Chairman " and turn them over to said
clerk of the council as collector , taking his receipt
therefor in duplicate , showing the number of said
bills so delivered and the aggregate amount
thereof ; and in cases where school and city taxes
are required by law to be placed upon the same bills
said receipt shall show the aggregate amount of
school taxes and city taxes separately and a copy
of said receipt shall be certified to the board of edu-
cation . And said clerk, as such collector , shall have
all the powers and responsibilities as are now con-
ferred and imposed upon city tax collectors , and he
shall be dealt with by other officers as other city col-
lectors . Where the duties of collector are imposed
upon said clerk after his election , he may be al-

CHAPTER 78 241
lowed by the council a reasonable compensation
for the added duties for the remainder of his unex-
pired term .
Three . In all cases in which the council shall
require the clerk of the council to perform the duties
of collector as herein authorized , it may further
provide by ordinance that all local taxes due
or to become due for improvement of streets under
Section three thousand five hundred sixty -three
( 3563 ) , Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , whether said
taxes have already been assessed or shall there-
after be assessed , shall be payable to said clerk of
the council , at his office , instead of at the office of
the city treasurer , as now provided by Section
three thousand five hundred sixty -three ( 3563 ) ;
and in such case the clerk of the council shall keep
in his office , in a book to be provided for that pur-
pose , a record of all assessments of local taxes
thereafter assessed as provided in said Section ,
showing the name and portion of the street in which
the improvement is made , the character of the im-
provement and the names of the persons against
whose property the assessments are made , or , if
the assessments have already been made , the book
or books showing such record shall be delivered to
said clerk of the council . Upon payment of any
improvement tax to the clerk of the council , he will
make proper entry thereof in the record book
herein provided for , whereupon the lien for such
tax shall stand released . Said clerk shall carefully
keep a separate account of the fund arising from
assessments for each particular improvement , and
each of said funds shall , upon the order of the coun-
cil , be turned over to the city treasurer who shall
also keep a separate account of said funds , and
thereafter same shall be applied as is now provided
by said Section three thousand five hundred sixty-

242 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
three ( 3563 ) . And in all such cases said clerk shall
have the same power to coerce payment of said
local taxes as is now possessed by the city treas-
urer .
The provisions of this Act are hereby declared
to be severable , and the invalidity of one part shall
not affect the validity of any other part .
Approved February 27 , 1936 .
CHAPTER 79 .
AN ACT empowering any city or town owning and operating a
public water supply system to extend said system into any
territory contiguous to and within five ( 5 ) miles of its limits ,
to furnish and sell water therein , to install therein distribu-
tion pipes , meters , fire hydrants and other necessary ap-
purtenances , to acquire franchises , rights and rights of way ,
and to condemn rights or rights of way for said purposes ,
and declaring an emergency to exist .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
One . ( 1 ) . That any city or town in the Common-
wealth of Kentucky , owning or operating a system
of public water supply , shall have the power to
extend said system into , and to furnish and sell
water to any person or corporation within , any ter-
ritory contiguous to said city that lies within five
( 5 ) miles of the corporate limits thereof , and to
install within said territory distribution pipes , me-
ters , fire hydrants and other necessary appurte-
nances ; and to carry out said purposes may acquire
franchises , rights and rights of way , and may con-
demn said rights or rights of way as private cor-
porations may do .
Two . ( 2 ) . Inasmuch as various communities in
the neighborhood of cities and towns of this Com-
monwealth are in urgent need of public water sup-
ply and fire protection , an emergency is declared to

CHAPTER 80 243
exist , and this Act shall become immediately effec-
tive upon its passage and approval .
Approved February 25 , 1936 .
CHAPTER 80 .
AN ACT to amend and re-enact Article four ( 4) of Chapter
sixteen ( 16 ) of the Acts of the General Assembly of one
thousand nine hundred six (1906 ) , the same being Section
three thousand twenty -four a-four ( 3024a -4 ) of Carroll's Ken-
tucky Statutes one thousand nine hundred thirty ( 1930 ) , by
authorizing compensation to members of the board of water
works in cities of the first class .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One ( 1 ) . That Article four ( 4 ) of Chap-
ter sixteen ( 16 ) of the Acts of one thousand nine
hundred six ( 1906 ) of the General Assembly, the
same being Section three thousand twenty -four
a-four ( 3024a -4 ) of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes ,
one thousand nine hundred thirty ( 1930 ) , be and
the same hereby is amended and re -enacted so that
said section , as amended and re -enacted , shall read
as follows :
" Said board shall annually elect a president , a
treasurer , a secretary and a chief engineer , who
shall hold their offices for one year , or until their
successors have been duly elected and qualified , and
devote all their time to the duties of their respec-
tive offices . No member of said board , except the
president , shall receive a salary , but the board may
allow compensation to each member thereof , other
than the salaried officers , for attendance upon the
meetings of the board , not exceeding ten ( $ 10.00 )
dollars , for each meeting attended . The president
shall be elected from the members of the board .
The president , secretary and treasurer shall each

244 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
give bond , with approved surety , in such amount as
may be fixed by the board, which bond shall be pay-
able to the board of water works , and obligate the
makers thereof to perform faithfully the duties of
their several offices and faithfully account for any
pay over all money or other things of value which
may come into their several hands . The combined
salaries of the president, treasurer and secretary
shall not exceed the sum of nine thousand dollars .
The board shall have the authority to appoint such
other agents or employees as they may deem nec-
essary or proper and to fix the compensation of its
officers , agents or employees : Provided , however ,
that the salary of no officer , agent or employee shall
exceed the sum of five thousand dollars per
annum . " Approved February 18 , 1936 .
CHAPTER 81 .
AN ACT to amend and re -enact Sections one ( 1 ) , two (2 ) , and
nineteen ( 19 ) of an Act entitled "An Act to authorize cities
of the second , third and fourth class , to acquire water works
and to issue bonds , therefor , payable from the revenues of
such works ," being Chapter one hundred thirty -three ( 133 ) of
the Acts of the General Assembly of one thousand nine hun-
dred twenty-six ( 1926 ) , and to amend and re -enact the amend-
ment to said Sections one ( 1 ) , two ( 2 ) , and nineteen ( 19 ) of
said Act which amendment extended the provisions thereof
to include the acquisition of sewerage systems within said
cities, which amendment is Chapter one hundred nine (109 )
of the Acts of the General Assembly of one thousand nine
hundred thirty -two ( 1932 ) , and extending the provision of
said original Act and amendment to cities of the fifth and
sixth class , declaring an emergency to exist , and repealing all
conflicting laws.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Sections one ( 1 ) , two ( 2 ) , and nineteen ( 19 )
of an Act entitled " An Act to authorize cities of the
second , third and fourth class to acquire water works

CHAPTER 81 245
and to issue bonds therefor , payable from the reve-
nues for such works " being Chapter one hundred
thirty-three ( 133 ) of the Acts of the General As-
sembly of one thousand nine hundred twenty - six
( 1926 ) , and the amendment to said Sections one ( 1 ) ,
two ( 2 ) , and nineteen ( 19 ) of said Act which amend-
ment extended the provisions of the original act to
include the acquisition of sewerage systems within
said cities , which amendment is Chapter one hun-
dred nine ( 109 ) of the Acts of the General Assembly
of one thousand nine hundred thirty -two ( 1932 ) , be
and the same is hereby amended and re - enacted so
that the same as amended and re -enacted shall read
as follows , to -wit :
Section One . That cities of the second , third ,
fourth , fifth and sixth class whether organized under
the general law or special charter law , and including
such cities where the commission form of govern-
ment has been adopted , are hereby authorized and
empowered to purchase , establish , erect , maintain
and operate water works and/or sewerage system ,
and necessary appurtenances thereto , within or
without the corporate limits under the provisions of
this Act , for the purpose of supplying such city or
town and inhabitants thereof with water and/or
sewerage system .
Section Two . For the purpose of defraying the
cost of acquiring any such water works and/or sew-
erage system , either by purchase or construction ,
and appurtenances or extensions thereto , such city
may borrow money and issue negotiable bonds , pro-
vided no such bonds shall be issued unless and until
authorized by an ordinance specifying the proposed
undertaking, the amount of bonds to be issued and
the maximum rate of interest such bonds are to bear,
which shall not be more than six per cent ( 6 % ) per
annum .

246 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Section Nineteen . This act shall be deemed to
create an additional and alternate method for the
acquisition of water works and/or sewerage system
by any city of the second , third , fourth , fifth and
sixth class , and shall not be deemed to include , alter ,
amend or repeal any other statute , except such as
conflict herewith . No proceeding shall be required
for the acquisition of any water works hereunder or
the acquisition of any sewerage system , or the is-
suance of bonds hereunder except such as are pre-
scribed by this Act , any provision in the General
Laws of the Commonwealth of Kentucky or charter
of any city to the contrary notwithstanding . All
the powers heretofore vested in cities of the second ,
third and fourth class relative to the acquisition of
water works and the issuance of bonds therefor , as
provided in Chapter one hundred thirty -three ( 133 )
of the Acts of the General Assembly of one thou-
sand nine hundred twenty -six ( 1926 ) , and as amend-
ed by Chapter one hundred nine ( 109 ) of the Acts
of one thousand nine hundred thirty -two ( 1932 ) ex-
tending the provisions of said original Act to the
acquisition of sewerage system in said cities are
hereby extended in full to cities of the fifth and
sixth class .
Whereas , many of the cities to which this Act is
applicable are now without adequate water works
or sewerage facilities , thus endangering the health ,
safety , and convenience of the inhabitants thereof ,
and whereas , some of the cities are unable to procure
loans from the Federal Government until this Act
becomes a law , therefore , an emergency is declared
to exist and this Act shall take effect , immediately
upon its passage and approval by the Governor .
All laws or parts of laws in conflict herewith are
hereby repealed to the extent of such conflict .
Approved February 21, 1936 .

CHAPTER 82 247
CHAPTER 82.
AN ACT relating to the government of cities of the third
class in the Commonwealth of Kentucky .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One . That any city of the third class may
become organized and be governed under the pro-
visions of this Act by proceeding as hereinafter pro-
vided . Organizing under this Act shall not change
the corporate entity of any such city, but the body
politic and corporate shall remain the same body
that it now is.
Section Two . All laws applicable to and govern-
ing cities of the Third Class and not inconsistent
with the provisions of this Act shall continue to
apply to and govern each city that may organize
under this Act ; and all by -laws , ordinances and reso-
lutions in force in any such city and not inconsistent
with the provisions of this Act shall continue to be
in force until altered or repealed in manner provided
for in this Act .
Section Three . It shall be the duty of the County
Judge in the County in which is located a city of the
Third Class proposing to take the sense of its voters ,
as herein provided , upon the question of organizing
and being governed under this Act , upon the ap-
plication of written petition signed by a number of
legal voters of said city equal to twenty per centum
(20% ) of the votes cast in said city at the last pre-
ceding election for mayor , to receive said petition
and at the next regular term thereafter to make an
order, on his order book directing an election to be
held in said city at the next regular election and
not earlier than sixty days after said application is
lodged with said judge , which order shall direct the
sheriff or other officer of said county who may be

248 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
appointed to hold said election , to open the polls at
each and all voting places of said city for the pur-
pose of taking the sense of the qualified voters of
said city upon the question as to whether or not the
citizens in said city are in favor of the organization
and government of said city under the provision of
this Act . The question to be submitted to the voters
shall be : " Are you in favor of the organization and
government of the city of ( naming the city in which
said vote is to be taken) under the city manager
form of government , according to the provisions of
an Act entitled (naming this Act , and giving the
year or date of passage , or approval , or both ) ? ”
It shall be the duty of the county clerk to give to the
sheriff of the county , or to such officer as may be
appointed to hold said election , a certified copy of
the order of the county court , as it appears on the
order book , within five days after such order is
made ; and it shall be the duty of said sheriff , or
other such officer , to have said order published in
some weekly or daily newspaper , published or sub-
mitted in said county , for at least two weeks before
the election , and also to advertise the same by print-
ed or written handbills , posted in at least three
places in said city , for the same length of time . If
there is no weekly or daily newspaper published in
said city, or the proprietor of said newspaper refuses
to publish such notice , the printed or written hand-
bills provided shall be sufficient notice . If it shall
be found that a majority of the legal votes cast in the
election hereinabove provided for are against said
proposition submitted , it shall be the duty of the
canvassing board to certify that fact , which certifi-
cate shall be delivered to the Clerk of the County
Court , and a copy thereof delivered to the Clerk or
Recorder of the city, and at the next regular term of
said court the county judge shall cause the same to

CHAPTER 82 249
be spread on the order book of said court , and the
entry of said certificate in the order book , or a copy
thereof, shall be prima facie evidence of the facts
therein contained . When a majority of the votes
cast shall be in favor of organizing and governing
the city under the provisions of this Act , and said
facts be certified as hereinabove provided , by the
said canvassing board to the county clerk, and a copy
thereof shall also be certified to the city clerk , said
city shall thereafter be organized and governed by
the provisions of this Act . Said copy of said cer-
tificate shall be spread upon the records of said
county , as herein provided , and upon the records of
said city at the next regular meeting of the city legis-
lative body .
Section Four . All of the present city offices save
those of the Mayor and Police Judge and City Prose-
cuting Attorney , shall at the expiration of the
Mayor's term , be ipso facto abolished ; provided ,
however , that the term of any officer elected by the
people shall not expire until the expiration of such
term .
Section Five . If the vote shall be in favor of the
organization and government of the city under the
city manager form of government , then , at the first
succeeding regular election at which the Mayor of
such city is elected , there shall be elected by the
qualified voters of said city , two commissioners , and
a mayor, and a police judge , as the case may be , who
shall hold office under the provisions of this Act , and
such city manager form of government shall become
effective and go into operation and installation in
the office of the mayor and the commissioners elected
at such election .
Section Six . No person shall be elected to said
office of mayor or police judge , or commissioner ,

250 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
without first having been nominated in the manner
hereinafter prescribed . On the third Saturday be-
fore the day for regular election , there shall be held
a Primary Election . Said primary election shall be
conducted by the same officers chosen and acting in
the same manner , with the same rights and duties ,
as in the later regular election . Each applicant for
nomination shall, at least ten days before the day of
said Primary Election , file with the county clerk a
petition signed by at least fifty voters ( 50 ) , and in
the following form :
of .
We the undersigned , qualified voters of the city
(naming it) , and residing each at
the places indicated opposite his or her name signed
hereto , do hereby request the name of.......
(naming the applicant ) , to be placed on
the ballot as that of an applicant for nomination for
the office of. (naming the office
sought ) at the Primary election to be held in our
city on the third Saturday before the next regular
election . We know the applicant to be a qualified
voter of the city and a person of good character , and
is, in our judgment , qualified for the duties of said
office . Numbersnames
Streets .
Said petition shall be verified by the affidavit of some
person or persons as to the genuineness of the sig-
natures and addresses of the signers . No voter
shall sign more than one such petition with reference
to each office that is to be filled ; and in the event
that any voter shall sign more than one petition of
applicants for nomination for mayor or police judge ,
or shall sign the petitions of more than two appli-
cants for nomination for commissioners , then the
name of such petitioner shall not be counted as a
valid name on any of said petitions with reference to

CHAPTER 82 251
such office . Immediately upon the expiration of the
time for filing such petitions the said county clerk
shall cause to be published for three consecutive
days in the official newspaper of the city , and in
such other papers as he may designate , in proper
form , the names of the persons as they will appear
upon the primary ballots to be printed , authenticat-
ed with a facsimile of his signature . Provided , how-
ever , that immediately upon the expiration of the
time for filing such petitions it should appear that
there are not more than two applicants for the nom-
ination as mayor or police judge , as the case may
be, or not more than four applicants for the nomina-
tion for commissioner , such applicants for mayor or
police judge , as the case may be, and such applicants
for commissioner shall thereby be nominated , and
the names of such nominee which will be placed upon
the primary ballots for such primary election , and
no primary election shall be held for the nomination
for such office . Upon said ballots the names of said
applicants for each nomination shall be printed as
provided in Section nine ( 9 ) of this Act , with the
square at the right of each name , and above the
names of the applicants for nomination for mayor,
or police judge , as the case may be , shall be printed
the words , "Vote for one , " and above the names of
the applicants for nomination for commissioners
shall be printed the words , " Vote for two . "
The ballots shall contain no party designation , or
emblem of any kind , nor any sign indicating the ap-
plicant's political belief , or party application . Hav-
ing caused said ballots to be printed , the said county
clerk shall cause to be delivered at each polling place
a number of said ballots equal to twice the number
of votes cast in such polling precinct at the last pre-
ceding general municipal election for mayor . The
persons who are qualified to vote at the general

252 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
municipal election shall be qualified to vote at such
primary elections . The officers of election shall,
then conform with the provisions of the laws of this
state regarding the counting of the ballots cast .
After said ballots have been counted in conformity
with the laws of the State of Kentucky , said result
shall be published in the official newspaper of the
city at least once . At such Primary Election the two
applicants receiving the highest number of votes
for nomination of mayor or police judge , as the case
may be , shall be thereby nominated ; and the four
applicants receiving the highest number of votes
for nomination for commissioners shall be thereby
nominated .
Section Seven . The expense of all primary
elections under the provisions of this Act shall be
paid by the City .
Section Eight. At the regular elections held
under this Act the names of the nominees shall be
placed upon ballots differing from those used at the
nomination only in that they ( the election ballots )
shall contain only the names of the successful nom-
inees , and the heading " City Ballots " , and the one
nominee receiving the greater number of votes cast
for mayor or police judge , as the case may be , and
the two nominees receiving the greater number of
votes for commissioners shall be elected , respec-
tively , mayor or police judge , as the case may be,
and commissioners .
Section Nine . At all elections held under this Act
the ballots shall be on a different sheet from all
other ballots to be used in any election and shall be
in the form therein prescribed , except the word
City Ballot " , shall be on the head thereof and the
names of all candidates for each office shall be print-
ed on said ballot in a single column . The names
of the candidates for each office shall be printed , on

CHAPTER 82 253
the first fifty ballots as arranged in alphabetical
order and on each of the succeeding fifty ballots the
names shall be printed in the same order except that
the first name on the preceding fifty ballots shall be
shifted to the last place on the next succeeding fifty
ballots , which last name so shifted from the first
place on said previous lot of fifty ballots shall be
succeeded by the second name thereon at all times ,
and soon thereafter throughout a like change or
rotation being made in the printed order of names
for every fifty ballots.
The provisions for the general election law of the
State of Kentucky as to the duties of the county
clerk and other public officers in the matter of print-
ing and distributing ballots , of the issuing of them
to the voters , of receiving and depositing them in the
ballot boxes and of counting and preserving them ,
and in all other particulars except as otherwise pro-
vided herein shall be applicable in all respects to the
election of officers under the provisions of this Act ,
provided, that it shall be the duty of the sheriff of
each county in which a city of the Third Class oper-
ating under the provisions of this Act , to provide for
each precinct in each city a separate box for the
reception of ballots used in the election of the mu-
nicipal officers and provided , further , that it shall
be the duty of the officers of the election in each pre-
cinct to issue the ballots herein provided for in the
same manner as other ballots are issued at said
election , by writing the name and the residence of
the voter upon the primary stub , and his register
upon the secondary stub of the city ballot, and by
his observing as to these ballots , such other regula-
tions for the issuing and deposit of ballots as may
be prescribed for elections generally . It shall be
unlawful for any election officer or other person
within the election booth to tell or to indicate , by

254 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
word of mouth or otherwise , to a voter what may be
the political affiliation of any candidate , and the
violation of this provision shall be a misdemeanor ,
punishable by a fine not exceeding two hundred
( $200.00 ) dollars . At the opening of the polls at
any election herein provided for , each judge shall
appoint an inspector and challenger , whose duty it
shall be to act as such in the precinct in which he is
appointed and is so acting said inspector and chal-
lenger shall be governed by the provision of the gen-
eral election law with reference to inspectors and
challengers .
Section Ten . Every fourth year after the elec-
tion at which a mayor and said two commissioners
shall be elected , and at the corresponding times in
the year , a primary election and a regular election
shall be held in the same manner and for the same
offices ; and every second year after the election at
which such mayor and said two commissioners shall
be elected , and at the corresponding times in the
year, a primary election and a regular election shall
be held in the same manner and for the election of
two commissioners .
Section Eleven . Any legal voter not less than
twenty-five years of age and possessing the other
qualifications prescribed by law, shall be eligible
to election to any office under this Act .
Section Twelve . The mayor shall be elected for
a term of four years , beginning on the first Monday
in January , following his election ; and shall receive
an annual salary of not more than seven hundred
fifty dollars ( $ 750.00 ) ; but the mayor and commis-
sioners elected under this Act may at any time re-
duce said salary to an amount not less than one
dollar ($1.00 ) per annum .
Section Thirteen . The commissioners shall be
elected each for a term of two years , beginning the

CHAPTER 82 255
first Monday in January , following their election ;
and each shall receive an annual salary of not more
than five hundred dollars ($ 500.00 ) ; but the mayor
and commissioners elected under this Act may at
any time reduce said salary to an amount not less
than one dollar ( $1.00 ) per annum .
Section Fourteen . The mayor and the two com-
missioners shall constitute a board of commission-
ers . In this board of commissioners shall be vested
all the legislative , executive and administrative
power of the city save as herein otherwise provided .
Section Fifteen . Two members of the board of
Commissioners shall constitute a quorum , but the af-
firmative vote of at least two members shall be neces-
sary to the adoption of any motion , resolution or
ordinance , to the making or approval of any con-
tract , or to the passage of any measure . Upon each
vote the yeas and nays shall be recorded and each
motion , resolution and ordinance shall be reduced
to writing and read before the vote is taken thereon .
Each resolution , measure or ordinance shall be
signed by the mayor or by two commissioners , and
recorded before it shall take effect . The mayor shall
have no veto power . The board of commissioners ,
shall, at the beginning of its term of office , elect by
a majority of its members one commissioner to act
as mayor pro tem , and the commissioner so chosen
shall be invested with all the powers and shall per-
form all the duties of the mayor in the event of his
absence from the city or of his inability to attend to
the duties of his office .
Section Sixteen . The mayor shall preside at meet-
ings of the board of commissioners and perform
such other duties consistent with his office as
may be imposed by the board of commissioners . He
shall be recognized as the official head of the city

256 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
for all ceremonial purposes , by the courts for the
purpose of serving civil process , and be the governor
for military purposes . In time of public danger or
emergency , he may , with the consent of the board of
commissioners take command of the police and main-
' tain order and enforce the laws .
Section Seventeen . Under the City Manager
Form of Government the board of commissioners
shall select and employ a city manager who shall not
be any officer or official of the city , but the executive
agent of the mayor and board of commissioners in
the management of the city affairs . Neither the
mayor nor board of commissioners nor any com-
missioner shall dictate the appointment of any per-
son to office or employment by the city manager , or
in any manner interfere with the city manager or
prevent him from exercising his own judgment in the
appointment of officers and employees in the admin-
istrative service . Except for the purpose of inquiry
the board of commissioners and its members shall
deal with the administrative service solely through
the city manager and neither the board of commis-
sioners nor any member thereof shall give orders
to any of the subordinates of the city manager , eith-
er publicly or privately . Any such dictation , pre-
vention , orders , or other interference on the part of
the mayor or of a member of the Board of Commis-
sioners with the administration of the city shall be
deemed to be a misdemeanor and upon conviction of
any member so charged shall be subject to a fine of
not more than Five Hundred ( $ 500.00 ) Dollars or
imprisonment for a term not exceeding sixty days or
both , and such conviction shall operate to remove
him, and the judgment of the court shall declare the
office vacant .
Section Eighteen . The city manager shall be
chosen by the board of commissioners solely on the

CHAPTER 82 257
basis of his executive and administrative qualifica-
tions . The choice shall not be limited to inhabitants
of the city or state . He shall be employed for a
period of not exceeding four years . He shall be re-
movable at will by the board of commissioners , but
such removal shall not be effected by a reduction
in the compensation of the city manager , and if re-
moved at any time after having served for six
months he may in writing demand written charges
and a public hearing on the same before the board
of commissioners to the date on which his final re-
moval shall take effect , but during such hearing the
board of commissioners may suspend him from
office . Such public hearing shall be fixed by the
Board of Commissioners not to exceed thirty days
after such demand therefor . During the absence
or disability of the city manager the Board of Com-
missioners shall designate some properly qualified
person to perform the duties of the office . The city
manager shall receive such compensation as may be
determined by the Board of Commissioners .
Section Nineteen . The city manager shall be re-
sponsible to the Board of Commissioners for the
proper administration of all affairs of the city, and
to that end shall make all appointments , except as
otherwise provided in this law . Except when the
Board of Commissioners is considering his removal ,
the city manager shall be entitled to be present at
all meetings of the Board of Commissioners and of
its committees and to take part in their discussions .
Section Twenty . The Board of Commissioners
shall , upon the written advice of the City Manager ,
create by ordinance such administrative departments
as in the judgment of the Board are reasonably re-
quired for the efficient , orderly and economical ad-
ministration of the business affairs of the city ; and
may at any time create new departments , combine
Acts- 9

258 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
1
or abolish existing departments , or establish tem-
porary departments ; and may also prescribe by or-
dinance the functions of each department and pro-
vide for the appointment or employment of a di-
rector of each , or of one or more of such depart-
ments . Directors of departments shall be appoint-
ed by the city manager , and may be removed by him
at anytime . Any person employed in any depart-
ment may be removed at any time by the director
of such department , or by the city manager , subject
to such civil service rules and regulations as may be
deemed necessary for the efficient and non -political
handling of city affairs .
Section Twenty -One . The Board of Commission-
ers , the city manager , and any officer or board au-
thorized by them , or either of them , shall have the
power to make investigations as to city affairs , to
subpoena witnesses , adminster oaths , and compel
the production of books and papers .
Section Twenty -Two . Once each year the city
manager shall prepare and submit to the Board of
Commissioners an annual budget for the ensuing
fiscal year , based upon detailed estimates furnished
by the several departments and other divisions of
the city government , according to a classification as
nearly uniform as possible . The budget shall pre-
sent the following information : (A ) An itemized
statement of the appropriations recommended by
the city manager for current expenses and for per-
manent improvements for each department and each
division thereof for the ensuing fiscal year with com-
parative statements in parallel columns of the ap-
propriations and expenditures for the current and
next preceding fiscal year , and the increases and de-
creases in the appropriations recommended ; ( B ) An
itemized statement of the taxes required and of the
estimated revenues of the city from all other sources

CHAPTER 82 259
for the ensuing fiscal year , with comparative state-
ments in parallel columns of the taxes and other
revenues for the current and next preceding fiscal
year, and of the increases and decreases estimated
or proposed ; ( C ) A statement of the financial con-
dition of the city ; and , ( D ) Such other information
as may be required by the Board of Commissioners .
Copies of the budget shall be printed and available
for distribution not later than two weeks after sub-
mission to the Board of Commissioners and a public
hearing shall be given thereon by the Board of Com-
missioners or a committee thereof before action by
the Board of Commissioners .
Section Twenty -Three . The Board of Commission-
ers shall pass an annual appropriation ordinance ,
which shall be based on the budget submitted by the
city manager . The total amount of appropriations
shall not exceed the estimated revenues of the city .
Before the annual appropriation ordinance has been
passed the Board of Commissioners with the recom-
mendation in writing of the city manager , may make
appropriations for the current department expenses ,
chargeable to the appropriations for the year when
passed , to an amount sufficient to cover the necessary
expenses of the various departments until the an-
nual appropriation is in force . No other liabilities
shall be incurred by any officer or employee of the
city , except in accordance with the provisions of the
annual appropriation ordinance , or under continuing
contracts and loans authorized under the provisions
of this charter . At any meeting after the passage of
the appropriation ordinance , and after at least one
week's notice , the Board of Commissioners may
amend such ordinance , so as to authorize the trans-
fer of unusual appropriations for one purpose to an-
other purpose , to appropriate available revenues not
included in the annual budget .

260 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Section Twenty -Four . At the termination of each
fiscal year an annual audit shall be made of all the
accounts of all city officers . The Board of Commis-
sioners shall require said audit to be made of said
accounts by qualified public accountants , selected by
the Board of Commissioners , who have no personal
interest , direct or indirect , in the financial affairs of
the city, or of any of its officers or employees , and
the Board of Commissioners shall cause the result
of such examination to be published in the official
newspaper and in pamphlet form , and a copy of the
report in pamphlet form shall be given to each voter
who may apply for same at the proper office . Pro-
vided , further , that the Board of Commissioners
may , at any time , provide for an examination or
audit of the accounts of any officer or department
of the city government .
Section Twenty -Five . The mayor and each com-
missioner shall execute a bond to the city, upon
which an action may be maintained by any person
or persons , interested in the keeping of the cove-
nants therein contained , in the penal sum of ten
thousand dollars , conditioned upon the faithful per-
formance of his official duties , such bonds to be ap-
proved by the county judge , and filed as matters of
public record . The Board of Commissioners shall
require bonds with adequate surety of such amounts
as it may deem advisable , from the city manager ,
and from any other city employees whose duties , in
the opinion of said board , require a bond for faith-
ful performance . Any premiums on any or all bonds
provided for in this Act may , at the discretion of
the Board of Commissioners , be paid by the city .
Section Twenty - Six . Nothing herein shall be con-
strued to apply to the organization , existence , or
conduct of the affairs of the Board of Education ,

CHAPTER 82 261
Section Twenty - Seven . Every ordinance or reso-
lution ordering the construction or reconstruction
of any street or sewer , or making or authorizing any
contract involving the expenditure of more than one
thousand dollars , or granting any franchise of the
right to use or occupy the streets , highways , bridges ,
or public places of the city for any except a merely
temporary purpose shall after its introduction and
before its adoption remain on file at least one week
for public inspection in the completed form in which
it shall be put upon its final passage ; and no such
ordinance or resolution shall go into effect until the
expiration of ten days after its passage , except in
case of emergency the public health or safety shall
require that it take immediate effect , which facts
shall be declared by the unanimous vote of the
Board of Commissioners .
Section Twenty -Eight. The Board of Commis-
sioners shall meet at least once a week for the trans-
action of its business . It shall fix by ordinance the
times for holding its regular meetings . Any special
meeting may be called by the mayor or by two com-
missioners . All meetings shall be public .
Section Twenty -Nine . In case of the death , resig-
nation or inability of the mayor, causing a perma-
nent vacancy in said office , the mayor pro tem , shall
act as mayor and shall possess all the rights and
powers and perform all the duties of the mayor and
receive his salary , under the official title , however ,
of mayor pro tem , until such vacancy in the office
of mayor be filled by an election ordered by the
Board of Commissioners for that purpose , at which
time the mayor pro tem , if his term as commissioner
be then unexpired, shall resume his duties as such
commissioner . In the event that any commissioner
shall for more than thirty days be necessarily ab-
sent from the city, or for the same period shall be

262 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
unable from sickness or other cause to discharge
the duties of his office , or in the event the mayor
pro tem shall be filling the office of mayor as con-
templated hereinabove provided for such period , the
Board of Commissioners may fill such office tempo-
rarily by appointment , and said appointee shall dis-
charge the duties of the commissioner whose place
may thus be temporarily filled until such commis-
sioner shall return or become fit for and resume his
duties , and any such temporary appointee shall serve
without compensation . In the event of a permanent
vacancy in the Board of Commissioners caused by
the death , resignation or inability of any member
thereof, or in the event of a permanent vacancy in
the office of mayor shall be filled by the mayor pro
tem such vacancy shall be filled by appointment by
the other members of the board until the vacancy
shall be filled by the election of a successor at the
next regular election of municipal officers , as is pre-
scribed by law , and such temporary appointee shall
for the time he serves as such , receive the salary of
the commissioner whose place he has been appointed
to fill . A vacancy shall exist when any elective of-
ficer fails to qualify within ten days after notice of
his election , or dies ( or resigns ) , or moves his domi-
cile outside the city, or remains outside the city , for
a period of six months , or is convicted of a felony ,
or is judicially declared insane , or is removed from
office in any manner .
Section Thirty . In case of misconduct , inability
or willful neglect in the performance of the duties of
his office , the mayor or any commissioner may be re-
moved from office by an unanimous vote of the other
two members of the Board of Commissioners . But
no such officers shall be so removed without having
been given the right to have a full public hearing
with representation by counsel , and with witnesses .

CHAPTER 82 263
summoned in his behalf and required to testify . The
findings of fact at any such hearing and the reasons
for any such removal , shall be stated in writing and
filed as matter of public record .
Section Thirty -One . If during the ten days next
following the passage of any such ordinance , as can-
not within said ten days become effective , a petition
signed by a number of voters equal to at least twen-
ty-five per centum ( 25 % ) of the total number of
votes cast for both candidates for mayor at the last
preceding regular election for mayor , stating the
residence of each signer , and verified as to signa-
tures and residences by the affidavits of some one or
more persons , shall be presented to the Board of
Commissioners , protesting against the passage of
such ordinance , such ordinance shall be suspended
from going into effect , and shall be reconsidered by
the Board of Commissioners . If such ordinance be
not then repealed , the board shall submit to the vot-
ers of the whole city at either a special or a regular
election according to law , the following question :
" Shall the ordinance (briefly describing it ) go into
effect ? " and if a majority of the votes cast upon
such question be in the negative , the ordinance shall
not go into effect . But if a majority of the votes
cast upon such question be in the affirmative , the
ordinance shall go into effect as soon as the result
is officially ascertained and declared .
Section Thirty -Two . If a petition signed by a
number of voters equal to at least twenty-five per
centum ( 25 % ) of the total number of votes cast for
both candidates for mayor at the last preceding reg-
ular election for mayor, stating the residence of each
signer , and verified by affidavits of some one or more
persons as to the signatures and residences , request-

nance therein set forth-if such petition be present-

Board has a legal right to pass , then the board shall
