submit the question of its passage to the voters of
the city at the next regular election . At such elec-
tion the question submitted shall be : " Shall the pro-
posed ordinance ( briefly describing it ) , be passed ? ”
If a majority of the votes cast upon said question
be in the affirmative , the proposed ordinance shall
be thereby passed and shall become effective , as

may be voted on at any election . The Board of

favor of the repeal or amendment , such ordinance
shall be thereby repealed , or amended , as the case
may be.
Section Thirty -Three . Whenever a question of

nance is to be submitted to the voters at an election ,
the Board of Commissioners shall cause the pro-
posed ordinance or ordinances , or the ordinance and
amendment , as the case may be, to be printed once
in the official newspaper of the city, and in such
other newspapers as the Board of Commissioners
may direct , before such election .
Section Thirty -Four . Whenever the citizens of

of the county or to such other officer as may be ap-

pointed to hold said election , a certified copy of the

CHAPTER 85 .
AN ACT repealing , amending and re -enacting Sections five hun-
dred twenty-eight ( 528 ) , five hundred thirty -three ( 533 ) and
five hundred thirty -five ( 535 ) of the Kentucky Statutes , Car.
roll's Edition of one thousand nine hundred thirty (1930 ) ,
regulating and defining the duties of Coroners , and providing
penalty for failure to report sudden deaths , and creating the
office of Deputy Coroner .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Sections five hundred twenty- eight ( 528 ) ,
five hundred thirty -three ( 533 ) and five hundred
thirty -five ( 535 ) of the Kentucky Statutes , Car-
roll's edition of one thousand nine hundred thirty
( 1930 ) , relating to the duties of Coroners , be
amened and when so amended and re -enacted shall
read as follows :
Section Five Hundred Twenty -Eight. It shall be
the duty of the Coroner , upon request by any re-
sponsible citizen or when he has reason to believe

270 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
the death of a human being has resulted from un-
natural cause within his county , resident or non-
resident of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , with-
out the attendance of a physician qualified to sign
the certificate of death , or by reason of crime , hom-
icide , suicide , drowning or otherwise suddenly killed ,
to investigate such death and hold an inquest
over said dead body .
In the event that death results from violence or
accident within ninety days from onset , it shall be
the duty of the Coroner to hold an inquest the same
as if death occurred immediately .
It shall be the duty of any person or persons dis-
covering or finding or having in their possession
within the county the dead body of any person
slain , drowned or otherwise suddenly killed or
whose death occurred from unnatural cause with-
out the attendance of a physician , to immediately
notify the Coroner and not remove such body , nor
any thing therefrom until directed to do so by the
Coroner or other duly authorized person .
Any person violating the provisions of this Act
shall be guilty of a misdemeanor and fined not less
than ten dollars nor more than one hundred
dollars , or be confined in jail for not less than ten
days nor more than fifty days , or both so fined and
imprisoned in the discretion of the court or jury
trying the case .
Section Five Hundred Thirty -Three . Jury , When
Summonsed , Etc.- In holding inquests , the Coroner
shall not require a jury except in cases of probable
homicide , or where death was caused by the negli-
gence of some person other than the deceased , or
upon the request of some member of the family .
The jury summonsed and sworn by the Coroner must
be composed of six reputable citizens of the county ,
and upon oath , shall inquire and state , in writing ,

CHAPTER 86 271
if they know, in what manner the person came to
his or her death , when , where , how and by whom .
The jury shall be allowed for their service the
sum of fifty cents each, the same to be paid by the
county clerk and a voucher drawn on the county
treasurer .
Section Five Hundred Thirty -Five . Inquests ,.
when a Deputy Coroner or Justice may Hold .
When the Coroner is in the county and at his office
or place of business and able to attend to his duties ,
an inquest shall not be held by any other person ,
but if the attendance of the Coroner can not be had
on account of sickness or absence from the County ,
a Deputy Coroner appointed by the Coroner , with
no additional expense to the County or City, may
act as Coroner and have same power and authority
as the Coroner . In case no such Deputy is desig-
nated or appointed by the Coroner or the Coroner
is absent from the County or sick , and the condition
or situation of the body be such as to require imme-
diate interment , any Justice of the Peace may hold
an inquest , as Coroners are authorized to do , and
shall be allowed for the service the same compen-
sation as the Coroner and to be paid in the same
Approved February 27 , 1936 .manner .

CHAPTER 89 .
AN ACT to amend an Act , entitled an Act to amend an Act , in
relation to the Kentucky State Board of Dental Examiners ,
which Act became effective June third ( 3 ) , nineteen hundred

CHAPTER 89 275
thirty -two ( 1932 ) and being Section two thousand six hun-
dred thirty -six ( 2636 ) with its twenty -three ( 23 ) subsections
of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , and to regulate the practice
of dentistry in the State of Kentucky .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
CONTINUANCE OF BOARD AND MANNER OF APPOINT-
MENT
One ( 1 ) . That the Kentucky State Board of
Dental Examiners heretofore created be continued ,
to consist of five practicing dentists , whose duty
it shall be to carry out the purposes and enforce the
provisions of this Act as hereafter specified . The
members of said board shall be appointed by the
Governor from a list of three names , for each mem-
ber to be appointed to be recommended by the Ken-
tucky State Dental Association , at its annual meet-
ing , and at the time of their appointment upon said
board must have been actual residents and legally
licensed practicing dentists of this State for a
period of four years or more immediately preced-
ing their appointment : Provided , however , That
no person shall be eligible to appointment to said
board who is in any way connected with or inter-
ested in any dental college or dental department of
any institution of learning or dental supply busi-
ness . The term for which the members of said
board shall hold office shall be four years , provided
the present members of the board in office at the
time of the passage of this Act shall continue in
office until their respective terms have expired and
until their successors are appointed and qualified .
All vacancies in said board shall be filled by the
Governor from said list .
OFFICERS , MEETINGS , RECORDS , Etc. ,
Two ( 2 ) . Said Board of Dental Examiners shall

276 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
choose one of its members President , and one Sec-
retary -Treasurer , thereof, at an annual meeting on
the first Tuesday in June of each year . Said board
may meet oftener if necessary in the discretion of
the board at such places as it may deem proper , for
the examination of applicants who desire to prac-
tice dentistry in this State , and for the transaction
of any other business that may come before it .
Said board shall keep a record book in which shall
be registered the names , addresses , license num-
bers , of all persons legally entitled to practice den-
tistry in this State . A majority of the members of
said board shall at all times constitute a quorum
for the transaction of business , and the proceedings
of said board shall be recorded in a minute book
open at all reasonable times to public inspection .
The Secretary - Treasurer shall execute to the said
board bond with approved security for the faithful
performance of his duties .
Said Board of Dental Examiners shall have pow-
er to make rules and regulations for its own
conduct and procedure , may provide for the affilia-
tion with the National association of Dental Exam-
iners and send delegates to its meetings and pay the
expense thereof ; to select the subjects and the
standard of proficiency and percentage for exami-
nations to practice dentistry ; to employ an attor-
ney to advise and assist it in carrying out and en-
forcing the provisions of this Act and pay him
reasonable compensation therefor , and to regulate
the practice of dentistry in this State not contrary
to law.
LICENSES AND REGISTRATION AND HOW GRANTED
a
Three ( 3 ) . No person shall dentistry in this
State or attempt to do so after the passage
of this Act without first applying for and obtaining

CHAPTER 89 277
a license for such purpose from the said Kentucky
State Board of Dental Examiners , and registering
such license as herein provided , and this provision
applies to all persons whether they have hereto-
fore practiced dentistry or not in this State , except
such persons as have been heretofore licensed and
registered . Application shall be made to the said
board in writing for license and shall in every in-
stance be accompanied by the examination fee
of forty dollars , which sum is authorized to be
charged for each examination by said board . The
applicant must be of good moral character , at least
twenty -one years of age at the time of making the
application , and the application of each person
seeking a license must be accompanied by satisfac-
tory evidence to said board that the applicant so
applying is a graduate of and has a diploma from
the faculty of a reputable dental college , school , or
dental department of a reputable university , rated
as A or B by the Dental Educational Council of
America and should the Dental Educational Coun-
cil of America cease to exist the board of examiners
shall rate dental colleges , dental schools , or dental
departments of a university . Examinations must
be clinical and written and of such a character as to
satisfactorily test the qualifications of the appli-
cant to practice dentistry , and the board may , in
its discretion, refuse to grant license to any person
they find guilty of cheating , deception , or fraud
during such examination . Examination papers and
clinical examination results where possible shall be
retained by the Secretary of the board for a period
of two years.
REGISTERING LICENCE WITH COUNTY CLERK
Four ( 4) . Every person licensed to practice
dentistry in this State by the said Kentucky State
Board of Dental Examiners , as herein provided

278 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
shall , before beginning the practice of dentistry ,
cause said license to be registered with the county
clerk of each county in which such person desires
to engage in the practice of dentistry by appearing
before such clerk and filing his affidavit , showing
that he has been examined and licensed as herein
provided , and the county clerk of each county before
whom such persons appear shall attest the filing
of the license certificate in duplicate on blanks fur-
nished to the licensee by the board , one copy to be
retained by the licensee and the other copy to be
forwarded to the Secretary of the board by the
licensee , and the county clerk of each county is au-
thorized to receive a registration fee of fifty cents
for each registration .
DISPLAY OF LICENSE AND CERTIFICATE OF REGISTRA-
TION
Five ( 5 ) . The license to practiceto practice dentistry
herein provided , and the certificate of registration
(together with the annual license renewal certifi-
cate ) shall at all times be displayed in a conspicu-
ous place in his or her office wherein he or she shall
practice the profession of dentistry , and if there
are more dentists than one practicing or employed ,
in any dental office , the proprietor of such office
shall post and display or cause to be posted or dis-
played in like manner the name , license , and regis-
tration certificate of each dentist so practicing and
so employed therein . Any person practicing den-
tistry within the meaning of this Act , who shall fail
to so post and display , or cause to be posted or dis-
played , the name , license , and registration certifi-
cate of himself and any person practicing or em-
ployed to practice as a dentist in his or her dental
office shall be guilty of a misdemeanor , and upon
conviction thereof they shall be subject to a fine of
not less than twenty -five dollars nor more than fifty

CHAPTER 89 279
dollars ($50.00 ) for the first offense , and for every
subsequent offense he or she shall be subjected to a
fine of not less than one hundred dollars nor more
than two hundred and fifty dollars , or by imprison-
ment for a period of not less than sixty days , or by
both such fine and imprisonment , and he or she shall
whenever requested , exhibit such license to mem-
bers of the Board of Dental Examiners or its au-
thorized agent .
REFUSAL OF LICENSE
Six ( 6 ) . The State Board of Dental Examiners
may refuse to issue license for any of the following
causes :
The presentation to the board of any diploma ,
license , or certificate illegally or fraudulently ob-
tained, or one obtained from an institution which
is not reputable , or an unrecognized or irregular in-
stitution or state board or the practice of any fraud
or deception , or failure to meet the required stand-
ard of proficiency or percentage for examinations
fixed by the State Board .
REVOCATION OR SUSPENSION OF LICENSE
Seven ( 7 ) . Said State Board of Dental Exam-
iners may revoke or suspend the certificate of license
of any dentist for any of the following causes :
One ( 1 ) . His conviction of a felony or misde-
meanor involving turpitude , in which case the record
of conviction or a certified copy thereof, certified by
the clerk of the court or by the judge in whose court
the conviction is had , shall be conclusive evidence .
Two ( 2 ) . For renting or loaning to any person
his or her license or diploma to be used as a license
or diploma to be used as a license or diploma of
such person.
Three (3 ) . For unprofessional conduct , or for

280 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
gross ignorance or inefficiency in his profession .
Unprofessional conduct shall mean employing what
are known as " cappers " or "steerers " to obtain
business ; the obtaining of any fee by fraud or mis-
representation ; willfully betraying professional
secrets ; employing directly or indirectly any stu-
dent or any suspended or unlicensed dentist to per-
form operations of any kind , or to treat lesions of
the human teeth or jaws , or correct malimposed
formations thereof ; making use of any advertising
statements of a character tending to deceive or mis-
lead the public ; advertising professional superiority
or the performance of professional services in a su-
perior manner ; advertising prices for professional
service ; advertising by means of large display , glar-
ing light signs , or containing as a part thereof
the representation of a tooth , teeth , bridge work
or any portion of the human head ; employing or
making use of advertising solicitors of free publicity
press agents ; or advertising and free dental work ,
or free examination ; or advertising to guarantee
any dental service , or to perform any dental opera-
tion painlessly ; or habitual intemperance or gross
immorality . The proceedings under this section may
be taken by the board from the måtters within its
knowledge , or may be taken upon the information
of another ; provided , however , that if the inform-
ant is a member of the board, the other members of
the board shall constitute the board for the purpose
of finding judgment of the accused . All accusations
must be in writing , verified by some party familiar
with the facts therein charged , and three copies
thereof must be filed with the secretary of the board .
Upon receiving the accusation the board shall , if it
deem it sufficient , make an order setting the same
for hearing , and requiring the accused to appear
and answer it at said hearing , at a specified time

CHAPTER 89 281
and place , and the secretary shall cause a copy of
the order and of the accusation to be served upon
the accused at least ten ( 10 ) days before the day
appointed in the order for said hearing . The ac-
cused must appear at the time appointed in the
order and answer the charges and make his defense
to the same , unless for sufficient cause the board
assign another day for that purpose . If he does not
appear the board may proceed and determine the
accusation in his absence . If the accused plead
guilty or refuse to answer the charges , or , upon the
hearing thereof , the board shall find them or any
of them true , it may proceed to a judgment of either
revoking his license or suspending it . The board
and the accused may have the benefit of counsel , and
the board shall have power to administer oaths ,
take the depositions of witnesses in the manner
provided the law in civil cases and compel them to
attend before it in person the same as in civil cases ,
by subpoena issued over the signature of the secre-
tary and the seal of the board and in the name of
the people of the State of Kentucky . Upon the rev-
ocation of any license the fact shall be noted upon
the records of the board of dental examiners and
the license shall be marked as canceled upon the
date of its revocation . The secretary of the board
shall, upon judgment of suspension or revocation
being entered , transmit to the county recorder
wherein the license of the licensee affected by such
judgment is recorded a copy of such judgment order ,
certified to as such by said secretary of the board,
for record, and the same shall be recorded in the
same manner and the same book wherein is kept
the record of the certificate to practice dentistry .
In case of the revocation of a license by the board
the licentiate whose license shall have been revoked
by the said board shall have the right to appeal

282 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
from said revocation within thirty ( 30 ) days of the
cancellation of said license . Such appeal shall be
to the circuit court in and for the county in which
was held the meeting of the board in which such
revocation was made . In case a person desires to
take such appeal he shall serve , or cause to be
served , upon the secretary of said board a written
notice of such appeal , which shall contain a state-
ment of the grounds of such appeal , and shall file
in the office of such secretary an appeal bond with
good and sufficient surety , to be approved by such
secretary , to the State of Kentucky , conditioned
for the speedy prosecution of such appeal and the
payment of such costs as may be charged against
him upon such appeal . Said secretary , within ten
( 10 ) days after the service of said notice of ap-
peal , and the filing and approval of said bond , shall
transmit to the clerk of the circuit court , to which
said appeal is taken , a certified copy under the seal
of said board of accusations on which the revoca-
tion , was based , the ground for appeal , together
with the notice and bond of appeal . The clerk of
such court shall thereupon docket such appeal causes
and they shall be tried in all respects as ordinary
civil actions and like proceedings shall be had
thereon . Upon such appeal said cause shall be tried
de novo . Either party may appeal from the judg-
ment of this circuit court to the Supreme Court in
the manner as civil actions may be appealed thereto .
The judgment shall be stayed from the date of the
approval of said bond until final determination of
said appeal .
FAILURE TO REGISTER LICENSE
Eight (8 ) . Any failure , neglect or refusal on the
part of any person obtaining a license to practice
to practice dentistry from the State Board of Dental

CHAPTER 89 283
Examiners , to register said license with the county
clerk of some county in this State within ninety days
from the date of the issual of such license , or on
removal to another county within the State , to
cause his license to be registered in the same manner
as above provided , shall work a forfeiture of such
license , and no license when once forfeited shall be
restored except upon payment to the said board of
the sum of twenty -five dollars for such neglect ,
failure , or refusal to register such license .
EXAMINATION FEES AND COMPENSATION TO MEMBERS
OF THE BOARD
Nine ( 9 ) . In order to provide the means for
carrying out and enforcing the provisions of this
Act , the said board shall charge each person apply-
ing for an examination for a license to practice
dentistry in this State an examination fee of forty
dollars , and in addition thereto , a fee of five dollars
for every license and five dollars for every dupli-
cate license issued by said board, and out of the
funds coming into possession of the said board under
the provisions of this Act , the members of said board
shall each receive as compensation the sum of ten
dollars for each day actually engaged in duties of
the office , and all necessary expenses incurred in
attending the meetings of said board ; provided that
the secretary of the board shall be allowed reason-
able salary to be fixed by the board and no per diem ;
all expenses shall be paid from the fees and penal-
ties received and recovered by the board under the
provisions of this Act ; provided that no part of
said expenses shall be paid out of the State Treas-
ury. All money received in excess of said per diem
allowances and other expenses , herein provided ,
shall be held by the Secretary -Treasurer of said
board as a special fund for meeting the other ex-
penses of said board, and for such use as the said

284 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
board may deem necessary in the enforcement of
this Act , and said board shall make an annual report
of its proceedings to the Governor on or before the
thirty -first ( 31st ) day of December each year, show-
ing all money received and disbursed by it pursuant
to this Act .
RENEWAL CERTIFICATES OF LICENSE
Ten ( 10 ) On or beforeOn or before the thirty-first ( 31st )
day of December each year , every dentist legally
licensed to practice dentistry in this State shall
transmit to the Secretary of the said Board of
Dental Examiners his signature and address , to-
gether with the fee of three dollars and the number
of his or her registration certificate , and receive
therefor a renewal license certificate . Provided that
the license shall not be renewed , if the holder thereof
has not been in active practice for five years im-
mediately preceding . Any license granted by said
board shall be canceled and annulled if the holder
thereof fails to secure the renewal certificate herein
provided for by March first (1st ) of each year, pro-
vided any license thus canceled may be restored by
the board upon the payment of fee of twenty -five
dollars if paid within one year after such cancella-
tion .
PENALTY FOR FRAUD IN OBTAINING CERTIFICATE OF
REGISTRATION
Eleven ( 11 ) . Any person filing or attempting to
file as his own the diploma or license of another , or
a forged affidavit of identification or qualification,
shall be deemed guilty of a felony and be punished
upon conviction by imprisonment in the penitentiary
of not less than one or more than five years .
Any person who shall assist any applicant to cheat
or deceive the examining board or any member
thereof, or who shall give or attempt to give assist-

CHAPTER 89 285
ance to any applicant in the course of his or her
examination , or assist any applicant who is not en-
titled shall upon conviction thereof be adjudged
guilty of misdemeanor .
DENTISTRY DEFINED
Twelve ( 12 ) Any person shall be regarded as
practicing dentistry , within the meaning of this Act ,
who shall diagnose , x-ray , or treat or profess to
treat, any of the diseases or lesions of the oral
cavity, teeth , gums , maxillary bones ( or their de-
pendent tissues ) , or extract teeth , or shall prepare
or fill cavities in human teeth , correct malposition
of teeth or jaws , or supply artificial teeth as sub-
stitutes for natural teeth , or administer anesthetics ,
general or local , or any other practice included in
the curricula of recognized dental colleges , or who
shall use the word " Dentist , " dental surgeon , dental
laboratory , the letters D. D. S. , D. M. D. , or other
letters or titles in connection with his or her name ,
or any other manner represent themselves as being
engaged in the practice of dentistry . Provided , that
nothing in this Act shall be so construed as to pre-
vent regularly licensed physicians and surgeons
from extracting teeth or treating any disease com-
ing within the province of the practice of medicine .
EXEMPTION FROM JURY SERVICE
Thirteen ( 13 ) . All dentists of this State shall
be exempt from service as jurors in any of the courts
of this State .
SIGNATURE , SEAL , ETC. , ON LICENSE
Fourteen ( 14 ) . All licenses issued by said board
shall bear a serial number , the full name of the ap-
plicant , the date of the issue , the seal of the board ,
and be signed by a majority of the members , and
attested by its President and Secretary ,

286 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
UNLAWFUL TO PRACTICE UNDER NAME OF A COMPANY
Fifteen ( 15 ) . It shall be unlawful for any per-
son or persons to practice or offer to practice
dentistry or dental surgery under the name of any
company , association , or corporation , and every
person or persons practicing or offering to practice
dentistry or dental surgery , shall practice under
his or her own respective name or names only, and
shall not use his name in connection with that of
another dentist .
PENALTY FOR PRACTICING WITHOUT LICENSE OR
CERTIFICATE
Sixteen ( 16 ) Any person who shall practice or
attempt to practice dentistry in this State without
having been licensed and registered for that pur-
pose , or who has had his license revoked or who
shall at the time have his license suspended , as
herein provided , or who shall violate any of the
provisions of this Act , for which no specific penalty
has been provided herein , shall be subject to fine
of not less than one hundred dollars nor more than
five hundred dollars for the first offense . Each
offense shall constitute a practicing of dentistry
within the meaning of this Act and each day that a
person shall hold himself out as practicing dentistry
within the meaning of this Act , and each day that
a person shall hold himself out as practicing in any
name except his own shall be deemed a separate
offense . Any person who shall be guilty of a second
offense shall be deemed guilty of a felony and upon
conviction shall be punished by a fine of one hun-
dred dollars nor more than five hundred dollars or
imprisonment of six months or both . The opening
of an office for the purpose of practicing dentistry ,
or to announce to the public in any way a readiness
to do any act defined herein as being dentistry , shall

CHAPTER 89 287
be deemed to engage in the practice of dentistry ,
within the meaning of this Act .
DISPOSITION OF FEES
Seventeen ( 17 ) . All fees , imposed and collected
under the provisions of this Act , shall be paid to
the Secretary-Treasurer of the Kentucky State
Board of Dental Examiners for the use of said
Board .
DRUGGISTS MAY FILL DENTISTS ' PRESCRIPTIONS
Eighteen ( 18 ) . Legally licensed druggists of this
State may fill prescriptions of legally licensed
dentists of this State for any drug necessary to the
practice of dentistry . Dentists may sign death cer-
tificates the same as physicians , when necessary in
the line of their profession .
Nineteen ( 19 ) . This Act shall not prevent stu-
dents from performing dental operations under the
supervision of competent instructors within the
dental school , college , or dental department of a
university recognized by the Kentucky State Board
of Dental Examiners : Provided , the board at its
discretion may authorize the students of any dental
college , dental school , or dental department of a
university to practice dentistry , in any of the State
or municipal institutions , or public schools , or under
the board of health , or public clinic , or charitable
institution ; provided they are at all times under the
direct supervision of a licensed dentist in Kentucky ,
who is an instructor of the institution at which the
students are studying .
The provisions of this Act are severable , and if
any of its provisions shall be held unconstitutional
the decisions of courts shall not affect or impair
any of the remaining provisions of this Act . It is
hereby declared as a legislative intent that this Act

288 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
would have been adopted had not such unconstitu-
tional provisions been included therein .
REPEALING FORMER DENTAL LAWS
Nineteen ( 19 ) . All laws or parts of laws in con-
flict with this Act are hereby repealed .
Approved February 18, 1936 .
CHAPTER 90 .
AN ACT to amend the following sections of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , one thousand nine hundred thirty ( 1930 ) Edition :
Sections one thousand five hundred ninety-nine a-one
(1599a -1) , one thousand five hundred ninety -nine a-three
( 1599a -3) , one thousand five hundred ninety -nine a -four
( 1599a -4 ) , one thousand five hundred ninety -nine a -eight -a
(1500a -8-a) , one thousand five hundred ninety -nine a-ten ,
( 1599a -10 ) , one thousand five hundred ninety -nine a-seventeen
( 1599a -17 ) , and the following sections of Baldwin's one thou-
sand nine hundred thirty -three ( 1933 ) Supplement to Carroll's
Kentucky Statutes : Sections one thousand five hundred
ninety-nine a -seven (1599a -7 ) , one thousand five hundred
ninety-nine a-eight ( 1599a -8 ) , and one thousand five hundred
ninety-nine a-eleven ( 1599a -11 ) .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One ( 1 ) . That Section one thousand five
hundred ninety-nine a-one ( 1599a - 1 ) of Carroll's
Kentucky Statutes , one thousand nine hundred thirty
( 1930 ) Edition be , and the same is hereby amended
so that when so amended the same shall read as fol-
lows :
The Board heretofore kown as the State Board of
Embalming of Kentucky shall hereafter be known
and designated as the State Board of Embalmers
and Funeral Directors of the State of Kentucky .
The said Board shall consist of five members to be
appointed by the Governor , and all vacancies occur-
ring on the Board shall be filled by the Governor

CHAPTER 90 289
for the unexpired term . Each member of said
Board shall serve for a term of four years from
date of his appointment . The members of said
Board as now constituted shall continue in office un-
til their respective terms expire, thereafter all ap-
pointees of the Governor on said Board shall be
residents of the State of Kentucky , shall be licensed
embalmers and practicing funeral directors with a
minimum of five years consecutive experience in the
practice of embalming and the care and disposition
of dead human bodies and funeral directing in this
State immediately preceding their appointment .
Section Two ( 2 ) That Section one thousand five
hundred ninety-nine a -three ( 1599a - 3 ) of Carroll's
Kentucky Statutes , one thousand nine hundred thirty
(1930 ) Edition be , and the same is hereby amended
so that when so amended the same shall read as
follows :
The Governor shall furnish each person appoint-
ed to serve on the State Board of Embalmers and
Funeral Directors of the State of Kentucky a cer-
tificate of appointment , each member to be selected
from three names to be furnished each year by the
State Funeral Directors Association , and such ap-
pointee shall qualify by taking the usual oath of
office before any officer authorized by law to admin-
ister oaths in this State within ten days after such
appointment has been made and this fact shall be
noted on the certificate of appointment and filed with
said Board .
Section Three ( 3) That Section one thousand
five hundred ninety-nine a-four ( 1599a -4 ) of Car-
roll's Kentucky Statutes , one thousand nine hun-
dred thirty ( 1930 ) Edition be , and the same is here-
by amended so that when so amended the same shall
read as follows :
The said Board of Embalmers and Funeral Di-
Acts- 10

290 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
rectors of the State of Kentucky shall have the power
and it shall be its duty ( a ) to select out of its own
members a President , and it may also select a Secre-
tary and Treasurer and adopt and promulgate and
enforce such rules and regulations for the transac-
tion of its business and the management of its af-
fairs and the betterment and promotion of the edu-
cational standards of the profession of embalming
and the standards of service and practice to be fol-
lowed in the profession of embalming and funeral
directing in the State of Kentucky as it may deem
expedient and consistent with the laws of the State
of Kentucky .
All the members of the said Board with the ex-
ception of the Secretary shall serve without com-
pensation but shall be reimbursed for their neces-
sary traveling expenses and other necessary ex-
penses incident to their attendance upon the busi-
ness of the Board , and in addition thereto the sum
of ten dollars ( $ 10.00 ) per diem , not to exceed sixty
( 60 ) days per year actually spent by said mem-
bers upon the business of the Board . The Secretary
shall receive and be paid an annual salary not to ex-
ceed two thousand four hundred dollars ( $ 2,400.00 )
per annum , the amount and method of payment of
which shall be fixed by said Board , and in addition
thereto such Secretary shall receive his necessary
traveling and other incidental expenses incurred in
the performance of such duties , and all such ex-
penses , per diem , and compensation shall be paid
out of the receipts of the Board and/or any ap-
propriation made therefor by the General Assembly
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky .
(b) To adopt a common seal ; to meet at least
once in each year and oftener as the proper and
efficient discharge of its duties may require . The
Secretary of the said Board shall mail notices to

CHAPTER 90 291
all embalmers and undertakers in this State known
to him at least fifteen days before any embalmers '
or undertakers ' examination is to be held by said
Board , advising them of the time and place where
such examination is to be conducted . All notices
to be required to be mailed shall be directed to the
last known post office address of the party to whom
the notice is sent .
(c) There may be recommended by said Board
an agent whose title shall be " Field Counsel ' for
the State Funeral Directors . This recommendation
by the Board must be ratified by the State Funeral
Directors Association at their regular June meet-
ing each year , the majority of members present
voting . The duties of the Field Counsel shall be
and is authorized to enter the office , premises , estab-
lishment, or place of business of any embalmer or
funeral director in the State of Kentucky or any
office , premises , establishment , or place of business
where embalming or funeral directing is carried on ;
for the purpose of inspecting said office , premises ,
establishment , or place of business and for the pur-
pose of inspecting the license and registration of
embalmers and funeral directors , and apprentices
operating therein ; also to make investigations in
cases where violations have been made that are con-
trary to the provisions of this Act ; said Agent or
"Field Counsel " shall receive such compensation as
the State Association and State Board of Em-
balmers and Funeral Directors may determine .
(d) To employ such clerical help or assistance
as may be necessary to carry out the provisions of
this Act , the term , condition , and compensation of
and for such employment to be designated by the
Board .
(e) To prescribe standards of efficiency as to the
qualification of those engaged and who may engage

292 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
in the practice of embalming and undertaking in
connection with the care and disposition of dead
human bodies in this State .
Section Four (4) That Section one thousand
five hundred ninety-nine a - seven ( 1599a -7 ) and Sec-
tion one thousand five hundred ninety-nine a - eight
( 1599a - 8 ) , Baldwin's Supplement , one thousand
nine hundred thirty -three ( 1933 ) , to Carroll's Ken-
tucky Statutes , be , and the same is hereby amended
so that when so amended the same shall read as
follows :
After the passage of this Act no person not here-
tofore licensed or registered in this State shall be
granted a license to engage in the practice of em-
balming dead human bodies within the Common-
wealth of Kentucky , who has not at the time of mak-
ing application for such license attained the age
of twenty -one ( 21 ) years , nor shall such person be
granted such license unless at the time of making
said application , such person shall have a high
school education , the same to be evidenced by a cer-
tificate or diploma from a high school or a satisfac-
tory showing to the Board of having standard
credits or units which would entitle such person to
a high school diploma or certificate , providing this
Act shall not effect any person now engage in em-
balming.
Such person shall have had , before making ap-
plication for license a course of study of not less
than twenty -six ( 26 ) weeks in the science and art
of embalming , disinfection and sanitation in a reg-
ular school of embalming recognized by said State
Board of Embalmers and Funeral Directors .
Each person who applies for license to engage in
the practice of embalming must have served an ap-
prenticeship of three years under a licensed em-
balmer and undertaker, said embalmer and under-

CHAPTER 90 293
taker must have an established place of business ,
and said apprenticeship must be served in three
consecutive years , when beginning on this appren-
ticeship the person must file a certificate of regis-
tration with the State Board of Embalmers and
Funeral Directors as an assistant , paying a fee of
five dollars ($5.00 ) . This certificate must have the
name of the person under whom he will work , the
date of registration and a sworn statement that the
person who is filing will do actual service as a paid
assistant in the preparation and care of dead human
bodies and will devote the major part of his working
hours to such service .
Every person desiring to engage in the practice
of embalming dead human bodies in the Common-
wealth of Kentucky shall make a written applica-
tion to the State Board of Embalmers and Funeral
Directors , accompanying the application with a fee
of twenty -five dollars ( $25.00 ) and in said applica-
tion shall give the board full and detailed informa-
tion as to the requirements above set out with refer-
ence to age, school , education , education in the
school of embalming , apprenticeship and experience
in embalming , such information to be given on blanks
furnished by the Board for such purpose . The ap-
plicant shall furnish the Board with two certificates
of good moral character of the applicant , same to
be signed by a licensed embalmer and a licensed
undertaker engaged in business in Kentucky . There-
after , and within a reasonable time , the Board shall
notify the applicant of the time and place to be
fixed by the Board for an examination of the ap-
plicant for license .
Upon such examination , if the Board shall find
the applicant of the required age ; of good moral
character , possessed of the requisite school credits ,
and to have served the required three years ap-

294 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
prenticeship as above set out under a member of
an established firm , the Board shall issue a license
to said applicant to engage in the practice of em-
balming and the care and preparation of the dead
and shall register such applicant a certificate or
license signed by a majority of the Board and
authenticated by the Board's seal .
Such persons as may receive such certificate or
license shall have said license or certificate regis-
tered in the office of the County Court Clerk of the
county of his residence and shall pay the clerk of
said court a fee of one dollar ( $ 1.00 ) for such regis-
tration , and shall display said license or certificate
in a conspicuous place in the business of such
licensee .
Section Five ( 5 ) That Section one thousand five
hundred ninety -nine a -eight -a ( 1599a - 8 -a ) of Car-
roll's Kentucky Statutes , one thousand nine hun-
dred thirty ( 1930 ) Edition , be and the same is here-
by amended , so that when so amended the same shall
read as follows :
Every person not heretofore licensed or regis-
tered in this State desiring to engage in the prac-
tice or business of funeral directing shall make
written application to the State Board of Embalm-
ers and Funeral Directors , accompanying the same
with a license fee of Twenty -five Dollars ( $25.00 ) ,
containing the name , age, and place of business of
the applicant , the name and place of business of the
firms under who he had served three consecutive
years as a registered assistant , and that he had
served as a paid assistant in the performance of the
duties of an assistant in the care and preparation
of dead human bodies , being paid the salary of a
registered assistant ; that he is of good moral char-
acter , twenty -one years of age or over , accompanied
with proofs of such facts , by affidavit or otherwise ,

CHAPTER 91 .
AN ACT authorizing the various counties of the Commonwealth
to contract with cities owning and operating Hospitals for
Hospitalization for Poor and Indigent persons .
3

CHAPTER 92 299
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
One ( 1 ) . That the various Counties of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky be and they are authorized
to enter into contracts with any city in the Common-
wealth owning and operating its own municipal or
city hospital for hospitalization of poor and indigent
persons , residents of the counties so contracting ,
who may require hospitalization and are financially
unable to pay for same , and to pay for same out of
the County funds ; provided , however , that in any
county of the Commonwealth wherein there is no
city -owned hospital , the Fiscal Court of such county
or counties are authorized to enter into such con-
tracts with any privately owned hospital of such
county , or if there is no hospital in such county ,
then said Fiscal Court may enter into such contract
with a hospital of some adjoining county and pay
therefor as herein provided .
Two (2) All laws and parts of laws in con-
flict herewith are hereby repealed to the extent of
such conflict . Approved February 18 , 1936 .
CHAPTER 92 .
AN ACT to amend and re -enact Section one ( 1 ) of Chapter
one hundred forty-five (145 ) of the one thousand nine hun-
dred thirty -four Acts of the General Assembly of Ken-
tucky, creating a Public Service Commission and providing
for the regulation and control of utilities within the Common-
wealth of Kentucky as the word "utilities " is therein defined ,
and being Section three thousand nine hundred fifty -two -one
(3952-1 ) of the Kentucky Statutes as shown by Baldwin's
one thousand nine hundred thirty -four ( 1934 ) Supplement to
Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , one thousand nine hundred thirty
(1930 ) Edition .

300 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One ( 1 ) . Definitions .
(a ) The term " corporation " , when used in this
act , includes private , quasi public and public cor-
porations , an association , a joint stock association ,
or a business trust .
(b ) The term " person " , when used in this act ,
includes a natural person , a partnership , or two or
more persons having a joint or common interest ,
and a corporation as hereinbefore defined .
(c ) The term " utility " or " utilities " , when used
in this Act , shall mean and include persons and
corporations or their lessees , trustees or receivers
that now or may hereafter own , control, operate or
manage (one ) any facility used or to be used for or
in connection with the generation , production , trans-
mission or distribution of electricity to or for the
public for compensation for lights , heat , power or
other uses ; ( two ) any facility used or to be used
for or in connection with the production , manufac-
ture, storage , distribution , sale or furnishing to or
for the public for compensation natural or manufac-
tured gas , or a mixture of same , for light , heat ,
power or other uses ; (three ) any facility used or to
be used for or in connection with the transporting or
conveying of gas , crude oil or other fluid substance
by pipe line to or for the public for compensation ;
(four ) any facility used or to be used for or in con-
nection with the diverting , developing , pumping , im-
pounding, distributing or furnishing water to or for
the public for compensation ; (five ) any facility
used or to be used for or in connection with the
transmission or conveyance over wire , in the air or
otherwise , of any message either by telephone or
telegraph for the public for compensation ; ( six ) any
C

CHAPTER 92 301
facility used or to be used for or in connection with
the transportation of persons or property by street ,
suburban or interurban railways for the public for
compensation : Provided , however , that for the pur-
poses of this act the term " utility" or " utilities "
shall not mean or include any city or town or water
districts established in pursuance of Chapter one
hundred thirty -nine ( 139 ) , Acts one thousand nine
hundred twenty -six ( 1926 ) , and amendments there-
to, owning, controlling , operating or managing any
facility or facilities enumerated in this paragraph .
(d ) The term " facility " or " facilities " when
used in this Act, shall be construed in its broadest
and most inclusive sense and shall include all prop-
erty , real , personal , tangible and intangible , and all
other means and instrumentalities in any manner ,
owned , operated , leased , licensed , or used , furnished
or supplied for , by , or in connection with the busi-
ness of any utility .
(e) The term " rate " , when used in this Act ,
shall mean and include the plural number as well as
the singular , and every individual or joint rate fare ,
toll , charge , rental or other compensation for service
rendered or to be rendered by any utility , and every
rule , regulation , practice , act , requirement or privi-
lege in any way relating to such rate , fare , toll ,
charge or other compensation , and any schedule or
tariff, or part of a schedule or tariff thereof .
(f) The term " service " , when used in this Act ,
is used in its broadest and most inclusive sense , and
includes every practice or requirement in any way
relating to the service of any utility , including the
voltage of electricity ; the heat units , the pressure of
gas ; the purity , pressure and quantity of water , and
in general the quality , quantity , and pressure of any
commodity or product used or to be used for or in
connection with the business of any utility .

302 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
(g) The term " commission " , when used in this
Act, shall refer to and mean the Public Service
Commission of Kentucky , unless otherwise indi-
cated .
(h ) The term " commissioner " , when used in
this Act , shall mean one of the members of the
commission . Approved February 21 , 1936 .

CHAPTER 94.
AN ACT to provide old age assistance : to prescribe qualifica-
tions and elgibility of persons entitled to same ; to provide
for the reimbursement by the Federal government of fifty
per cent ( 50 % ) of all amounts granted and expnded under
the terms of this Act ; to provide for the establishment of a
division of public assistance in and by the Department of
Public Welfare for the administration of this Act ; to pro-
vide for the personnel of said division of public assistance
and for the salaries thereof ; provide for the adjudication of
controversial claims arising under this Act in a court of com-
petent jurisdiction ; and to provide for the repeal of all laws
or parts of laws in conflict herewith . This Act shall be known
and cited as the " McCarthy -Ramey Old Age Assistance Act ."
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One . Persons eligible to receive old age
assistance .- Old age assistance shall be given under

304 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
www
this Act beginning July one ( 1 ) , one thousand nine
hundred thirty- six ( 1936 ) , to any person who
(a ) Has attained the age of sixty-five ( 65 ) years ;
(b ) Has income which , with contributions ,
money , subsistence , or service from relatives or
others , is inadequate to provide a reasonable sub-
sistence ;
(c) Is a citizen of the United States ;
(d ) Has been a resident of the State of Ken-
tucky for one year continuously preceding the date
on which application is made for old age assistance ,
and for at least five of the nine years immediately
preceding his application ;
(e) Is not at the time of receiving assistance an
inmate of any public or private institution , except
in the case of temporary medical or surgical care in
a hospital ;
(f ) Has not made a voluntary assignment or
transfer of property for the purpose of qualifying
for such assistance , except as hereinafter provided ;
and
(g) Is not because of his physical or mental con-
dition in need of continued institutional care .
Section Two . Amount of assistance .- It shall be
the duty of the State agency administering this Act
to provide a reasonable subsistence for those eligible
for old age assistance under this Act. The amount
of old age assistance to any such person shall be de-
termined by the State agency , with due regard to
the conditions existing in each case , and subject to
the rules and regulations and standards to be de-
vised and set up by the Department of Public Wel-
fare ; but in no case shall the amount awarded
any individual exceed the sum of fifteen dollars
($15.00 ) per month .
1

CHAPTER 94 305
Section Three . Application .- Any eligible per-
son may request old age assistance , and he shall
make his application to the State agency in writing ,
upon standard forms prescribed by the agency . An
inmate of a public or private institution may make
application while in such institution , but the assist-
ance, if granted , shall not be paid the applicant until
after he has ceased to be an inmate of such institu-
tion .
Section Four . Administration .- There is hereby
created in the Department of Public Welfare a di-
vision to be known as the division of public assist-
ance, which shall be in charge of and conducted by
a director , appointed by the Governor of the State
for a term of four years , but may be removed by the
Governor at any time at his pleasure . The salary
of the said director shall be three thousand six hun-
dred dollars ( $ 3,600.00 ) per annum . The director
shall appoint such assistants , investigators , and
clerical help as are necessary to properly adminis-
ter this Act ; and he shall fix the terms of employ-
ment and the compensation of such appointees ; pro-
vided , such appointments , terms , and salaries shall
be subject to the approval of the Governor . The De-
partment of Public Welfare shall prescribe the form
of , and print and supply the blanks for , applications ,
reports, affidavits and such other forms as it may
deem necessary and advisable . The Department of
Public Welfare shall make rules and regulations for
determining or establishing the date of birth or age
of all applicants for assistance , for determining the
need for assistance of all applicants , and for
further carrying out the provisions of this
Act , and shall determine and fix the amount
of assistance to be granted, which in no
case shall exceed fifteen dollars ( $ 15.00 ) per month .
The amount of assistance granted any applicant may

306 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
be changed or discontinued at any time upon investi-
gation of the need for such assistance . This Act
shall be administered uniformly throughout the
State , with due regard for the varying costs of liv-
ing in different parts of the State ; but there shall be
no discrimination between applicants as to amounts
granted , except as based on the varying costs of
living in different parts of the State . The director
of the division of public assistance and all official
representatives of the division are hereby author-
ized to administer oath to any person questioned for
the purpose of determining the eligibility for assist-
ance of applicant .
Section Five . Investigation of applicants.-
Whenever an application for old age assistance is
received , an investigation and record shall be
promptly made of the circumstances of the appli-
cant to ascertain the facts supporting the applica-
tion and to secure such other information as may
be required by the rules and regulations prescribed
by the Department of Public Welfare .
Section Six . Granting of assistance .-Upon the
completion of such investigation the Department of
Public Welfare shall decide whether the applicant is
eligible for old age assistance under this Act , the
amount of such assistance , and the date on which
payment shall begin . It shall notify the applicant ,
in writing, of the decision regarding his application ;
and , if any applicant is denied assistance or is
awarded an amount , deemed by him inadequate , or
if his assistance be revoked or modified he may ap-
peal to and have a fair hearing before , the division
of public assistance . All awards of assistance made
by the division of public assistance shall at all times
be subject to review by the Department of Public
Welfare .

CHAPTER 94 307
Section Seven . Recipient not to receive other
public assistance .-No person receiving old age as-
sistance under this Act shall at the same time re-
ceive any other relief from the State or from any
politcal subdivision thereof , except for medical and
surgical purposes .
Section Eight . Payment to individuals found in-
capable . The Division of Public Assistance shall
provide in its regulations for payments to aged in-
dividuals found by a court to be incapable of taking
care of his money , so that such assistance shall be
used solely for the benefit of such aged persons .
Section Nine. Discontinuance of payments.-
Payments of assistance granted under this Act shall
immediately be ceased for any good or valid cause .
Section Ten . Assignment of assistance .- All as-
sistance given under this Act shall be inalienable
by any assignment or transfer , and shall be exempt
from levy or execution under the laws of the State .
Section Eleven . Claims against the estate of as-
sisted person.-The total amount paid to any re-
cipient of old age assistance under this Act shall
constitute a lien upon the estate of such recipient .
On the death of a person receiving assistance under
this Act , or of the survivor of a married couple ,
both of whom were assisted , the total amount paid
as assistance , with interest , shall be allowed and de-
ducted from the estate by the court having jurisdic-
tion to settle the estate , and paid to the State . The
Deparment of Public Welfare shall require , as a
condition to granting assistance in any case , that
the applicant submit a properly acknowledged agree-
ment to reimburse the State for all assistance grant-
ed . At any time the Department of Public Welfare.
may execute and file with the clerk of the probate
court a certificate , in form to be prescribed by the

308 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Department of Public Welfare , showing the amount
of assistance paid to said person . In the event of a
controversy over the validity of a claim, either the
Department of Public Welfare or the claimant may
have it adjudicated in a court of competent jurisdic-
tion . One half of any recovery under this section
shall be paid to the United States in such form and
at such times as requested by the Social Security
Board ,
Section Twelve . Assistance ; how paid . — The De-
partment of Public Welfare , on or before the first
day of each month , shall certify to the Auditor of
Public Accounts the amount due and payable to each
person who has been granted old age assistance , and
the Auditor shall cause to be issued warrant on the
State Treasurer for the amount so certified . All
monies awarded and payable under the provisions
of this Act shall be paid from funds appropriated
by the General Assembly in its biennial appropria-
tion Act. The General Assembly shall also appro-
priate such sum as it deems necessary for the ex-
penses of administering this Act .
Section Thirteen . Fraudulent acts .-Any person
who by means of a willfully false statement or repre-
senting , by impersonation , or other fraudulent de-
vice not named herein , obtains , attempts to obtain ,
or aids or abets any person to obtain :
( a ) assistance to which he is not entitled ;
(b) greater assistance than that to which he is
justly entitled ;
or
(c) payment of any forfeited installment grant ;
(d) disposal of the property of the recipient of
assistance without the consent of the Department of
Public Welfare shall be guilty of a misdemeanor ,

CHAPTER 95 309
and fined not less than fifty dollars ( $ 50.00 ) nor
more than five hundred dollars ( $ 500.00 ) .
Section Fourteen . Federal participation .- The
Department of Public Welfare is hereby authorized
and directed to make to the Federal Government
such reports as may be required of it , and the De-
partment of Public Welfare shall make application
to the Federal Government , at such time and in
such manner as is prescribed in the Federal Social
Security Act and the rules and regulations there-
under, for such amounts of money as are provided
in the Social Security Act for aiding states to grant
old age assistance . All monies thus received from
the Federal Government shall be covered into the
State Treasury and used solely for paying of old age
assistance , except that such amounts as are provid-
ed by the Federal Government for the purpose of
defraying the expense of carrying out this Act shall
be employed and expended solely for that purpose .
Section Fifteen . Provisions severable .— The pro-
visions of this Act are severable . If any provision
hereof or the application of any provision to any
situation or person is declared unconstitutional or
otherwise invalid , such declaration shall not impair
the validity or affect the operation of any other pro-
vision or of its application .
Section Sixteen. Repealing clause .- All laws or
parts of laws in conflict herewith are hereby repealed
to the extent of such conflict .
Approved February 20, 1936 .
CHAPTER 95 .
AN ACT relating to real estate mortgages and vendor's liens ,
and providing that said mortgages and liens shall follow
standing timber, buildings , minerals , and improvements in
the nature of fixtures real , which are included in said mort-

310 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
gages or liens , and which are purchased or removed or
severed , without the written consent of the record holder
of said mortgage or lien , and converted to the use of another ;
and further providing for liability for such Acts and the
measure of damage therefor ; and declaring the existence of
an emergency , and providing that said Act shall become
effective immediately upon its passage and approval by the
Governor ; and repealing all Acts and parts of Acts and all
laws and parts of laws , in conflict herewith.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One ( 1 ) . Any person , firm or corpora-
tion who shall , with actual or constructive notice of
a recorded real estate mortgage or vendor's lien ,
and without the written consent of the record holder
of same , purchase or sever or remove from the land
covered by said mortgage or lien , any standing tim-
ber , buildings , minerals , or improvements in the
nature of fixtures real , covered by and included in
said mortgage or lien, and who shall convert same
to his own use , shall take same subject to said mort-
gage or vendor's liens , and the respective priorities
thereof, and shall be liable to the holders of said
mortgages or liens for a return of same or for the
reasonable market value of said property at the
time of the purchase or severance or removal and
conversion thereof ( at the option of the holder of
said mortgage or lien ) , provided , however , that no
holder of a mortgage or a vendor's lien shall recover
more than the amount of the indebtedness secured
by his mortgage or vendor's lien ; and provided fur-
ther that any and all amounts recovered by the said
holder of said mortgage or vendor's lien shall be
credited on the indebtedness secured thereby .
Section Two ( 2 ) Any term used in the singular
may , as required by the context hereof , be read as
plural and vice versa . And any term used in the

CHAPTER 96 311
masculine gender shall include the feminine or neu-
ter genders as well.
Section Three ( 3 ) If any provision of this Act
shall be held invalid , the remainder of the Act shall
not be affected thereby .
Section Four ( 4 ) Because of the fact that a
great amount of existing indebtedness is secured by
mortgages and vendors ' liens on real estate situated
within the Commonwealth of Kentucky ; and because
of the fact that the security of great numbers of said
mortgages and vendors ' liens is being endangered ,
impaired and destroyed in the manners above set
forth ; and because of the fact that the existing laws
do not afford adequate security to said holders of
said mortgages or vendors ' liens ; and because of the
fact that, for said reasons , the procurement of loans
or credit on such security is becoming difficult and
is being curtailed and terminated by certain large
and important lending agencies , both public and
private ; and because of the fact that the continuance
of the granting of loans and the advancement of
credit, based upon and secured by real estate mort-
gages and vendors ' liens , is necessary for the best
interests and general welfare of the people of Ken-
tucky -an emergency is hereby declared to exist ,
and this Act shall become of full force and effect im-
mediately upon its passage and approval by the
Governor .
Section Five ( 5 ) . All Acts and parts of Acts ,
and all laws and parts of laws , in conflict herewith ,
are hereby repealed . Approved February 6, 1936 .
CHAPTER 96 .
AN ACT providing for the acquisition by the Commonwealth
of Kentucky of the stereotype plates , books , copyrights and

312 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
all other proprietary rights of and in the first seventy -seven
(77 ) volumes of the Reports of the Court of Appeals of Ken-
tucky from the present non -resident owners thereof , The W. H.
Anderson Company , in order that the Commonwealth máy
acquire , own , preserve and control the publication and sale
of the same , and to provide for the negotiations and the pur-
chase of same , subject to the consent and approval of the
Trustees of the Law Library , to wit : The Judges of the
Court of Appeals and the Attorney General ; and to provide
for the storage of said property and for the reprinting , sale
and distribution of said Reports in the future ; and providing
an appropriation to pay for same in the event of purchase .
WHEREAS , the copyrights and the stereotype plates pertain-
ing to the first seventy -seven ( 77 ) volumes of the Reports of
the Court of Appeals of Kentucky , are now owned by a non-
resident corporation , to wit : The W. H. Anderson Company of
Cincinnati , Ohio .
WHEREAS , it is to the interest of the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky and the citizens thereof, that the same be preserved ,
and that the Commonwealth acquire , own and control all the
rights pertaining to the aforesaid Court Reports . Now,
therefore ,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One ( 1 ) . That the Law Librarian is
hereby directed , authorized and empowered to ne-
gotiate with the present owners of the said stereo-
type plates , copyrights , books and all other pro-
prietary rights in the first seventy - seven ( 77 ) vol-
umes of the Reports of the Court of Appeals of Ken-
tucky, for the purpose of purchasing same for the
Commonwealth of Kentucky , and that subject to the
consent and appoval of a majority of the Trustees
of the Law Library , the said Law Librarian is here-
by directed , authorized and empowered to enter into
a contract for the purchase of said property on and
after the effective date of this Act at a cost not to
exceed fifteen thousand dollars ( $ 15,000.00 ) . In
the event of a purchase of said property , the Law
Librarian , subject to the approval of a majority of

CHAPTER 97 313
the Trustees of the Law Library , shall provide for
the permanent ownership , control and distribution
of the said Reports by the Commonwealth .
Section Two ( 2 ) . In the event of the acquisition
of said property , the storage , sale and distribution
of the said Reports , and the reprinting and sale of
any volumes which may be exhausted , shall be vested
in and under the supervision of the officials as pro-
vided in Section nine hundred fifty -five a- one
(955a - 1 ) , to and including Section nine hundred fifty-
five a-ten ( 955a - 10 ) , of the Kentucky Statutes , Car-
roll's one thousand nine hundred thirty ( 1930 ) Edi-
tion, pertaining to the control , sale and distribution
of the current Reports of the Court of Appeals .
Section Three (3 ) . In the event of a purchase of
the aforesaid property , the payment for the prop-
erty acquired under the provisions of this Act , is to
be made out of the general fund of the Common-
wealth , and the Auditor shall draw and issue a war-
rant in payment of the purchase price . The pro-
ceeds received from the printing , sale and distribu-
tion of any or all of said first seventy - seven ( 77 )
reports is to be credited and paid into the general
fund of the Commonwealth .
Section Four ( 4 ) . There is hereby appropriated
out of the general fund of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky , a sum sufficient to pay the purchase price
of said property and rights , in the event of a pur-
chase thereof, not to exceed the sum of fifteen thou-
sand dollars ( $15,000.00 ) .
Section Five ( 5 ) . All laws , or parts of laws in
conflict herewith , are now hereby repealed .
•
Approved February 25 , 1936 .
CHAPTER 97,
AN ACT to provide for the certification of property assessment
in annexed territory to the annexing authority : to provide

314 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
for the equalization of such assessment and levy of tax
thereon by the annexing authority .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One . That all assessments of prop-
erty heretofore made or hereafter made in territory
annexed thereafter to another civil division , prior to
such annexation shall be certified by the assessor
making the same , to the annexing civil division and
by the proper authority thereof , submitted to its
board of equalization to be equalized as provided by
law ; and when so equalized the regular annual tax
of the annexing division shall be levied by the proper
authority thereof and placed upon its tax bills made
therefor and collected as its other taxes ; and said
taxes if allowed to become delinquent , shall be sub-
ject to the same interest and penalty charged and
enforced in the manner provided by the law govern-
ing such annexing division .
Section Two . Should the equalization board
be about to adjourn when , or have adjourned before
said assessment is certified as provided in the pre-
ceding section , the time of such board may be ex-
tended for a reasonable time or it shall be recon-
vened for the purpose of passing upon such certified
assessment ; and should , for any reason , such assess-
ment and equalization be completed after the regu-
lar levy of taxes for that year have been levied , a
levy may thereafter be made as soon as practicable
for that year's tax .
Section Three . Whereas territories in this
State have been annexed at the last election , and the
property therein could otherwise not be taxed for
the ensuing year because the levies are directed in
the months of March and April , an emergency is

CHAPTER 98 315
hereby declared to exist and this Act shall take effect
immediately upon its passage .
Approved February 27 , 1936 .
CHAPTER 98 .
AN ACT relating to revenue and taxation and repealing Chap-
ter one hundred forty -eight of the Acts of the General As-
sembly of one thousand nine hundred thirty -four , being Sec-
tion four thousand nineteen ( 4019 ) , Kentucky Statutes , Bald-
win's one thousand nine hundred thirty -four ( 1934 ) Kentucky
Statute Service .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One . No State tax shall be levied , as-
sessed or collected on real estate . Provided , how-
ever , that nothing in this Act shall be construed as
releasing or prohibiting the collection of any real
estate taxes which may be due the State at the time
of the effective date of this Act.
Section Two . A tax at the rate of fifty cents
(50c ) upon each one hundred dollars ( $100.00 ) of
the value of all property which is directed by law
to be assessed for taxation except real estate , and
except as provided in paragraphs ( a ) and (b ) shall
be paid annually by the owner , person , or corpora-
tion assessed . ( a ) A tax at the rate of one -tenth of
one per cent shall be paid annually upon the amount
of deposits in any bank , trust company , or combined
bank and trust company , organized under the laws
of this State , or in any national bank of this State ,
as now provided by law ; and (b ) Building and Loan
Associations incorporated under the laws of this
State , and making loans to members only, shall be
assessed except as otherwise provided by law in the
manner and at the rate now set forth in Chapter
fourteen ( 14 ) of the Acts of the extraordinary ses-

316 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
sion of the General Assembly of one thousand nine
hundred seventeen ( 1917 ) [ Sections four thousand
nineteen a -six ( 4019a - 6 ) , four thousand nineteen a-
seven (4019a -7 ) , four thousand nineteen a - eight
( 4019a - 8 ) , Kentucky Statutes , Carroll's one thou-
sand nine hundred thirty ( 1930 ) edition ] . ( c) The
aggregate amount of taxes realized under this Act
shall be paid in to the State Treasury and credited
to the General Fund to be distributed and applied
as may be provided by law.
Section Three . The State Tax Commission shall
have no power to increase or decrease the aggregate
assessed valuation of the real estate of any county or
taxing district thereof , or to increase or decrease
the assessed valuation of real estate of any indi-
vidual , company , association , co -partnership or cor-
poration , either before or after the meeting of the
County board of supervisors . The State Tax Com-
mission shall have exclusive power in the assessment
of all minerals .
(Three a) It is hereby provided that, pursuant
to the provisions of this Act , the county tax com-
missioners shall be paid for making property tax
assessments for one thousand nine hundred thirty-
six ( 1936 ) taxes as provided by Section four thou-
sand forty-two a -eight (4042a - 8 ) , Kentucky Stat-
utes , Carroll's one thousand nine hundred thirty
( 1930 ) edition . For the assessments for taxes for
the year one thousand nine hundred thirty - seven
( 1937 ) and thereafter , the county tax commissioners
shall be compensated in the same way and manner
as provided for the taxes of the year one thousand
nine hundred thirty -six ( 1936 ) except that the fiscal
courts of the several counties shall pay county tax
commissioners all compensation occasioned by the
assessment of real property . The amount of com-
pensation due county tax commissioners on account

CHAPTER 99 317
of real estate assessments shall be computed on the
basis of the difference between the amount which
would have been due on account of personal prop-
erty assessments computed without respect to real
property and the amount which would have been due
on account of all property assessments .
Section Four . Chapter one hundred forty-eight
of the Acts of the General Assembly of one thou-
sand nine hundred thirty -four ( 1934 ) , being Section
four thousand nineteen (4019 ) , Kentucky Statutes ,
Baldwin's one thousand nine hundred thirty -four
( 1934 ) Kentucky Statute Service , hereby is repealed
in its entirety , and all other laws and parts of laws
in conflict herewith , to the extent of such conflict ,
hereby are repealed . Approved February 25 , 1936 .
CHAPTER 99 .
AN ACT to amend and re -enact Section four thousand two
hundred thirty -eight (4238 ) , Kentucky Statutes , one thousand
nine hundred thirty -three (1933 ) Supplement to Carroll's one
thousand nine hundred thirty ( 1930 ) , Edition , relating to
revenue and taxation , so as to limit the State tax on the
recording of chattel mortgage .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky .
One . That Section four thousand two hun-
dred thirty -eight ( 4238 ) , Kentucky Statutes , one
thousand nine hundred thirty -three ( 1933 ) , Supple-
ment to Carroll's one thousand nine hundred thirty
( 1930 ) Edition , be and the same is amended and re-
enacted so that said section as amended and re-
enacted shall read as follows :
On each original action or proceeding in the cir-
cuit court, commencing with or without original proc-
ess , and on each original action or proceeding in

318 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
•
other courts , when the amount in controversy , ex-
clusive of interest and cost , exceeds fifty dollars
($50.00 ) ; on each appeal to the circuit or quarterly
court ; on each traverse or forcible entry or detain-
er ; on each proceeding to revive a judgment ; fifty
cents (fifty cents ) ; on each deed or power of attor-

shall be paid before the survey is filed , but not to

( $5.00 ) for each survey in excess of ten acres ; for

office , or the duties of the register , twenty -five cents
(25c ) ; for issuing a land warrant , one dollar ( $ 1.00 ) ;
for each official certificate with seal of office affixed ,
one dollar ( $1.00 ) ; for a copy of a plat , fifty cents
(50c ) ; for a copy of a course and distances of any

CHAPTER 100 319
entry or land warrant , fifty cents ( 50c ) ; for a copy
of an assignment , twenty -five cents ( 25c ) ; for a copy
of a caveat , two cents ( 2c ) for every twenty words ;
