CHAPTER 100 .
AN ACT authorizing fiscal courts in counties having one or
more cities of the second class to appropriate county funds
for the aid and benefit of " Dependent " children committed to
recognized associations , institutions , or corporations situated
in such counties , and which embraces in its purposes the
care , guardianship , and subsistence of children , and stipulat-
ing limitation of appropriation therefor .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One . Appropriation by Fiscal Court .
That the fiscal court of each county of the State hav-
ing one or more cities of the second class may levy ,
collect and disburse such sums of money from the
general funds of its county as may be necessary to
comply with the provisions of this Act .
Section Two . The Dependent Children's Relief .
That each county having one or more cities of the
second class may contribute to the support of each
dependent child living within and under the care ,
guardianship , and subsistence of any recognized as-
sociation , institution , or corporation , being and lying
within such county , which embraces in its purposes
the care , guardianship , and subsistence of dependent
children, a sum not to exceed one hundred ( $ 100.00 )
dollars per annum to each such dependent child , said
sum to be known as " The Children's Relief " , and
payable quarterly in equal installments due in the
months , of February , May , August , and November ,
and on the first day thereof .

320 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Section Three . Children Eligible to Aid ; Applica-
tion ; Procedure . That before any such dependent
child shall be entitled to the benefits of this Act , such
child must first have been duly and regularly reg-
istered , and accepted as a ward of such association ,
institution , or corporation , at least ninety days prior
to application for the benefits of this Act, and such
association , institution , or corporation must file in
the office of the clerk of the county an affidavit sworn
to by the superintendent or other executive officer
of the association , institution , or corporation mak-
ing the application , in support of the acceptance and
registration of such child as contained in the ap-
plication ; also the said superintendent or other
executive officer shall file therewith a petition de-
claring the child named in the application is a de-
pendent child . The said clerk of the county shall
thereafter refer the application and petition to the
county judge , who shall make diligent investigation
as to the eligibility of the child as a " Dependent
Child " as defined by Section three hundred thirty-
one e-one ( 331e - 1 ) of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes.
of Edition of one thousand nine hundred thirty
( 1930 ) . If it be determined that the child is a " De-
pendent Child ", he shall thereafter commit said
child to the association , institution , or corporation
making the application , and said child be entitled to
the benefits as herein provided .
Section Four . This Act shall take effect and be in
force from and after its passage .
Approved February 27 , 1936 .
CHAPTER 101 .
AN ACT reepaling Chapter 25 of the Acts of the Extraordinary
Session of the General Assembly of one thousand , nine hun-
dred thirty -four known as the "Gross Receipts Tax Law of

CHAPTER 102 321
one thousand nine hundred thirty -four", and declaring an
emergency .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One . That Chapter twenty -five of the
Acts of the Extraordinary Session of the Gen-
eral Assembly of one thousand nine hundred thirty-
four , known as the " Gross Receipts Tax Law
of one thousand nine hundred thirty -four ," be
and the same hereby is repealed . Provided , how-
ever , that the enforcement provisions of said law
shall remain in full force and effect as to all taxes
which may have become payable under said Act , and
which have not been paid into the State Treasury ,
at the time of the effective date of this Act .
Section Two . Whereas the people of Kentucky
have expressed themselves as predominately in
favor of the repeal of the aforesaid " Gross Re-
ceipts Act of one thousand nine hundred thirty-
four" ; and , whereas said tax constitutes an un-
fair and unreasonable burden on certain classes of
tax payers , an emergency hereby is declared to exist
and this Act shall become effective immediately upon
its passage and approval by the Governor .
Approved January 15 , 1936 .
CHAPTER 102 .
AN ACT to relieve tax officials of cities of the first class against
carrying uncollectable city tax bills on personal property
that are more than five years overdue .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That all city tax bills on personal property in cities
of the first class , which are overdue for more than
five years, and are found by the city tax receiver
Acts- 11

322 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
to be uncollectable , shall be canceled and annulled
on certification by the city tax receiver to the city
assessor that they are uncollectable .
Approved February 18 , 1936 .
CHAPTER 103 .
AN ACT to repeal , amend , and re -enact Section three thousand
one ( 3001 ) concerning Tax Collection ; lien ; sale ; and fees .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Section three thousand one ( 3001 ) ; Ken-
tucky Statutes , Carroll's one thousand nine hundred
thirty ( 1930 ) Edition , be and the same is hereby re-
pealed and re -enacted as follows :
Section Three Thousand One ( 3001 ) . Collection ;
lien ; sale ; fees .-Immediately upon the filing of
these lists the tax receiver or one of his deputies
shall distrain the goods and chattels owned by, or
in the rightful possession of the person from whom
the tax is due , notwithstanding the existence of any
lien upon same , except that for State taxes , and may
proceed to sell the title of such person in so much
thereof as will pay the tax due , with penalties , in-
terest and costs . If there be no personal property
reasonably available , out of which said bills can be
collected by such distraint , the tax receiver shall sell
any real estate belonging to such delinquent tax-
payer, or enough thereof to pay the taxes due , with
interest, penalties , and costs except that where there
are bona fide mortgages or vendors ' liens of record
on real estate , only liens now given by law shall be
enforced by such sale . No warrant shall be required
for such distraint or sale of either personal or real
property , but the tax bill , unpaid in whole or in part ,
shall give sufficient power for such distraint and

CHAPTER 103 323
sales , and all subsequent proceedings thereunder .
He shall thus in each case make out of the property
of the person assessed , the amount of the tax bill , or
the unpaid part thereof, together with interest at
the rate of one-half ( 2 ) of one ( 1 ) percent per
month , or fraction of a month from the first of May
last past , and ten ( 10 % ) percent on the face of the
bill . Said tax bills shall have the force and effect
of an execution issued upon a judgment against the
delinquent , in addition to the tax lien now provided
by law . The city shall have a lien on personal prop-
erty as provided in the case of real property for its
taxes . A sale of personalty under such tax bills
shall be for cash , and the levy , advertisements , sale
and delivery to purchasers shall be made in like man-
ner as of goods levied under execution on replevin
bond . When such tax bills are against a married
woman , they may be also levied upon such goods of
her husband , nor exempt from levy under a like tax
bill against himself , and found on any of the lands
or improvements for which the married woman is
assessed . A sale of real property under such bills
shall be for cash , and in such cases the tax receiver
shall sell at public auction so much of the property
of the delinquent as shall pay the taxes due , with
penalties , interest and costs , in the same manner
that property is sold under execution , except that
the sale shall be had at the door of the city hall of
the city in which the land lies , and need not be ap-
praised or levied on . Such sale shall be advertised
by posting for fifteen days before this sale a written
or printed notice at the city hall door , and by pub-
lication at least four weeks prior to the day of sale
by one insertion in a newspaper published in the
English language in said city , selected by the gen-
eral council to print its proceedings and . ordinances ;
and the tax receiver shall , not less than fifteen days

324 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
before the sale , mail to the delinquent a postal card
addressed to his place of residence , or place of busi-
ness , if such can be ascertained , notifying him of
the time and place of the sale ; and in order to cover
the cost of said advertisement and notification the
tax receiver shall have two ( $ 2.00 ) dollars for each
person whose property is advertised to be paid by
the delinquent , but in no event to be paid by the City,
and the money so received shall be paid by the tax
receiver into the city treasury ; but the failure of
the tax receiver to send , or of the taxpayer to re-
ceive the mail notice of sale herein required , shall
not invalidate the sale or any subsequent proceed-
ings thereunder . Approved February 18 , 1936 .
CHAPTER 104 .
AN ACT to amend and re -enact Section three thousand one hun-
dred eighty -seven ( 3187 ) of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , one
thousand nine hundred thirty ( 1930 ) Edition , relating to the
sale of real estate for taxes , for cities of the second class .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Section three thousand one hundred eighty-
seven (3187 ) of Carroll's Kentucky Statutes ,
one thousand nine hundred thirty ( 1930 ) Edition ,
be amended and re - enacted so that said sec-
tion as amended and re -enacted shall read as follows :
Section Three Thousand One Hundred Eighty-
Seven ( 3187 ) . Sale of Real Estate for Taxes ;
Procedure . On the second Monday in Decem-
ber of each year , or as soon thereafter as
practicable , the treasurer shall sell а suf-
ficient amount of realreal estate , whether the
same be encumbered or not , or each delinquent
taxpayer to satisfy and discharge all unpaid taxes

CHAPTER 104 325
of such delinquent with interest and penalties there-
on and the further sum of two dollars ($ 2.00 ) for
the cost of advertisement and sale . Said sale shall
be made to the highest and best bidder for cash at
public auction , at or about noon , at the main en-
trance of the city building . One or more parcels of
real estate may be sold for all of the taxes of the
delinquent whether the same be for taxes on the real
estate sold or for the taxes on other real estate , real
or personal . When a parcel of real estate is offered
for sale , the treasurer will inquire if anyone will
buy an interest therein , less than the whole thereof,
and pay the taxes , interest , penalty and costs there-
on , and he will sell only so much thereof or such an
interest therein as will produce the amount to be
realized . If no one will take less than the whole of
the property and pay the taxes , interest, penalties
and cost thereon , he will sell the whole of the prop-
erty . If no one will bid said amount , it shall be
the duty of the treasurer to purchase the property
for the amount of said taxes , interest, penalties and
costs . If one or more persons , own a life estate or
other particular estate in the property sold with
remainder over to others , the treasurer will first
offer the particular estate for sale , and if no one will
bid enough therefor to pay the taxes , interest, pen-
alties and cost , he will then sell the whole property .
Except as otherwise provided herein , said sale shall
be made in the same manner that real estate is sold
by sheriffs under execution , except that no levy or
appraisal shall be necessary . Should any person
purchase property at such sale , and then fail to pay
for same , the treasurer shall immediately resell the
property . Real estate sold for delinquent taxes may
be resold, in like manner , for taxes for succeeding
years . If, for any reason , real estate is not sold at
the time fixed in this section , it may be advertised

326 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
and sold at any time thereafter in the manner here-
in provided . Before making said sale , the treasurer
will cause three notices thereof to be published in
the official newspaper of the city during the two
weeks period just preceding the day of sale , show-
ing the time , place and terms of the sale , the names
of delinquent taxpayers , numbers of the tax bills , the
total amount of taxes , interest, penalties and costs
due on each bill , and a brief description , by lot and
block numbers as shown on the city maps , or by such
other descriptions , sufficiently accurate to identify
each parcel of property to be sold . Within thirty
days after the sale the treasurer shall cause to be
delivered to each delinquent whose real estate has
been sold , or if the delinquent be an infant or a per-
son of unsound mind or a non -resident , then to his
guardian , committee or agent , a written notice of
the sale , the time within which the property can be
redeemed and the amount required to redeem it . If
notice is not served within thirty days , it may be
served as soon thereafter as practicable ; provided ,
that if the notice is not given six months before the
expiration of the period of redemption shall be ex-
tended until six months after the service of such
notice , but failure to give notice shall not defer the
purchaser's right to possession as provided in Sec-
tion three thousand one hundred eighty- seven c
(31870 ) . Such notice may be executed by any police
officer of the city or and other officer or person au-
thorized by law to serve a notice . Any purchaser
of property sold for taxes may cause such notice to
be given with the same effect as if given by the
treasurer . In either case the notice with the officer's
written return thereon , showing the time and man-
ner of service shall be returned to the treasurer's ,
office and recorded in a book provided for that pur-
pose and be preserved therein . In no event shall a

CHAPTER 105 327
deed be executed to a purchaser when notice has not
been given as required . All moneys received by the
treasurer for property sold for taxes under this and
preceding section , including money collected for
costs , shall be promptly accounted for and paid over
by him to the city in the same manner as taxes col-
lected in the usual and regular way.
This Act shall take effect and be in force from and
after its passage . Approved February 26 , 1936 .
CHAPTER 105 .
AN ACT to repeal and re -enact Chapter one hundred forty -two
(142 ) of the Acts of the General Assembly of Kentucky of
one thousand nine hundred thirty -two ( 1932 ) , the same being
Section four thousand one hundred fifty -one-two ( 4151-2 ) of
the one thousand nine hundred thirty -two (1932 ) Supplement
to the one thousand nine hundred thirty ( 1930 ) edition of
Carroll's Kentucky Statutes , and relating to redemption of
land sold for taxes and interest and penalty thereon and the
possession thereof.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Chapter one hundred forty -two ( 142 ) of the
Acts of the General Assembly of Kentucky of one
thousand nine hundred thirty -two ( 1932 ) , the same
being Section four thousand one hundred fifty one-
two (4151-2 ) of the one thousand nine hundred thir-
ty-two (1932 ) Supplement to the one thousand nine
hundred thirty ( 1930 ) edition of Carroll's Kentucky
Statutes , be and the same is hereby repealed and
amended and re -enacted to read as follows :
Section Four Thousand One Hundred Fifty - One-
two ( 4151-2 ) . Land sold for taxes ; redemption ; pos-
session ; clerk may collect delinquent tax ; duties of
county judge . When real estate is sold for taxes ,
if no one will bid for and purchase such real estate

328 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
·
at the amount of tax, interest, penalty and cost , in-
cluding the cost for advertising and the sheriff's or
collector's commission , it shall be the duty of the
sheriff or collector , to purchase the same for the
state , county and taxing district having taxes against
the delinquent for the amount of tax due , interest,
penalty , cost and commissions thereon , and he shall
make return to the county clerk as provided in
Section forty (40 ) [ K. S. four thousand one hundred
sixty-two ( 4162 ) of Article eight ( 8 ) ] , and the clerk
shall certify same to the auditor of public accounts ,
and the sheriff or collector shall have credit for the
amount of such tax due the State in the settlement
with the auditor of public accounts . The owner of
such real estate , his representatives , heirs or as-
signs , shall have the right to redeem the same from
the State , county and district or any other purchaser
at any time within five years after the day of sale ,
by paying the purchase money , with interest at the
rate of six per centum ( 6 % ) per annum , and in ad-
dition two per centum (2% ) penalty upon the total
amount of the purchase price and the amount of all
costs . The State , county and district shall have the
right to possession of lands purchased by them at
any time after the expiration of the five ( 5 ) years
allowed for redemption by giving thirty days
notice to the owner , and the purchaser other than
the State , county and district , shall have the right
to possession of the lands purchased by him at any
time after the expiration of five years allowed for
redemption by giving six months notice to the owner
thereof . In the redemption of lands sold to the State ,
county and district , for delinquent taxes at any
time within the period of five years after the sale ,
or until the revenue agent , under the direction of
the auditor of public accounts , assumes charge of
the collection by sale or otherwise , the county clerk

CHAPTER 105 329
is hereby vested with the authority to collect such
delinquent taxes , interest, cost , commissions and
penalties as prescribed by law, and he shall make
report thereof to the auditor of public accounts , the
treasurer of the county and the proper officer of the
district as often as the latter officers may require ,
and not less than once in every thirty days ; and he
shall pay into the state treasury all moneys collected
by him due the State , and the county treasury all
moneys due the county , and all moneys due the dis-
trict to the proper officer thereof , as herein author-
ized , on the first day of every month and oftener if
required by the auditor of public accounts or either
of the other officers mentioned . Said report of the
county clerk showing the name of each owner and
each piece of property redeemed , the year's taxes for
which delinquent , the assessed valuation for the
same for each year, and the total amount paid to the
State , county and district for redemption shall be
examined by the judge of the county court , and if
correct certified to the auditor of public occounts ;
together with the total sum due the State , as shown
on said report , less five per centum (5 %) commis-
sion for the collection which he shall retain for his
services .
Nothing contained herein or in Chapter one hun-
dred forty-two ( 142 ) of the Acts of one thousand
nine hundred thirty -two ( 1932 ) of the General As-
sembly of Kentucky , shall be construed as affecting
the duties of the county attorney , with reference to
the giving of notice as provided in Section four thou-
sand one hundred fifty -three (4153 ) , Carroll's Ken-
tucky Statutes , or as affecting his compensation as
provided in said section .
Approved February 26 , 1936 .

330 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
1
CHAPTER 106 .
AN ACT authorizing and empowering the State Park Commis-
sion to acquire lands , equipment and facilities for park and
recreational purposes ; and to issue bonds for funds to acquire
same ; bonds to be payable from revenues ; and to operate
such parks and facilities ; conferring powers of eminent
domain ; and prescribing rules ; and to repeal all conflicting
laws .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
One . That the State Park Commission is
hereby authorized and empowered to acquire lands
by purchase , lease , rentals or otherwise on which to
locate and operate state parks and such facilities ,
equipment and devices as may be advantageous
thereon ; and to acquire by purchase , lease , rental
and/or to build or otherwise place , create and pro-
cure equipment , facilities , devices and conveniences
in state parks and to operate same for the enjoy-
ment , recreation , education and convenience of the
general public and to levy and collect fees for the use
of state parks , their facilities , equipment , devices
and conveniences .
Two . To defray the costs of acquiring the
land , facilities , devices equipment and conven-
iences mentioned in Section one ( 1 ) of this Act and
to fix all such in condition for proper use the State
Park Commission may issue or cause to be issued
negotiable bonds , provided that such bonds , includ-
ing interest and maturity payments , shall be pay-
able from the revenues accruing from the operations
of the parks , facilities , devices , equipment and con-
veniences , and such bonds shall not bear interest in
excess of six per centum per annum and pro-
vided that each issue of such revenue bonds shall be
designated for one individual park and the proceeds
from each bond issue shall be expended only for and

CHAPTER 106 331
in the park designated and the revenues from each
individual park for which bonds have been issued
shall only be used for the costs of operations , main-
tenance and extensions within that park and the
payment of interest and maturities of the bonds is-
sued for that park .
Three. That the State Park Commission shall
adopt rules and regulations as may be neces-
sary and/or advantageous for the operation , main-
tenance and use of such State parks and such rules
and regulations shall fix fees and charges for the use
of the parks , facilities , devices , equipment and con-
veniences as will produce sufficient revenue annually
to pay the interest accrued on bonds and in addition
an annual proportionate sum which will equal the
maturity payments on bonds when bonds mature
and , such other costs or charges for operations ,
maintenance or other items as may be required and ,
such rules and regulations shall include the setting
up of an accounting system to show the exact re-
ceipts and expenditures of each State park separate-
ly and each facility , device , equipment or conven-
ience in each State park separately and , provide for
setting aside and sacredly keeping a sufficient por-
tion of the revenues in each year to pay the accrued
interest on bonds and an annually proportionate sum
which will equal the maturity payments of bonds
when due .
Four . The State Park Commission is hereby
authorized to exercise the right of eminent domain
in securing and acquiring lands upon which to
establish State parks , and to prosecute condemna-
tion proceedings in accordance with laws already
established .
Five . All laws or parts of laws in conflict with

332 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
this Act are hereby repealed to the extent of such
conflict . Approved February 27 , 1936 .
CHAPTER 107 .
AN ACT providing that the publication of a fair and impartial
report or the publication of the whole or a fair synopsis of
any bill, report, resolution , bulletin, notice , petition or pro-
ceeding before any State or city legislative or executive body ,
board , or officer , or the publication of any pleading , indict-
ment , warrant or other document in any criminal or civil
action or proceeding in any court of competent jurisdiction
shall be privileged , unless it shall be proved that such publi-
cation was maliciously made .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
The publication of a fair and impartial report of
the proceedings before any State or City Legislative
or Executive body , board or officers , or the whole
or a fair synopsis of any bill , report , resolution ,
bulletin , notice , petition , or other document present-
ed , filed , or used in any proceeding before any State
or City Legislative or Executive body , board or of-
ficer , shall be privileged , unless it shall be proved
that such publication was maliciously made .
The publication of a fair and impartial report or
the whole or a synopsis of any indictment , warrant ,
affidavit , pleading or other document in any crim-
inal or civil action or proceeding in any court of
competent jurisdiction , shall be privileged , unless it
is proved that the same was published maliciously ,
or that the defendant has refused or neglected upon
request of the plaintiff to publish a reasonable ex-
planation or contradiction thereof , giving the ex-
planation or contradiction the same prominence and
space as given the original publication , or that the
publisher has refused upon the request of the

CHAPTER 108 333
plaintiff to publish the subsequent determination of
such suit or proceeding . Provided that nothing in
this Act shall authorize the publication of any in-
decent matter . Approved February 25 , 1936 .
CHAPTER 108 .
AN ACT giving the consent of the Commonwealth of Kentucky
to the acquisition by the United States , under any Act of the
Federal Congress , of such lands as may be needed for the
establishment of a national forest reserve within the
boundaries of said Commonwealth , either to be permanently
retained and maintained by the Federal Government , or
eventually to be turned over to the Commonwealth under the
terms of the particular Federal statute under which they
were established .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One ( 1 ) . That the consent of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky is hereby given to the ac-
quisition by the United States Government , by pur-
chase , or gift, or by condemnation according to law
of such lands within the boundaries of the Common-
wealth as in the opinion of the Federal Government
may be needed for the establishment of national
forest reserves within prescribed areas within the
boundaries of the Commonwealth : Provided , that
the written approval and consent of the Governor
of this Commonwealth shall be given as to the area
of lands so acquired by the Federal Government , and
which shall be evidenced of record in his office be-
fore such establishment : And , provided , further ,
that the Commonwealth shall retain a concurrent
jurisdiction with the United States in and over such
lands so far that civil process in all cases , and such
criminal process as may issue under the authority
of the Commonwealth against any person charged

334 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLŸ
with the commission of any crime without or within
said jurisdiction , may be executed in like manner as
if this Act had not been passed .
Section Two ( 2 ) . When such forest reserves are
established by the Federal Government as herein
provided , either under existing Federal statutes or
any that might be hereinafter enacted , then consent
of this Commonwealth is hereby given for the Con-
gress of the United States to pass such laws and
to make or provide for the making of such rules and
regulations , of both civil and criminal nature , and
provide punishment therefor , as in its judgment
may be necessary for the management , control , and
protection of such land as may be , from time to time ,
acquired by the United States under the provisions
of this Act . Approved February 18 , 1936 .
CHAPTER 109 .
AN ACT relating to unfair competition and discrimination ,
making certain unfair and discriminatory practices unlawful ,
defining the duties of the Attorney General in regard thereto ,
declaring certain contracts illegal and forbidding recovery
thereon , providing for actions to enjoin unfair competition
and discrimination and to recover damages therefor, making
the violation of the provisions of this Act a misdemeanor and
providing penalties .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One . It shall be unlawful for any per-
son , firm or corporation , doing business in the State
of Kentucky and engaged in the production , manu-
facture , distribution or sale of any commodity , or
product, or service or output of a service trade , of
general use or consumption , or the product or serv-
ice of any public utility , with the intent to de-
stroy the competition of any regular established

CHAPTER 109 335
dealer in such commodity , product , or service , or to
prevent the competition of any person , firm , private
corporation , or municipal or other public corpora-
tion , who or which in good faith , intends and at-
tempts to become such dealer , to discriminate
between different sections , communities or cities or
portions thereof , or between different locations in
such sections , communities , cities or portions
thereof in this State , by selling or furnishing such
commodity , product or service at a lower rate in
one section , community or city, or any portion
thereof, or in one location in such section , commu-
nity , or city or any portion thereof than in another
after making allowance for difference , if any , in the
grade or quality , and in the actual cost of transpor-
tation from the point of production , if a raw prod-
uct or commodity , or from the point of manufac-
ture , if a manufactured product or commodity .
Motion picture films when delivered and under a
lease to motion picture houses shall not be deemed
to be a commodity or product of general use , or con-
sumption , under this Act . This Act shall not be
construed to prohibit the meeting in good faith of a
competitive rate , or to prevent a reasonable classi-
fication of service by public utilities for the pur-
pose of establishing rates . The inhibition hereof
against locality discrimination shall embrace any
scheme of special rebates , collateral contracts or
any device of any nature whereby such discrimina-
tion is , in substance or fact , effected in violation of
the spirit and intent of this Act .
Section Two . Any person who , either as direc-
tor, officer , or agent of any firm or corporation
or as agent of any person , violating the provisions
of this Act , assists or aids , directly or indirectly ,
in such violation shall be responsible therefor
equally with the person , firm or corporation for

336 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
whom or which he acts . In the prosecution of any
person as officer , director or agent it shall be suf-
ficient to allege and prove the unlawful intent of
the person, firm or corporation for whom or which
he acts .
Section Three . It shall be unlawful for any per-
son , partnership , firm , corporation , joint stock com-
pany, or other association engaged in business
within this State , to sell , offer for sale or advertise
for sale any article or product, or service or output
of a service trade , at less than the cost thereof to
such vendor , or give , offer to give or advertise the
intent to give away any article or product, or serv-
ice or output of a service trade for the purpose of
injuring competitors and destroying competition ,
and he or it shall also be guilty of a misdemeanor
and on conviction thereof shall be subject to the
penalties set out in Section Eleven of this Act for
any such act . The term " cost " as applied to pro-
duction is hereby defined as including the cost of
raw materials , labor and all overhead expenses of
the producer ; and as applied to distribution "cost "
shall mean the invoice or replacement cost , which-
ever is lower, of the article or product to the dis-
tributor and vendor plus the cost of doing business
by said distributor and vendor . The "cost of
doing business " or " overhead expense " is defined
as all costs of doing business incurred in the con-
duct of such business and must include without lim-
itation the following items of expense : Labor ( in-
cluding salaries of executives and officers ) , rent ,
interest on borrowed capital , depreciation , selling
cost , maintenance of equipment , delivery costs ,
credit losses , all types of licenses , taxes , insurance
and advertising .
Section Four . In establishing the cost of a given
article or product to the distributor and vendor , the
�
.

CHAPTER 109 337
invoice cost of said article or product purchased at
a forced bankrupt , closeout sale , or other sale out-
side of the ordinary channels of trade may not be
used as a basis for justifying a price lower than
one based upon the replacement cost as of date of
said sale of said article or product replaced through
the ordinary channels of trade , unless said article ,
or product is kept separate from goods purchased
in the ordinary channels of trade and unless said
article or produce is advertised and sold as mer-
chandise purchased at a forced bankrupt , closeout
sale or by means other than through the ordinary
channels of trade, and said advertising shall state
the conditions under which said goods were so pur-
chased , and the quantity of such merchandise to be
sold or offered for sale .
Section Five . In any injunction proceeding or
in the prosecution of any person as officer , director
or agent , it shall be sufficient to allege and prove
the unlawful intent of the person , firm or corpora-
tion for whom or which he acts . Where a particu-
lar trade or industry , of which the person , firm or
corporation complained against is a member , has
an established cost survey for the locality and vicin-
ity in which the offense is committed , the said cost
survey shall be deemed competent evidence to be
used in proving the costs of the person , firm or cor-
poration complained against within the provisions
of this Act .
Section Six. The provisions of Sections Three ,
Four and Five shall not apply to any sale made :
(a ) In closing out in good faith the owner's stock
or any part thereof for the purpose of discontinu-
ing his trade in any such stock or commodity , and
in the case of the sale of seasonable goods or to the
bona fide sale of perishable goods to prevent loss
to the vendor by spoilage or depreciation , provided

338 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
notice is given to the public thereof : ( b ) When the
goods are damaged or deteriorated in quality and
notice is given to the public thereof ; ( c ) By an offi-
cer acting under the orders of any court ; (d )
In an endeavor made in good faith to meet the
legal prices of a competitor as herein defined sell-
ing the same article or product , or service or out-
put of a service trade , in the same locality or trade
area . Any person , firm or corporation who per-
forms work upon , renovates , alters or improves
any personal property belonging to another , person ,
firm or corporation , shall be construed to be a vend-
or within the meaning of this Act .
Section Seven . The secret payment or allow-
ance of rebates , refunds , commissions , or unearned
discounts , whether in the form of money or other-
wise, or secretely extending to certain purchasers
special services or privileges not extended to all
purchasers purchasing upon like terms and condi-
tions, to the injury of a competitor , and where such
payment or allowance tends to destroy competition ,
is an unfair trade practice , and any person , firm ,
partnership , corporation , or association resorting
to such trade practice shall be deemed guilty of a
misdemeanor and on conviction thereof shall be
subject to the penalties set out in Section Eleven of
this Act .
Section Eight . Upon the third violation of any
of the provisions of Sections one to seven , inclu-
sive , of this Act , by any corporation , it shall be the
duty of the Attorney General to institute proper
suits or quo warranto proceedings in any court of
competent jurisdiction for the forfeiture of its char-
ter , rights , franchises or privileges and powers ex-
ercised by such corporation , and to permanently
enjoin it from transacting business in this State .
If in such action the court shall find that such cor-

CHAPTER 109 339
poration is violating or has violated any of the pro-
visions of Sections one to seven , inclusive , of this
Act , it must enjoin said corporation from doing
business in this State permanently or for such time
as the court shall order , or must annul the charter ,
or revoke the franchise of such corporation .
Section Nine . Any contract , express or implied ,
made by any person , firm or corporation in viola-
tion of the provisions of Section one to seven , in-
clusive , of this Act , is declared to be an illegal con-
tract and no recovery thereon shall be had .
Section Ten . Any person , firm , private corpora-
tion or municipal or other public corporation , or
trade association , may maintain an action to enjoin
a continuance of any Act or acts in violation of Sec-
tions one to seven , inclusive , of this Act , and if in-
jured thereby , for the recovery of damages . If, in
such action , the court shall find that the defendant
is violating or has violated any of the provisions
of Sections one to seven , inclusive , of this Act , it
shall enjoin the defendant from a continuance
thereof . It shall not be necessary that actual dam-
ages to the plaintiffs be alleged or proved . In ad-
dition to such injunctive relief , the plaintiff in said
action shall be entitled to recover from the defend-
ant three times the amount of the actual damages ,
if any , sustained . Any defendant in an action
brought under the provisions of this section may
be required to testify ; in addition , the books and
records of any such defendant may be brought into
court and introduced , by reference , into evidence ;
provided, however , that no information so obtained
may be used against the defendant as a basis for a
misdemeanor prosecution under the provisions of
Sections one to seven , inclusive , and eleven , of
this Act .
Section Eleven . Any person , firm or corpora-

340 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
tion , whether as principal , agent , officer , or direc-
tor, for himself , or itself , or for another person , or
for any firm or corporation , or any corporation
who or which shall violate any of the provisions of
Sections one to seven , inclusive , of this Act , is
guilty of a misdemeanor for each single violation
and upon conviction thereof , shall be punished by a
fine of not less than one hundred ( $ 100 ) dollars nor
more than one thousand ($1000 ) dollars , or by im-
prisonment not exceeding six months or by both
said fine and imprisonment , in the discretion of the
court .
Section Twelve . If any section , sentence , clause
or phrase of this Act is for any reason held to be
unconstitutional such decision shall not affect the
validity of the remaining portions of the Act . The
Legislature hereby declares that it would have
passed this Act , and each section , sentence , clause
or phrase thereof , irrespective of the fact that any
or more other sections , sentences , clauses or phrases
be declared unconstitutional . The remedies herein
prescribed are cumulative .
Section Thirteen . The Legislature declares that
the purpose of this Act is to safeguard the public
against the creation or perpetuation of monopolies
and to foster and encourage competition by prohib-
iting unfair and discriminatory practices by which
fair and honest competition is destroyed or pre-
vented . This Act shall be literally construed that
its beneficial purposes may be subserved .
Section Fourteen . This Act shall be known and
designated as the " Unfair Practices Act ."
Approved February 24 , 1936 .
CHAPTER 110 .
AN ACT authorizing water districts in counties of this State
which have been organized and operating under and in pur-

CHAPTER 110 341
suance to an Act of the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky , approved March twenty -seven ( 27 ) , one
thousand nine hundred twenty-six (1926 ) , and the amendments
thereto , to issue refunding bonds .
WHEREAS , in establishing water districts in pursuance to
the above Act , it became necessary to levy and assess against
the property in said district , large and excessive assessments ,
and
WHEREAS , from experience in some of the districts , it was
ascertained that a great portion of the property which was
included in the original district could not be benefited from
the creation of said water district and that other properties
not included in the said water district , lying within munici-
palities and exempted from the assessments , is and will con-
tinue to receive great benefits by the creation of said water
districts , and
WHEREAS , some of the property which was originally as-
sessed in the districts have since been stricken off and out
of the district by an order of the court , as is provided by the
Act , making it impossible to receive sufficient revenue from
said assessments to pay the bonded indebtedness which was
created in pursuance to said Act in establishing said district ,
and
WHEREAS , to put an additional assessment on the property
holders within the district , who have already been over-
burdened by the original assessment , would be unjust and
inequitable , Now, therefore ,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One (1) That Water Districts in coun-
ties of this State which have been organized and
operating under and in pursuance to an Act of the
General Assembly of the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky, approved March twenty - seven ( 27 ) , one
thousand nine hundred twenty -six ( 1926 ) , and the
amendments thereto , are hereby authorized and em-
powered to issue refunding bonds for the purpose
of refunding any bonded debt , including assessment
bonds which have heretofore been issued under the
said Act and upon which a default in the payment
of principal and interest have occurred and such

342 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
other bonds as may hereafter mature in whole or in
part .
Section Two ( 2 ) Said bonds shall be issued
under the signature of the Chairman of the Board
of Commissioners , the counter - signature of the
Treasurer of said Commission and the seal of the
district , serially numbered , and in such form and
denominations , and to run for such time or times ,
not exceeding thirty years , however , and to mature
and be redeemable , as the Board of Commissioners
in said Water District shall prescribe by ordinance
or resolution . Said bonds shall bear , not exceeding
six per cent ( 6 % ) per annum interest, as the Board
of Commissioners may direct , payable either an-
nually or semi -annually , and shall have interest cou-
pons attached , and shall not be sold for less than par
and accrued interest , and their proceeds shall be
used exclusively for the refunding of such bonded
debts as herein set out and for no other purpose
whatsoever . In case any of the officers whose sig-
natures or counter -signatures appear on the bonds
or coupons , shall cease to be such officers before
delivery of such bonds , such signatures or counter-
signatures shall nevertheless be valid for all pur-
poses the same as if they had been in office until such
delivery .
Section Three ( 3 ) Upon the issuance of said
bonds , each of said Water Districts shall annually,
from delinquent assessment collection and other
revenues , carry to the sinking fund of the said
Water District , an amount sufficient to pay the an-
nual interest on said bonds and create a fund
for their purchase from time to time ; and whenever
there is a sufficient sum in the sinking fund over
and above the amount required for the payment of
said interest, it shall be used in the purchasing of
so many of said bonds as practical . Any and all

CHAPTER 111 343
such bonds shall have and are hereby declared to
have in the hand of bona fide holders all the qual-
ities of negotiable instruments under the law mer-
chant and shall not be subject to taxation .
Section Four (4) This being a case of emer-
gency , in the fact that Water Districts have bonded
debts , some of which are now past due and in
default, due to the fact that a number of the prop-
erty holders are now delinquent and in default on
the payment of their assessments and the foreclos-
ure of said lien at this time would cause said prop-
erty holder to sacrifice his property , and due to the
further fact that there are no provisions made in
the said law to permit the refunding of said bond ,
therefore , this Act shall take effect from and after
its passage and approval by the Governor .
Approved February 18 , 1936 .
CHAPTER 111 .
AN ACT to provide for the codification , revision and publica-
tion of the Statutes of Kentucky to create a committee to be
composed of four members to be known as the Statutes Com-
mittee , to be appointed by the Governor of Kentucky , from
a list of eight persons submitted by the Board of Commis-
sioners of the Kentucky State Bar ; to provide for copyright-
ing, annotation , publication and sale of said Statutes , and to
limit the price thereof ; and to provide for the reception of
said Statutes as prima facie evidence of the laws of the State
of Kentucky , and for filling any vacancies within said com-
mittee .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One . That the Governor of Kentucky be
and he is hereby directed , authorized and empow-
ered to appoint a committe composed of four mem-
bers to be selected from a list of eight persons ,
learned in law , submitted by the Board of Commis-

344 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
sioners of the Kentucky State Bar , to be known as
the Statute Committee , which members shall hold
office for four years . Vacancies in said Committee
shall be filled by the Governor from names sub-
mitted in the same manner , two names to be sub-
mitted for each vacancy to be filled .
Section Two . Said Committee shall revise ,
codify, annotate and publish the Statute laws of
Kentucky , including the Acts of one thousand nine
hundred thirty - six ( 1936 ) , or provide for the same
to be done , through contract with some person , firm
or corpoation, which the Committee is authoized to
do through receiving competitive bids , or otherwise ,
in their discretion .
Section Three . When so codified revised , anno-
tated and published , said Statutes shall be received
in all the courts as prima facie evidence of the
Statutory laws of Kentucky , in force at the time of
such publication .
Section Four . The Committee , in its discretion ,
shall formulate , prepare, and execute , or cause to be
formulated , prepared and executed , a definite law
for the revision , classification , arrangement , annota-
tion , indexing, printing , binding , publication , copy-
righting , and distribution of said Statutes .
Section Five . The cost of each copy of such
Statutes as so published shall not exceed the price
fixed and determined by the Committee , and the
Committee is authorized to fix and determine the
sale price of same to the State of Kentucky , its sub-
divisions , and to the bar of the State .
Section Six . Before acting , the Committee mem-
bers shall qualify by taking oath of office .
Section Seven . The Committee shall not alter
the language or sense of any Act of the General

CHAPTER 113 345
Assembly. Said CommitteeSaid Committee shall report to the
Board of Commissioners of the Kentucky State Bar ,
as and when required so to do .
Approved February 27 , 1936 .

Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the

That there is hereby established and created as a

wealth of Kentucky a road beginning at a point on

WHEREAS , the State Highway Commission has constructed

Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One . There is hereby established as a

ways in this Commonwealth the following route , to
wit :

poration limits of said city where said Robert and
Mary Avenue joins and connects with said Federal
Highway number sixty ( 60 ) .
Section Two . The Commonwealth of Kentucky ,
by and through its agent , the State Highway Com-
mission of Kentucky , be and it is hereby authorized
and directed to ascertain the actual cost of construc-

and within the corporate limits thereof, and after
having done so , said Highway Commission shall cer-
tify its findings of the cost of construction of said
highway through said city to the Auditor of Public
Accounts , who thereupon be and he is hereby au-
thorized and directed to draw his warrant upon the
State Treasurer payable to the order of the City
of Grayson for such sum as shall be certified to him
by said State Highway Commission and as provided
in Chapter one seventy -eight ( 178 ) of the Acts of
one thousand nine hundred twenty -eight ( 1928 ) .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

CHAPTER 408 .
RESOLUTION authorizing R. Young and Joseph Young to sue
the Commonwealth of Kentucky and the State Highway Com-
mission , or either .
WHEREAS on March twenty-third ( 23rd ) , one thousand nine
hundred thirty -five ( 1935 ) , Joseph Young of Daviess County ,
Kentucky , was driving a wagon and team , the wagon being
loaded with coal , along and over highway sixty ( 60 ) immedi-
ately east of Owensboro , in Daviess County , Kentucky , and was
traveling east on his right hand side of the road , a portion
of his wagon and team traveling on the shoulder of the road
on his right . Said team of horses and wagon was the property
of R. Young, and Joseph Young was driving said team of
horses and wagon as the agent and servant of R. Young .
Traveling west on said highway sixty ( 60 ) at said time were
two trucks owned by the State Highway Commission and at
the time engaged in its service . One of said trucks was at-
tached to the rear of the other by a cable some ten or twelve
feet long , and while being driven west along said highway
by an employee of the State Highway Commission the rear
truck was by the carelessness and negligence of the driver
permitted to sway from side to side of the road , and by reason
of the carelessness and negligence of the employees of the
State Highway Commission in charge of said trucks , said
rear truck was permitted to swing with great force across
said highway striking said wagon and team driven by Joseph
Young in such a manner as to demolish said wagon , kill both
horses , throwing the driver , Joseph Young , from the wagon
to the pavement severely injuring and bruising him around
the head and body to such an extent that the injuries he
received are permanent . Said accident was caused and brought
about by the trucks being used and operated at said time by

558 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
the employees of the State Highway Commission in the per-
formance of their duties , and
WHEREAS the said R. Young by reason of the carelessness and
negligence of the State Highway Commission and its em-
ployees engaged in the performance of their duties in using
said truck , is deprived of two horses and his wagon ; and
Joseph Young severely injured , bruised and permanently
injured by reason of said negligence , all of which damage
and injuries were caused and brought about by the alleged
carelessness and negligence of the employees of the State
Highway Commission in the conduct of the State's business ,
therefore ,
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That said R. Young and Joseph Young are hereby
authorized and permitted to sue the Commonwealth
of Kentucky and the State Highway Commission ,
or either , in the Circuit Court of the County of their
residence for such damages for personal injuries
and property they or either of them may have suf-
fered , if any, by reason of or received by them
through the carelessness or negligence of the State
Highway Commission , its agents or employees . Said
suit for R. Young for property damage shall be for
any amount not exceeding the sum of five hundred
( $500.00 ) dollars , and said suit for Joseph Young
for personal injuries received by him shall be for
any amount not exceeding the sum of three thousand
( $3,000.00 ) dollars , and in the event any judgment is
recovered by said R. Young for property damage or
by Joseph Young for personal injuries received by
him or same is compromised or settled , same shall
be paid by the Auditor of Public Accounts by war-
rants drawn on the State Treasurer and paid out of
the general fund .
Section Two (2 ) . Either party to said suit or
suits may appeal from any judgment which may be
entered therein as in any other civil suit, and the

CHAPTER 409 559
case may be settled and adjusted by and with the
consent and approval of the Attorney General of
Kentucky in the same way as any other civil case
may be settled or adjusted .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 409 .
SENATE RESOLUTION authorizing R. L. Bayne and Dell
Brown to sue the Commonwealth of Kentucky and the State
Highway Commission .
WHEREAS in the summer of one thousand nine hundred thirty-
four ( 1934 ) , the State Highway Commission of Kentucky con-
structed a "Y" junction over a strip of land obtained from
R. L. Bayne in Shelby County and adjacent to a field of
tobacco owned by said R. L. Bayne and Dell Brown and
whereas by the construction of said junction , dust , dirt , lime
and other damaging substances were blown and washed over
said tobacco greatly damaging it to the great loss to said
Bayne and Brown , depriving the said Brown of his year's
work ; therefore ,
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That R. L. Bayne and Dell Brown be and they
are hereby authorized and permitted to sue the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky and the State Highway Com-
mission in the Circuit Court of Shelby County , Ken-
tucky , for such damages as they may have sustained
by reason of the dirt and debris permitted to be
blown and thrown on their said tobacco .
No recovery shall be for a greater sum than seven
hundred and fifty dollars ( $750.00 ) .
No plea of limitation shall be interposed .
The Attorney General may investigate and com-
promise said claim if he deems advisable .
If recovery is had by suit or compromise , the
amount so adjudged or compromised shall be paid
by the Auditor of Public Accounts by warrant drawn

560 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
on the State Treasurer and paid out of the general
fund . Neither approved nor disapproved .

work and the more complete endowment and support of land-

eighty -two -seventy -fourth ( 182-74th ) Congress ] , and

Therefore ;

562 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Accounts who shall draw his warrant in favor of the
Clerk of the Senate of itemized accounts for said
employees . Said amount shall not include any
charge by the Clerk . Any unexpended funds here-
by appropriated shall revert to the general fund .
Since persons have been employed for stenographic
help and as Secretary to the Senate and have not
been paid for their services performed and there be-
ing no fund appropriated for paying them in the fu-
ture an emergency is declared to exist and this reso-
lution shall be effective upon its adoption by the Gen-
eral Assembly and approval by the Governor .
Approved February 4, 1936 .
CHAPTER 413 .
RESOLUTION for the relief of R. G. Holbrook , sheriff of Owen
County, Kentucky , for mileage and expenses incurred by him
as agent for the Commonwealth of Kentucky .
WHEREAS , R. G. Holbrook on March sixteenth ( 16 ) , one thou-
sand nine hundred thirtfy -four ( 1934 ) , was duly appointed
by the Governor of the Commonwealth of Kentucky as agent
of the Commonwealth to go to Greenville, North Carolina , to
present requisition papers of this Commonwealth to the Gov-
ernor of North Carolina and to bring back William Orville
Reed , a fugitive from the justice of this State , under indict-
ment for felony in Owen County , Kentucky ; and
WHEREAS , said R. G. Holbrook , under said appointment , pro-
ceeded to the State of North Carolina and to the city of
Greenville to bring back said fugitive but found on arrival
there that said fugitive had escaped and that his whereabouts
were unknown , although he enlisted the aid of North Carolina
officers and made a thorough search for said fugitive , and
was compelled to return to Kentucky without him ; and
WHEREAS the said R. G. Holbrook , in performing his said
duty as agent aforesaid , incurred expenses and traveled one
thousand four hundred twenty -six ( 1,426 ) miles to and from
North Carolina and paid a fee to the Governor of North Caro-
lina for honoring said requisition , amounting in all to the
sum of one hundred and two dollars and fifty-six cents
($102.56 ) ; and

CHAPTER 414 563
WHEREAS said R. G. Holbrook has heretofore made demand
of the Auditor of Public Accounts for payment of his said
expenses and that said Auditor has refused to pay him , it ap-
pearing that under Section one thousand nine hundred thirty-
four ( 1934 ) of the Kentucky Statutes , as construed by the
Kentucky Court of Appeals , that payment can not be made
where the agent of the Commonwealth returns without his
prisoner ; therefore ,
Be it Resolved by the Senate and House of Rep-
resentatives :
That the said amount of one hundred and two dol-
lars and fifty - six cents ( $ 102.56 ) be and is hereby
appropriated to be paid to the said R. G. Holbrook ,
and the Auditor of Public Accounts is directed to
draw his warrant upon the Treasurer for said
amount and deliver same to R. G. Holbrook for the
purpose aforesaid . Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 414 .
A JOINT RESOLUTION providing for the payment of five thou
sand nine hundred seventy -three dollars seventy -three cents
( $5,973.73 ) out of the General Expenditure Fund for the pur-
pose of paying to the State Journal Company , printers to the
Commonwealth , its claim for materials , printing and work
furnished , ordered and done by and for the various depart-
ments of the Commonwealth and for services rendered the
Commonwealth .
WHEREAS , the State Journal Company entered into a contract
with the Commonwealth of Kentucky to furnish material and
labor and to print for it and for the departments and agencies
thereof any order properly presented to it ; and
WHEREAS , in the years one thousand nine hundred thirty-
three ( 1933 ) , one thousand nine hundred thirty -four ( 1934 )
and one thousand nine hundred thirty -five ( 1935 ) , many orders
were presented to said State Journal Company for printing
by the various departments of the Commonwealth , all of
which were duly filled in the manner required , and thereafter
a bill was rendered to the Commonwealth , through the Audi-
tor , for payment , after approval by the heads of the various
departments and by the Department of Public Property ; and

564 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
WHEREAS , the Auditor of Public Accounts of the Common-
wealth admits the Commonwealth's liability and that the
claim should be paid but has refused to issue warrant for the
payment of said claim for the reason that no budget appro-
priation for its payment is available ; and
WHEREAS , said claim is a debt for labor and materials
furnished the Commonwealth by said State Journal Company
which is due , just and should have long since been paid ; now ,
therefore ,
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
One ( 1 ) . That there is hereby appropriated out
of the General Expenditure Fund of the Common-
wealth the sum of five thousand nine hundred
seventy -three dollars and seventy -three cents ($5 ,-
973.73 ) for the purpose of paying to the State Jour-
nal Company , printers to the Commonwealth , its
just claim for printing and work ordered and done
by and for the various departments of the Common-
wealth and for services performed and rendered the
Commonwealth of Kentucky by said State Journal
Company.
Two ( 2 ) . That it shall be the duty of the Auditor
of Public Accounts of the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky to draw his warrant upon the Treasurer for
said sum and when done it shall be the duty of the
State Treasurer to pay said warrant .
Three ( 3 ) . Because said claim is just , due and
should have long since been paid , an emergency is
declared to exist and this Act shall take effect and
be in full force from and after its passage and ap-
proval . Approved February 21, 1936 .
CHAPTER 415 .
RESOLUTION authorizing R. E. Stanley to sue the Common-
wealth of Kentucky and the State Highway Commission or
either.

CHAPTER 415 565
WHEREAS , in the year one thousand nine hundred twenty-five
( 1925 ) , by condemnation proceedings , the Commonwealth of
Kentucky acquired , from M. E. Anderson and N. B. Anderson ,
in Carlisle County , Kentucky , a strip of land sixty feet in
width and about one mile in length , for the purpose of con-
structing a hard surface road thereon , which road is at pres-
ent known as State project No. 1 and Federal project No. 131 ,
and
WHEREAS , a stream of water known as Truman Creek flowed
under said highway, and
WHEREAS , the State Highway Commission did begin the con-
struction of said highway where the present highway now
runs , from Bardwell , Kentucky , north to Wickliffe , Kentucky ,
in the year one thousand nine hundred twenty-six ( 1926 ) and
which highway was , by said Highway Commission , completed
in the year one thousand nine hundred thirty ( 1930 ) , and
WHEREAS , in the construction of said highway, the State
Highway Commission did cut some very deep barrow pits or
canals lengthwise with said highway and deeper than the
bed of Truman Creek and did divert the waters which had
formerly flown in Truman Creek from its original channel
and through said barrow pit for a distance of more than a
mile and which did cause the original channel of Truman
Creek to fill up with earth and sand by reason of the way the
barrow pits or canals were so cut or constructed , and
WHEREAS , the original channel of Truman Creek , on the east
side of said public highway and from the point where the
barrow pit or canal was cut from Truman Creek , lengthwise
with its highway, and which changed the water course so
that Truman Creek has not existed since , because it was filled
up with earth and sand for a distance of a mile and a half ,
and
WHEREAS in one thousand nine hundred twenty-eight (1928 ) ,
the State Highway Commission did plant a lot of growing
Willows in said barrow pit or canal , so constructed by it,
and did construct a wire fence in the bed and bottom of said
barrow pit or canal and that said Willows have grown in
great volume and , together with the fence construced therein ,
have caught new earth and sediment and have caused said
barrow pit or canal to fill up to such an extent that the
waters from Truman Creek will not flow therein but are
spreading over the lands of M. E. Anderson and N. B. Ander-
son and , yearly , since one thousand nine hundred twenty-

566 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
eight ( 1928 ) , have been backing up and filling up the channel
of Truman Creek with new made earth and sand , and
WHEREAS , R. E. Stanley owns a tract of land , consisting of
143 % acres , which lies just above and adjacent to and on the
west side of said highway and on Truman Creek, and
WHEREAS , in one thousand nine hundred thirty -two ( 1932 ) ,
one thousand nine hundred thirty -three (1933 ) , one thousand
nine hundred thirty -four ( 1934 ) and one thousand nine hun-
dred thirty -five ( 1935 ) , twenty acres of said tract of land of R.
E. Stanley begun to be overflown by the waters from Truman
Creek and which land has been overflown yearly with the
waters from Truman Creek for the years one thousand nine
hundred thirty -two ( 1932 ) , one thousand nine hundred thirty-
three ( 1933 ) , and one thousand nine hundred thirty -four ( 1934 )
and one thousand nine hundred thirty -five (1935 ) , which de-
stroyed the crops growing thereon, through the crop season ,
and has injured and destroyed the land, because of the filling
up of the channel of Truman Creek below his land and caus-
ing the waters to back up over his land and stand , and
WHEREAS , the waters of Truman Creek had never , prior to
one thousand nine hundred thirty -two ( 1932 ) , backed up and
overflown and injured the lands , fences , and crops thereon ,
and
WHEREAS , he has lost and has been damaged in his crops
and fences , which were washed down , injured and his lands
damaged , and
WHEREAS , all of which has been brought about solely by the
negligent construction and maintenance of said highway and
in the changing of the channel of Truman Creek, and
WHEREAS , the said R. E. Staley has been damaged and sus-
tained damages by reason of the careless and negligent con-
struction of said State highway by the State Highway Com-
mission in the construction of said highway, said barrow pit
or canal , and in the diversion of the waters of Truman Creek
in the filling up of said canal or barrow pit, since one thou-
sand nine hundred twenty -eight (1928 ) , and of the channel
of Truman Creek which caused the water , since one thousand
nine hundred thirty -one ( 1931 ) , to overflow the lands of R. E.
Stanley and destroy his growing crops and fences and injure
his lands , therefore ,
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That R. E. Stanley be and he is hereby authorized

CHAPTER 416 567
and permitted to sue the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky and the State Highway Commission , or either
of them , in the Circuit Court of the county of Car-
lisle , where said land is situated , for such damages
as he may have sustained , if any , by reason of in-
jury or damage received by him for the destruction
of growing crops during the years of one thousand
nine hundred and thirty -two ( 1932 ) , one thousand
nine hundred and thirty -three ( 1933 ) , one thousand
nine hundred and thirty -four ( 1934 ) , and one thou-
sand nine hundred and thirty -five ( 1935 ) , and the
use of said lands so covered with water , and his
fencing, and such damages or injury to his land by
reason of its having been overflown by the waters
from Truman Creek through the carelessness or
negligence of the State Highway Commission , its
agents or employees . Said suit shall be for any .
amount and , in the event a judgment is received by
said R. E. Stanley in said suit for damages or same
is compromised or settled , same shall be paid by
the Auditor of Public Accounts by warrant drawn
on the State Treasury and paid out of the general
funds .
Second , either party to said suit may appeal from
from any judgment which may be entered therein
and the same procedure shall be followed as is pro-
vided in the Civil Code for other civil suits and the
case may be settled and adjusted with the consent
and approval of the Attorney General of Kentucky
in the same way as any other civil case .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

RESOLUTION authorizing Boone Dick to sue the Common-

CHAPTER 416 569
filling up the channel of Truman Creek with new made earth

WHEREAS , Boone Dick owns a tract of land consisting of 171

WHEREAS , in one thousand nine hundred thirty -two ( 1932 ) ,

and
WHEREAS , the waters of Truman Creek had never , prior to
1932 , backed up and overflown and injured the lands , fences ,
and crops thereon , and
WHEREAS , he has lost and has been damaged in his crops and
fences , which were washed down , injured , and destroyed , and
his lands damaged , and
WHEREAS , all of which has been brought about solely by the

WHEREAS , the said Bone Dick has been damaged and sus-
tained damages by reason of the careless and negligent con-
struction of said State highway by the State Highway Com-
mission in the construction of said highway , said barrow pit
or canal , and in the diversion of the waters of Truman Creek
and in the filling up of said canal or barrow pit , since one
thousand nine hundred twenty -eight ( 1928 ) , and of the channel
of Truman Creek which caused the waters , since one thou-
sand nine hundred thirty -one ( 1931 ) , to overflow the lands
of Boone Dick and destroy his growing crops , fences , and
injure his lands : Therefore ,
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Boone Dick be and he is hereby authorized

570 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
and permitted to sue the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky and the State Highway Commission , or
either or them , in the Circuit Court of the
County of Carlisle , where said land is situated ,
for such damages as he may have sustained , if any ,
by reason of injury or damages received by him for
the destruction of growing crops during the years of
one thousand nine hundred thirty -two ( 1932 ) , one
thousand nine hundred thirty -three ( 1933 ) , one thou-
sand nine hundred thirty -four ( 1934 ) , and one thou-
sand nine hundred thirty -five ( 1935 ) , and the use
of said lands so covered with water, and his fencing
and such damages or injury to his land by reason
of its having been overflown by the waters from Tru-
man Creek through the carelessness or negligence of
the State Highway Commission , its agents or em-
ployees . Said suit shall be for any amount , and in
the event a judgment is recovered by said Boone
Dick in said suit for damages or same is compro-
mised or settled , same shall be paid by the Auditor
of Public Accounts by warrant drawn on the State
Treasury and paid out of the general funds .
Second , either party to said suit may appeal from
any judgment which may be entered therein and the
same procedure shall be followed as is provided in
the Civil Code for other civil suits and the case may
be settled and adjusted with the consent and ap-
proval of the Attorney General of Kentucky in the
same way as any other civil case .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

CHAPTER 418 .
RESOLUTION authorizing and permitting Charlie Coogle to sue
the Commonwealth of Kentucky and the State Highway Com :
mission or either .

574 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Section One ( 1 ) . WHEREAS in the fall of one thousand nine
hundred thirty -five ( 1935 ) , while driving an automobile on the
Elizabethtown -Vertrees Road , Charlie Coogle was run into by
a truck which was owned at the time and being operated by
the employees of the State Highway Commission and was
severely , painfully and permanently injured on and about the
shoulder, arm and hand , and
WHEREAS it is claimed that said collision was entirely caused
and brought about by the gross negligence and gross careless-
ness of the agents , servants and employees of the said State
Highway Commission in the operation of said truck belonging
to the State of Kentucky , or the State Highway Commission ,
and
WHEREAS he sustained said serious , painful and permanent
injuries because of the alleged carelessness and negligence of
the said employees while in the discharge and conduct of the
State's business : Therefore ,
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky
That said Charlie Coogle be, and he hereby is
authorized and permitted to sue the Commonwealth
of Kentucky and the State Highway Commission ,
or either in the Circuit Court of the county of his
residence , for such damages as he may have suf-
fered if any , by reason of any injuries received by
him through the carelessness or negligence of the
State Highway Commission , its agents or em-
ployees . Said suit shall be for any amount not ex-
ceeding the sum of two thousand five hundred dol-
lars ( $ 2,500.00 ) , and in the event any judgment is
recovered by said Charlie Coogle in said suit for
injuries to himself , or same is compromised or
settled , same shall be paid by the Auditor of Public
Accounts by warrant drawn on the State Treasury
and paid out of the general fund .
Section Two ( 2 ) . Either party to said suit may
appeal from any judgment which may be entered
therein as in any other Civil Suit and the case may
be settled and adjusted with the consent and ap-

CHAPTER 419 575
proval of the Attorney General of Kentucky in the
same way as any other civil case .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 419 .
RESOLUTION authorizing the personal representative of Sarah
Marks , and Gladys Madison and Daisy Carter, to sue the Com-
monwealth for damages for the death of said women who lost
their lives in the State Normal School at Frankfort .
WHEREAS , on December thirteenth ( 13 ) , one thousand nine
hundred twenty -six ( 1926 ) , the dormitory for girls at the Ken-
tucky Normal and Industrial College for colored persons near
Frankfort , Kentucky , was destroyed by fire , and
WHEREAS , Daisy Carter, and Gladys Madison , and Sarah Marks ,
and Annie May Pennington , each lost her life in said fire , and
WHEREAS , the family of each one named are worthy poor per-
sons , and
.WHEREAS , the General Assembly of one thousand nine hun-
dred thirty ( 1930 ) has already authorized a suit to be filed for
the death of said May Pennington , now , Therefore :
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That the personal representative of Sarah Marks ,
daughter of E. K. Marks of Fayette County , Ken-
tucky ; and the personal representative of Gladys
Madison , daughter of J. L. Madison of Wayne
County , Kentucky ; and the personal representative
of Daisy Carter , daughter of John Carter of An-
derson County , Kentucky ; be and each of them is
hereby empowered and authorized to file suit and
to prosecute appropriate actions against the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky for the purpose of deter-
mining the liability of the Commonwealth for such
death , if any liability there should be established .
In the event of judgment , same shall be paid by the
Auditor of Public Accounts by warrant drawn of
the State Treasurer . Such action may be brought

576 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
in the Franklin Circuit Court , the county where said
fire occurred , and all parties shall have the right of
appeal as in other civil cases .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 420 .
RESOLUTION authorizing Nina Ruth Bennett to sue the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky and the State Highway Commission ,
or either .
WHEREAS , on or about the twenty -nine (29th ) day of November ,
one thousand nine hundred thirty -four ( 1934 ) , Nina Ruth Ben-
nett, a resident of Bardstown , Nelson County , Kentucky , re-
ceived severe injuries to her back , spine , head and body while
traveling on the State highway leading from Springfield to
Perryville in Washington County , Kentucky , wherein it is
claimed that the said injuries were caused by the car in
which she was riding running on , over and across a large hole
in said highway about five feet wide and two feet deep at the
widest and deepest point and which hole extended across most
of the highway and that the Commonwealth of Kentucky
and/or the State Highway Commission by and through its
agents and employees knew or by the exercise of ordinary
care should have known of the existence of said condition
and that the defective condition of the highway was the proxi-
mate cause of her injuries , Therefore :
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Nina Ruth Bennett be , and she is hereby
authorized and permitted to sue the Commonwealth
of Kentucky and the State Highway Commission ,
or either , in the Nelson Circuit Court , the county of
the residence of said Nina Ruth Bennett, for dam-
ages for the loss of earning power, physical and
mental suffering and permanent injuries and for
hospital and medical bills of the said Nina Ruth
Bennett , which were all brought about and caused
by the negligence of the employees of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky and/or the State Highway Com-

CHAPTER 421 577
mission . Said suit shall be for any amount not ex-
ceeding five thousand ( $ 5,000.00 ) dollars , and in the
event any judgment is recovered by said Nina Ruth
Bennett in said suit for injuries to herself , or same
is compromised or settled , said judgment shall be
paid by the Auditor of Public Accounts by warrant
drawn on the State Treasurer and paid out of the
State Highway Fund .
Either party to said suit may appeal from any
judgment which may be entered therein as in any
other civil suits , and the case may be settled and

CHAPTER 421 .
RESOLUTION authorizing the personal representative of Clay
Lightfoot , deceased , and Rena Lightfoot to sue the Common-
wealth of Kentucky and the State Highway Commission of
the State of Kentucky or either of them .
WHEREAS , on or about November nineteen ( 19 ) , one thousand
nine hundred thirty -five ( 1935 ) , Clay Lightfoot , then sixty-six
( 66 ) years of age and able -bodied , and his wife , Rena Light-
foot, then seventy -two ( 72 ) years of age and able -bodied , of
Bowling Green , Warren County , Kentucky , while walking
along a public highway known as the Russellville Road , Fed-
eral Highway No. sixty-eight ( 68 ) , were injured by a truck
then and there owned and operated by the State Highway
Commission of the State of Kentucky then and there acting
through its agents and employees in the usual and customary
performance of their duties as such , and ,
WHEREAS , the said Clay Lightfoot , as the sole , direct and
proximate result of his injuries received at the time and place
stated by being struck by said truck , died on or about Decem-
ber fourteen (14 ) , one thousand nine thirty -five ( 1935 ) , and
Rena Lightfoot at the time and place stated was seriously ,
painfully and permanently injured in such a manner as to
incapacitate her from the performance of any labor whatever
for the remainder of her life , and ,
Acts- 19

578 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
WHEREAS , the said Clay Lightfoot at the time of his injuries
was earning seven hundred dollars ( $700.00 ) per year and
was required to expend for medical and hospital expenses
prior to his death as a result of his injuries three hundred
dollars ( $300.00 ) , and Rena Lightfoot was earning at the time
of her injuries two hundred dollars ( $ 200.00 ) per year and
was required to expend for medical attention , drugs , and
doctors ' bills for treatment as a result of her injuries three
hundred fifty dollars ( $350.00 ) , and ,
WHEREAS , the injuries of the said Clay Lightfoot , deceased ,
and the injuries of Rena Lightfoot , and of each of them , were
caused and produced by the carelessness and negligence of
the State Highway Commission then and there acting through
its agents and employees then and there in the performance
of their duties as such while they themselves , and each of
them , were free of any negligence whatever which attributed
to or in any wise brought about their said injuries , now ,
therefore :
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That the personal representative of the estate
of Clay Lightfoot , deceased , and Rena Lightfoot
be , and they hereby are , authorized and per-
mitted to sue the Commonwealth of Kentucky
and the State Highway Commission of the State
of Kentucky , or either of them, in the circuit
court held in and for Warren County in the State of
Kentucky , this being the county of their residence
and the county in which said collision occurred and
in which said claims for damages arose , for such
damages , if any , as they , and each of them , may have
suffered by reason of the injuries received and the
resulting death of Clay Lightfoot , deceased , there-
from , arising through the carelessness or negligence
of the State Highway Commission of the State of
Kentucky , then and there acting through its agents
or employees .
Section Two ( 2 ) . Said suit of the said Rena
Lightfoot shall be for an amount not exceeding the

CHAPTER 422 579
sum of five thousand dollars ( $ 5,000.00 ) , and said

counts by a warrant or warrants drawn on the State

as in any other civil suit , and each of said suits , may
