CHAPTER 422 .
A RESOLUTION authorizing Floyd Mays to sue the Common-
wealth of Kentucky and the State Highway Commission , or
either .
WHEREAS , on or about the ninth ( 9th ) day of September ,
one thousand nine hundred thirty -three (1933 ) , William Mays ,
a resident of Perry County , Kentucky , while employed as a
fireman on a Louisville -Nashville Railroad Company train , was
injured by reason of the engine , upon which he was riding and
working , turned over resulting in injuries to said Mays ; and ,
WHEREAS , it is the contention of said Mays that the said

the employees of the State Highway Commission of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky , in that they spread over the tracks

580 Acts of the GenERAL ASSEMBLY
six inches of black -top where the railroad tracks cross the
highway, at a crossing located on a sharp curve , and that no
notice was given to said Mays or those in charge of the train
of the fact that the said black-top had been placed on said
tracks , nor was any notice or signal given of this fact by any
of the employees of the State Highway Commission , and his
said engine in attempting to back across said highway was
caused to leave the rails resulting in the engine turning over
on the highway resulting in the injuries to said Floyd Mays .
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Floyd Mays be and he is hereby authorized
and permitted to sue the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky and the State Highway Commission , or either ,
in the Perry Circuit Court , the county of the resi-

accident occurred , for damages for the loss of earn-
ing power , loss of time from work , physical suffer-
ing and for hospital and medical bills of the said
Floyd Mays , which were all brought about and
caused by the negligence of the employees of the
State Highway Commission , and in the event any
judgment is recovered by Floyd Mays in said suit
for injuries to himself , or if same is compromised
or settled , said judgment shall be paid by the
Auditor of Public Accounts of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky by warrant drawn on the State Treas-
urer and paid out of the General Fund . Either
party to said suit may appeal from any judgment
that may be entered therein as in any other civil
suit, and the case may be settled or adjusted with
the consent and approval of the Attorney General
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky in the same way
as any other civil case .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

CHAPTER 425 .
SENATE RESOLUTION number thirty four ( 34 )
WHEREAS , it is the present policy of the Federal Government
to construet highways to connect the National Parks of the
United States , and
WHEREAS , the Mammoth Cave National Park in the State
of Kentucky , and the Great Smoky Mountain National Park
in Tennessee , are two of the most important National Parks
in the United States which are not now connected by Federal
Highways , and
WHEREAS , the Honorable Cordell Hull, a native Tennessean ,
and the present Secretary of State of the United States , has
rendered outstanding service to the nation , both as a member
of Congress and as Secretary of State , and a highway connect-
ing these two Great National Parks passes through the native
county and the former home of this distinguished statesman ,
and
WHEREAS , the General Assembly of the State of Tennessee
did , on July nineteenth ( 19 ) , one thousand nine hundred and
thirty -five ( 1935 ) , approve , and by such approval did establish
such a Memorial Highway to the honor of its distinguished
son , Therefore ,

584 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That in honor of this great and distinguished
Tennessean , Honorable Cordell Hull , and as a trib-
ute to his services to the Nation , that a highway
beginning at the Tennessee -Kentucky State line , on
Highway No. fifty -one ( 51 ) , near Boles , Kentucky ,
and running by way of Tompkinsville , Kentucky ,
to Glasgow, Kentucky , and thence to the Mammoth
Cave National Park, by the most direct and feasible
route between such points , being that part of the
highway connecting the Great Smoky Mountain Na-
tional Park and the Mammoth Cave National Park ,
be and the same is named and designated the Cor-
dell Hull Parkway . Approved February 25 , 1936 .

CHAPTER 428 .
RESOLUTION providing for the purchase of copies of the Legis
lative Digest adopted at the session of one thousand nine
hundred and twelve , and every succeeding session since that
date , as the official publication for the General Assembly
and State and certain other public officials .
WHEREAS , It is a matter of importance and convenience that
each Senator and Representative be advised as to the various
and detailed steps of legislation ; and ,
WHEREAS , This General Assembly for the past seventeen ces-
sions has approved and adopted as its official publication
"The Legislative Digest ," a publication under the editorship
and management of competent and experienced persons , the
purpose of which is to fully advise from day to day the
officials and public of the Commonwealth as to matters affect-
ing them in an intelligent and prompt manner : Therefore,
Be it Resolved by the Senate of Kentucky , the
House of Representatives concurring :
That the publishers of the Legislative Digest are
hereby directed to furnish daily a copy to each mem-
ber and the clerks of the General Assembly, and to

of this session . That the publishers are allowed the
sum of Ten Dollars for each subscription for the
Session and the Auditor of Public Accounts is
directed to draw his warrant upon the Treasurer for
said amount when such service has been concluded .
The total number shall not exceed two hundred
(200 ) copies , which are to be paid for under this
resolution . Neither approved nor disapproved .

CHAPTER 430 .
A RESOLUTION authorizing suits to be maintained for the
recovery of taxes paid under the provisions of Chapter 149
of the Acts of the General Assembly of one thousand nine
hundred thirty ( 1930 ) ; prescribing the venue and jurisdiction
for the bringing of such suits ; the manner in which they may
be prosecuted , and waiving any defenses which the Common-
wealth might assert on account of limitations and on account
of any failure to pay said taxes under protest .
WHEREAS , The General Assembly of one thousand nine hun-
dred thirty ( 1930 ) passed an Act which was approved by the
Governor on March seventeen ( 17 ) , one thousand nine hun-
dred thirty (1930 ) , which is compiled as Chapter one hundred
forty -nine ( 149 ) of the Acts of the General Assembly of one
thousand nine hundred thirty ( 1930 ) , imposing an excise or
license tax on retail merchants and prescribing heavy penal-
ties for the failure to report and pay the taxes therein im-
posed ; and ,
WHEREAS , The principal purpose of said Act was to tax chain
stores ; and
WHEREAS , The large chain stores and a number of other large

CHAPTER 430 589
stores filed suits in the Federal Courts to enjoin and did en-
join the State from collecting said taxes ; and ,
WHEREAS , In the case of Kroger Grocery and Baking Co.
et al. v. Lewis , et al ., two hundred ninety -four ( 294 ) U. S.
five hundred fifty ( 550 ) , fifty-five ( 55 ) S. Ct . five hundred
twenty - five ( 525 ) , the Supreme Court of the United States
held the aforesaid Act to be unconstitutional , and on April
twenty -ninth ( 29 ) , one thousand nine hundred thirty -five
( 1935 ) , overruled a petition for a rehearing filed on behalf
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky in said case ; and ,
WHEREAS , By reason of said decision and of the aforesaid
suits the large chain stores and other large merchants escaped
entirely the payment of said tax ; and ,
WHEREAS , Before said Act was held unconstitutional a large
number of merchants had paid said tax from year to year as
and when due to avoid the penalties in said Act ; and ,
WHEREAS , The total amount so paid into the State Treasury
for the five -year period from the effective date of the Act until
same was held unconstitutional amounts in the aggregate to
three hundred nine thousand one hundred thirty -six dollars
fifty six cents ( $309,136.56 ) ; and ,
WHEREAS , The Act under which said taxes were paid does not
authorize the taxpayers to maintain suits for a refund thereof
except within two years from the date said taxes were paid ,
and then only if paid under protest ; and ,
WHEREAS , They are justly entitled to a refund of said taxes ,
and the limitations imposed in said Act have barred the re-
covery of the taxes paid during the first three years of the
operation of said Act unless waived by the General Assembly
of Kentucky ; and ,
WHEREAS , If the State should fail to authorize a refund
thereof, the merchants who paid same would thereby be dis-
criminated against in favor of the large chain stores that
escaped the payment of said taxes ; Now, Therefore ,
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
One ( 1 ) . Any person , firm , or corporation that
paid into the State Treasury of Kentucky any taxes
under the provisions of Chapter one hundred forty-
nine ( 149 ) of the Acts of the General Assembly of

590 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
one thousand nine hundred thirty ( 1930 ) may file
with the Auditor of Public Accounts a sworn state-
ment as to the taxes so paid and interest thereon
from the date of payment , and the said Auditor of
Public Accounts may , if he deems it necessary , de-
mand additional proof as to the validity of said
claims , and if the Auditor of Public Accounts ' rec-
ords show the claim to be valid , he shall issue his
warrant upon the State Treasurer in favor of said
claimant for the amount due , and when the warrant
is presented to the State Treasurer , he shall issue
his check payable to the payee therein for the
amount of the warrant , and both the warrant and
the check shall be charged to the General Fund ; pro-
vided , further that in the event the Auditor of Public
Accounts refuses to issue his warrant upon the State
Treasurer , then in that event any person , firm or
corporation may file suit against the Auditor of
Public Accounts to recover the taxes so paid and
interest thereon from the date of each payment .
If the amount sought to be recovered be less than
two hundred dollars ( $ 200.00 ) , the suit may be filed
in the Quarterly Court of Franklin County . If the
suit be for more than two hundred dollars ( $200.00 ) ,
it may be filed in the Franklin Circuit Court . The
plaintiff must allege in the petition the amount of
taxes paid by the plaintiff in each year and the ap-
proximate date of payment , and may maintain one
suit for the total amount paid under said act , to-
gether with interest thereon from the date of each
payment .
Two ( 2 ) . Prior to the filing of any suit under
this resolution the taxpayer shall file with the
Auditor of Public Accounts his claim for a refund of
the taxes paid under said Act , stating the amount
and approximate date of each payment .

CHAPTER 430 591
Three (3 ) . The penalties imposed by Chapter
one hundred forty -nine ( 149 ) of the Acts of the
General Assembly of one thousand nine hundred
thirty ( 1930 ) being so severe it is deemed and con-
sidered that payments of said taxes under the pro-
visions of said Act were made under compulsion ;
and , whether expressly paid under protest or not ,
any and all defenses based upon failure to pay said
taxes under protest hereby are waived .
Four (4 ) . Chapter one hundred forty -nine ( 149 )
of the Acts of the General Assembly of one thousand
nine hundred thirty ( 1930 ) having been held consti-
tutional by the Court of Appeals of Kentucky in the
case of Moore , et al . v . State Board of Charities and
Corrections , two hundred thirty -nine ( 239 ) Ky . seven
hundred twenty -nine ( 729 ) , forty ( 40 ) S. W. second
(2d ) three hundred forty -nine ( 349 ) , and also hav-
ing been held constitutional and valid by a three-
judge Federal Court in the cases of Kroger Grocery
and Baking Co. , et al . v . Lewis , et al ., seven ( 7 ) Fed .
Sup . four hundred thirty -eight (438 ) , decided De-
cember twenty ( 20 ) , one thousand nine hundred
thirty -three ( 1933 ) ; and , later having been held un-
constitutional by the Supreme Court of the United
States on an appeal from the aforesaid decision ,
finally decided by the Supreme Court on March
eleven ( 11 ) , one thousand nine hundred thirty -five
( 1935 ) , it is deemed by the General Assembly that
the two year limitation imposed by Section ten (10 )
of said Chapter one hundred forty nine ( 149 ) of the
Acts of the General Assembly of one thousand nine
hundred thirty ( 1930 ) and the limitations imposed
by Section one hundred sixty three ( 163 ) , Kentucky
Statutes , Carroll's one thousand nine hundred thirty
( 1930 ) Edition , should be and the same hereby are
waived and the limitations thereby imposed shall
not be pleaded as a defense and shall not be a de-

592 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
fense to any claim or suit for a refund of the afore-
said taxes . Provided , however , the waiver of the
defenses of limitations herein provided shall only
apply to suits filed within sixty days from the effec-
tive date of this resolution .
Five ( 5 ) . If judgment be rendered for plaintiff
in any suit brought under the provisions of this
resolution , the Auditor of Public Accounts upon
presentation to him of an attested copy thereof shall
issue his warrant upon the State Treasurer in favor
of the plaintiff for the amount of the judgment and
when the warrant is presented to the State Treas-
urer he shall issue his check payable to the payee
therein for the amount of the warrant , and both the
warrant and the check shall be charged to the Gen-
eral Fund .
Six ( 6 ) . WHEREAS , the budget for the next
biennial period will become effective July one ( 1 ) ,
one thousand nine hundred thirty - six ( 1936 ) ; and ,
WHEREAS , the aforesaid taxpayers have paid said
taxes under an unconstitutional Act and have been
deprived of the money so paid without adequate
recourse for the recovery thereof, an emergency
hereby is declared to exist and this resolution shall
become effective immediately upon its passage and
approval by the Governor .
Approved February 25, 1936 .

teen hundred thirty -five ( 1935 ) , and prior thereto , Mrs. Alice

gency Relief Administration , their agents , servants ,

594 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
was negligence on the part of any such employee or

ing Five Hundred ( $ 500.00 ) Dollars .
In the event a judgment is recovered by said Mrs.
Alice Maher in said suit as damages , same shall be
paid by the Auditor of Public Accounts by warrant
drawn on the State Treasurer . The Commonwealth
of Kentucky and the Kentucky Emergency Relief
Administration , or its successors , or either of them ,
and the said Mrs. Alice Maher shall have the right
to appeal in any other civil case . The recovery
herein shall be limited to and not exceed Five Hun-
dred ( $ 500.00 ) Dollars .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 432 .
SENATE RESOLUTION No. forty -four ( 44 ) .
A JOINT RESOLUTION authorizing James Lewis , an infant ,
by his next friend , and Mrs. Ruth Lewis , his mother , to sue
the Commonwealth of Kentucky and the State Highway Com-
mission , either ;
WHEREAS , On the twenty-sixth ( 26th ) day of July, one thou-
sand nine hundred thirty -five ( 1935 ) in Jefferson County , Ken-
tucky , near what is known as Schuff's Lane when said infant ,
James Lewis , was driving a motor vehicle of Ruth Lewis , by
reason of the gross negligence and gross carelessness of the
servant and agent of the State Highway Commission a truck
of said Highway Commission was caused to collide with and
injure said James Lewis , resulting in disfigurement , and
WHEREAS , said motor vehicle he was then and there operating

CHAPTER 432 595
was the property of Mrs. Ruth Lewis , his mother , and same
was demolished , and
WHEREAS , It is the contention of James L. Lewis and Mrs.
Ruth Lewis that said injuries to each of them was caused
by the negligence and carelessness of the State Highway
Commission of the Commonwealth of Kentucky .
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That said James Lewis and his mother , Mrs.
Ruth Lewis , be and they are hereby authorized and
permitted to sue the Commonwealth of Kentucky ,
or the State Highway Commission , or either , in the
Jefferson Circuit Court , the county of their resi-
dence , and in which said accident and injuries
occurred , for damages for personal injuries to the
said James Lewis , infant , and for damage and in-
jury to the machine that he was then and there
driving belonging to Mrs. Ruth Lewis ; that the said
James Lewis by his next friend may sue for his per-
sonal injuries , loss of earning power , loss of time
from work , physical suffering, and for hospital and
medical bills , and the said Mrs. Ruth Lewis , the
owner of said machine , may sue for the recovery for
damages inflicted upon it . In the event said suits
are compromised or judgment rendered , the same
shall be paid by the Auditor of Public Accounts for
the Commonwealth of Kentucky by warrant drawn
on the State Treasury and paid out of the General
Fund .
Either party to said suits may appeal from any
judgment that may be entered herein , as in other
civil suit, and the case may be settled or adjusted
with consent and approval of the Attorney General
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky in the same way
as any other civil case .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

CHAPTER 433 .
RESOLUTION , Authorizing William C. Wells and Mary Helen
Wells , his wife , and Matt Wells , who reside in Louisville ,
Jefferson County , Kentucky , to file suits against the Common-
wealth of Kentucky and the State Highway Commission or
either .
WHEREAS , On November fourteen (14 ) , one thousand nine hun-
dred ( 1934 ) , on Highway 60 , in Breckinridge County , Ken-
tucky , between Harned and Hardinsburg , William C. Wells ,
and his wife, Mary Helen Wells and his father , Matt Wells ,
were riding in an automobile owned by William C. Wells ,
which automobile was run into and collided with by a truck
owned by the Commonwealth of Kentucky , and then being
operated and used by an employee of the State Highway De-
partment and working upon the State Highway , thereby
causing them to be severely and critically injured and the
automobile of said William C. Wells to be damaged :
NOW , THEREFORE , In order that they may determine their
rights by judicial action , and the question of negligence , if
any, causing said injuries to each of them , may be determined
by judicial action ,
Be it Resolved that the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :

and name , Mary Helen Wells , in her own right and
name , and Matt Wells , in his own right and name ,
be and they are hereby empowered and authorized
to file and prosecute suits , severally and respec-
tively , against the Commonwealth of Kentucky , and
the State Highway Commission , or either , for the
purpose of determining the liability of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky , and the State Highway Com-
mission, or either , for such injuries and damages ,
if any , there be , arising from above stated facts , and
in the event any judgment in either action is re-
covered by said William C. Wells , Mary Helen
Wells , and Matt Wells , or either of them, or same
are compromised and settled , judgments or amount
agreed on in compromise , if any, shall be paid by the

Auditor of Public Accounts , by warrant drawn on
the State Treasury and paid out of the general
fund .
Two ( 2 ) . Such actions may be brought in any
circuit court of the Commonwealth of Kentucky ,
which has jurisdiction of the subject matter , pro-
vided that any such action shall be instituted within
one year from the date this resolution becomes
effective .
Three ( 3 ) . Either party to any suit may appeal
from any judgment which may be entered therein ,
as in any other civil suit and the cases may be
settled and adjusted with the consent and approval
of the Attorney General of Kentucky , in the same
way as any other civil case.

CHAPTER 434 .
RESOLUTION authorizing Mrs. Joe A. Westerfield , personal
representative of Joe A. Westerfield , deceased , to sue the
Commonwealth of Kentucky and the State Highway Commis-
sion .
WHEREAS , the State Highway Commission of Kentucky con-
structed a public highway on project number eleven ( 11 ) in
Ohio County , Kentucky , which work was begun in May, one
thousand nine hundred twenty -five ( 1925 ) , and completed dur-
ing said year , and
WHEREAS , said grade and drain of said road as constructed by
Ellis and Smeathers , contractors , employed by the State High-
way Commission of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , and
WHEREAS , said contractors acting pursuant to their contract
with the State Highway Commission of Kentucky , removed
from the old embankment four hundred and ten (410 ) feet ,
of open and only put in one hundred and thirty ( 130 ) feet in
the road as constructed , and
WHEREAS , said grade and drain was so negligently and care-
lessly constructed and maintained as not to have a sufficient
of openings in the levee thereby causing the water accumulat-
ing on the north of the levee to run through the small open-

598 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
ing with force as to injure and damage the lands owned by Joe
A. Westerfield on south side of said levee and adjacent to it,
and
WHEREAS , by reason of said negligent construction and main-
tenance the lands owned by Joe A. Westerfield on the south
side of said road and adjacent to it were greatly injured and
damaged by the waters concentrating in these small openings
with such force as to wash and damage the lands owned by
Joe A. Westerfield over which it was forced to go , thereby
changing the natural course of the water and not giving suf-
ficient openings for the water to pass through the levee with-
out injury to the land on the south side of said levee , and
WHEREAS , the lands owned by said Joe A. Westerfield have
been greatly damaged by the negligent construction and main-
tenance of said road , and
WHEREAS , the said Mrs. Joe A. Westerfield alleges and claims
that said damage and injury to his lands was caused entirely
by the negligent and carelessness of the State Highway Com-
mission , its agents and employees in the construction and
maintenance of said road , and
WHEREAS , if the said injury and damage to said land was
caused by the negligence and carelessness of the said High-
way Commission , its agents and employees the said Mrs. Joe
A. Westerfield the personal representative of Joe A. Wester-
field , deceased , should be compensated for the damages done
to his said lands : therefore,
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One . That Mrs. Joe A. Westerfield be
and she is hereby authorized and permitted to sue
the Commonwealth of Kentucky and the State High-
way Commission of Kentucky , in the Ohio Circuit
Court for such damage as he may have sustained if
any , by reason of any injury or damage to his lands
through the carelessness or negligence of the State
Highway Commission of Kentucky , its agents or
employees , and in the event any judgment is recov-
ered by the said Mrs. Joe A. Westerfield in said suit
for damage to Joe A. Westerfield's lands , same
shall be paid by the Auditor by warrant on the
Treasurer .

CHAPTER 435 599
Section Two . Either party to said suit may ap-
peal from any judgment which may be rendered in
the Ohio Circuit Court to the Court of Appeals and
the case may be settled and adjusted with the con-
sent of the Attorney General of Kentucky in the
same way as any other civil case.
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 435 .
RESOLUTION authorizing Francis Phelps to sue the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky and the State Highway Commission ,
or either .
WHEREAS , on the fourteenth ( 14 ) day of December , one thou-
sand nine hundred thirty -five ( 1935 ) , Francis Phelps was

lessness of the driver of said truck , in that he was traveling
at an excessive rate of speed and on the wrong side of the
road and that said accident took place on a sharp curve .
WHEREAS , the Ford car , one thousand nine hundred thirty -six
( 1936 ) Model , being driven by the said Francis Phelps was
damaged in the sum of one hundred and twenty -five dollars
($125.00 ) , by reason of the collision , etc.
WHEREAS , the truck herein mentioned was and is the prop-
erty of the State of Kentucky , and was being used and op
erated at the time herein mentioned by the employees of the
State Highway Commission in the performance of their duties .
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Francis Phelps , and he is hereby authorized
and permitted to sue the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky and the State Highway Commission , or either ,
in the Circuit Court in the county of his residence
for such damages as he may have suffered , if any ,
by reason of the collision and negligence of the em-
ployees of the State Highway Commission . Said
suit shall not be for an amount exceeding the sum
of one hundred twenty -five ($ 125.00 ) dollars , and in

the event any judgment is recovered by the said

eral Fund . Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 436 .
RESOLUTION authorizing Bud Philpott to sue the State High-
way Commission and the Commonwealth of Kentucky , or
either .
Said Resolution is as follows , viz.:
WHEREAS , while some grading operations were being carried
through in the town of Tompkinsville , Kentucky , the con-
tractor deviated from the plans and specifications and the
blue prints of the surveys , thereby damaging the real estate
of Bud Philpott , and making it impossible for him to enter
his barn from the new road by cutting an embankment to a
depth of at least eight feet , causing great inconvenience ,
property damage and handicap .
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That said Bud Philpott be and he is hereby au-
thorized to sue the State Highway Commission and
the Commonwealth of Kentucky , or either , for dam-
ages to his property , damages to his barn , inconven-
iences caused and handicap brought about through
the carelessness , negligence and failure to carry out
original plans and surveys by agents or employees
of the State Highway Commission . Said judgment
to be a fair and reasonable consideration for dam-
ages set out in suit , and in the event judgment is
obtained by said Bud Philpott , or same compro-
mised or settled , same shall be paid by the Auditor
of Public Accounts , by warrant , drawn on the State
Treasurer , and paid out of the General Funds .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

CHAPTER 440 .
RESOLUTION authorizing Joe Sparrow to sue the Common-
wealth of Kentucky and the State Highway Commission of
Kentucky or either .
WHEREAS , the State Highway Commission of Kentucky dur-
ing the years of one thousand nine hundred thirty -two ( 1932 )
and nineteen hundred thirty -three ( 1933 ) constructed a public
highway on project number twenty ( 20 ) in Anderson County ,
Kentucky , and
WHEREAS , the grade and drain of said road was constructed
by the Phelps Construction Company , contractors , acting pur-
suant to their contract with the said State Highway Com-
mission of Kentucky , and
WHEREAS , said road runs over and through the lands of Joe
Sparrow on Salt River near the village of Glensboro in Ander-
son County, Kentucky , and said grade and drain was so con-
struced and has since been maintained by the said State High-
way Commission of Kentucky , through its agent and em-
ployees , as not to have a sufficient amount of openings in the
levee thereby causing the water in creeks and branches flow-
ing into said river to be diverted and to accumulate and

said Joe Sparrow around his dwelling house and outbuildings ,

604 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
thereby diminishing the value of said buildings and causing

NOW , THEREFORE , to determine by judicial action the liability

Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the

and permitted to sue the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky and the State Highway Commission of Ken-
tucky, or either , in the Anderson Circuit Court for
such damage as he may have sustained , if any ,

Circuit Court to the Court of Appeals , and the case

sonal representative of the estate of Otis Wilson Wright in

sion and Carroll County , or any one or more of them ,

such damages as he suffered by reason of the death

same shall be paid by the Auditor of Public Ac-

CHAPTER 442 .
WHEREAS , no legislative investigation or inspection of the
properties of the Commonwealth of Kentucky has been made
for several years , and
WHEREAS , it is the duty of the General Assembly of the Com-
monwealth to investigate , inspect and know the physical con-
dition of said properties , and
WHEREAS , the General Assembly will at its special session
take up for consideration the rehabilitation of the State insti-
tutions and desires to be in possession of all the facts con-
cerning the physical condition and the management of such
institutions and other State properties , and
WHEREAS the Governor of the Commonwealth desires that
such inspection and investigation be made prior to the con-
sideration of the rehabilitation of such institutions : Now,
therefore ,

CHAPTER 442 607
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That there is hereby created a committee of the
General Assembly on the investigation and inspec-
tion of the properties of the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky and said committee shall be composed of ten
( 10 ) members , six ( 6 ) from the House of Representa-
tives and four (4 ) from the Senate , and the Speaker
of the House shall appoint the six ( 6 ) members of
the House of Representatives and the President of
the Senate shall appoint the four (4) members of
the Senate ; immediately upon said appointment be-
ing made in the House and in the Senate , said Com-
mittee shall meet and organize by electing one of its
members Chairman and one Secretary . After the
organization of the Committee , said Committee shall
arrange its visits to said properties as soon as prac-
ticable and on such dates as shall be determined by
the Committee , and the Secretary of said Committee
shall notify the person or persons in charge of said
properties of the day or days that said Committee
will visit said property and inspect the same .
the purpose of making said inspection and investiga-
tion said committee is hereby empowered and auth-
orized , when in the judgment of the Committee it is
deemed advisable , to summons to appear before it
any persons to testify before it concerning the na-
ture, condition or management of said property , and
is hereby authorized to administer to such persons
the proper oath and to proceed and question such
persons and to command them to bring before it any
records in their possession relating or pertaining to
the operation of said properties . Said committee is
further vested with the power to punish for contempt
any person refusing to obey said summons , or an-
swering the summons to refuse to answer any ques-
For

608 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
tions relevant to the matter in hand , propounded
to them . Whenever such testimony is taken such
Committee shall be empowered to employ a stenog-
rapher to take such hearings and to transcribe them
for the use of the Committee and of the General As-
sembly . All Peace officers of the Commonwealth of
Kentucky shall obey the orders of said Committee
and serve any and all papers that may come into
their hands and make due return thereof to the
Chairman of the Committee and for such services
shall be paid the fees provided by law for the serv-
ing of such process . Such fees due to any officer
shall be certified by the Chairman of said Committee
to the Auditor of Public Accounts for payment in
the manner prescribed by law for the payment of
claims against the Commonwealth of Kentucky . All
expenses of said Committee , including transporta-
tion and hotel expenses when said Committee is ab-
sent from Frankfort , Kentucky , shall be paid by
the Commonwealth of Kentucky ; and in the event
said Committee shall not complete its duties before
the adjournment of the regular session , said Com-
mittee is hereby authorized to continue its work ,
and in addition to the expenses heretofore provided
for , shall be allowed the regular per diem allowed
them for legislative services , such expenses and the
per diem entitled to be received by the Committee
shall be certified by the Chairman of the Committee
of the Auditor of Public Accounts who shall author-
ize the payment of same in the same manner as now
prescribed by law for the payment of claims against
the Commonwealth of Kentucky . For the purpose
of paying said expenses , per diem , if any, there is
hereby appropriated from the general fund of the
Commonwealth the sum of five thousand dollars
($5,000.00 ) , but any unexpended part thereof upon
the dissolution of said Committee shall revert to and

CHAPTER 443 609
become part of the general fund . The report of said
Committee being necessary for the report by the
General Assembly of the rehabilitation of the State
Institutions in order that the General Assembly
may have the facts concerning the conditions and
management of such Institutions when it considers
such matters in a special session of the General As-
sembly called for such purposes and it being neces-
sary that said Committee begin to perform its duties
as soon as possible in order to have its report ready ,
an emergency is declared to exist and this resolution
shall become effective upon its adoption by the Gen-
eral Assembly and approval by the Governor .
Approved February 6, 1936 .

his father, the said Barner Wiley and an uncle and while
traveling along and upon the State highway or road known
as the Bowling Green -Morgantown road and about three miles
west of Bowling Green at the Jennings Creek Bridge where
a road scraper being the property of the State of Kentucky
and being used and operated at the time by the employees of
the State Highway Commission in the performance of their
duties was so negligently and carelessly operated as to be
permitted to get out of control and strike said wagon and
the said Ben Wiley , and
WHEREAS , the said Wiley's pelvis bone and legs were broken
and he suffered other serious wounds about his head , body
and person and that he was painfully and permanently in-
jured , disabling and incapacitating him from manual labor
for the remainder of his life , and
WHEREAS the said Ben Wiley is now fourteen ( 14 ) years of
age and is unable because of his said injuries to do any labor
and that he was permanently crippled and was further re-
quired to expend the sum of five hundred dollars ( $500.00 ) for
medical attention and was confined to the Bowling Green
City Hospital for ten ( 10 ) weeks and required to pay hospital
and medical bills in the sum of five hundred dollars ( $500.00 ) ,
and whereas he suffered great mental and bodily pain and
anguish and is permanently crippled by reason of one leg
being shorter than the other , and
WHEREAS he has sustained these serious and permanent in-
juries and suffered great mental pain and anguish by reason
of the alleged carelessness and negligence of the employees
of the State Highway Commission in the conduct of the State's
business : Therefore,
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One . That the said Ben Wiley be and
he is hereby authorized and permitted to sue by his

612 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Guardian or next friend and that his father , Barner
Wiley be permitted to sue the Commonwealth of
Kentucky and the State Highway Commission or
either in the Circuit Court of the County of his
residence for such damages and for such mental pain
and anguish as he may have suffered , if any , by
reason of any injury received by him through the
carelessness or negligence of the State Highway
Commission , its agents or employees . Said suit shall
be for any amount not exceeding the sum of ten thou-
sand dollars ( $ 10,000.00 ) and in the event any judg-
ment is recovered by said Wiley in said suit for in-
juries to himself or same is compromised or settled ,
same shall be paid by the Auditor of Public Accounts
by warrant drawn on the State Treasurer and paid
out of the General Fund .
Section Two . Either party to said suit may ap-
peal from any judgment which may be entered there-
in as in any other civil suit, and the case may be
settled and adjusted with the consent and approval
of the Attorney General of Kentucky in the same
way as any other civil case.
Neither approved nor disapproved .

CHAPTER 447.

Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the

ages as he may have sustained . This action may be

CHAPTER 448 .

wealth of Kentucky and the State Highway Commission , or

thousand nine hundred thirty -three ( 1933 ) , William Cox , a
resident of Cooper , Wayne County , Kentucky , was run over
and injured on the highway leading from Monticello to Cooper ,

the said William Cox was walking the highway he was struck
and injured , both of his legs being broken , and receiving
other injuries , by a truck operated by one of the employees
of the State Highway Commission , in a very careless , negli-
gent manner , the employee driving the truck at an excessive
rate of speed , on the wrong and improper side of the road ,
and while he was in an intoxicated condition , therefore ,
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That William Cox be, and he is hereby authorized
and permitted to sue the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky and the State Highway Commission , or either
in the Wayne Circuit Court , the County of the resi-
dence of said William Cox for damages for the loss
of earning power , physical suffering , and for hos-
pital and medical bills of the said William Cox, which

were all brought about and caused by the negli-
gence of the employee of the State Highway Com-
mission . Said suit shall be for any amount not ex-

rant drawn on the State Treasurer and paid out of

Either party to said suit may appeal from any
judgment which may be entered therein as in any
other civil suit, and the case may be settled and ad-
justed with the consent and approval of the Attorney
General of Kentucky in the same way as any other
civil case . Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 449 .
RESOLUTION authorizing Ohio County to sue the State Road
Commission and the Commonwealth of Kentucky .
WHEREAS , the legal voters of Ohio County , Kentucky , on the
third day of March , one thousand nine hundred twenty-three
( 1923 ) , voted a road and bridge bond authorizing a bi -partisan
commission named , to contract indebtedness of three hundred
thousand dollars ( $300,000.00 ) for the construction of high-
ways and bridge in said county , and ,
WHEREAS , said commission , together with the fiscal court ,
issued bond in the sum of three hundred thousand dollars
($300,000.00 ) , and said bonds , after due publication , were sold
to the highest and best bidder ; were purchased by Caldwell
& Company of Nashville , Tennessee , whose bid was one hun-
dred and one ( 101 ) , and the further agreement to pay five
per cent ( 5 % ) on the daily balance of said sum , and ,
WHEREAS , the principle and interest accumulated on said sum ,
amounting in the aggregate of three hundred thirty -four thou-
sand seven hundred twenty-five dollars and sixty-six cents
( $ 334,725.66 ) , which, from time to time was withdrawn by the
State Highway Commission , or their agents , leaving an amount
over and above said sum of five thousand five hundred dol-

616 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
lars ( $5,500.00 ) which was later withdrawn by the officials of
Ohio County , Kentucky , and placed in the sinking fund for
the purpose of redeeming coupons on said road and bridge
bonds , and ,
WHEREAS , the sinking fund or any funds in the hands of Cald-
well & Company which was in excess of three hundred thou-
sand dollars ( $300,000.00 ) constitutes a part of the sinking
fund for the redemption of bonds and coupons , and as such
was inviolate , and ,
WHEREAS , the said Ohio County , by and through its agent ,
Gilmore Keown , has demanded a refund of both the State
Treasury and the State Highway Commission , and that the
State Treasury and Elam Huddleston has likewise demanded
refund to said Ohio County of thirty -four thousand seven
hundred twenty-five dollars and sixty -six cents ( $34,725.66 ) .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One . That Ohio County, Kentucky , be
and it is hereby , authorized and permitted to sue the
State Highway Commission and the Commonwealth
of Kentucky in the Franklin Circuit Court for such
interest as may be due on account of the premises
hereof and in the event of the recovery by said Ohio
County, Kentucky in said suit, or, upon the presenta-
tion of the facts and agreement of the parties or
their agents , said amount shall be paid by the State
of Kentucky by warrant on the Treasury but without
an execution being issued , but the judgment or pay-
ment shall , in no event exceed the sum of thirty -four
thousand seven hundred twenty-five dollars and six-
ty-six cents ( $ 34,725.66 ) , and legal interest thereon ,
from January 1 , one thousand nine hundred twenty-
eight ( 1928 ) , to date of payment , and in the event
of disagreement or exception to a judgment .
Section Two . Either party shall have the right
to appeal as in any other civil case .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

CHAPTER 453 .
RESOLUTION for the relief of Cecil Pennington , Sheriff of

by him as agent for the Commonwealth of Kentucky .
WHEREAS , Cecil Pennington on August nine ( 9) , one thousand
nine hundred thirty -four ( 1934 ) , was duly appointed by the
Governor of the Commonwealth of Kentucky as agent of the
Commonwealth to go to Burley , Idaho , to present requisition
papers of this Commonwealth to the Governor of Idaho and
to bring back Joe Middleton , a fugitive from the justice of
this State , under indictment for murder in Elliott County ,
Kentucky , and
WHEREAS , Cecil Pennington, under said appointment , pro-
ceeded to the State of Idaho and to the city of Burley to bring
back said fugitive and found on arrival said fugitive , which
he , Cecil Pennington, delivered to Elliott County , Kentucky ,
on August eighteenth ( 18 ) , one thousand nine hundred thirty-
four ( 1934 ) and said fugitive was convicted and sentenced for
a period of seventeen (17 ) years in Kentucky Reformatory ,
and
WHEREAS Cecil Pennington, in performing his said duty as
agent aforesaid , incurred expenses and traveled four thousand
five hundred seventy -two ( 4,572 ) miles to and from Idaho at

620 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
six cents ( 6c ) per mile , total of two hundred seventy -four
dollars and thirty -two cents ( $274.32 ) -10 meals for prisoner,
five dollars ( $5.00 ) train fare for prisoner , Joe Middleton ,
forty -two dollars and twenty-eight cents ( $42.28 ) ; fees paid
P. D. Dade , Sheriff at Burley , Idaho , twenty-five dollars
($25.00 ) , making the total three hundred and forty -six dollars
and sixty cents ( $346.60 ) ; therefore ,
Be it Resolved by the House of Representatives
and the Senate :
That the said amount three hundred and forty-six
dollars and sixty cents ( $ 346.60 ) , be and is hereby
appropriated to be paid to the said Cecil Pennington
and the Auditor of Public Accounts is directed to
draw his warrant upon the Treasurer for the said
amount and deliver the same to Cecil Pennington
for the purpose aforesaid .

CHAPTER 454 .
JOINT RESOLUTION authorizing Harry D. Kremer to sue the

WHEREAS , On September seven ( 7 ) , one thousand nine hun-
dred thirty -five ( 1935 ) , Corporal C. L. Snow , a member of the
Military Department of Kentucky , was operating an army
truck then and there belonging to and in the service of the
Military Department of Kentucky , and
WHEREAS , It is claimed by Harry D. Kremer that Snow, on
said date , drove said truck on the wrong side of public high-
way number twenty-five ( 25 ) , in Fayette County , Kentucky ,
and while doing so , ran into and collided head -on with the
passenger automobile of Harry D. Kremer , then and there
being driven and operated by Harry D. Kremer , Jr. , on the
proper side of the highway, as claimed by Kremer , and
WHEREAS , It is claimed that the impact of said army truck
with said passenger automobile greatly damaged the latter ,
and
WHEREAS , It is claimed that the repair of said damages to
said passenger automobile cost Harry D. Kremer the sum
of two hundred twenty-five dollars and thirty -five cents
($ 225.35 ) , and it is claimed that the said passenger automo-

CHAPTER 455 621
bile was damaged by said army truck to the extent of two
hundred twenty-five dollars and thirty -five cents , and

be repaid by the Commonwealth of Kentucky ; Therefore ,
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Harry D. Kremer be , and he is hereby , au-
thorized and permitted to sue the Commonwealth
of Kentucky in the Franklin Circuit Court for such
damages as he may have suffered , if any , by any
wrongful injury to his automobile by reason of the

judgment is received by said Kremer in said suit as

judgment shall not exceed the sum of two hundred
twenty-five dollars and thirty-five cents ( $ 225.35 ) ,
plus interest and costs , and the Commonwealth of
Kentucky shall have the right to appeal as in any
other civil case. Neither approved nor disapproved .

CHAPTER 456 .
Be it Resolved by the House of Representatives
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , the Senate con-
curring :
That , in recognition of the work of the Honorable
J. Lon Foust , Chancellor of the Third Chancery
Division of the State of Tennessee , in founding ,
fostering , and for twelve years directing the com-
pletion of the work of getting a direct highway con-
structed between Lexington , Kentucky , and Chat-
tanooga , Tennessee , now known as a section of
United States highway number twenty - seven ( 27 ) ,
and in view of the action of the Tennessee General
Assembly, in regular session in 1935 , in memoraliz-
ing the founder of said highway by naming Ten-
nessee's part of the road for the Honorable J. Lon
Foust , the section from Lexington to the Tennessee
state line over the present route of United States
highway number twenty -seven (27 ) be named The
Foust Highway , and , if consistent , designated by
the erection of suitable markers bearing such name ,
by the Kentucky State highway commission .
Be it further resolved ,
That the Clerk of this House send a copy of this
resolution to the Honorable Governor of the State

CHAPTER 458 623
of Tennessee and to Hon . J. Lon Foust of the City
of Chattanooga , Tenn . Approved January 28 , 1936 .

Wilson and caused same to back up on their land , and whereby
their property is now unfit for cultivation and that they have
been damaged by reason thereof by the State Highway De-
partment and its employees : Now . Therefore ,

Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That , Herbert Seale , Arch Wilson , and Luther F.
Morgan be and they are hereby authorized and per-
mitted to sue the Commonwealth of Kentucky and
the State Highway Commission , or either , in the

to be unfit for cultivation and thereby permanently

the use of same and its value .
Neither approved nor disapproved .

of

CHAPTER 459 .
RESOLUTION authorizing J. B. .Lacefield , of Central City ,
Muhlenberg County , Kentucky , to sue the Commonwealth of
Kentucky and the State Highway Commission .
WHEREAS , The State Highway Commission and the Common-
wealth of Kentucky were constructing a bridge on the Owens-
boro -Livermore highway in Daviess County , Kentucky , and
WHEREAS , They had removed the old bridge , leaving an open
ditch across the main highway , and
WHEREAS , they had piled stones and other debris along the
banks of said ditch and across said highway obstructing the
highway , and ,
WHEREAS , on the night of December twelfth ( 12 ) , one thou-
sand nine hundred thirty -five ( 1935 ) , J. B. Lacefield , a deputy
sheriff of Muhlenberg County , Kentucky , while proceeding to
Owensboro , Kentucky , on official business ran into the em-
bankment which was placed across the highway by the State
Highway Commission , and received severe personal injuries
and also damaged his automobile to a considerable extent ,
and ,
WHEREAS , the State Highway Commission and the Common-
wealth of Kentucky had not placed adequate lights or any
other warning to motorists using this highway of the presence
of this obstruction placed across the highway by them , and
WHEREAS , because of said negligence of the State Highway
Commission and the Commonwealth of Kentucky , J. B. Lace-
field received severe injuries and considerable damage to his
person and automobile through no fault of his own ; Therefore ,
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One . That J. B. Lacefield be and he is

Kentucky and the State highway commission of

for such damages as he may have sustained , if any ,

the State highway commission of Kentucky , its
agents or employees , and in the event any judg-
ment is recovered by the said J. B. Lacefield in said
suit for damages to his person or property same
shall be paid by the Auditor of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky by warrant on the Treasurer .
Section Two . Either party to said suit may
appeal from any judgment which may be rendered
in the Daviess circuit court to the Court of Appeals
and the case may be settled and adjusted with the
consent of the Attorney General of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky in the same way as any other
civil cases may be settled or adjusted .

WHEREAS , on or about May seventh ( 7th ) , one thousand nine
hundred thirty -four ( 1934 ) , G. B. Hall , of Floyd County , while
riding in an automobile on U. S. Highway number twenty-
three ( 23 ) , was run into by a grader which was owned at the
time and being operated by the employees of the State High-
way Commission and was severely , plainfully and permanently
injured on and about the head and face , causing a derange-
ment of the mind .
WHEREAS , it is claimed that said collision was entirely caused
and brought about by the gross negligence and gross care-

628 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
lessness of the agents , servants and employees . of the said
State Highway Commission in the operation of said grader be-
longing to the State of Kentucky or the State Highway Com-
mission ; and
WHEREAS , he sustained said serious , painful and permanent
injuries because of the alleged carelessness and negligence
of the said employees while in the discharge and conduct of
the State's business : Therefore ,
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That said G. B. Hall be , and he hereby is author-
ized and permitted to sue the Commonwealth of
Kentucky and the State highway commission , or
either in the circuit court of the county of his resi-
dence , for such damages as he may have suffered
if any, by reason of any injuries received by him
through the carelessness or negligence of the State
highway commission , its agents or employees .
Said suit shall be for any amount not exceeding the
sum of five thousand dollars ( $5,000.00 ) , and in the
event any judgment is recovered by said G. B. Hall
in said suit for injuries to himself , or same is com-
promised or settled , same shall be paid by the Audi-
tor of Public Accounts by warrant drawn on the
State Treasurer and paid out of the general fund .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 462 .
A RESOLUTION authorizing Mrs. Alice Maher to sue the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky , and the Kentucky Emergency Re-
lief Administration , or its successor , or either of them , and
the statute of limitations not to apply until from the passage
of this resolution .
WHEREAS , on the twenty-fourth ( 24th ) day of October , one
thousand nine hundred thirty -five ( 1935 ) , and prior thereto ,
Mrs. Alice Maher was employed by the Kentucky Emergency
Relief Administration in the conduct of its operations ; and
WHEREAS , said Mrs. Alice Maher, while in the performance

CHAPTER 462 629
of her duties was injured through the negligence of some per-
son in the operation of the elevator in the building in which
she was employed , and incurred expenses in the sum of three
hundred eighty -two and 95/100 ($382.95 ) dollars for the treat-
ment of said injuries ; therefore ,
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That Mrs. Alice Maher , of Newport , Campbell
County, Kentucky , be and she is hereby authorized ,
empowered , and permitted , to sue the Common-
wealth of Kentucky , and the Kentucky Emergency
Relief Administrator or its successor , or either of
them , in the Campbell Circuit Court for such dam-
ages as she may have sustained , if any , by reason
of being injured by the falling or operation of the
elevator and elevator gate in the building at Ninth
and Orchard Streets in the City of Newport , Camp-
bell County, Kentucky , and which building was
leased by the Kentucky Emergency Relief Adminis-
tration for the conduct of its business . And caused
by the negligence of the agents , servants , and em-
ployees of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , or the
Kentucky Emergency Relief Administration , or its
successors , or either of them .
And in such suit she shall be permitted to show
all the facts and circumstances as to whether or not
the employees or any employee of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky , or the Kentucky Emergency
Relief Administration were negligent in reference
to said elevator and elevator gate and shaft, or neg-
ligent in any manner in the care and operation of
the elevator in said building , on the part of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky , or the Kentucky Em-
ergency Relief Administration , their agents , serv-
ants , and employees ; and if a jury shall decide that
there was negligence on the part of any such em-
ployee or employees of the Commonwealth of

630 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Kentucky or the Kentucky Emergency Relief Ad-
ministration , and that as a direct and proximate
result thereof said Mrs. Alice Maher received her
injuries , then she may recover of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky , or the Kentucky Emergency
Relief Administration , or both, such sum or sums
as will fairly and reasonably compensate her for
the damage sustained , not exceeding five hundred
($500.00 ) dollars .
In the event a judgment is recovered by said Mrs.
Alice Maher in said suit as damages , same shall be
paid by the Auditor of Public Accounts by warrant
drawn on the State Treasurer . The Commonwealth
of Kentucky and the Kentucky Emergency Relief
Administration , or its successors , or either of them ,
and the said Mrs. Alice Maher shall have the right
to appeal as in any other civil case. The recovery
herein shall be limited to and not exceed five hun- .
dred ($500.00 ) dollars .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 463 .
RESOLUTION authorizing E. S. Howard to sue the Common-
wealth of Kentucky and the State Highway Commission of
Kentucky . Said resolution is as follows , viz .:
WHEREAS , the State Highway Commission of Kentucky con-
structed a public bridge across Rough River at Hartford ,
Kentucky , in Ohio County , which work was begun about
June , one thousand nine hundred thirty -four ( 1934 ) , and com-
pleted in the fall of one thousand nine hundred thirty -four
(1934 ) , and
WHEREAS , said work or construction was performed by Ruby
Lumber Company , contractors , employed by the State High-
way Commission of the Commonwealth of Kentucky , and
WHEREAS , said contractors of the State Highway Commission
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky removed the metal tilings
from the drainage and crossing into and upon the property of
E. S. Howard , and elevated the highway, being highway
number seventy -one (71 ) some three or more feet in front

CHAPTER 463 631
of said property of the said E. S. Howard , and left an open
ditch all along said property in such a way that prohibits
egress and ingress and excavated a large amount of land be-
longing to the said Howard and left said property in such
condition as to cause large ditches or gullies to wash along
the entire front adjoining the highway and leaving same with-
out any approach to enter said property in such a manner
as to greatly injury and damage the said E. S. Howard , and
WHEREAS , by reason of the negligent construction and main-
tenance the property owned by E. S. Howard , in the city limits
of Hartford , adjacent to highway number seventy -one ( 71 )
and adjacent to the said bridge , has been greatly injured and
damaged because of the destruction of the crossings , the large
ditches along the front prohibiting his egress and ingress
and by reason of removing his soil making the property
practically worthless , and
WHEREAS , the said E. S. Howard alleges and claims that said
damage and injury to his property was caused entirely by
the negligence and carelessness of the State Highway Com-
mission , its agent and employees in the construction and
maintenance of said bridge and highway, and
WHEREAS , if the said injury and damages to said land or
property was caused by the negligence and carelessness of
the State Highway Commission , its agents and employees the
said E. S. Howard should be compensated for his drainage
and injury to his property : Therefore ,
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That E. S. Howard be and he is hereby authorized
and permitted to sue the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky and the State highway commission of Ken-
tucky , in the Ohio County circuit court for such
damage as he may have sustained , if any , by reason
of any injury or damage to his property through
the carelessness or negligence of the State highway
commission of Kentucky , its agents or employees
and in the event any judgment is recovered by the
said E. S. Howard in said suit for damages to his
land or property , same shall be paid by the Auditor
by warrant on the Treasurer .
Either party to said suit may appeal from any

632 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
judgment which may be rendered in the Ohio
county circuit court to the Court of Appeals , and
the case may be settled and adjusted with the con-
sent of the Attorney General of Kentucky in the
same way as any other civil action .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 464 .
RESOLUTION authorizing William C. Wells and Mary Helen
Wells , his wife , and Matt Wells , who reside in Louisville ,
Jefferson County , Kentucky , to file suits against the Common-
wealth of Kentucky and the State Highway Commission or
either .
WHEREAS , on November fourteen ( 14 ) , one thousand nine hun-
dred thirty -four ( 1934 ) , on highway sixty ( 60 ) , in Breckin-
ridge County , Kentucky , between Harned and Hardinsburg ,
William C. Wells , and his wife , Mary Helen Wells and his
father, Matt Wells , were riding in an automobile owned by
William C. Wells , which automobile was run into and collided
with by a truck owned by the Commonwealth of Kentucky ,
and then being operated and used by an employee of the
State Highway Department and working upon the State High-
way, thereby causing them to be severely and critically in-
jured and the automobile of said William C. Wells to be
damaged ,
NOW THEREFORE , in order that they may determine their
rights by judicial action , and the question of negligence , if
any, causing said injuries to each of them , may be determined
by judicial action ,
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
Section One . That William C. Wells , in his own
right and name , Mary Helen Wells , in her own right
and name , and Matt Wells , in his own right and
name , be and they are hereby empowered and au-
thorized to file and prosecute suits , severally, and
respectively , against the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky, and the State highway commission , or either ,
for the purpose of determining the liability of the

CHAPTER 465 633
Commonwealth of Kentucky , and the State high-
way commission , or either, for such injuries and
damage , if any, there be , arising from above stated
facts , and in the event any judgment in either action
is recovered by said William C. Wells , Mary Helen
Wells , and Matt Wells , or either of them , or same
are compromised and settled , judgments or amount
agreed on in compromise , if any , shall be paid by
the Auditor of Public Accounts , by warrant drawn
on the State Treasury and paid out of the General
Fund .
Section Two . Such actions may be brought in
any circuit court of the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky , which has jurisdiction of the subject matter ,
provided that any such action shall be instituted
within one year from the date this resolution be-
comes effective .
Section Three . Either party to any suit may
appeal from any judgment which may be entered
therein, as in any other civil suit and the cases may
be settled and adjusted with the consent and ap-
proval of the Attorney General of Kentucky , in the
same way as any other civil case .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
CHAPTER 465 .
RESOLUTION designating a highway beginning at the Ken-
tucky-Tennessee State line and running to the Mammoth Cave
National Park as the Cordell Hull Parkway .
WHEREAS , it is the present policy of the Federal Government
to construct highways to connect the national parks of the
United States , and
WHEREAS , the Mammoth Cave National Park in the State of
Kentucky , and the Great Smoky National Parks in Tennessee ,
are two of the most important national parks in the United
States , which are now not connected by Federal highways ,
and
WHEREAS , the Honorable Cordell Hull , a native Tennessean ,

634 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
and the present Secretary of State of the United States , has
rendered outstanding service to the nation , both as a member
of Congress and as Secretary of State , and a highway con-
necting these two great national parks passes through the
native county and former home of this distinguished states-
man ; therefore ,
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
State of Kentucky :
That in honor of this great and distinguished
Tennessean , the Honorable Cordell Hull , and as a
tribute to his service to the nation , that a highway
beginning at the Kentucky - Tennessee State line at
a point near Boles , Kentucky , and adjoining the
Cordell Hull Parkway as designated by an Act of
the Tennessee Legislature adopted on the nine-
teenth (19 ) day of July , one thousand nine hun-
dred thirty -five (1935 ) , and running by way of
Tompkinsville , Kentucky , thence by the most direct
and feasible route to the Mammoth Cave National
Park , being that part of the highway connecting
the Great Smoky Mountain National Park and the
Mammoth Cave National Park which is in Ken-
tucky, be and the same is named and designated the
Cordell Hull Parkway .
Neither approved nor disapproved
CHAPTER 466 .
JOINT RESOLUTION for the payment of Burnam Bach , ex-
deputy sheriff of Breathitt County , for his expenses and
charges insistent thereto , in the extradition of Winston Elkins ,
a fugitive from justice , Leavenworth, Kansas , to Breathitt
County on the twenty -fourth ( 24th ) of November , one thou-
sand nine hundred thirty -three ( 1933 ) .
That , Whereas , on November eight ( 8) , one thousand nine hun-
dred thirty -three ( 1933 ) , Winston Elkins was indicted in the
Breathitt Circuit Court for grand larceny and upon request
of the officials of Breathitt County , Burnam Bach was re-
quested to proceed to Leavenworth , Kansas , and return said

CHAPTER 466 635
Winston Elkins to Breathitt County to answer said indict-
ment , and whereas at the time it was impossible for him to
secure a requisition from the Governor of Kentucky , and that
he in compliance with the request of the officers of Breathitt
County, made the trip to Leavenworth , Kansas , and appre-
hended said Winston Elkins , and conveyed him back to
Breathitt County . Said Elkins was convicted and is now serv-
ing a three-year term in the State penitentiary at Frankfort ,
at the time the sheriff went after the prisoner the officials
were unable to contact the Governor to obtain a requisition ,
and that he immediately filed his claim for said expenses and
six cents (6c ) per mile for one thousand six hundred twenty-
six ( 1,626 ) miles , amounting to ninety -seven dollars and fifty-
six cents ($97.56 ) , and for gas and oil for car of ( $20.32 ) , and
for prisoners food one dollar forty cents ( $1.40 ) , making a
total of one hundred nineteen dollars and twenty -eight cents
( $119.28 ) , and that the Auditor of Public Accounts refused
to pay said claim on the grounds that he had not obtained a
requisition from the Governor of Kentucky : Therefore ,
Be it Resolved by the General Assembly of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky :
That the Auditor of Public Accounts is directed
to draw his warrant upon the treasurer for the sum
of one hundred nineteen dollars and twenty-eight
cents ($119.28 ) payable to Burnam Bach for his
services in the extradition of Winston Elkins from
Leavenworth , Kansas to Breathitt County, incurred
during the month of November , one thousand nine
hundred thirty -three ( 1933 ) .
Neither approved nor disapproved .
